title: Acciughe alla Marinara (Anchovies in the Sailor's Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh anchovies
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil
- Water
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare anchovies in an oil-based stew with minced garlic, parsley, salt, pepper and oil.
2. When almost cooked, add a little water mixed with vinegar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Acciughe Fritte (Fried Anchovies)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Anchovies or sardines
- Flour
- Egg
- Salt
- Oil

## Directions

1. Remove the head from the anchovies and coat them with flour.
2. Take them one by one by the tail, dip them in beaten and well-salted egg.
3. Dip them again in flour and fry them in a pan with boiling oil.
4. For a better result with larger fish, open them along the back by cutting with a fine knife and remove the spine, leaving them joined at the tail.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Acetosa di Lampone (Raspberry Vinegar)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberry syrup
- High-quality wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Form this vinegar by substituting excellent quality wine vinegar for citric acid, which is poured into the raspberry syrup when it is removed from the fire
2. Regulate the dose of vinegar by tasting, putting in a little at first and tasting the syrup in a little water, increasing the dose as needed
3. This beverage will be more refreshing than the others and equally pleasant
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Agnello all'Orientale (Oriental-Style Lamb)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder or leg (cosciotto)
- lard strips
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Lard the lamb entirely with a larding needle using strips of lard seasoned with salt and pepper.
2. Oil it with butter and milk or with milk alone and salt it halfway through cooking.
3. Roast it on a spit until tender and delicate.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Agnello coi Piselli all'Uso di Romagna (Lamb with Peas Romagna Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of lamb from the hind part
- two cloves of garlic cut into strips
- sprigs of rosemary
- a piece of lard or a slice of cured meat
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- a small piece of butter
- tomato juice or tomato preserve dissolved in broth or water
- peas

## Directions

1. Take a quarter of lamb from the hind part, lard it with two cloves of garlic cut into strips and with some sprigs of rosemary.
2. Take a piece of lard or a slice of cured meat and mince them finely with a knife.
3. Put the lamb on the fire in a pan with this battuto and a little oil; season with salt and pepper and let it brown.
4. When it has taken color, add a small piece of butter, tomato juice or tomato preserve dissolved in broth or water, and cook it until perfectly done.
5. Remove the lamb for a moment, pour the peas into the sauce, and when they have boiled a little, put it back on them, cook them together, and serve them as a side dish.
6. The same method can be used to cook a piece of milk veal in the loin or rump.
7. In Tuscany these dishes are prepared in the same manner, but only oil is used.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Agnello in Frittata (Lamb in Omelet)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb loin
- Lard
- Eggs, 4 or 5
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cut a lamb loin, which is the part that best lends itself to this dish, into small pieces and fry it in pure lard; little is enough because in that place the meat is rather fatty.
2. At mid-cooking season the lamb with salt and pepper and when it is totally cooked pour in four or five beaten eggs and lightly seasoned also with salt and pepper.
3. Mix, making sure the eggs set only slightly.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Agnello in Umido (Braised Lamb)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Two young onions or three to four small white onions
- Butter
- Lamb
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Broth

## Directions

1. Slice the onions and sauté them in a piece of butter until they take on a deep red color
2. Add the lamb and season with salt and pepper
3. Wait for the meat to begin to color and add another piece of butter dusted with flour; stir and let it take on a nice color
4. Finish cooking the meat with broth added little by little
5. Serve not dry, but with a certain amount of its gravy
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Agnello Trippato (Lamb with Egg Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams of lamb from the loin
- virgin lard for frying
- garlic
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- two eggs
- parmesan cheese, grated
- half a lemon

## Directions

1. Cut 500 grams of lamb from the loin into small pieces and fry it with virgin lard.
2. Make a soffritto in a pan with the fat remaining in the pan, garlic, and parsley, and when the garlic has taken color, throw in the already fried lamb, season with salt and pepper, turn it well, and leave it on the fire for a while so the seasoning incorporates.
3. Then bind it with the following sauce: beat in a pot two eggs with a good pinch of grated parmesan and half a lemon.
4. Pour it into the lamb, stir, and when the egg has somewhat set, serve at table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Albicocche in Composta (Apricots in Compote)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Slightly underripe apricots, 600 grams
- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Water, one glass

## Directions

1. Make an incision in the apricots to remove the pit in such a way that they do not spoil, and place them on the fire with the aforementioned water
2. When this begins to boil, pour in the sugar and, shaking the saucepan from time to time, let them cook
3. Once they become soft and somewhat wrinkled, remove them one by one with a spoon and place them in a compote dish; drain the liquid they have brought from the saucepan and let the remaining water reduce on the fire, which you will pour into the compote dish over the apricots when it has thickened like a syrup
4. Serve them cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Amaretti Fritti (Fried Amaretti)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 small amaretti
- rum or cognac
- batter (recipe N. 156)
- lard, butter or oil
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Take 20 small amaretti and lightly dip them so they don't soften too much in rum or cognac
2. Roll them in the batter from recipe N. 156, which is a sufficient amount
3. Fry them in lard, butter or oil
4. Lightly dust with powdered sugar and serve them hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Amaretti I (Almond Macaroons)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- White powdered sugar, 250 grams
- Sweet almonds, 100 grams
- Bitter almonds, 50 grams
- Egg whites, 2

## Directions

1. Peel the almonds and dry them in the sun or by the fire, then chop them very fine with a crescent-shaped chopper.
2. Work the sugar and the whites with a spoon for at least half an hour, and add the almonds to form a stiff paste so as to make little balls the size of a small walnut; if it turns out too soft add more sugar and if too hard another bit of white, this time beaten.
3. If you would like to give the macaroons a color tending toward brown, mix a little burnt sugar into the mixture.
4. As you form the said balls, which you will flatten to the thickness of a centimeter, place them on wafers or on pieces of paper.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Amaretti I (Bitter Almond Macaroons)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet almonds, 250 grams
- Bitter almonds, 50 grams
- Powdered sugar, amount not specified
- Egg whites, 2

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds and dry them in the sun or by fire, then chop them very finely with a crescent-shaped knife.
2. Work the sugar and egg whites together with a wooden spoon for at least half an hour, and add the almonds to form a firm paste from which to make small balls about the size of a small walnut; if it turns out too soft add more sugar and if too hard add a little more egg white, this time whipped.
3. If you wish to give the macaroons a color tending toward brown, mix a little browned sugar into the mixture.
4. As you form the said balls, flatten them to the thickness of one centimeter, place them on wafers or on pieces of paper, or in a baking pan buttered and sprinkled with half flour and half powdered sugar; but at a reasonable distance from one another because they spread a lot and puff up, remaining hollow inside.
5. Bake them in an oven at moderate heat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Amaretti II (Bitter Almond Macaroons, Second Recipe)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered white sugar, 300 grams
- Sweet almonds, 180 grams
- Bitter almonds, 20 grams
- Egg whites, 2

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds and dry them in the sun or by fire; then pound them fine in a mortar with one egg white poured in several times.
2. Having done this, mix in half the sugar, working the mixture with a hand.
3. After pour it into a bowl and, always working so it incorporates, add half an egg white, then the other half of the sugar and afterwards the last half egg white.
4. You will obtain, thus worked, a homogeneous mixture of proper consistency that you can shape into a stick to cut into equal pieces.
5. Pick them up one by one with slightly dampened hands to form balls about the size of walnuts.
6. Flatten them to the thickness of one centimeter and for the rest proceed as for the previous ones, but sprinkle them lightly with powdered sugar before putting them in a hot oven, and I say oven because a country oven would not be suitable for this paste.
7. With this amount you will obtain about thirty macaroons.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anatra Domestica (Braised Domestic Duck)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- domestic duck
- onion
- parsley
- carrot
- celery
- tomato sauce or tomato paste
- water or broth
- butter
- Parmigiano cheese
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Prepare it like the mallard duck (recipe 270) and put it on the fire with a similar mixture of finely chopped vegetables.
2. When the duck has taken color, bathe it with tomato sauce or tomato paste and bring it to cook with water or broth.
3. Strain the sauce, remove the excess fat and put it back on the fire with the duck and a small piece of butter.
4. With this sauce and Parmigiano cheese you can dress a soup of strips or homemade lasagne noodles and serve the duck with a side of vegetables cooked in a little of that same sauce.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anatra Domestica Arrosto (Roasted Domestic Duck)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One duck
- Lard, in wide and thin slices
- Oil
- Salt
- String

## Directions

1. Salt it inside and wrap the entire breast with wide and thin slices of lard held in place with string.
2. Brush it with oil and salt it when cooking is almost complete.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anatra Domestica Arrosto (Roasted Domestic Duck)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- domestic duck
- salt
- lard
- oil

## Directions

1. Salt the duck inside
2. Wrap the entire breast with wide, thin slices of lard held in place with twine
3. Oil and salt near the end of cooking
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anatra Domestica col Contorno di Riso (Domestic Duck with Rice Garnish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- domestic duck
- 1/4 of a large onion
- parsley
- carrot
- celery
- 50 grams fat and lean prosciutto
- 2 tablespoons oil
- salt
- pepper
- tomato sauce or tomato paste
- water
- a pinch of dried mushrooms
- 200 grams rice
- 40 grams butter
- hot water
- Parmigiano cheese

## Directions

1. Make a mixture with a quarter of a large onion and all the herbs, that is parsley, carrot and celery chopped together with 50 grams of fat and lean prosciutto.
2. Put it on the fire with two tablespoons of oil and the duck on top, seasoned with salt and pepper.
3. Once browned, bathe it with tomato sauce or tomato paste and the necessary water to bring it to cook, adding at the same time a pinch of dried mushrooms to cook in that sauce which will then be strained through a sieve and defatted, keeping the mushrooms to add to the rice.
4. Put the rice, in the amount of 200 grams, raw as is, in a pot with 40 grams of butter and when it begins to take color pour hot water little by little, giving it flavor with the duck sauce and Parmigiano cheese when you are about to remove it from the fire.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anguilla Arrosto (Roasted Eel)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- bread croutons
- sage or laurel leaves
- salt
- lemon

## Directions

1. If the eel is large and you want to cook it on a spit, it is better to skin it.
2. Cut it into pieces three centimeters long and thread them between two croutons with some sage or laurel leaves if you don't fear that the latter, for its too sharp smell, will turn your stomach.
3. Cook it plain over moderate heat and finally give it a good flame to make a crispy crust.
4. For seasoning, salt only and lemon wedges when brought to the table.
5. Alternatively, for medium-sized eel, roast it on a grill with its skin, which when softened with lemon juice at the table can offer a pleasant flavor when sucked.
6. Season with salt and pepper only. The eels require red and dry wine to accompany them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anguilla coi piselli (Eel with Peas)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- peas
- water

## Directions

1. Stew it as in the recipe for Stewed Eel, and when it is cooked remove it dry to cook the peas in its sauce.
2. Put it back among the peas to reheat it and serve it.
3. No tomato juice is used here, but water if needed.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anguilla col vino (Eel with Wine)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One eel of about half a kilogram or more than one of the same total weight
- sand
- salt
- pepper
- garlic clove
- sage leaves
- lemon rind
- oil
- tomato juice or tomato preserve
- red or white dry wine
- water

## Directions

1. Rub the eels with sand to clean them of mucus, wash them, and cut them into chunks.
2. Place in a pan one garlic clove cut into thin slices, three or four sage leaves cut roughly, the rind of a quarter lemon, and not much oil.
3. Put it on the fire, and when the soffritto has taken on color, place the eels in it and season with salt and pepper.
4. When the stew begins to reduce, loosen them with the tip of a knife so they do not stick, and brown them on one side.
5. Brown them on the other side, pour in a good finger of red or white dry wine mixed with two fingers of water, cover them, and let them finish cooking over low heat.
6. Serve them at table with some of their sauce and serve to four people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anguilla in Gratella (Grilled Eel)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- medium eel
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place the medium eel on a grill with its skin.
2. Season with salt and pepper only at half cooking.
3. The lemon juice softens the skin when it is brought to the table and can offer a pleasant flavor when sucked.
4. Eat them hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anguilla in umido (Stewed Eel)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eels
- onion
- parsley
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- tomato juice or tomato preserve
- water
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. If the eels are large, do not peel them; cut them into short pieces.
2. Finely chop a generous amount of onion and parsley, put it on the fire with a little oil, pepper, and salt.
3. When the onion has taken on color, add the eel.
4. Wait for it to absorb the flavor of the soffritto, then finish cooking it with tomato juice or tomato preserve dissolved in water.
5. Ensure there remains plenty of sauce if you want to serve it at table over barely toasted bread slices.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anguilla in umido all'uso di Comacchio (Stewed Eel Comacchio Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One kilogram of eels
- three onions
- celery
- carrot
- parsley
- lemon rind
- water
- salt
- pepper
- strong vinegar
- tomato preserve
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Cut all the vegetables except the lemon into large pieces and boil them with water, salt, and pepper.
2. Cut the eels into chunks, leaving the chunks connected to each other by a strip of flesh.
3. Take a suitable pot and make a layer of eel at the bottom, then layer the vegetables mentioned above that are almost cooked (discarding the lemon), then another layer of eel, another of vegetables, and so on until everything is used.
4. Cover everything with the water in which the vegetables boiled.
5. Put the well-sealed pot to boil slowly, shaking and turning it, but never stirring with a spoon as you would break everything up.
6. Surround the pot with ash and embers up to more than halfway, in front of a clear wood fire, always shaking and turning.
7. When the chunks, which were connected by a strip, become detached from each other, they are almost cooked.
8. Add a good spoonful of strong vinegar and tomato preserve, taste the broth to correct the salt and pepper (be generous), give it a few more boils, and serve the pot at table.
9. Serve in hot dishes on bread slices.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anguille alla fiorentina (Eels Florentine Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized eels
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- garlic cloves
- sage leaves
- breadcrumbs
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the eels by making a circular incision under the head, holding it firmly with a cloth to prevent slipping due to the fish's abundant mucus, and pull down the skin which will come off entirely.
2. Cut the eels into pieces about one finger length or slightly less, and season them with oil, salt, and pepper, leaving them to rest for one or two hours.
3. Use an iron pan or earthenware dish, cover the bottom with a thin layer of oil, two whole garlic cloves, and sage leaves.
4. Sauté briefly, then take the eel pieces one at a time, coat them in breadcrumbs, and arrange them in the pan side by side, pouring the remaining seasoning over them.
5. Cook them between two fires, and when they have taken on color, pour a little water into the pan.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Animelle alla Bottiglia (Sweetbreads in Bottle Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb sweetbreads or from larger animals
- Water for partial cooking
- Flour
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Marsala or Madeira wine
- Optional: meat broth, butter and flour for sauce

## Directions

1. Those of lamb need no preparation; those of larger animals must be given half-cooking in water, skinning them if necessary. Leave the first ones whole, cut the second ones into pieces and flour all of them well and put them to brown in butter seasoning them with salt and pepper. Then moisten them with Marsala or Madeira wine, and after make them come to a single boil. You can also make the sauce separately with a pinch of flour, a small piece of butter and the wine.
2. If you enhance them with meat sauce, from being good as they are, they will become exquisite.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Anolini alla Parmigiana (Parmesan-Style Anolini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean beef from the thigh, boneless, 500 grams
- Lard, about 20 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- One quarter of a medium onion
- Breadcrumbs from day-old bread, lightly toasted, 100 grams
- Grated Parmesan, 50 grams
- Nutmeg scent
- One egg and the meat broth
- Three eggs for the pasta sheets
- Red wine
- Salt, pepper and spice scent

## Directions

1. Lard the piece of meat, tie it and season with salt, pepper and spice scent, then put it on the fire in an earthenware pot or casserole with butter and finely chopped onion to brown it with the butter.
2. Pour two ladle-fuls of broth into the pot and close it with several sheets of paper held secure by a bowl containing some red wine.
3. Boil the meat gently in this way for eight or nine hours, to obtain four or five spoonfuls of a reduced and flavorful broth which you will strain through a sieve, squeezing well, and which you will save for the next day.
4. Form the filling for the Anolini with: toasted breadcrumbs, grated Parmesan, nutmeg scent, one egg and the meat broth. Make everything into a homogeneous mixture.
5. Make three sheets of pasta kept somewhat tender and fill the scalloped disc, folding it in two to obtain the shape of a small half moon. With this amount you will obtain about one hundred, which will be good in broth or dry like tortellini.
6. Cook the remaining meat alone or with a side of vegetables and it will be a stracotto.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arigusta (Lobster)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster
- onion
- carrots
- parsley
- bay leaves (2)
- vinegar (2 tablespoons)
- salt
- oil
- lemon

## Directions

1. Fold the lobster's tail under and tie it before throwing it into boiling water
2. Flavor the water with a bundle made of onion, carrots, parsley and two bay leaves, adding two tablespoons of vinegar and a pinch of salt
3. Boil for 30 to 40 minutes depending on its size
4. Let the lobster cool in its broth
5. When removed, drain the water by squeezing the tail and after drying it, rub it with a few drops of oil to make it shiny
6. Send to table with an incision from head to tail to easily extract the flesh
7. Serve simply seasoned with oil and lemon juice, or accompanied with mayonnaise sauce or other spicy sauce
8. Alternatively serve with a sauce made as follows: extract the flesh from the head and mince it finely with a hard-boiled egg yolk and some parsley leaves, place the mixture in a sauce boat, season with pepper, little or no salt and dilute with fine oil and the juice of half a lemon, or vinegar
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Aringa Ingentilita (Refined Herring)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- herring, male preferred
- milk, boiling
- oil
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Open the herring from the back, remove the head and flatten it
2. Place in infusion in boiling milk and leave for eight to ten hours, changing the milk once if possible
3. Dry with a cloth
4. Cook on a grill like common herring
5. Season with oil and very little vinegar, or if preferred, with oil and lemon juice
6. Alternatively, place the herring on the fire with cold water, bring to boil for three minutes, then hold briefly in fresh water, dry it, remove the head, open from the back and season as above
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arista (Tuscan Roasted Pork Loin)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork loin with ribs, 3-4 kilograms
- Garlic
- Rosemary sprigs
- Cloves, a few
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Lard the meat with garlic, bunches of rosemary, and a few cloves of garlic, but sparingly, as these are flavors that easily become overwhelming.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Cook it roasted on a spit, which is better, or send it to the oven without further ado.
4. Use the fat that it renders for browning potatoes or for making vegetable dishes.
5. Serve cold.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arnioni alla Fiorentina (Kidneys Florentine-Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- kidneys
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Open and remove fat from the kidneys as in recipe N. 291 and split them in half lengthwise.
2. Place a pan on the heat with a piece of butter proportionate to the amount and when it is about to boil, place the kidney in it, leaving it for a moment, then remove it from the heat and season with salt, pepper and a pinch of chopped parsley.
3. Coat it well in the seasoning and, after several hours, cook it in the same pan, or on a grill, coated in bread crumbs.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arnioni alla Parigina (Kidneys Parisian-Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal kidney
- 30 grams of butter
- salt
- pepper
- one large or two small anchovies

## Directions

1. Take a veal kidney, remove the fat, open it and cover it with boiling water.
2. When the water has cooled, dry it well with a cloth and thread it lengthwise and crosswise with clean sticks so that it stays open (in Paris silver pins are used).
3. Season it with 30 grams of melted butter, salt and pepper, and leave it prepared this way for an hour or two.
4. Given that the kidney weighs 600 or 700 grams, take another 30 grams of butter and one large or two small anchovies and clean them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arnioni alla Parigina (Veal Kidney Prepared in the Parisian Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal kidney (a piece of veal)
- 30 grams butter, melted
- salt
- pepper
- 30 grams butter
- one large anchovy or two small anchovies

## Directions

1. Take a veal kidney, remove the fat, open it and cover it with boiling water.
2. When the water has cooled, dry it well with a cloth and skewer it lengthwise and crosswise with clean skewers so that it stays open (in Paris they use silver pins).
3. Season it with 30 grams of melted butter, salt and pepper, and let it sit prepared for one or two hours.
4. If the kidney weighs 600 or 700 grams, take another 30 grams of butter and one large anchovy or two small ones, clean them, chop them and crush them with the blade of a knife together with the butter and form them into a ball.
5. Cook the kidney on a grill, but not too much so that it remains tender, place it on a platter, spread it while hot with the ball of butter and anchovy and serve it to the table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arnioni per Colazione (Kidneys for Breakfast)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- milk-fed veal, castrated lamb, pork or similar kidneys
- parsley
- half a clove of garlic
- juice of half a lemon
- five or six slices of bread marrow, dried by the heat
- 400 or 500 grams total kidney weight
- 50 to 60 grams of butter
- salt
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. Prepare a fine battutino (small chopped mixture) composed of parsley, half a clove of garlic, the juice of half a lemon and five or six slices of bread marrow, dried by the heat.
2. Open the kidneys to remove the fat and cut them into thin slices crosswise.
3. For a total of 400 or 500 grams, throw them in a pan with 50 to 60 grams of butter over high heat.
4. Move them often and as soon as they begin to brown add the battutino, season with salt and pepper and, always moving them with a spoon, pour in the lemon juice and finally a ladle of broth.
5. The operation should take about five minutes total and before serving pour them over the slices of bread.
6. This will be enough for four people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arnioni Saltati (Sautéed Kidneys)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one large animal kidney (or several from smaller animals)
- salt
- hot water
- flour
- butter
- white wine
- parsley
- broth

## Directions

1. Open the kidney and remove all the fat as it has an unpleasant odor.
2. Cut it crosswise into thin slices, place in a bowl, salt it and pour over it enough boiling water to cover.
3. When the water has cooled, remove it dry and place in a pan to evaporate the water, which you will discard.
4. Sprinkle a pinch of flour over it, add a small piece of butter and, stirring often, let it grill for only five minutes.
5. Season with salt, pepper and a scant half glass of white wine, leave it briefly longer on the heat and when about to remove it add another small piece of butter, a pinch of chopped parsley and a bit of broth if needed.
6. Note: kidneys held too long on the heat become tough. It is good to boil the wine separately first until reduced by a third; if instead of white wine you use marsala or champagne, so much the better.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrostini di Vitella di Latte alla Salvia (Milk-Fed Veal Cutlets with Sage)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk-fed veal loin free of skin and with its bone attached, cut into thin scaloppine
- Sage leaves
- Butter, proportioned amount
- Salt
- Flour, one teaspoon
- Marsala wine, just under one finger (of a glass)

## Directions

1. Use a sauté pan or a copper skillet, and put it on the fire with some whole sage leaves and a proportioned piece of butter. When it has sautéed a little, throw in the scaloppine and while they brown over high heat, salt them on both sides, then sprinkle a little flour over them and finish cooking them with Marsala. They should remain with little moisture.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto d'Agnello all'Aretina (Lamb Roast Aretine Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a leg or quarter of lamb
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- a drop of vinegar
- a sprig of rosemary

## Directions

1. Season the lamb with salt, pepper, oil and a drop of vinegar.
2. Pierce it here and there with the point of a knife and let it sit in this mixture for several hours.
3. Thread it onto the spit and frequently baste it with this liquid using a sprig of rosemary until cooked, which serves to remove any stable smell from the lamb, if you fear it has one, and to give it a pleasant taste.
4. If you prefer a more pronounced rosemary aroma, you can stud the piece with some sprigs of the same, removing them before serving at table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto di Lepre I (Roasted Hare I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- hind quarters of hare
- three glasses of water
- half a glass of vinegar or less
- three or four shallots
- one or two bay leaves
- a bunch of parsley
- salt
- pepper
- lardons of fine quality lard
- cream

## Directions

1. Infuse the hare for twelve to fourteen hours in a liquid prepared as follows: put on the fire three glasses of water with half a glass of vinegar or less in proportion to the size, three or four chopped shallots, one or two bay leaves, a small bunch of parsley, a bit of salt and a pinch of pepper; boil for five or six minutes and pour cold over the hare.
2. Remove from infusion and dry it well.
3. Stud it entirely with fine lardons using a larding needle.
4. Cook over gentle heat, salt sufficiently and baste with cream and nothing else.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto di Lepre II (Roasted Hare II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- hind quarters of well-aged hare
- lardons of salted lard
- butter
- paper
- salt
- a sprig of rosemary

## Directions

1. Remove the thickest membranes from the external muscles.
2. Stud the entire piece with lardons of lard that you have salted beforehand.
3. Thread onto the spit, wrap it in buttered paper sprinkled with salt.
4. When cooked, remove the paper and brush with a sprig of rosemary dipped in butter, make it brown, salting it a bit more.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto di vitella di latte (Roasted Milk-Fed Veal)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- milk-fed veal, loin or rump
- oil
- salt

## Directions

1. The pieces most suitable for roasting on a spit are the loin and the rump, and they need only oil and salt for seasoning.
2. These same pieces can also be cooked in a pan, lightly larded with garlic and rosemary, with oil, butter and a small amount of minced cured meat, salt, pepper and tomato juice, cooking fresh peas in the braising liquid.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto morto (Braised Veal)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- milk-fed veal, a nice piece from the loin including the stone
- oil
- butter
- salt
- broth

## Directions

1. Roll the meat and tie it with string so it stays together.
2. Place it in a pot with a small amount of fine oil and butter.
3. Brown it on all sides.
4. Salt it halfway through cooking and finish cooking it with broth so that little or no sauce remains.
5. If you do not have broth, use tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in water.
6. If you like it more flavorful, add finely minced cured meat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto morto coll'odore dell'aglio e del ramerino (Braised Veal with Garlic and Rosemary)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- milk-fed veal from the loin
- oil
- butter
- salt
- broth
- garlic, whole cloves
- rosemary sprigs
- potatoes or vegetables
- tomato juice or paste (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal as in the previous recipe for braising in a pot with oil and butter.
2. Instead of studding the meat with garlic and rosemary pieces, throw a whole clove of garlic and two sprigs of rosemary into the pot.
3. When you send the roast to the table, strain its reduced sauce without squeezing it and surround the meat with potatoes or vegetables cooked separately if you wish.
4. If you like, you can also enhance the meat with a very small amount of tomato juice or paste.
5. Lamb shanks are also very good prepared this way, cooked between two fires.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto morto coll'odore dell'aglio e del ramerino - Lamb Version (Braised Lamb Shanks with Garlic and Rosemary)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shanks
- oil
- butter
- salt
- broth
- garlic
- rosemary

## Directions

1. Cook the lamb shanks between two fires following the method for braised veal with garlic and rosemary, placing whole garlic cloves and rosemary sprigs in the pot during cooking.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto Morto di Pollo alla Bolognese (Dead Roast Chicken Bologna Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Oil
- Butter
- One slice of fat and lean prosciutto, finely minced
- A few small pieces of garlic
- One small sprig of rosemary
- Tomatoes in neat pieces without seeds, or tomato paste dissolved in water
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Place the chicken over the fire with oil, butter, finely minced fat and lean prosciutto, a few small pieces of garlic, and one small sprig of rosemary.
2. When it is browned, add tomatoes in neat pieces without seeds, or tomato paste dissolved in water.
3. Once the chicken is cooked, remove it and in that sauce cook potatoes cut into small pieces, then return the chicken to the fire to reheat it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arrosto Morto Lardellato (Braised Larded Roast)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a short, thick piece of lean veal or beef, from the thigh or rump, about one kilogram
- 30 grams of fat and lean ham cut into thin slices
- 30 grams of butter
- one quarter of an onion divided into two pieces
- three or four celery ribs less than a finger length
- the same number of carrot strips
- salt
- pepper
- water
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Stud the meat with the ham slices using a larding needle.
2. Tie it with string to keep it compact and place it in a pot with butter, onion pieces, celery ribs and carrot strips.
3. Season with salt and pepper and when the meat has browned, turning it often, moisten with two small ladles of water.
4. Cook over gentle heat, allowing much of the moisture to reduce, but ensure it does not dry out and become black.
5. When serving, strain the small amount of remaining sauce and pour it over the meat, which can be surrounded with potato wedges browned in butter or oil.
6. Alternatively, you can put the braised roast on the fire with only butter and cook it in the covered pot with a bowl full of water placed on the lid.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Arselle o Telline alla Livornese (Mussels or Clams Livorno Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- clams or mussels
- onion
- oil
- pepper
- parsley
- tomato juice or tomato paste
- bread

## Directions

1. Make a fine mixture of onion and put it on the fire with oil and a pinch of pepper. When the onion has taken color, add a pinch of finely chopped parsley and shortly after add the clams or mussels with tomato juice or tomato paste. Shake them often and when they open, pour them over slices of toasted bread prepared beforehand on a platter.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Babà (Italian Sweet Yeast Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Hungarian flour or finest flour, 250 grams
- Butter, 70 grams
- Powdered sugar, 50 grams
- Sultana raisins, also called currants, 50 grams
- Malaga raisins with seeds removed, 30 grams
- Beer yeast, 30 grams
- Milk or, better, cream, approximately 1 deciliter
- Eggs, 2 whole and 1 yolk
- Marsala, 1 tablespoon
- Rum or cognac, 1 tablespoon
- Candied fruit cut into strips, 10 grams
- Salt, a pinch
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix the beer yeast with a quarter of the flour and a little of the lukewarm milk, and form a dough of appropriate consistency. Make a cross cut in the dough with a knife so that it shows the necessary swelling in time. Allow it to rise near the fire at very moderate heat, in a covered vessel with a drop of milk, which will take about half an hour.
2. While it rises, beat the eggs in a bowl with the sugar; then add the remaining flour, the risen small dough, the melted and lukewarm butter, the marsala and rum, and if the dough becomes too stiff, soften it with lukewarm milk. Work it thoroughly with a spoon until the mixture detaches from the bowl.
3. Finally add the raisins and candied fruit, and allow it to rise. When it has puffed up, stir it slightly with a spoon and pour it into a buttered mold dusted with powdered sugar mixed with flour.
4. The best mold form for this cake is copper with ribs; but note that it must be twice as large as the contents. Cover it with a lid so it does not get air and place it in a warm bath or inside a country oven, very slightly hot, to rise it; this will take perhaps more than two hours. If the rising is perfect you will see the mixture grow to double, that is, reach the mouth of the mold. Then bake it, taking care that it does not get air in the meantime. Doneness is tested by inserting a pomegranate stick which should come out dry; nevertheless leave it to dry further in the oven at moderate heat, which is necessary because of its thickness. When the Babà is turned out, if it is well baked, it should...
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Baccalà alla Bolognese (Salt Cod Bologna Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- oil
- garlic
- parsley
- pepper
- butter pieces
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces as large as the previous recipe and place raw in a pan or baking dish oiled
2. Sprinkle with a finely chopped mixture of garlic and parsley on top and season with pepper, oil and small pieces of butter
3. Cook over high heat and turn gently because, not being floured, it breaks easily
4. When cooked, squeeze lemon over it and serve
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Baccalà alla Fiorentina (Salt Cod Florentine Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- flour
- oil
- garlic cloves, whole and slightly crushed
- pepper
- tomato sauce or tomato conserve diluted in water

## Directions

1. Cut the salt cod into pieces as wide as the palm of your hand and flour well
2. Heat a pan or baking dish with considerable oil and two or three whole, slightly crushed garlic cloves over the fire
3. When the garlic begins to color, add the salt cod and brown on both sides, stirring often so it does not stick
4. Season with little to no salt (taste first) and a pinch of pepper
5. Finally pour a few spoonfuls of tomato sauce or diluted tomato conserve over it
6. Boil a little longer and serve
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Baccalà Dolce-Forte (Salt Cod Sweet and Sour)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- oil
- flour
- pepper
- vinegar
- water
- sugar
- pine nuts
- raisins

## Directions

1. Cook like salt cod recipe 506, without garlic, and when browned on both sides, pour the sweet-sour sauce over it
2. Boil a little longer and serve hot
3. For the sweet-sour sauce, prepare in advance in a glass: if the salt cod weighs about 500 grams, use one finger-length of strong vinegar, two finger-lengths of water, sugar to taste, pine nuts and raisins in proportion
4. It is good to boil this sauce separately a bit before pouring it over the salt cod
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Baccalà Fritto (Fried Salt Cod)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- water
- red wine
- cloth
- batter

## Directions

1. To boil it, place in cold water over the fire and remove as soon as it reaches a boil, as it is then cooked
2. Without further handling it can be eaten seasoned with oil and vinegar
3. Alternatively, after boiling, place the whole piece of salt cod in infusion in red wine and keep for several hours
4. Dry in a cloth and cut
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Baccalà in Gratella (Salt Cod Grilled)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- white paper
- oil
- pepper
- rosemary sprigs, optional

## Directions

1. To keep it less dry, cook slowly over a sheet of white paper, oiled beforehand
2. Season with oil, pepper and, if desired, a few small sprigs of rosemary
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Baccalà in Pastella (Salt Cod in Batter)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt cod
- red wine
- flour
- water
- oil
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the salt cod in cold water and remove it as soon as it reaches its first boil.
2. Place the whole piece of salt cod in an infusion of red wine and keep it there for several hours.
3. Dry it in a cloth and cut it into small pieces, cleaning it of bones and spines.
4. Lightly flour it and dip it into a simple batter made of water, flour, and a drop of oil without salt.
5. Fry it in oil and sprinkle it with sugar when it has lost its first boil.
6. Serve hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Baccalà in Salsa Bianca (Salt Cod in White Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt cod, soaked, 400 grams
- butter, 70 grams
- flour, 30 grams
- potato, about 150 grams
- milk, 3 1/2 deciliters
- parsley, chopped
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil the salt cod and clean it of skin, spines, and bones.
2. Boil the potato and cut it into small pieces.
3. Make a béchamel sauce with the milk and flour.
4. When cooked, add some chopped parsley and flavor with nutmeg.
5. Pour in the potato pieces and salt.
6. Add the salt cod in pieces, mix well, and after a brief rest serve it.
7. Garnish with hard-boiled egg wedges if desired.
8. This quantity is sufficient for four people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Baccalà montebianco (Salt Cod Montebianco)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fleshy soaked salt cod, 500 grams
- Superfine oil, 200 grams
- Cream or excellent milk, 1 deciliter

## Directions

1. Clean the salt cod of its bones, small bones, skin and sinews, which present themselves like threads; this will leave approximately 340 grams of cleaned cod.
2. After having prepared it thus, pound it in a mortar and then place it in a small pot, together with the cream or milk, over a fire that is not too hot, stirring continuously.
3. When it has absorbed the cream or milk, begin to pour the oil drop by drop, as you would do for mayonnaise, always working it very much with the "wielded weapon", that is with the wooden spoon, so that it becomes refined and does not separate.
4. Remove it when it seems to you cooked to the point and serve it cold with a garnish of raw truffles cut into very thin slices, or with slices of fried bread, or caviar crostini.
5. This quantity can suffice for eight persons.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Balsamella (Béchamel Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 1 tablespoon
- Butter, the volume of an egg
- Milk, half a liter of the best

## Directions

1. Put in a pan on the fire a tablespoon of flour and a piece of butter the volume of an egg.
2. Use a spoon to dissolve the butter and flour together and when this begins to take a hazelnut color, pour in half a liter of the best milk little by little, stirring continuously with the spoon until you see the liquid thickened like a cream of milky color.
3. This is the balsamella.
4. If it becomes too thick add milk, if too thin put it back on the fire with another small piece of butter mixed with flour.
5. The dose is abundant, but you can proportion it according to circumstances.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bastoncelli Croccanti (Crunchy Breadsticks)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 150 grams
- Butter, 60 grams
- Powdered sugar, 60 grams
- One egg
- Grated lemon peel flavoring

## Directions

1. Make a dough without stirring it too much.
2. Roll it out thin to obtain two dozen breadsticks ten centimeters long.
3. Bake them in a country oven on a baking sheet without any preparation.
4. They can be served with tea or with bottled wine.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bavarese lombarda (Bavarian Cream Lombard Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter of good quality and very fresh, 180 grams
- Powdered sugar, 180 grams
- Long savoiardi or sponge cake, approximately 150 grams
- Hard-boiled egg yolks, 6
- Vanilla sugar, enough to give it the flavor
- Rosolio liqueur, as much as needed to lightly dip the savoiardi

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs for only seven minutes and remove the yolks, dissolve them in the butter, then pass them through a sieve, then add the powdered sugar and the vanilla sugar, and work the mixture well with a spoon to whip it
2. Take a mold, preferably one with ribs, dampen it with rosolio, cut in half lengthwise the savoiardi, dip them lightly in the rosolio, or half in rosolio and half in alkermes and with these line the mold alternating the two colors
3. Then pour the mixture in the middle, cover it with other savoiardi dipped in the same way, leave it in the ice for at least three hours and serve it
4. The mixture, if convenient, can be made a day ahead and this quantity is enough for eight people. It is a very refined pudding
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bianco Mangiare (Almond Blancmange)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet almonds with three bitter almonds, 150 grams
- Powdered sugar, 150 grams
- Fish gelatin in sheets, 20 grams
- Cream or milk, half a glass
- Water, one and a half glasses
- Orange blossom water, two tablespoons

## Directions

1. First prepare the fish gelatin by pressing it with your fingers at the bottom of a glass, cover it with water and let it soften, then when you need it, discard the water and wash it
2. Shell and pound the almonds in a mortar, moistening them from time to time with a teaspoon of water, and when you have reduced them to a fine paste, dilute them with the aforementioned water and pass them through a strong, fine cloth, taking care to extract all the substance
3. At this point, prepare a mold of appropriate capacity
4. Then put on the fire in a pan the almond milk, the cream, the sugar, the gelatin, the orange blossom water; mix everything together and let it boil for a few minutes
5. Remove it from the fire and when it has cooled, pour it into the mold immersed in fresh water or ice
6. To unmold it, simply pass a cloth dampened in boiling water around the mold
7. Note: boiling is necessary so that the fish gelatin incorporates with the rest; otherwise it may precipitate to the bottom of the mold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotti (Cookies)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 250 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Powdered sugar, 50 grams
- Ammonia powder, 5 grams
- A pinch of salt
- Vanilla flavoring with vanilla sugar
- Lukewarm milk, approximately one deciliter

## Directions

1. Make a well in the mound of flour and place in it the aforementioned ingredients except the milk, which you will use to knead this dough, which should turn out soft and must be worked thoroughly to refine it
2. Then stretch out a sheet about one scudo thick, dusting it with flour if needed, and finally pass over it a ridged rolling pin, or use a grater or fork to make some decorations
3. After that, cut the cookies into whatever shape you prefer, or if you do not want to make long strips just slightly longer than a finger and two centimeters wide as I do
4. Place them directly in a copper baking pan and bake them in the oven or in a country oven
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotti Croccanti I (Crunchy Biscuits I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 500 grams
- Powdered sugar, 220 grams
- Sweet almonds, whole and blanched mixed with some pine nuts, 120 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Anise, a pinch
- Eggs, 5
- Salt, a pinch

## Directions

1. Set aside the almonds and pine nuts to add later.
2. Mix everything together with four eggs, keeping the fifth egg in reserve in case it is needed to form a somewhat soft dough.
3. Make four loaves the thickness of a finger and as wide as a hand.
4. Place them on a baking sheet buttered and floured, and gild them on top.
5. Do not bake them so much that they cannot be sliced, which will be better done the next day when the crust softens.
6. Put the slices back in the oven to toast them lightly on both sides, and you will have crunchy biscuits.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotti Croccanti II (Crunchy Biscuits II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 400 grams
- Sugar, 200 grams
- Butter, 80 grams
- Almonds, 40 grams
- Sultana raisins, 30 grams
- Pine nuts, 20 grams
- Citron or candied squash, 20 grams
- Anise, a pinch
- Spirits of wine, 2 tablespoons
- Baking soda, a scant teaspoon
- One whole egg and three yolks

## Directions

1. These biscuits are finer than the previous ones.
2. Blanch the almonds, and leave both these and the pine nuts whole.
3. Cut the candied fruit into small pieces.
4. Make a well in the mound of flour and place the eggs, sugar, butter, spirits, and baking soda in it.
5. Mix the dough without working it too much, then open it and spread it to add the rest.
6. Roll out a somewhat compressed stick about one meter long, divide it into four or five parts so it fits in the baking sheet.
7. Gild it and bake it in the oven.
8. Once baked, cut it in the shape of biscuits into slices slightly thicker than one centimeter.
9. Toast them lightly on both sides.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotti da Famiglia (Family Biscuits)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 250 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Powdered sugar, 50 grams
- Ammonium powder, 5 grams
- A pinch of salt
- Vanilla flavoring with vanilla sugar
- Lukewarm milk, about one deciliter

## Directions

1. Make a well in the mound of flour and place the ingredients listed above except the milk, which you will use to mix this dough, which should be soft and must be stirred well to refine it.
2. Roll out a sheet about the thickness of a scudo coin, dusting it with flour if necessary.
3. Finally pass a notched rolling pin over it, or use a grater or a fork to make some decoration.
4. Cut the biscuits in the shape you prefer.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotti della Salute (Health Cookies)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 350 grams
- Brown sugar, 100 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Cream of tartar, 10 grams
- Baking soda, 5 grams
- Eggs, 2
- Vanilla sugar flavoring
- Milk, as needed

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar with the flour, make a well in it to place the rest and knead it with the addition of a little milk to obtain a somewhat soft dough, which you will shape into a slightly flattened cylindrical form about half a meter long
2. To bake it in the oven or country oven, butter a baking pan, and since this stick must fit in it, divide it into two pieces, keeping them separate as they will puff up greatly
3. The next day, cut them into cookie shapes, from which you will get about thirty, and toast them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotti Teneri (Soft Biscuits)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Wheat flour, 40 grams
- Potato flour, 30 grams
- Sugar, 90 grams
- Sweet almonds, 40 grams
- Candied citron or orange, 20 grams
- Fruit preserve, 20 grams
- Eggs, 3

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, cut them in half crosswise and dry them in the sun or by the fire.
2. Cut the candied fruit and preserve, which may be quince or other firm fruit, into small dice.
3. Work the egg yolks with the sugar and a little of the flour for more than half an hour.
4. Add the stiffly beaten egg whites and after mixing them in, add the flour, letting it fall from a small sieve.
5. Mix gently and scatter the almonds, candied fruit, and preserve into the dough.
6. Butter a tin box and dust it with flour.
7. Cut the biscuits the next day, toasting them on both sides if you like.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscottini Puerperali (Puerperal Biscuits)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks, 8
- Powdered sugar, 150 grams
- Cocoa powder, 40 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- Vanilla flavoring by means of vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Place these ingredients in a vessel and work them with a spoon for over a quarter of an hour
2. Then pour the mixture into four paper boxes, approximately eight centimeters long and six centimeters wide
3. Place them in a covered copper baking pan, set it over a burner with very little fire underneath and on top so the mixture firms up somewhat without forming a crust, as it must be taken up in spoonfuls
4. Therefore the name biscottini is quite improper
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotto (Sponge Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs, 6
- powdered sugar, 250 grams
- wheat flour, 100 grams
- potato flour, 50 grams
- butter, 30 grams
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Work for at least half an hour the egg yolks with the sugar and one tablespoon of the said flours, using a spoon. Beat the egg whites until very stiff and add them; mix gently and, when they are blended together, let the two flours fall from a small sieve, which you will have dried in the sun or over heat beforehand.
2. Bake it in the oven or country oven in a pan where it comes up about three fingers high, but first grease it with cold butter and dust it with powdered sugar mixed with flour.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotto alla Sultana (Sultan's Cookie)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered sugar, 150 grams
- Wheat flour, 100 grams
- Potato flour, 50 grams
- Sultana raisins, 80 grams
- Candied fruit, 20 grams
- Eggs, 5
- Lemon zest flavoring
- Rum or cognac, two tablespoons

## Directions

1. First place on the fire the raisins and candied fruit cut to the size of watermelon seeds with enough cognac or rum to cover them; when this boils, ignite it and let it burn outside the fire until the liquor is consumed; then remove this mixture and let it dry among the folds of a towel
2. After this operation, work very well with a wooden spoon for half an hour the sugar and egg yolks where you have placed the lemon zest
3. Beat the egg whites until stiff with a whisk, and pour them into the mixture; then add the two flours letting them fall through a small sieve and at the same time mix slowly and gently so that everything combines well, without working it too much
4. Finally add the raisins, candied fruit and the two tablespoons of rum or cognac mentioned and pour the mixture into a smooth mold or in a copper pot that gives the dessert a tall and round shape
5. Butter the mold and dust it with powdered sugar and flour, being careful to put it in the oven immediately to prevent the raisins and candied fruit from sinking to the bottom. If this happens, another time leave out one egg white
6. Serve cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotto da Servirsi con lo Zabaione (Cake to Serve with Zabaglione)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potato starch flour, 50 grams
- Wheat flour, 20 grams
- Powdered sugar, 90 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Lemon zest flavor

## Directions

1. First work the egg yolks with the sugar for almost half an hour, add the well-beaten egg whites and let the flour fall from a sifter, mixing everything nicely so that the mixture remains light
2. Pour it into a ring mold that you have first buttered and dusted with flour and powdered sugar
3. Put it immediately in the oven to cook, and once cooled and unmolded, pour the zabaglione from recipe 684 into the hole and send it immediately to the table
4. This is a dose that can serve five or six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Biscotto di Cioccolata (Chocolate Sponge Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs, 6
- powdered sugar, 200 grams
- wheat flour, 150 grams
- vanilla chocolate, 50 grams

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate and put it in a bowl with the sugar and egg yolks and beat them with a spoon; then add the flour and work the mixture for more than half an hour; finally the beaten egg whites mixing gently.
2. Bake it as the previous recipe.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bistecca alla Fiorentina (Florentine-Style Steak)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- beef steak from the loin, thick as one or one and a half fingers
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Place the steak on the grill over hot charcoal, either natural as it comes from the animal or at most washed and dried.
2. Turn it several times and season with salt and pepper when it is cooked.
3. Send it to table with a small piece of butter on top.
4. It should not be too cooked because its beauty is that when you cut it, it releases abundant juice on the plate.
5. Do not salt it before cooking because the heat will dry it, and if you season it beforehand with oil or other things, as many do, it will taste rancid and be nauseating.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bistecca nel Tegame (Steak in the Pan)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one large steak
- a piece of butter
- a little oil
- garlic
- rosemary
- broth or water (if needed)
- tomato juice or puree (optional)
- potatoes

## Directions

1. If you have a large steak that, for being from an animal not very young or not recently slaughtered, makes you doubt its tenderness, instead of cooking it on the grill, place it in a pan with a piece of butter and a little oil.
2. Proceed as indicated, give it the flavor of garlic and rosemary.
3. Add, if necessary, a little broth or water, or tomato juice and serve it at table with potatoes cut into chunks cooked in its gravy, and if that is not enough, add more broth, butter and tomato preserves.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bocca di Dama I (Lady's Mouth I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchero in polvere, 250 grammi
- Farina d'Ungheria o finissima, 150 grammi
- Mandorle dolci con alcune amare, 50 grammi
- Uova intere, 6 e rossi 3
- Odore di scorza di limone

## Directions

1. Le mandorle, dopo averle sbucciate e asciugate bene, pestatele in un mortaio con una cucchiaiata del detto zucchero e mescolatele alla farina in modo che non appariscano bozzoli
2. Il resto dello zucchero ponetelo in una catinella coi rossi d'uovo e la raschiatura del limone, lavorandoli con un mestolo per un quarto d'ora
3. Versate la farina e lavorate ancora per più di mezz'ora
4. Montate con la frusta, in un vaso a parte, le sei chiare e quando saranno ben sode da sostenere un pezzo da due lire d'argento, versatele nella menzionata catinella e mescolate adagino adagino ogni cosa insieme
5. Versatela in una teglia di rame unta col burro e spolverizzata di zucchero a velo e farina, oppure in un cerchio di legno da staccio il cui fondo sia stato chiuso con un foglio
6. Cuocete in forno
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bocca di Dama II (Lady's Mouth II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchero, 250 grammi
- Farina finissima, 100 grammi
- Mandorle dolci con tre amare, 50 grammi
- Uova, 9
- Odore di scorza di limone

## Directions

1. Sbucciate le mandorle, asciugatele bene al sole o al fuoco, pestatele finissime con una cucchiaiata del detto zucchero e mescolatele alla farina
2. Il resto dello zucchero e i rossi delle uova uniteli insieme in una bacinella di rame o di ottone, e sopra al fuoco, a poco calore, batteteli colla frusta per più di un quarto d'ora
3. Versateci poscia, fuori del fuoco, la farina preparata con le mandorle, la scorza di limone grattata
4. Dopo averla lavorata ancora, aggiungete le chiare ben montate e mescolate adagio
5. Ponete il composto in una teglia unta col burro e spolverizzata di farina mista con zucchero a velo
6. Mandatela in forno
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bocconi di Pane Ripieni (Stuffed Bread Morsels)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 chicken livers
- some veal sweetbreads
- chicken or turkey gizzard, if available
- stale bread
- onion
- prosciutto
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg or spices
- flour
- meat gravy or broth
- 1 egg
- parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. If using gizzards, boil them first until half-cooked and remove the tender parts.
2. Mince all the organ meats, put them in a pan with a battuto of onion, prosciutto, and butter, and season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg or spices.
3. When it begins to brown, sprinkle in a scant teaspoon of flour, mix to incorporate, and moisten with meat gravy or broth.
4. Boil, then gradually add beaten egg while stirring constantly until the mixture sets.
5. Remove from heat, add a pinch of parmesan cheese, and pour into a dish.
6. Cut stale bread into thick slices about one centimeter, remove the crust, and cut into cubes about the size of a 10-centimeter coin.
7. Mound the filling generously on one side of each bread cube and half an hour before frying, dust with flour and spread on a tray.
8. Generously pour beaten egg over them so the bread absorbs it and the filling remains covered and well-coated.
9. Fry in a pan, starting with the filling side down.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bombe Composte (Composed Cheese and Mortadella Puffs)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Water, 180 grams
- Flour, 120 grams
- Gruyère cheese, 30 grams
- Butter, size of a walnut
- Mortadella from Bologna, 30 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Salt, a pinch

## Directions

1. Put water on the fire with butter and salt
2. When it begins to boil, throw in the cheese in small pieces and immediately all the flour at once, stirring vigorously
3. Keep the paste on the fire about 10 minutes, stirring always, then let it cool
4. Work it much and in stages with a spoon, adding one egg at a time, first the yolk then the beaten white
5. When about to fry it, add the mortadella in dice about one centimeter wide
6. If the mixture turns out a bit too firm due to the flour quality or because the eggs were small, add another egg
7. You will obtain enough for six people
8. If these puffs turn out well, you will see them puff up and remain empty inside
9. Serve hot as a side to fried meat or liver, or mixed with any other fried dish
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bombe di Semolino (Semolina Puffs)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 3 deciliters or 300 grams
- Fine-grain semolina, 130 grams
- Butter, size of a walnut
- Sugar, one teaspoon
- Salt, as needed
- Lemon zest
- Eggs, 3

## Directions

1. Put milk on the fire with butter and sugar
2. When it begins to boil, add the semolina little by little so it does not form lumps
3. Keep it on the fire until well firm, stirring always with a spoon so it does not stick to the bottom
4. Remove from the fire, salt it and immediately break in the first egg, then when about to fry it, add the other two, one at a time, beating the whites and working it always much with the spoon
5. When you throw it in the pan, give it the shape of small balls which will puff up to become very light puffs
6. Dust with powdered sugar once they have lost their strong heat
7. Use gentle fire and shake the pan
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bombe e Pasta Siringa (Choux Puffs and Piped Paste)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Water, 150 grams
- Fine Hungarian or finest flour, 100 grams
- Butter, size of a walnut
- Salt, a pinch
- Lemon zest
- Eggs, 2 whole and 1 yolk

## Directions

1. Put water, butter, and salt on the fire
2. When it boils, add all the flour at once and stir vigorously
3. Keep the paste on the fire until the flour is well cooked (10 minutes), stirring constantly
4. Remove from the pot and flatten to the thickness of a finger so it cools well
5. Begin working it early with an egg yolk; when it is incorporated, add a well-beaten egg white
6. Add another yolk and, working always with a spoon, another beaten egg white, and so on if the dose were double or triple the present one
7. By dint of working it, the mixture must turn out fine as an ointment in the end
8. If making puffs, drop by spoonfuls into a pan giving them round shape
9. If desiring piped paste, pass it through a piping bag with a stellate disc and cut to a length of 9 to 10 centimeters
10. When it has lost its first heat, dust with powdered sugar
11. This dose will serve eight or ten people when doubled or tripled
12. These puffs can also serve as a composed fried dish by making a small incision when cooked to introduce inside a little delicate minced meat; but then do not dust with sugar
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bracioline alla Bartola (Braised Veal Rolls, Bartola Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or beef meat (fillet, round, rump, or leg), net weight without bone, 500 grams
- fatty and lean prosciutto, 50 grams
- a small clove of garlic
- a small piece of onion
- one rib of celery, about one hand's length
- a good piece of carrot
- a pinch of parsley
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- clove buds
- tomato juice or preserve
- water
- large potato

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thick slices, almost a finger thick, to obtain no more than seven or eight braised rolls to which you endeavor to give a nice shape, and beat them with the back of a knife.
2. Make a mixture minced very finely with the prosciutto and the ingredients described above, then pour six tablespoons of oil in a sauté pan or copper pan on which, when cold, you will place the braised rolls spreading on each one a pinch of the said mixture.
3. Season them with little salt, pepper and only the flower of four or five cloves, and over high heat brown them from underneath, then turn them one by one with its mixture to brown this as well, and when it has softened sufficiently turn them back over so the mixture returns to the top picking up what has remained stuck to the pan, then bathe them with tomato juice or preserve dissolved in water, cover them and let them boil slowly for almost two hours; but half an hour before sending them to the table cook in the sauce of the same a large peeled potato and cut into ten or twelve pieces placing them in the spaces between one braised roll and the other.
4. It would be better to send them to the table in the vessel where they have been cooked; but if this is unseemly place them as they are in a serving dish with the potatoes around them.
5. This quantity is enough for four or five people, and it is not a dish to be despised, because it does not leave the stomach burdened.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bracioline alla Contadina (Veal Rolls Peasant Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal, well beaten
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Olives in brine
- Capers, squeezed of vinegar
- Anchovy
- Egg yolk (optional)
- Parmesan cheese (optional)
- Butter
- Tomato juice or conserve dissolved in water
- Onion (optional for soffritto)

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal rolls with well-beaten lean veal, oil them and season with salt and pepper.
2. Make a mixture of olives in brine, capers squeezed of vinegar and one anchovy, chopping everything finely.
3. Leave it simple, or add an egg yolk and a pinch of parmesan.
4. Fill the rolls with this mixture, tie them, and cook them with butter and tomato juice, or in an onion soffritto.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bracioline di Vitella di Latte all'Uccelletto (Veal Scallops, Little Bird Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lean veal breast, sliced thin
- oil
- butter
- sage leaves
- salt
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Pound the veal scallops well with the back of a knife blade.
2. Heat oil and butter in a saucepan or sauté pan with whole sage leaves and let them soften slightly.
3. Add the veal scallops, season with salt and pepper, and boil over high heat for five or six minutes.
4. Squeeze lemon juice over them and serve.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bracioline Ripiene (Stuffed Braised Veal Rolls)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- thin veal braised rolls, 300 grams
- lean veal or milk-fed veal meat, 70 grams
- rather lean prosciutto, 40 grams
- veal marrow, 30 grams
- grated Parmesan cheese, 30 grams
- egg, 1
- onion
- white celery
- carrot
- dried meat (cured meat), 20 grams
- butter, 20 grams
- salt
- pepper
- tomato juice or preserve
- water
- white wine

## Directions

1. From the braised rolls you will obtain 6 or 7 if you keep them as wide as a hand: beat them very well with a meat beater or with the handle of a knife dipped often in water to widen them.
2. Then mince finely the prosciutto with 70 grams of the second meat and to this mixture unite the Parmesan and the marrow first made pasty with the blade of a knife; lastly add the egg to bind the mixture and a pinch of pepper, salt not being necessary because of the prosciutto and Parmesan.
3. Spread out the braised rolls and on the middle of them distribute the said mixture; then roll them up and tie them crosswise with twine.
4. Now that they have already taken the required form, prepare a light mixture with a little onion, a small piece of white celery, a small piece of carrot and 20 grams of dried meat, and place it on the fire in a saucepan with 20 grams of butter, at the same time as you place the braised rolls.
5. Season them with salt and pepper, and when they have taken on color pour tomato juice or preserve and finish cooking them with water.
6. You can also add, if you like, a drop of white wine.
7. When you send them to the table remove the twine with which you had tied them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bracioline ripiene con carciofi (Braised Veal or Beef Rolls Stuffed with Artichokes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- slice of fatty and lean prosciutto
- butter
- veal or beef bracioline
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- oil
- tomato juice or tomato paste
- water

## Directions

1. Remove all the tough leaves from the artichokes and cut them into four or five wedges.
2. Take a slice of fatty and lean prosciutto and chop it very finely, mix it with a little butter and spread this mixture over the artichoke wedges.
3. Pound and flatten the bracioline, which can be of veal or beef, season them with salt and pepper and place two or three of the prepared artichoke wedges in the middle of each one, then roll them up and tie them with string in a cross pattern.
4. Make a battutino with a little onion, put it in a pot with butter and oil and when the onion is well browned place the bracioline in it and season them again with salt and pepper.
5. Brown them, then finish cooking them with tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in water, and when you serve them remove the string.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Braciuola di Manzo alla Sautè o in Teglia (Beef Braciole, Sautéed or in a Pan)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One beef braciole
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley, chopped
- Water

## Directions

1. Beat it very well with the back of a knife.
2. Put it on the fire with a piece of butter proportioned to its size.
3. Season it with salt and pepper.
4. Turn it often so it browns on both sides.
5. When it has absorbed almost all the butter, moisten it twice with a few drops of water.
6. When cooked, sprinkle a pinch of chopped parsley over it.
7. Keep it on the fire for another moment and serve it with its sauce.
8. You can garnish it with fried potatoes if you like.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Braciuola di Manzo Ripiena Arrosto (Roasted Stuffed Beef Roulade)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One beef cutlet, the thickness of a finger, weighing about 500 grams
- Lean milk veal, 200 grams
- Fat and lean prosciutto, 30 grams
- Salt tongue, 30 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 30 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Chicken livers, 2
- Eggs, 1
- One ball of fresh bread crumb about the size of a fist

## Directions

1. Make a fine mixture with onion the size of a walnut, a little celery, carrot and parsley; put it on the fire with the said butter and, once browned, throw in the milk veal in pieces and the livers, a little salt and pepper for seasoning, cooking the meat with a little broth.
2. Remove it dry to mince it finely with the mezzaluna and in the remaining liquid make a thick paste with the bread crumb, moistening it with broth if needed.
3. Now make a single mixture with the minced meat, the paste, the egg, the Parmesan, the prosciutto and the tongue cut into dice.
4. Once the filling is composed this way, just dip the beef cutlet in water to be able to stretch it better, beat it with the back of the knife blade and flatten it with the blade.
5. Place the filling in the middle and form a roll that you will tie tightly like a salami first from the long side and then crosswise.
6. Thread it onto the spit along its length and roast it with oil and salt.
7. You will have a delicate roast which can serve six or seven people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Braciuola Ripiena (Stuffed Braised Meat Roll)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one large thin sheet of meat for braising
- chicken breast, cut into fillets thicker than one centimeter
- two slices of mortadella
- two slices of prosciutto
- Butter, 30 grams
- salt
- pepper
- meat broth
- kitchen twine

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken breast and the mortadella and prosciutto slices into thick fillets.
2. Spread some of the prepared filling mixture on the meat sheet.
3. Arrange a third of the fillets on top, interspersing them, and spread more filling over them.
4. Repeat this layering process two more times.
5. Roll the meat sheet tightly around the filling (like a salami roll) and tie it securely with kitchen twine.
6. Heat 30 grams of butter in a pot and brown the roll, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Once browned, pour off the fat (which can be saved for other dishes).
8. Continue cooking for approximately three hours, adding broth gradually as needed.
9. Once cooled, remove the twine, slice into portions, and serve.
10. Serves 10 to 12 people, especially when garnished with meat gelatin.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Braciuole di castrato e filetto di vitella alla finanziera (Braised Mutton and Veal Fillet in Financier Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- slice of prosciutto
- butter
- carrot
- celery
- parsley stems
- whole braciuole of mutton from the loin
- salt
- pepper
- chicken gizzards
- chicken livers
- sweetbreads
- fresh or dried mushrooms
- broth
- flour
- good white wine

## Directions

1. Place a slice of prosciutto at the bottom of a pot with some butter, a small bundle made of carrot, celery and parsley stems, and on top of these place whole braciuole of mutton from the loin, which you will season with salt and pepper.
2. Brown them on both sides, add another piece of butter if needed, and add to the braciuole chicken gizzards and then livers, sweetbreads and fresh or dried mushrooms (already softened), all cut into pieces.
3. When these ingredients have also taken color, moisten with broth and cook over low heat.
4. Bind the sauce with a little flour, and lastly pour in half a glass, or even less, of good white wine, first reduced by half in a separate vessel over the fire, and let it boil a little longer so that it incorporates.
5. When ready to serve, remove the prosciutto and the bundle, strain the sauce through a colander and degrease it.
6. The same method can be used for a piece of veal fillet instead of mutton, adding peas to the ingredients mentioned.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Braciuole nella Scamerita (Pork Rolls in Pork Belly)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork rolls (from scamerita)
- Oil
- Garlic cloves with skin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Red wine
- Kale, blanched
- Water

## Directions

1. Place the rolls in a pan with very little oil, two or three cloves of garlic with their skin, slightly crushed, and season with salt and pepper.
2. When they have taken color on both sides, pour two or three fingers of a glass of red wine into the pan and let it reduce by half as it boils.
3. Set the rolls aside dry, keeping them warm.
4. In that liquid, adjust the seasoning of the blanched kale, pressed of water, cut not too finely and also seasoned with salt and pepper.
5. Serve at table with the kale underneath.
