source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Brigidini (Tuscan Waffle Cookies)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, 2
- Sugar, 120 grams
- Anise seeds, 10 grams
- Salt, a pinch
- Flour, as needed

## Directions

1. Make a rather firm paste, work it with your hands on a work surface and form small balls about the size of a small walnut.
2. Place them in the waffle iron on a waffle mold at a proper distance from one another and, turning the iron back and forth over the hot flame with wood fire, remove them when they have taken color.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Brioches
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Hungarian flour, 300 grams
- Butter, 150 grams
- Beer yeast, 30 grams
- Sugar, 20 grams
- Salt, 5 grams
- Eggs, 6

## Directions

1. Dissolve the beer yeast with lukewarm water in one quarter of the said flour; form it into a round small loaf of proper consistency, make a cross cut in it and place it to rise in a warm place inside a small copper pot with a veil of flour underneath
2. To the remaining flour make a well in the middle, place in it the sugar, salt and one egg, and with your fingers combine these three things together, then add the butter in small pieces and begin to knead the flour, first using the blade of a knife and then your hands, to form a dough which you will place in a bowl to work it better
3. Unite to this the risen small loaf, when it has grown double in size, and using your hand to work it a lot, add the other eggs one at a time
4. Then place the bowl in a warm and well-closed place, and when the dough has risen, spread it out somewhat and with it fill halfway about twenty buttered metal molds with ridges, which beforehand you will have buttered with liquid butter or lard and dusted with flour mixed with powdered sugar
5. Return them to rise, then glaze them and bake them in the oven or country oven
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Broccoli o talli di rape alla fiorentina (Broccoli or Turnip Greens Florentine-Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- broccoli di rapa (turnip greens)
- aglio (garlic)
- olio (oil)
- sale (salt)
- pepe (pepper)
- olio e aceto (oil and vinegar)
- lardo (lard)

## Directions

1. Clean the broccoli from the hardest leaves, boil them, squeeze out the water and cut them roughly.
2. Mince two or three garlic cloves or leave them whole, and once they have taken color with abundant oil in a pan, add the broccoli.
3. Season with salt and pepper, stir often and let them fry well.
4. They can be served as a side dish to boiled meat or eaten alone.
5. If you prefer, boil them and season with oil and vinegar.
6. In countries where oil is not perfect, you can substitute with lard; in fact, to my taste, fried with this they are better.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Broccoli romani (Roman Broccoli)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- broccoli romani (Roman broccoli)
- lardo vergine or strutto (pure lard or lard)
- sale (salt)
- pepe (pepper)
- vino bianco dolce (sweet white wine)
- olio (oil)
- aglio (garlic)
- acqua calda (hot water)

## Directions

1. Clean them from the hardest leaves and boil them. Remove from boiling water and place them in cold water, and after squeezing well, cut them roughly and place them in a pan with virgin lard, seasoning with salt and pepper.
2. Once it has absorbed all the fat, sprinkle with sweet white wine, continue to drag them in the pan until it has been completely absorbed and evaporated, then serve as they will be praised.
3. For another method of cooking these broccoli without boiling them first: use only the flower and the most tender leaves; cut these roughly and the flower into small pieces.
4. Put the pan on the fire with oil in proportion and one garlic clove cut into thin slices lengthwise.
5. When the garlic begins to brown, add the leaves and then the flower raw in the pan, salt and pepper for seasoning, and as they dry by boiling, stirring always, wet them with a drop of hot water and, almost when cooking is complete, with white wine.
6. Since I cannot give you the precise amounts of this dish, have the patience to make some trials (I make many!) to ascertain its best taste.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Brodo (Broth)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal or beef cut into thin slices
- Cold water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut lean veal or beef into thin slices and arrange them one on top of the other in a wide pan
2. Salt them somewhat and pour over them enough cold water so that they are submerged
3. Cover the pan with a plate that closes it securely
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Brodo per gli ammalati (Broth for the Sick)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal or beef cut into thin slices
- Cold water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut lean veal or beef into thin slices and arrange them one on top of the other in a wide pan
2. Salt them somewhat and pour over them enough cold water so that they are submerged
3. Cover the pan with a plate that closes it securely
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino alla Genovese (Genoese Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal, 150 grams
- Chicken breast, about 130 grams
- Prosciutto (fat and lean), 50 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 20 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Nutmeg
- Salt, a pinch
- Bread crumb soaked in milk
- Béchamel sauce, 3 tablespoons
- Chicken liver
- Broth

## Directions

1. Mince the veal, chicken breast, and prosciutto with a crescent-shaped knife. Place them in a mortar with the butter, Parmesan, a piece of bread crumb soaked in milk, and pound thoroughly until it can be passed through a sieve.
2. Place the sieved mixture in a bowl and add three tablespoons of béchamel (made to the consistency of a paste for this dish), the eggs, and nutmeg. Mix well.
3. Butter a smooth tin mold and place a sheet of buttered paper cut to size at the bottom. Pour in the mixture and cook in a bain-marie.
4. After unmolding, remove the paper and spread on that spot a sauce made from minced chicken liver cooked in broth.
5. Serve hot. If well made, it will be praised for its delicacy.
6. This serves six people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino alla Napoletana (Neapolitan Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Semolina
- Milk, 3 glasses
- Sugar
- Salt
- Lemon zest for flavoring
- Egg yolks, 3
- Egg whites, 2
- Butter or lard
- Short pastry crust, half the amount of recipe N. 589A
- Fruit preserves of various kinds such as raspberry, quince, apricot, etc.
- Powdered sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Cook the semolina in three glasses of milk, taking care that it does not become too firm
2. Remove from heat and season it with sugar, a pinch of salt and lemon zest; when it is no longer boiling, add three egg yolks and two egg whites, mixing everything well
3. Take a medium-sized copper baking dish, butter or lard it, and line it with a sheet of short pastry crust about half a scudo thick
4. Pour one third of the semolina into the dish and scatter over it, at some distance from one another, small pieces or spoonfuls of various fruit preserves such as raspberry, quince, apricots, etc.
5. Place a second and third layer on top of this first layer, always adorning them with the said preserves
6. Cover the top of the pudding with a sheet of the same pastry and moisten the edges with a finger dipped in water so that they stick together
7. Make some decorative marks, brush it with egg yolk and cook it in the oven
8. When you unmold it, dust it with powdered sugar and serve it cold
9. The preserves can be replaced with sultana raisins and candied fruit cut into pieces
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino alla Tedesca (German-Style Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine bread crumb, 140 grams
- Butter, 100 grams
- Sugar, 80 grams
- Eggs, 4
- Lemon zest flavoring
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. If you can find bread in loaf form, English style, use this as it is better than any other. Crumble or slice the bread crumb and soak it with ice-cold milk. When well soaked, squeeze it out with a cloth and pass it through a sieve. In winter, melt the butter in a bain-marie and work it with a wooden spoon together with the egg yolks until they are incorporated; add the whites, the bread crumb and the sugar and stir again. Pour the mixture into a mold greased with butter and dusted with breadcrumbs and cook it like the other puddings; that is in the oven. If you do it with care it will come out looking fine and tasting delicate. Serve hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Cervelli di Maiale (Pork Brain Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork brains, 3 (about 400 grams total)
- Eggs, 2 and 1 yolk
- Heavy cream, 240 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 50 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Nutmeg
- Salt, to taste

## Directions

1. Heat the brains with the butter, salt them, and cook, stirring often to prevent sticking, without browning them. Pass them through a sieve.
2. Add the Parmesan, nutmeg, beaten eggs, and cream. Mix well.
3. Pour the mixture into a smooth mold buttered with cold butter and cook in a bain-marie.
4. This is almost better cold than hot and serves six people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Cioccolata (Chocolate Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 8 deciliters
- Sugar, 80 grams
- Chocolate, 60 grams
- Ladyfinger cookies, 60 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Vanilla for flavoring

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate, put it in the milk and when this begins to boil throw in the sugar and the ladyfingers, crumbling them with your fingers
2. Stir from time to time, so that the mixture does not stick to the bottom and after half an hour of boiling pass it through a sieve
3. When it is cold add the beaten eggs and the vanilla, pour it into a smooth mold, the bottom of which you will have first covered with a layer of liquefied sugar, and cook it in a bain-marie
4. 50 grams of sugar are sufficient to cover the bottom of the mold
5. Serve cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di farina di riso (Rice Flour Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 1 liter
- Rice flour, 200 grams
- Sugar, 120 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Eggs, 6
- Salt, a pinch
- Vanilla for flavoring

## Directions

1. First dissolve the flour with a quarter of the milk cold
2. Add some of the hot milk when it is boiling and pour it into the rest of the milk when it boils; this will prevent lumps from forming
3. When it is cooked add the sugar, butter, and salt
4. Remove it from the fire and wait until it is lukewarm to mix in the eggs and flavoring
5. Cook this pudding like the previous one
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Limone (Lemon Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One large garden lemon
- Sugar, 170 grams
- Sweet almonds with three bitter almonds, 170 grams
- Eggs, 6
- One teaspoon of rum or cognac

## Directions

1. Cook the lemon in water, for which two hours will be sufficient
2. Remove it dry and pass it through a sieve
3. However, before passing it taste it, because if it tasted too bitter you would need to keep it in fresh water until it had lost that unpleasant flavor
4. Add to it the sugar, the blanched and very finely pounded almonds, the six egg yolks and the rum
5. Mix everything well, beat the six egg whites and add them to the mixture which you will pour into a mold to cook it in the stove or in the oven
6. It can be served either hot or cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Mandorle Tostate (Roasted Almond Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 8 deciliters or 800 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Ladyfinger cookies, 60 grams
- Sweet almonds, 60 grams
- Eggs, 3

## Directions

1. First prepare the almonds, that is blanch them in hot water and roast them on the fire over a stone or iron slab; then pound them reducing them almost to powder and, putting the mixture on the fire without the eggs, add the almonds to it and after a short cooking pass it through a sieve
2. Now add the beaten eggs and set it in a bain-marie with a layer of melted sugar on the bottom of the mold
3. No flavoring is needed
4. The roasting of the almonds will give this pudding a grayish color and will give it a flavor so pleasant as to deserve the praise of men and even more that of women of delicate taste
5. Both this and the chocolate pudding can be put on ice before serving them, and also, to give them a more beautiful appearance, they can be covered with a cream adorned with colored confetti, or with whipped cream
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Nocciuole (Hazelnut Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 7 deciliters
- Eggs, 6
- Shelled hazelnuts, 200 grams
- Sugar, 180 grams
- Savoiardi biscuits, 150 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Blanch the hazelnuts in hot water and dry them well in the sun or by the fire.
2. Then pound them very finely in a mortar with the sugar poured in little by little.
3. Put the milk on the fire and when it comes to a boil, crumble the savoiardi biscuits into it and let them boil for five minutes, adding the butter.
4. Strain the mixture through a sieve and put it back on the fire with the pounded hazelnuts to dissolve the sugar.
5. Let it cool, then add the eggs, first the yolks, then the whites beaten stiff.
6. Pour it into a mold buttered and dusted with breadcrumbs, not filling it completely.
7. Bake it in the oven or in a cooking stove and serve it cold.
8. This quantity will serve nine or ten people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Pane e Cioccolata (Bread and Chocolate Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine common bread, 100 grams
- Sugar, 70 grams
- Chocolate, 40 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Milk, 4 deciliters
- Eggs, 3

## Directions

1. Pour hot milk over bread cut into thin slices
2. After about two hours of steeping, pass it through a sieve to make it uniform
3. Put it on the fire with the sugar, butter, and grated chocolate
4. Stir the mixture frequently, let it boil somewhat and let it cool
5. Add the eggs, first the yolks and then the whipped whites
6. Cook it in a bain-marie in a smooth buttered mold and serve it cold
7. For better appearance, it would be good to cover it after unmolding with a cream
8. This dose will be sufficient for five people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Patate (Potato Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large floury potatoes, 700 grams
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- Flour, 20 grams
- Milk, 2 deciliters
- Eggs, 6
- A pinch of salt
- Cinnamon or lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes in water or steamed, peel them and pass them hot through a sieve. Put them back on the fire with the butter, flour and milk, poured in little by little, working them well with a wooden spoon; then add the sugar, salt and flavoring and let them rest until all these things are well incorporated together.
2. Remove from the fire; when they are lukewarm or cold, add the yolks and then the beaten whites.
3. Cook it like all the other puddings; that is in the oven or in the stove and serve it hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Ricotta (Ricotta Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ricotta, 300 grams
- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Sweet almonds, 100 grams and three or four bitter almonds
- Eggs, 5
- Lemon zest for flavoring

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds in hot water and pound them very finely in a mortar with one of the egg whites mentioned above
2. Mix them well with the ricotta, passing the ricotta first through a sieve if it were too hard or lumpy
3. Add the sugar and the eggs after having beaten them separately and pour the mixture into a pudding mold that you will have first buttered and sprinkled with breadcrumbs
4. Cook it in the oven or between two fires in a stove and serve it cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Ricotta (Ricotta Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ricotta cheese, 300 grams
- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Sweet almonds, 100 grams and three or four bitter almonds
- Eggs, 5
- Lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds in hot water and pound them very finely in a mortar with one of the egg whites mentioned above. Mix them well with the ricotta, passing this first through a sieve if it was too hard or lumpy. Add the sugar and the eggs after having beaten them separately and pour the mixture into a pudding mold that you will have first greased with butter and sprinkled with breadcrumbs. Cook it in the oven or between two
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di Riso (Rice Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Rice, 160 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Currants (sultanina), 80 grams
- Candied fruit, 30 grams
- Eggs, two whole and two yolks
- Rum or cognac, one small glass
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Cook the rice well in the milk and at half cooking throw into it the sugar, the currants, the candied fruit cut into very small pieces, a pinch of salt, and butter about the size of an egg minus a little. When cooked, remove it from the fire and while still hot, but not boiling, add the eggs, the rum and the vanilla, mixing everything well. Then pour it into a pudding mold well greased with butter and dusted with breadcrumbs; cook it in the oven or on the stove and serve it hot.
2. Set aside a third of the milk to add as needed as the rice thickens.
3. Will be enough for eight people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di semolino (Semolina Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 8 deciliters
- Semolina, 150 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Raisins, 100 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Eggs, 4
- Rum, 3 tablespoons
- Salt, a pinch
- Lemon zest for flavoring

## Directions

1. Prepare the mixture and remove it from the fire
2. Cook it in a smooth or worked mold, first greased with butter and dusted with breadcrumbs
3. If a common or country oven is lacking, puddings can also be cooked well in a hearth burner
4. Serve this pudding hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino di semolino e conserve (Semolina Pudding with Preserves)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, half a liter
- Semolina, 130 grams
- Sugar, 70 grams
- Butter, 15 grams
- Eggs, 2
- Salt, a pinch
- Lemon zest for flavoring
- Various fruit preserves

## Directions

1. Cook the semolina in the milk
2. Add the sugar and butter when it is boiling
3. Add the flavoring and salt when you remove it from the fire
4. Beat the eggs when it is still hot and mix very well
5. Prepare a pudding mold, smooth or worked, greased with butter and sprinkled with breadcrumbs
6. Pour the mixture into it little by little while cold, decorating it gradually with preserves in pieces or by spoonfuls according to whether they are liquid or solid; however, be warned that they should not touch the walls of the mold, as they would stick to them, and that there should not be too much of them, as they would become cloying
7. Serve it hot after cooking it on the hearth
8. The preserves that, to my taste, are most suited for this sweet are those of raspberry and quince; but those of apricots, currants, and peaches can also be used
9. For eight or ten persons, double the dose
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino Gabinetto (Cabinet Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Ladyfinger cookies, 100 grams
- Malaga raisins, 80 grams
- Sultanina raisins, 50 grams
- Apricot preserve, 50 grams
- Quince preserve, 50 grams
- Candied fruit, 20 grams
- Kirsch, half a deciliter
- Egg yolks, 6
- Egg whites, 4

## Directions

1. Boil the milk for half an hour with the sugar in it
2. Remove the seeds from the Malaga raisins; cut the candied fruit into small cubes and likewise the preserves, if they were firm, which in this case would be better
3. Burn the grapes and candied fruit with rum as in the Biscotto alla Sultana N.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino Gabinetto (Cabinet Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Savoiardi, 100 grams
- Malaga raisins, 80 grams
- Sultana raisins, 50 grams
- Apricot preserve, 50 grams
- Quince preserve, 50 grams
- Candied fruit, 20 grams
- Kirsch, half a deciliter
- Egg yolks, 6
- Egg whites, 4

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with sugar inside for half an hour
2. Remove seeds from the malaga raisins; cut the candied fruit into small dice and do the same with the preserves if they are firm, which in this case would be better
3. Flame the raisins and candied fruit with rum as in the Biscotto alla sultana No. 574
4. After the milk has boiled, let it cool and then add the beaten eggs and kirsch
5. Take a smooth cylindrical mold, grease it completely with cold butter and fill it in the following way: cover the bottom with a layer of the aforementioned fruit and over it spread a layer of savoiardi, then more fruit and preserves, then more savoiardi and so on until you run out of ingredients
6. Finally pour the milk prepared as described slowly and gradually into the mold
7. Cook the pudding in a water bath and serve it hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Budino Nero (Black Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg whites, 6
- Sweet almonds, 170 grams
- Powdered sugar, 170 grams

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, dry them well in the sun or by the fire, chop them with a mincing knife and put them on the fire in a saucepan when the sugar is dissolved
2. After the mixture has taken on the color of brittle, or the color of almond skin, pour it into a mortar and, once cooled, reduce it to powder
3. Mix this powder with the six beaten egg whites, put the mixture in a mold buttered with only cold butter and cook it in a bain-marie to serve it cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bue alla Brace (Braised Beef)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams lean and tender beef without bone
- 50 grams lard cut into thick strips about a finger's length
- salt and pepper
- one quarter of a medium onion
- half a carrot
- one celery rib about a palm's length
- 30 grams butter
- cold water
- 2 ladles hot water
- additional butter
- vegetables for garnish such as spinach, Brussels sprouts, carrots, fennel

## Directions

1. Lard the meat with the lard strips seasoned with salt and pepper
2. Make a battuto (soffritto) with the onion, carrot, and celery, chopping it coarsely
3. Place the battuto in a pot with 30 grams of butter and put the tied and seasoned meat on top
4. When the battuto is nearly consumed, wet it twice with a bit of cold water
5. Once the meat is colored and the battuto consumed, pour in two ladles of hot water
6. Cover the pot with double paper and let it boil slowly until the meat is cooked
7. Strain the sauce, remove the fat, and return it to the fire with another piece of butter
8. Season the sauce to taste and serve with a garnish of vegetables of your choice
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bue alla California (Californian-Style Beef)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 700 grams lean beef without bone from the rump, loin, or fillet
- 50 grams butter
- 2 deciliters cream
- 2 deciliters water
- 1 tablespoon strong vinegar or more if weak
- half an onion cut into four wedges
- 1 carrot cut into small pieces
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Place the meat in a pot with the butter, onion wedges, carrot pieces, salt and pepper
2. When the meat is well browned, pour in the vinegar
3. After some time, add the water
4. Then add the cream
5. Let it boil slowly for about three hours, adding more water if the sauce becomes scarce
6. Serve the meat sliced with its sauce strained through a sieve
7. This dish can serve five or six people as one of several courses
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bue alla Moda (Braised Beef)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Not less than 1 kilogram of lean meat from the hip or rump of beef
- Thick lardoon strips, wrapped in salt and pepper
- Salt
- 50 grams butter
- Half a calf's foot or a piece of a large calf's foot
- 1 large whole onion
- 2 or 3 whole carrots
- 1 bunch tied of aromatic herbs such as parsley, celery, basil and the like
- Lardoon rinds
- 1 bold glass of water or better 1 glass of degreased broth
- Half a glass of white wine or 2 tablespoons of brandy

## Directions

1. Take not less than 1 kilogram of lean meat from the hip or rump of beef and lard it with thick lardoon strips wrapped in salt and pepper
2. Tie the piece of meat so it takes a nice shape, salt it sufficiently
3. Place it in a braising pan with 50 grams of butter to brown it
4. Add the following ingredients: half a calf's foot or a piece of a large calf's foot, 1 large whole onion, 2 or 3 whole carrots, 1 bunch tied of aromatic herbs such as parsley, celery, basil and the like, some lardoon rinds, 1 glass of water or better 1 glass of degreased broth, and finally half a glass of white wine or 2 tablespoons of brandy
5. Put the well-covered pan on the fire and simmer slowly until the meat is cooked
6. Remove the carrots early so they remain whole
7. Discard the aromatic bunch, strain the sauce and degrease if necessary
8. Serve the meat not overcooked, surrounded by the carrots cut into round slices
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Bue Garofanato (Beef with Cloves)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- A nice piece of lean beef from the thigh or rump, about 1 kilogram
- Wine for soaking
- Lard for larding
- Four cloves
- Half an onion, sliced thin
- Butter and oil in equal quantities
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take a fine piece of lean from the thigh or rump, beat it, and place it in wine to soak the evening for the following morning. Given that the piece is about one kilogram, lard it with lardons and four cloves, tie it and put it on the fire with half an onion cut into thin slices, butter and oil in equal quantities and salt it. Brown it on all sides and render the onion, pour a glass of water and, covering the mouth of the pot with a sheet of paper in two or three layers held firm by the lid, let it boil gently until cooked. Release it and serve it with its sauce all around, strained and degreased. The lardons of lard, as I have told you other times, it is good to keep them the thickness of a finger and season them with salt and pepper.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cacciucco I (Tuscan Fish Stew)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 700 grams of fish
- half an onion, finely chopped
- oil
- parsley
- two cloves of garlic, whole
- 300 grams of tomatoes, in pieces, or tomato paste
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Finely chop half an onion and put it to fry gently with oil, parsley and two whole cloves of garlic.
2. As soon as the onion has taken on color, add 300 grams of tomatoes in pieces, or tomato paste, and season with salt and pepper.
3. Once the tomatoes are cooked, pour over them one finger of vinegar if it is strong, or two if it is weak, diluted in a good glass of water.
4. Let it boil for a few more minutes, then remove the garlic and pass the rest through a sieve, pressing well.
5. Put the strained liquid back on the fire, together with the fish you have ready, such as sole, red mullet, scorpionfish, dogfish, gobies, mantis shrimp, and other seasonal varieties, leaving small fish whole and cutting large ones into pieces.
6. Taste to see if the seasoning is right; but in any case it will not hurt to add a little oil, keeping rather sparing with the initial frying.
7. When the fish is cooked and the cacciucco is made, it is customary to bring it to the table in two separate serving dishes; in one the fish alone, in the other as many slices of bread, thick as a finger, as the remaining broth can soak, but first dry them by the fire without toasting them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cacciucco II (Lighter Tuscan Fish Stew)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 700 grams of fish
- three large cloves of garlic
- fresh ginger, or dried ginger
- oil
- one glass of liquid composed of one third dry white wine or red wine and the rest water
- salt
- tomato juice or tomato paste
- water
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Pound in a mortar three large cloves of garlic and fresh or dried ginger, reducing it to powder.
2. Put this mixture on the fire in a terracotta pan or pot with oil in proportion, and when it has fried gently, pour in a glass of liquid made up of one third dry white wine or red wine and the rest water.
3. Place the fish in it, salt it and shortly after add tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in a drop of water.
4. Boil over a strong fire, always keeping the pot covered, never touch the fish so as not to break it, and you will find it cooked in a few minutes.
5. Serve it as the previous one, with slices of bread set apart which you will dry first by the fire without toasting them.
6. If the fish, before cooking it, remains raw for several hours, it keeps better if salted; but then it is well to wash it before putting it on the fire.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cacimperio (Cheese Fondue)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fontina cheese, trimmed of rind, 400 grams
- butter, 80 grams
- egg yolks, 4 number
- milk, as needed

## Directions

1. Cut the Fontina into small dice and keep it soaking in milk for two hours
2. Put the butter on the fire and when it has taken color, add the Fontina, but of the milk in which it has been soaking, keep only two spoonfuls
3. Work it well with a wooden spoon without letting it boil and when the cheese is completely melted, remove it from the fire to add the yolks
4. Put it back on the fire for a little while, stirring again and in winter, pour it into a warm serving dish
5. If it has turned out well it should be neither grainy nor stringy, but have the appearance of a dense cream
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Caffè-latte Gelato (Iced Coffee and Milk)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Coffee, half a liter
- Sugar, 300 grams

## Directions

1. Put the sugar in the milk and dissolve it over heat
2. Pour the mixture into the ice cream maker, as for ice creams in general
3. Serve it, when it will be solidified, in cups or small glasses
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cappelletti all'uso di Romagna (Cappelletti Romagna Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ricotta, or half ricotta and half cacio raviggiolo, 180 grams
- Half breast of capon cooked in butter, seasoned with salt and pepper, and finely minced with a crescent-shaped knife
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 30 grams
- Eggs, one whole and one yolk
- Nutmeg flavor, few spices, lemon zest for those who like it
- A pinch of salt
- Flour for the pasta sheet, made with eggs only

## Directions

1. Taste the mixture so you can correct it if needed, as the ingredients do not always correspond in the same way. If capon breast is lacking, substitute with 100 grams of lean pork from the loin, cooked and seasoned in the same manner.
2. If the ricotta or raviggiolo are too soft, leave out the egg white or add another yolk if the mixture seems too firm.
3. To close them, make a rather tender pasta sheet of flour with eggs only, using also any remaining egg white, and cut it with a round disk the size of that shown on this page.
4. Place the mixture in the middle of the disks and fold them in two forming a half-moon; then take the two ends of the same, join them together and you will have the completed cappelletto.
5. If the pasta sheet dries out in your hand, dampen with a finger dipped in water the edges of the disks.
6. Cook the cappelletti in capon broth as is customary in Romagna.
7. A discreet eater is satisfied with two dozen.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cappone Arrosto Tartufato (Roasted Truffle-Stuffed Capon)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One capon with only the breast, that is empty, without the neck and legs, killed the day before, weighing about 800 grams
- Black or white truffles, fragrant, 250 grams
- Butter, 80 grams
- Marsala wine, 5 tablespoons
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Keep the truffles large as walnuts, lightly peel them and throw the skin thus raw inside the capon; you can also insert some slices of raw truffle under the skin.
2. Put the butter on the fire and when it is melted throw in the truffles with the Marsala, salt and pepper for seasoning and, on high heat, let them boil for only two minutes, stirring them constantly.
3. Removed from the pan, let them cool until the fat is set and then pour everything into the capon, sewing it both in the lower part and in the front where the neck was removed.
4. Keep it in a cool place to cook it after 24 hours, giving it thus three days of aging.
5. If it were a pheasant or a turkey, regulate yourself in proportion. In winter, it is good to keep them filled three or four days before cooking them, indeed for the pheasant you must wait.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cappone in Galantina (Capon in Galantine)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one large capon, plucked (approximately 1.5 kilograms), emptied and deboned (resulting in 0.7 kilograms)
- Lean veal, 200 grams
- Lean pork, 200 grams
- Half breast of young hen
- Lard, 100 grams
- Salted tongue, 80 grams
- Ham (fat and lean), 40 grams
- Black truffles, 40 grams
- Pistachios, 20 grams
- Pork and veal for the forcemeat, 200 grams total
- Breadcrumb marrow soaked in broth, 60 grams
- one egg
- salt
- pepper
- kitchen twine
- cloth for wrapping

## Directions

1. Cut the truffles into pieces the size of hazelnuts and blanch the pistachios in hot water to remove skins.
2. Cut the veal, pork, ham, and salted tongue into fillets the thickness of a finger and set aside after salting.
3. Make a forcemeat by pounding together 200 grams of pork and veal with 60 grams of breadcrumb marrow soaked in broth.
4. Add one egg, truffle skins, and scraps of tongue and ham to the forcemeat. Season with salt and pepper and pound until fine.
5. Pass the forcemeat through a sieve.
6. Lay out the deboned capon and salt it lightly.
7. Spread a thin layer of forcemeat on the capon, then arrange a layer of fillets interspersed with truffle pieces and pistachios.
8. Continue alternating layers of fillets and forcemeat, placing the young hen breast fillets toward the tail to avoid accumulating the same meat on the capon's breast.
9. Draw the sides of the capon together and sew it closed.
10. Tie it lengthwise with kitchen twine, wrap it tightly in a clean cloth, and tie both ends.
11. Boil in water for two and a half hours.
12. Remove from the cloth, rewrap it, and place it under a weight in a flat position for at least two hours to compress it into a flattened form.
13. The cooking water can be used for broth or gelatin.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cappone in Vescica (Capon in Bladder)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one large, thick bladder of beef or pork without defects
- one capon
- salt, 150 grams (plus a good handful for inside the capon)
- prosciutto, 150 grams (sliced very thin, more lean than fat)
- one wooden tube approximately one palm's length to serve as an air vent, shaped with a whistle-like mouth at top and a notch at bottom for tying

## Directions

1. Wash the bladder well in tepid water and soak it for one or two days.
2. Clean out the capon and remove the neck and legs.
3. Place a good handful of salt inside the capon, tuck in the leg joints, and fold the wings against the body so the tips do not pierce the bladder.
4. Sew closed the openings of the eviscerated cavity and neck.
5. Wrap the capon all over with 150 grams of thinly sliced prosciutto (more lean than fat), binding the slices tightly to the capon with twine.
6. Make a small incision in the bladder just large enough to fit the capon, then place the capon inside.
7. Sew the bladder closed.
8. Insert the wooden tube (with its whistle-mouth end on top and notched end at bottom) into the neck of the bladder and tie it in place.
9. Place the capon in a pot of tepid water and bring to a boil.
10. Boil gently for three continuous hours with the tube protruding, maintaining only small, sparse bubbles rising to the surface—this is the critical point.
11. If fat or other liquid comes from the tube, do not be concerned; collect it in a small pan.
12. Once cooked, let the capon cool in its water.
13. Serve the next day, discarding the prosciutto wrapper which will have lost its flavor.
14. Inside the capon you will find gelatin, and you may add additional gelatin as a garnish if desired.
15. Serves ten people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi Fritti (Fried Artichokes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Flour
- Egg white
- Egg yolk
- Salt
- Lard or oil for frying
- Lemon wedges

## Directions

1. Cut 8 to 10 pieces per artichoke, even if not very large. As you cut them, throw them into fresh water and when they have refreshed well, remove them and dry them roughly or squeeze them only, throwing them immediately into flour so it adheres well.
2. Whisk the egg white to half stiff peaks, as one is enough for two artichokes, then into the white mix the yolk and salt it. Put the artichokes in a small sieve to shake off excess flour and then pass them through the egg, mix and let them sit a little so the egg incorporates. Throw the pieces one by one into a pan with boiling lard and when they have taken on a nice golden color remove them and serve at table with lemon wedges because, as everyone knows, acid on fried foods that are not sweet always adds grace and excitement to good drinking. If you desire that the artichokes remain white, it is better to fry them in oil and squeeze half a lemon in the water when you soak them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi Fritti (Fried Artichokes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Fresh water
- Flour
- Egg white
- Egg yolk
- Salt
- Lemon wedges
- Oil or lard for frying

## Directions

1. Take, for example, two artichokes, clean them of tough leaves, trim the tips, clean the stem and cut them in two parts
2. Cut these half artichokes into wedges or rather into slices to get 8 or 10 per artichoke even if not very large
3. As you cut them, throw them into fresh water
4. When well refreshed, remove them and dry them roughly or simply squeeze them, throwing them immediately into flour so it sticks well
5. Beat to medium peaks the white of one egg (one is enough for two artichokes)
6. In the white mix the yolk and salt it
7. Put the artichokes in a small sieve to shake off excess flour and after pass them in the egg, mix and let them stay a little so the egg incorporates
8. Throw the pieces one by one into a pan with boiling fat and when they have taken on a fine golden color, remove them and send them to table with lemon wedges
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi in Gratella (Grilled Artichokes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender artichokes
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Choose tender artichokes, trim them, cut the stem at the base and leave them with all their leaves.
2. Open them out somewhat so they will take the seasoning well, which should be composed of nothing but oil, pepper and salt.
3. Place them upright on the grill, and if necessary to hold them firm, skewer them on a stick near the stem two or three together.
4. Give them another brushing of oil halfway through cooking and leave them on the fire until the outer leaves are burned.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi in Salsa (Artichokes in Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- parsley, chopped
- breadcrumbs, finely ground
- lemon juice
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Remove the hard leaves from the artichokes, trim them, and peel the stems.
2. Divide them into four parts, or at most six if they are large.
3. Place them on the fire with butter in proportion and season with salt and pepper.
4. Shake the pan to turn them, and when they have drawn a good part of their moisture, wet them with broth to cook them completely.
5. Remove them dry, and in the remaining sauce add a pinch of chopped parsley, one or two teaspoons of finely ground breadcrumbs, lemon juice, more salt and pepper if needed.
6. Bring to a boil while stirring; then remove the sauce from the fire and when it stops boiling, add one or two egg yolks according to the quantity.
7. Return it briefly to the fire with more broth to make it liquid.
8. Add the artichokes to reheat them and serve them especially as a side dish for boiled meat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi in teglia (Artichokes in a Pan)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes, prepared as in recipe N. 186
- good oil, sufficient quantity
- eggs
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the artichokes as described in N. 186 and after shaking off excess flour, spread them in a pan in which good oil has begun to sizzle in sufficient quantity
2. When the artichoke slices are browned on both sides, pour beaten eggs over them, but be careful not to cook them too much
3. Sprinkle the seasoning of salt and pepper partly on the artichokes and partly in the eggs before pouring them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi in Umido colla Nepitella (Artichokes Braised with Catnip)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- garlic
- nepitella (catnip), fresh
- tomato sauce or tomato paste dissolved in water

## Directions

1. Remove all inedible leaves from the artichokes and divide each into four wedges, or even six if they are large.
2. Flour them and place them on the fire in a copper pan with oil in proportion, seasoning with salt and pepper.
3. When you have browned them, add a small mixture composed of one clove of garlic, or half if the artichokes are few, and a good pinch of fresh nepitella.
4. When they have released their moisture, finish cooking them with tomato sauce or tomato paste dissolved in water.
5. They can serve as a side dish or be eaten alone.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi Ripieni (Stuffed Artichokes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- tender artichoke leaves from the center
- prosciutto, more fat than lean, 50 grams
- onion, young, one quarter
- garlic, a pinch
- celery leaves
- parsley leaves
- dried mushrooms, softened
- bread marrow from day-old bread, crumbled, a handful
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the stem at the base, remove the small outer leaves, and wash the artichokes.
2. Trim them as the previous ones and open their inner leaves so that you can cut out with a small knife the central choke, and once removed the hair if there is any in the center, keep only the tender small leaves to add to the filling.
3. For six artichokes, compose the filling from the aforementioned small leaves, 50 grams of prosciutto more fat than lean, one quarter of a young onion, garlic the size of a nail tip, a few celery and parsley leaves, a pinch of softened dried mushrooms, a handful of bread marrow from day-old bread reduced to crumbles, and a pinch of pepper.
4. First chop the prosciutto with a knife, then everything together with the crescent-shaped tool, and with this mixture fill the artichokes which you will season and cook as the previous ones.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi Ripieni di Carne (Artichokes Stuffed with Meat)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes, 6
- milk veal lean meat, 100 grams
- prosciutto more fat than lean, 30 grams
- one central choke from the artichokes
- onion, young, one quarter
- parsley leaves
- dried mushrooms, softened, a pinch
- bread marrow, crumbled, a pinch
- parmesan cheese, grated, a pinch
- salt
- pepper
- spice flavor

## Directions

1. For six artichokes, compose the filling as described.
2. When the artichokes have taken color with oil alone, pour a little water and cover them with a cloth dampened and held firm by the lid.
3. The steam that emanates, reaching them from all sides, cooks them better.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi Ritti (Upright Artichokes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- water

## Directions

1. Remove only the small and useless leaves near the stem, cutting the latter short.
2. Trim the top with a knife and spread apart the internal leaves somewhat.
3. Place them upright in a pan, together with the peeled and whole stems.
4. Season with salt, pepper, and oil, all in good measure.
5. Sauté them while keeping them covered, and when they are well browned, pour a little water into the pan and finish cooking them with the same.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carciofi Seccati per l'Inverno (Dried Artichokes for Winter)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Water
- Vinegar or lemon
- Aromatic herbs (such as thyme, basil, celery leaves)

## Directions

1. Prepare them at the peak of harvest when they cost little; however, choose good quality artichokes at the right degree of maturity.
2. Remove all the tough leaves, trim the tips, clean a good length of the stem and cut them into four wedges, removing the fuzzy center if any.
3. As you cut them, throw them into fresh water acidulated with vinegar or lemon so they do not become black.
4. For the same reason, put them on the fire in an earthenware pot containing boiling water to which it is well to give flavor with a bundle of aromatic herbs, such as thyme, basil, celery leaves and similar.
5. Ten minutes of boiling, or even only five if they are tender, will be sufficient to cook them halfway.
6. Drain them and put them on a grating to dry in the sun; then string them and finish drying them in the shade in a ventilated place.
7. If possible do not keep them so long in the sun lest they acquire the smell of hay.
8. When used for frying or as a side dish to stewed dishes, soften them in boiling water.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cardoni con la Balsamella (Cardoons with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoons
- butter
- salt
- milk or cream
- béchamel sauce (N. 137)
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Discard the hardest ribs, clean the others from filaments and boil them until half cooked.
2. Cut the cardoon ribs into pieces about three fingers long and cook them to taste with butter and sufficient salt, finish cooking them by adding milk, or better cream, then bind them with a little béchamel sauce N. 137.
3. Add a pinch of grated Parmesan and remove them immediately without letting them boil again.
4. This is an excellent side dish to braised meats, bracioles, stewed giblets and other similar dishes.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cardoni in Gratella (Cardoons on the Grill)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoons
- oil
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. After boiling the cardoons, dry them well, leave the ribs about a palm long, season them generously with oil, pepper and salt and brown them on the grill.
2. They can serve as a side dish to steak or grilled fish.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cardoni in Panna (Cardoon Stalks in Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoon ribs
- Butter
- Salt
- Milk or cream
- Béchamel sauce (recipe N. 137)
- Grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cut the cardoon ribs into pieces about three fingers long
2. Sauté them with butter and salt to taste
3. Finish cooking by adding milk or preferably cream
4. Bind them with a little béchamel sauce (recipe N. 137)
5. Add a pinch of grated Parmesan cheese and remove from heat immediately without boiling further
6. Serve as an excellent side dish to braised meats, braciole, or stewed offal
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cardoni in Teglia (Cardoons in a Pan)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoons
- flour
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. First remove the filaments from the cardoons carefully and give them half cooking in salted water, then throw them immediately into fresh water so they do not blacken.
2. Cut them into small pieces, flour them and when the oil begins to boil, throw them in and season with salt and pepper.
3. Beat the eggs first and wait to pour them when the cardoons are browned on both sides.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cardoni in Umido (Cardoons Stewed)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoons
- garlic
- parsley
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- lemon juice
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. After boiling them as in the preceding recipe, stew them with a mixture of garlic and parsley; oil, salt and pepper.
2. If you desire them more pleasing to the taste and of better appearance, cover them with an egg and lemon sauce when they are already on the serving dish.
3. Beat some eggs with lemon juice, put the liquid on the fire in a small pan stirring the spoon, and pour it when it begins to thicken.
4. If you do not use the sauce, season them at least with a pinch of Parmesan.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carne all'Imperatrice (Beef à l'Impératrice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean beef from the neck, 500 grams
- Ham, fat and lean, 50 grams
- Parmesan cheese, grated, 3 heaping spoonfuls
- Eggs, 2
- Butter, 30 grams
- Oil, 2 spoonfuls
- Garlic
- Rosemary
- Tomato juice or conserve
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. If you do not have a meat grinder to reduce to paste both the meat and the ham, use a knife and a mortar. Add the Parmesan and eggs, season the mixture with salt and pepper, mix it well, and with wet hands form it into a flattened patty two fingers high.
2. Place 30 grams of butter and two spoonfuls of oil on the fire in a baking dish or pot; when they begin to boil place the said flattened patty of meat on it and on the same sprinkle a clove of garlic cut into slices and some sprigs of rosemary. Let it boil, and when it begins to dry out wet it with tomato juice or conserve dissolved in water. Send it to table surrounded by its sauce.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carne alla genovese (Genoese-Style Veal)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lean braciuola of veal weighing 300 to 400 grams
- three or four eggs
- salt
- pepper
- Parmesan cheese
- parsley
- butter
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Take a lean braciuola of veal weighing 300 to 400 grams, pound it and flatten it well.
2. Beat three or four eggs, season them with salt and pepper, a pinch of Parmesan, some chopped parsley and fry them in butter in the form of a frittata, approximately the width of the braciuola on which you will spread it, trimming where it overlaps and placing the trimmings where it is lacking.
3. After this, roll the braciuola together with the frittata tightly and tie it; then dust it with flour and place it in a pot with butter, seasoning it with salt and pepper.
4. When it has been well browned on all sides, moisten it with broth to finish cooking it and serve it with its own sauce, which because of the flour will be somewhat thick.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Carote Fritte (Fried Carrots)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Salt
- Flour
- Egg

## Directions

1. Without peeling them, cut them into thin fillets the length of a finger
2. Salt them and after a few hours, taking them up still moist, pass them through flour and shake them free of it
3. Place them in egg, turn them in it, and taking the fillets one by one with your fingers, throw them into the pan
4. These carrots can serve as a side dish for a fry when there are no more zucchini
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Castagnole I (Fried Dough Balls)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- two eggs
- one tablespoon of meat stock
- lemon zest
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. On a work surface, make a firm dough with flour, two eggs, a tablespoon of meat stock, lemon zest and salt to taste.
2. Work it thoroughly and strongly with your hands as you would common bread, making it gradually absorb a tablespoon of fine oil little by little.
3. Finally, shape it into small sticks, cut them into pieces the size of a walnut and immediately put them in a pan over low heat, stirring continuously.
4. When the castagnole are cooked, dust them with powdered sugar and serve them cold, as they are better cold than hot.
5. Note: if you use cognac or brandy instead of meat stock, you will not obtain the same effect and they will not puff up as much.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Castagnole II (Fried Dough Balls)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- two tablespoons of water
- two tablespoons of meat stock
- butter, 20 grams
- sugar, 20 grams
- a pinch of salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Put the egg yolks, sugar, meat stock, water and salt in a bowl.
2. Mix, beat the egg whites and with these ingredients and the butter knead enough flour on a work surface to form a dough that can be worked with your hands.
3. Work it thoroughly to refine it, then make small balls the size of a small walnut and fry them as the previous ones over low heat in plenty of fat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cavallucci di Siena (Sienese Spiced Pastries)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 300 grams
- Blonde sugar, 300 grams
- Shelled walnuts, 100 grams
- Candied orange, 50 grams
- Anise seeds, 15 grams
- Spices and cinnamon powder, 5 grams
- Water

## Directions

1. Chop the walnuts to roughly the size of vetch.
2. Cut the candied orange into small cubes.
3. Place the sugar on the fire with one third of its weight in water and when reduced to thread stage, throw all the ingredients into it, stir and pour the hot mixture onto the work surface over the flour to knead it; you will need more flour to make the dough consistent.
4. Form the cavallucci, from which you will obtain over 40 with this amount, and since this dough sticks due to the sugar, dust them with flour on the surface.
5. Place them on a baking sheet and bake them in white at moderate heat.
6. Pay careful attention to the cooking of the sugar, because if it cooks too much it becomes dark. When, taking a drop between thumb and forefinger, it begins to form a thread, that is enough for this use.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cavolfiore all'uso di Romagna (Cauliflower Romagna Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one large head of cauliflower or two small ones
- garlic
- parsley
- oil
- water
- salt
- pepper
- tomato preserve
- hot water
- parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Divide the cauliflower into small florets and wash them
2. Cook the raw, unwashed florets as follows: heat a chopped mixture of garlic, parsley and oil over the fire, and when it is browned, stop the cooking with a little water
3. Add the cauliflower, season with salt and pepper, and when it has absorbed the mixture, finish cooking it using tomato preserve dissolved in hot water
4. Add grace and more flavor with parmesan cheese when serving it at table, where it can serve as a side dish to boiled meat, a stew, or a coteghino
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cavolfiore all'uso di Romagna (Cauliflower Romagna Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large head of cauliflower or 2 if small
- garlic
- parsley
- oil
- water
- salt
- pepper
- tomato paste
- hot water
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Divide the cauliflower into small florets and wash them
2. Place a proportionate amount of chopped garlic, parsley and oil over heat, and when it is browned, stop it with a drop of water
3. Then throw in the cauliflower, seasoning with salt and pepper
4. When it has absorbed the mixture, cook it through with tomato paste dissolved in hot water
5. Add grace and more flavor with Parmesan when you serve it
6. It can serve as a side dish to boiled meat, a stew or a coteghino
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cavolfiore Colla Balsamella (Cauliflower with Béchamel)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large head of cauliflower
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parmesan
- Béchamel sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves and green ribs from a large head of cauliflower, make a deep cross-cut in the stem and cook it in salted water.
2. Cut it into small florets and season to taste with butter, salt and pepper.
3. Place it in a fireproof dish, sprinkle a small pinch of Parmesan over it, cover it entirely with béchamel sauce and brown the surface.
4. Serve this cauliflower hot as a side dish or better accompanied by a meat stew or boiled chicken.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cavolo bianco per contorno (White Cabbage as a Side Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cavolo cappuccio or verzotto (cabbage)
- prosciutto (ham)
- cipolla (onion)
- burro (butter)
- brodo (broth)
- pepe (pepper)
- sale (salt)

## Directions

1. Take a head of cabbage, cut it crosswise from the stem to form four parts and cut each of these into small pieces.
2. Keep it soaked in fresh water, and blanch it in salted water and, removed from the fire, drain it well without squeezing it.
3. Make a beaten mixture of ham and onion and put it on the fire with a piece of butter.
4. When the onion has taken on red color, stop it with a ladle of broth, boil a little and then pass the gravy.
5. In this gravy put back the cabbage with a piece of ham, season with pepper and little salt and boil it slowly to finish cooking it.
6. Remove the ham and send it to the table as a side dish to boiled meat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cavolo nero per contorno (Black Cabbage as a Side Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cavolo nero (black cabbage)
- prosciutto (ham)
- cipolla (onion)
- burro (butter)
- brodo (broth)
- pepe (pepper)
- sale (salt)
- olio (oil)
- aglio (garlic)
- pane (bread)

## Directions

1. Remove the hard ribs, boil it and mince it finely.
2. If you do not have meat gravy, make a small beaten mixture of ham and onion, put it on the fire with a piece of butter and when the onion is well browned, wet it with a drop of broth and pass the gravy formed.
3. In it throw the cabbage, season with pepper, little or no salt, add another piece of butter and more broth, if needed, and serve it as a side dish to boiled meat or coteghino.
4. Some use for a soup to roast slices of bread thick a finger, rub them with garlic, dip them barely in the water in which the black cabbage has boiled, placing the cabbage itself on it, still hot, and seasoning it with salt, pepper and oil. This, which is called in Florence cabbage with slices, is a dish for Carthusians or to be inflicted as penance on a glutton.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cavolo ripieno (Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cavolo cappuccio or verzotto (cabbage)
- vitella di latte (milk veal)
- fegatini (chicken livers)
- animelle (sweetbreads)
- balsamella (béchamel sauce)
- parmigiano (Parmesan cheese)
- rosso d'uovo (egg yolk)
- noce moscata (nutmeg)
- burro (butter)
- sugo dello stracotto (gravy from the braised meat)
- midolla di pane (bread crumb)
- brodo (broth)

## Directions

1. Take a large head of cabbage, clean it from the hard leaves on the surface, trim the stem evenly and give it half cooking in salted water.
2. Place it upside down to drain, then open the leaves one by one up to the center head and pour the filling on it.
3. Pull all the leaves back over it, close it completely and tie it in a cross pattern.
4. For the filling, make it with braised milk veal alone, or united with chicken livers and sweetbreads, everything minced finely.
5. To grace and make the mixture delicate, add a little béchamel, a pinch of Parmesan, an egg yolk and the flavor of nutmeg.
6. Finish cooking the cabbage in the gravy of the said braised meat, adding a piece of butter, with light fire below and above.
7. If you do not want to fill the whole cabbage, you can fill the wider leaves one by one by wrapping them over themselves like bobbins.
8. To the béchamel, a bread crumb soaked in broth or gravy can substitute.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cavolo Verzotto per Contorno (Glazed Cabbage as a Side)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Water
- Butter
- Milk
- Salt
- Béchamel
- Parmigiano

## Directions

1. Parboil it, squeeze out the water, chop it with a crescent-shaped blade, put it on the fire with butter and milk to cook it through and salt it.
2. When it is well cooked unite to it béchamel that is rather firm; make sure it incorporates well on the fire with the cabbage and add grated parmigiano.
3. Taste it to check if it has flavor and if the seasoning is right and serve it as a side with boiled meat or a meat stew.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cefali in gratella (Mullet Grilled)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Mullet
- salt
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Remove the scales and gills from the fish but do not gut them as the entrails are the best part.
2. Season them with salt and pepper only and place them on the grill over a hot fire.
3. When cooked, place them between two hot plates away from the fire for five minutes.
4. At the moment of serving, turn the plates so the one on top goes underneath and the rendered fat remains spread over the fish.
5. Serve at table with lemon to squeeze over.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cenci (Tuscan Fried Pastries)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 240 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Powdered sugar, 20 grams
- Two eggs
- One tablespoon of brandy
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Make a fairly firm dough with these ingredients
2. Work it very thoroughly by hand and let it rest a little, floured and wrapped in a cloth
3. If it becomes too tender to work, add more flour
4. Roll out a sheet the thickness of a coin
5. Cut it with a knife or fluted wheel into strips about a palm long and two or three fingers wide
6. Make some cuts in these strips so you can fold, braid or crumple them before frying
7. Drop them into a pan where the fat, oil or lard, floats in abundance, in bizarre shapes
8. Dust with powdered sugar when they are no longer hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cialdoni (Waffle Rolls)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 80 grams
- Blonde sugar, 30 grams
- Virgin lard, barely warm, 20 grams
- Cold water, seven tablespoons

## Directions

1. Place in a pot the flour, sugar, lard, and cold water.
2. Dissolve first, with the water, the flour and sugar, then add the lard.
3. Place the waffle iron over a hot burner and when it is well heated open it and pour over it each time half a tablespoon of the said batter; press the two parts of the iron together, pass it over the fire on one side and the other, remove the excess with a knife and open it when you know the waffle has taken a hazelnut color.
4. Then detach it somewhat from one side with the knife and immediately while still hot on the iron itself or on a cloth spread on the hearth roll it up with a reed bocciuolo or simply with your hands. This last operation must be done very quickly because if the waffle cools you will no longer be able to roll it up on itself.
5. If the waffles stick to the iron, oil it from time to time with lard, and if they do not all turn out well, add a bit of flour.
6. You already know that cialdoni can be served alone; but it is better to accompany them with cream or whipped cream and also with brûléed milk or Portuguese milk.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Ciambelle ossia Buccellati I (Ring Cakes I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Finest flour, 1,700 kilograms
- Sugar, 300 grams
- Yeast, 200 grams
- Butter, 150 grams
- Lard, 50 grams
- Milk, 4 deciliters
- Marsala, 2 deciliters
- Rum, two tablespoons
- Eggs, 6
- Baking soda, one teaspoon
- A pinch of salt
- Lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. Dissolve the yeast in a bowl with half the milk, adding enough flour to make a bread of proper consistency
2. After forming it, let it sit surrounded by flour to a depth of more than a finger
3. Place the bowl in a warm place, protected from air, and when that bread is well risen (which will take eight to ten hours depending on the season), break it down and remake it larger with the rest of the milk and flour needed
4. Wait for it to rise again and be well puffed up (which will take about the same time), then pour it onto the work surface and knead it with the rest of the flour and all the ingredients mentioned
5. Work it very well and with force so that the dough refines and becomes completely homogeneous
6. Prepare iron baking sheets or tinned copper pans, greased with lard and floured, and in them place the ring cakes which you will make as large as you like, but so that they have plenty of room
7. Let them rise in the kitchen or in another place of warm temperature, and when they are well puffed up but not over-risen, make long incisions on the surface with the point of a knife
8. Brush them with egg and sprinkle coarsely crushed crystal sugar over them
9. Bake them in the oven at moderate heat
10. In winter it is advisable to knead the yeast with lukewarm milk and send the ring cakes to rise in a warm place. With half the dose you can obtain four beautiful ring cakes of about 350 grams each, if you do not want to make them smaller
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Ciambelle ossia Buccellati II (Ring Cakes II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Hungarian flour, 500 grams
- Sugar, 180 grams
- Butter, 90 grams
- Cream of tartar, 15 grams
- Baking soda, 5 grams
- Eggs, 2
- Lemon peel flavoring or anise or candied citron in small pieces

## Directions

1. Make a well in the flour to put the melted butter, eggs, and sugar in it
2. Mix the flour with these ingredients and with the milk needed to form a dough of proper consistency and work it well
3. Add the two powders and flavorings last
4. Instead of one ring cake you can make two and keep them with a wide hole, which come out quite thick
5. Make some incisions on the surface, brush them with egg yolk and bake them in the oven or country oven, greasing the pan with butter or lard
6. Also with half the doses you obtain a decent ring cake
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Ciambelline (Fried Pastry Rings)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Water, 180 grams
- Butter, one large walnut-sized piece
- Sugar, 2 teaspoons
- Salt, a pinch
- Flour, 120 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Vanilla extract
- Flour for dusting
- Lard or oil for frying
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan 180 grams of water, a piece of butter the size of a large walnut, two teaspoons of sugar and a pinch of salt. When the liquid boils, dissolve 120 grams of flour in it, throwing it all at once so no lumps form and stir immediately with a spoon. Remove it quickly from the heat and while it is still boiling break one egg into it and mix strongly until it is well incorporated; then at intervals, when the mixture is cool, add two more eggs, working always and much with the spoon until it is well worked; and this is known by the action of the spoon itself, which, in the spaces it leaves, draws behind itself a thin veil of paste. Give it the scent of vanilla and prepare on a work surface a certain quantity of flour on which you will pour this paste. Then begin to work it with your hands covered in the same flour and roll it in so that it takes enough flour to become manageable, but still rather soft.
2. Divide this dough into 16 or 18 parts, forming as many little balls that will be a little more than walnut-sized: to each of these balls make a hole in the middle by pressing them with the tip of a finger against the work surface and turning them on themselves; flip them and do the same on the opposite side so the hole becomes large and graceful; thus these balls will take the form of little rings. Now put a wide-mouthed pot of water on the fire, and when the water is quite hot, but not boiling, throw the rings into it three or four at a time. If they stick to the bottom lift them slightly with a slotted spoon, turn them and when they come to the surface remove them dry and place them on a cloth, then with the tip of a knife make around and around each one, both on the outside and inside, an incision or even two at a certain distance, so they can puff better.
3. In this state you can leave them for hours if it suits you. Fry them with plenty of fat, lard or oil, over low heat, stirring the pan often; if they have come out well you will see them grow to an extraordinary volume, remaining dry. While still hot, but not boiling, dust them with powdered sugar and serve them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Ciarlotta (Charlotte)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 2 decilitres
- Sugar, 30 grams
- Flour, half a spoonful
- Eggs, 1
- Lemon zest for flavoring
- Water, 2 decilitres
- Sugar, 50 grams
- Sponge cake, 300 grams
- Fruit preserves

## Directions

1. Make a cream with the milk, sugar, flour, egg and lemon zest
2. Make a syrup by boiling the sugar in water for ten minutes; when cooled squeeze a lemon into it
3. Take 300 grams of sponge cake and with it, cut into slices, with fruit preserves and the said cream, fill a well-worked pudding mold
4. Lastly pour the said syrup into it to soak the sponge cake or ladyfingers, and after several hours unmold it and serve it
5. This dose will be enough for eight people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Ciarlotta di mele (Apple Charlotte)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Reinette apples, 500 grams
- Powdered sugar, 125 grams
- Crumb of stale bread, as much as needed
- Fresh butter of good quality, as much as needed
- A piece of whole cinnamon
- Half a lemon

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, cut them into quarters, remove the seeds and the compartments that contain them and place them in fresh water where the lemon has been squeezed
2. Remove these quarters of apple dry and cut them crosswise into thin slices, which you will place on the fire in a saucepan without water and with the piece of cinnamon
3. When they begin to liquefy, add the sugar and stir, moving them often until they are cooked
4. Then remove the cinnamon and use it as follows
5. Melt the butter on the fire and when it boils, that is very hot, dip as many slices of bread crumb as needed, less than one centimetre thick, which you will have prepared before
6. With these cover the bottom of a smooth round mold and line the walls with them so that no empty spaces remain
7. Pour the preserve in the middle and cover it with the same slices of bread dipped in butter
8. Cook it
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cibreo (Chicken Giblets Fricassee)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken livers (removing the gall bladder)
- Chicken crests
- Chicken testicles
- Butter, in proportion
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Beef broth as needed
- Egg yolks
- Flour
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the gall bladder from the livers, blanch the crests with boiling water, cut them in two or three pieces and cut the livers in half.
2. Put on the fire with butter in proportion, first the crests, then the livers and last the testicles and season with salt and pepper, then broth if needed to cook these things.
3. In a small pot, put one or two egg yolks with a teaspoon or half only of flour, lemon juice and boiling broth whisking so the egg does not curdle.
4. Pour this sauce over the giblets when they are cooked, let it boil for a while and add more broth, if necessary, to make it more liquid, and serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cicale Fritte (Fried Mantis Shrimp)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Mantis shrimp
- Water
- Flour
- Egg
- Salt
- Oil

## Directions

1. Wash the mantis shrimp and boil them in a little water, covered with a cloth with a weight on top; 15 minutes of boiling should be sufficient.
2. Shell them after cooking and, exposing the flesh, cut it into two pieces.
3. Dust with flour, dip in beaten and salted egg, and fry it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cicale Fritte (Fried Mantis Shrimp)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- mantis shrimp
- water
- flour
- egg
- salt
- oil for frying

## Directions

1. After washing them, boil in a little water, covered with a cloth with a weight on top; 15 minutes of boiling is sufficient.
2. After cooking, peel them and, with the flesh exposed, cut it into two pieces.
3. Dust with flour, dip in beaten and salted egg, and fry in oil.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cicale in Umido (Stewed Mantis Shrimp)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- mantis shrimp
- fresh water
- garlic
- parsley
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- tomato juice or conserve
- bread
- scissors

## Directions

1. Before cooking, keep the mantis shrimp in fresh water so they don't drain and instead swell.
2. Make a battuto with garlic, parsley and oil; once browned, place the whole mantis shrimp in it and season with salt and pepper.
3. When they have taken on the seasoning, wet them with tomato juice or conserve and serve them on slices of bread dried over the fire.
4. Before bringing them to the table, make an incision with scissors along the back so they can be peeled more easily.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cicale Ripiene (Stuffed Mantis Shrimp)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Mantis shrimp (_Squilla mantis_)
- Breadcrumbs
- Parsley
- Garlic
- Oil
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Split the mantis shrimp along the back.
2. Fill it with a mixture of breadcrumbs, parsley and garlic scent.
3. Season both the filling and the fish with oil, pepper and salt.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cieche alla Pisana (Transparent Gobies Pisan Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Transparent gobies, 300-350 grams
- oil
- garlic, 1-2 cloves
- sage leaves
- salt and pepper
- water
- eggs, 2
- Parmesan cheese, 2 spoonfuls
- breadcrumbs, 1 spoonful
- lemon, half

## Directions

1. Wash the gobies several times until they no longer produce foam.
2. Drain them on a sieve.
3. Heat oil with garlic cloves (slightly crushed) and sage leaves.
4. When the garlic is colored, add the gobies.
5. If still alive, cover with a lid so they do not jump away.
6. Season with salt and pepper, stir often with a spoon and moisten with a little water if they dry out too much.
7. When cooked, bind them with beaten eggs mixed separately with Parmesan, breadcrumbs, and lemon.
8. If serving in the cooking vessel, place between two fires at the end so they form a white crust on the surface.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cieche Fritte I (Fried Transparent Gobies I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Transparent gobies
- oil
- garlic, whole
- sage
- eggs
- salt
- Parmesan cheese
- breadcrumbs
- lemon, cut into wedges

## Directions

1. Cook the gobies in oil with whole garlic and sage as described in the Cieche alla Pisana recipe.
2. Remove the garlic and finely chop the gobies.
3. Beat eggs in proportion, salt them, add Parmesan and a little breadcrumbs.
4. Mix the gobies into the egg mixture.
5. Fry by spoonfuls to make small fritters.
6. Serve with lemon wedges.
7. Few will notice it is a fish dish when eaten this way.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cieche Fritte II (Fried Transparent Gobies II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Transparent gobies
- wheat flour or corn flour
- oil

## Directions

1. Flour the gobies only with wheat flour or corn flour.
2. Fry in a pan.
3. This method makes them simpler but less good than the previous preparation.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cignale dolce-forte (Sweet and Sour Wild Boar)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar piece, approximately one kilogram, with skin and one finger-width of fat
- Onion, half
- Carrot, half of a large one
- Celery, two white ribs, one palm-length
- Parsley, a pinch
- Prosciutto fat and lean, 30 grams
- Oil
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Make a battuto with half an onion, half of a large carrot, two white celery ribs about one palm-length, a pinch of parsley, and 30 grams of prosciutto (both fat and lean).
2. Mince finely with a crescent knife (lunetta) and place in a pot with oil, salt, and pepper under the boar to cook together.
3. Brown the meat on all sides.
4. Drain off most of the fat and continue as directed in the recipe (the text is cut off at this point).
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cignale fra Due Fuochi (Wild Boar Between Two Fires)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar, about 1 kilogram
- marinade as for hare (recipe N. 531)
- three or four thin slices of lard, thin as paper
- salt
- pepper
- one whole onion
- a bouquet garni
