- a small piece of butter
- half a glass of white wine (if boar is about 1 kilogram)
- more butter for coating paper
- broth

## Directions

1. Keep the wild boar in a marinade like that for hare (recipe N. 531) for 12 or 14 hours.
2. Remove it from the marinade, dry it with a cloth and then prepare it as follows.
3. Place three or four slices of lard, thin as paper, on the bottom of a pot, place the piece of wild boar on top of them, season with salt and pepper and add one whole onion, a bouquet garni, a small piece of butter and, if the boar is about 1 kilogram, half a glass of white wine.
4. Spread three or four more slices of the same lard on top of the meat and cover it with a sheet of paper coated with butter that adheres well.
5. Cook it with heat below and above, and when it begins to dry out, baste it with broth.
6. When cooked, strain the sauce without squeezing it, degrease it and add it to the boar when serving.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cignale in Dolce-Forte (Wild Boar in Sweet-Strong Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar, about 1 kilogram with skin and a finger's width of fat
- half an onion
- half a large carrot
- two stalks of white celery about a palm's length
- a pinch of parsley
- 30 grams of fatty and lean prosciutto
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- hot water
- 40 grams raisins
- 30 grams chocolate
- 30 grams pine nuts
- 20 grams candied fruit in small pieces
- 50 grams sugar
- vinegar, as needed

## Directions

1. Make a battuto (finely chopped mixture) with half an onion, half a large carrot, two stalks of white celery about a palm's length, a pinch of parsley and 30 grams of fatty and lean prosciutto. Mince it finely with a crescent-shaped knife and place it in a pot with oil, salt and pepper under the wild boar to cook together.
2. When the piece has browned on all sides, drain most of the fat, sprinkle a pinch of flour over it, and finish cooking it with hot water poured in from time to time.
3. Meanwhile prepare the sweet-strong sauce in a glass with the following ingredients and add it to the pot after straining the cooking liquid: 40 grams raisins, 30 grams chocolate, 30 grams pine nuts, 20 grams candied fruit in small pieces, 50 grams sugar, and vinegar as needed (use sparingly as you can add more later).
4. Before serving, let it boil again so that the sauce incorporates well. Note that the sweet-strong sauce works better if made a day in advance.
5. If you prefer a simpler version, make the sweet-strong sauce with only sugar and vinegar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Ciliege Visciole in Guazzo (Sour Cherries Preserved in Syrup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cherries, 1 kilogram
- White sugar, 300 grams
- A small piece of cinnamon

## Directions

1. From the sour cherries, separate 200 grams of the worst or damaged ones, extract their juice and strain it
2. The others, having removed the stem, layer them in a crystal vase: one of cherries and one of sugar, then pour the said juice over them
3. Remove the pits from some of the damaged cherries and throw these along with the cinnamon into the vase
4. Close the vase and do not move it for at least two months
5. You will see that the sugar will gradually dissolve and the cherries will first float in the liquid, then as this converts to alcohol the cherries sink to the bottom and then they are edible and good
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cioccolata (Hot Chocolate)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams of chocolate for an abundant cup, or 50 grams if light is preferred, up to 80 grams if very thick is desired
- two decilitres of water
- cinnamon or vanilla for flavoring

## Directions

1. Break the chocolate into pieces and place in a chocolate pot with the water mentioned above
2. When it begins to be hot, stir it so that it does not stick and dissolves well
3. As soon as it comes to a boil, remove it from the fire and whisk it for five minutes
4. Then bring it to a boil again and serve it
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cipolline Agro-Dolci (Sweet and Sour Pearl Onions)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pearl onions, about 300 grams
- Sugar, 40 grams
- Flour, 15 grams
- Water, two-thirds of a glass
- Vinegar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Choose small white onions, about the size of a walnut
2. Peel them and trim away any excess
3. Blanch them in salted boiling water
4. In a saucepan, heat the sugar over dry heat until liquefied
5. Add the flour and stir continuously with a wooden spoon
6. When the mixture turns red, gradually add two-thirds glass of water mixed with vinegar
7. Let the liquid boil until any lumps dissolve
8. Add the pearl onions and shake the saucepan frequently, being careful not to touch them with a spoon to avoid damaging them
9. Taste before serving and adjust sugar or vinegar if needed
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cipolline in Istufa (Braised Pearl Onions)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pearl onions
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Meat stock
- Flour
- Lard
- Mirepoix bundle
- Prosciutto slice
- Broth

## Directions

1. Peel the onions and trim the top and bottom evenly
2. Boil them in salted water for ten minutes
3. Brown a piece of butter until it turns hazelnut color
4. Add the onions, season with salt and pepper, and brown on one side
5. Turn them over and brown on the other side
6. Wet them with meat stock and bind with flour mixed with butter
7. Alternatively, if meat stock is unavailable, cook them in white: after boiling and cooling in fresh water, place them in a saucepan with a mirepoix bundle, a small slice of prosciutto, a piece of butter, and a ladle of broth
8. Season with pepper and little salt
9. Cover with very thin slices of lard and place a sheet of butter-greased paper on top
10. Finish cooking between two fires and serve as a side dish with the reduced sauce
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cipolline per Contorno ai Coteghini (Pearl Onions as a Side to Cotechino Sausage)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pearl onions, 28 or 30 pieces
- Butter, 50 grams
- Broth from cotechino sausage, half a ladle
- Vinegar, half a tablespoon
- Sugar, one teaspoon
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the onions as described in the previous recipe
2. Sauté them in butter and season with salt and pepper
3. Wet them with defatted broth from the cotechino sausage
4. Add vinegar and sugar to enhance them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Claret Cup (English Beverage)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Red wine of excellent quality, 5 decilitres
- Water, 5 decilitres
- Lemons, 5 number
- White sugar, 500 grams

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in the water for five minutes.
2. Removed from the fire, squeeze the lemons into this syrup and pour in the wine, then strain it through a cloth.
3. Return it to the fire to boil slowly for 25 minutes and when it is cold bottle it.
4. Use it diluted with water and chilled with ice in summer.
5. If you need to keep it for a long time, keep it in the cellar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Composta di Cotogne (Quince Compote)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces, 500 grams
- Fine sugar, 180 grams
- Water, one and a half glasses

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces and cut them into wedges not too large, removing the middle part that was part of the core
2. Place them on the fire with water and when they have boiled covered for a quarter of an hour, pour in the fine sugar
3. Once cooked, remove them dry and place them in the dish you want to serve at the table; reduce the remaining juice to make it into a syrup, then pour it over the quinces and serve them cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conchiglie Ripiene (Stuffed Shells)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine fish (cooked), 130 grams
- Parmesan cheese (grated), 20 grams
- Flour, 20 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Egg yolks, 2
- Milk, 2.5 deciliters

## Directions

1. Make a béchamel sauce with the milk, butter and flour
2. When removing it from the fire, add the Parmesan and, no longer at boiling point, the egg yolks and the minced fish, seasoning with salt and pepper
3. Pour it into shells buttered beforehand with cold butter
4. Brown it slightly in a country oven and serve it
5. Alternatively, the shells could be filled with minced cooked chicken flesh keeping the same proportions
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Coniglio Arrosto (Roasted Rabbit)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- hind quarters of rabbit
- lardons of lard
- oil or butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Stud it with lardons.
2. Baste with oil or, better, with butter and salt when cooking is almost complete.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Albicocche (Apricot Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-ripened apricots of good quality
- White fine sugar in powder, 800 grams per kilogram of apricots

## Directions

1. Take well-ripened apricots of good quality, as it is an error to believe that with inferior fruit one can obtain the same effect.
2. Remove the pit and put them on the fire without water, and while they boil, break them down with a spoon to reduce them to a pulp.
3. When they have boiled for about half an hour, pass them through a strainer to clean them of skins and filaments.
4. Then return them to the fire with white fine sugar in powder in the proportion of eight tenths, that is 800 grams of sugar for every kilogram of apricots passed.
5. Stir them often with a spoon until they reach the consistency of preserve, which is known by pouring a spoonful from time to time onto a plate, on which it should run slowly.
6. Pour it hot into jars and when it is cold cover it with oiled butcher's paper adhering to the preserve, and seal the mouth of the jar with thick paper tied with twine around it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Aranci (Orange Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges, 12 number
- One garden lemon
- Fine white sugar, equal to the weight of the oranges
- Water, half the weight of the oranges
- Genuine rum, four tablespoons

## Directions

1. With the prongs of a fork prick the entire skin of the oranges.
2. Keep them soaked for three days, changing the water morning and evening.
3. On the fourth day cut them in half and each half into strips about half a centimeter thick, discarding the seeds.
4. Weigh them and only then regulate the sugar and water according to the proportions indicated.
5. Put them on the heat first with water alone and after ten minutes of boiling add the lemon cut like the oranges.
6. Immediately after add the sugar and stir continuously until the liquid resumes a strong boil, because otherwise the sugar precipitates to the bottom and could stick to the pan.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Aranci (Orange Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Water
- Sugar (amount determined by weight of fruit)
- Lemon (optional)
- Rum (optional)

## Directions

1. Pierce the entire peel of the oranges with the tines of a fork.
2. Soak them in water for three days, changing the water morning and evening.
3. On the fourth day, cut them in half and then each half into thick strips about half a centimeter wide, removing the seeds.
4. Weigh them and then regulate the sugar and water according to the indicated proportions.
5. Put them on the fire first with only water, and after ten minutes of boiling add the lemon cut the same way as the oranges.
6. Immediately after, pour in the sugar and stir continuously until the liquid returns to a strong boil, otherwise the sugar will precipitate to the bottom and could stick to the pot.
7. To determine the point of cooking, occasionally pour a few drops onto a plate, blow on it, and if it is slow to flow remove it immediately.
8. Wait until it is lukewarm before adding the rum, and pour it into jars to preserve it like all other fruit preserves.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Aranci Forti (Bitter Orange Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Bitter oranges
- Water
- Sugar, 150 grams per 100 grams of fruit (white, fine)

## Directions

1. Boil the bitter oranges in water until they can be easily passed through from one side to the other with a stick.
2. Remove them from the boiling water and place them in cold water and keep them there for two days, changing the water often.
3. Cut them as the previous recipe, cleaning them of seeds and those white filaments found inside.
4. After weighing them, put them on the fire without water with 150 grams of white fine sugar for every 100 grams of fruit.
5. Boil slowly and be careful that the syrup does not condense too much, otherwise the oranges will harden.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Azzeruole (Medlar Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- White azzeruoles, 1 kilogram
- White sugar, 800 grams
- Water, 7 deciliters

## Directions

1. Throw the azzeruoles into boiling water with their stems attached.
2. Boil for ten minutes.
3. While still hot, with the point of a small knife remove the pits from the flower end.
4. If any lose their shape, reshape with your fingers.
5. Peel them without removing the stem.
6. Dissolve the sugar in the 7 deciliters of water (which can be the water in which they boiled).
7. Add the azzeruoles and when the syrup taken with a spoon begins to fall in drops, remove them with their liquid and preserve in jars.
8. They remain like candied fruit and are very good.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Lampone (Raspberry Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries, any quantity
- White powdered sugar, equal weight to the strained raspberries

## Directions

1. Put the raspberries on the heat as they are without fermentation.
2. Boil for about twenty minutes.
3. Pass through a sieve.
4. Weigh the strained raspberries.
5. Return to the heat with an equal amount of white powdered sugar.
6. Boil until it reaches the consistency of preserve, which you will know by the methods already indicated.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di More (Blackberry Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Blackberries, 1 kilogram
- White sugar, 200 grams

## Directions

1. Crush the blackberries by hand.
2. Bring to boil for about ten minutes.
3. Pass through a sieve.
4. Return to the heat with the mentioned sugar to reduce to the consistency of fruit preserves.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Pomodoro Senza Sale (Tomato Preserve Without Salt)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Field tomatoes
- Copper pot (not tinned)

## Directions

1. Choose field tomatoes rather than garden tomatoes, as they are less watery, and prefer small ones to large ones.
2. Crush them roughly and put them in a copper pot (not tinned) over a wood fire. Do not worry about the acid attacking the copper when it is on the fire and retains the heat of boiling.
3. When they are cooked and broken down, pour them into a tightly-woven sack held suspended and, once the water has drained, pass them through a strainer to clean them of seeds and skins, squeezing them well.
4. Wash the pot carefully and return them to the fire to reduce them as needed. To know the precise point of consistency the preserve should have, pour a few drops onto a plate and if you see that it does not run and does not present watery seroseness around it, that will mean this is the correct point of cooking.
5. Then bottle it, and you will have another proof of its sufficient density if you see it descend with difficulty through the funnel.
6. Prefer small bottles to consume them quickly; they can remain unopened for 12 or 13 days without the preserve suffering. Use white bottles that come in commerce with Recoaro water, or in their absence, half-bottles of dark beer. Seal them with cork stoppers put on by hand, but which seal well, and tie them with twine, taking care to leave a little air between the stopper and the liquid.
7. Place the said bottles in a pot amongst hay, rags, or similar things so that they stand tightly together, and pour into the pot enough water that reaches up to the neck of the bottles and heat from underneath. Watch as soon the stopper of the bottles will show signs of rising and spraying away if it were not tied, and then stop the fire, because the operation is truly finished.
8. Remove the bottles when the water is cold or even before, replace with a finger the displaced stoppers to put them back in place and keep the bottles in the cellar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Rose (Rose Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- White fine sugar, 600 grams
- Rose petals (net), 200 grams
- Water, 6 deciliters
- Half a lemon
- Breton, one teaspoon

## Directions

1. Place the roses in a bowl with 200 grams of the sugar and the juice of half a lemon and with your hands rub them, chop them as much as possible to reduce them to nearly a paste.
2. Dissolve the rest of the sugar in the water over the fire and add the roses to it to boil until the syrup is condensed, which is known if, taking a drop between the fingers, it begins to stick; but be careful that it does not reach the thread stage.
3. Before removing them from the fire, give them color with breton, which you can omit if you do not care about the beautiful color.
4. When the preserve is cooled, put it in small jars to preserve it like all the other similar ones.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Susine (Plum Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums, preferably Claudia variety, any quantity
- White powdered sugar, 60 grams per 100 grams of fresh plums

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the plums.
2. Boil for a few minutes.
3. Pass through a sieve.
4. Return to the heat with white powdered sugar in the proportion of 60 grams of sugar per 100 grams of fresh plums.
5. If mold forms after some time, it indicates insufficient cooking; remedy by returning to the heat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva di Susine (Plum Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Mature Claudia plums
- White powdered sugar, 60 grams of sugar per 100 grams of plums

## Directions

1. Any variety may do, but mature Claudia plums are to be preferred.
2. Remove the pit and after a few minutes of boiling pass them through a strainer and return them to the fire with white powdered sugar in the proportion of 60 grams of sugar for every 100 grams of plums in their natural state, that is as they come from the plant.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva Dolce di Pomodoro (Sweet Tomato Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes, 1 kilogram
- White sugar, 300 grams
- Juice of one lemon
- Vanilla flavoring and lemon zest

## Directions

1. The tomatoes for this use must be very ripe, pulpy, and possibly round.
2. Place them in very hot water so they can be peeled easily. After peeling, cut them in half and with the handle of a teaspoon remove the seeds.
3. Dissolve the sugar in the fire in two fingers (of a glass) of water, then throw in the tomatoes, the juice of the lemon, and a little of its grated skin.
4. During boiling over low heat in an uncovered pan, stir somewhat, and if any seeds appear that have remained, remove them.
5. Finally give it flavor with vanillated sugar and remove it when it has reached the consistency of common preserves.
6. Note: It is difficult to specify the quantity of sugar in this preserve, as it depends on how watery the tomatoes are. Make double the dose as it reduces greatly.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva Liquida di Cotogne (Liquid Quince Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces, any quantity
- Water
- White powdered sugar, equal to the weight of the raw quinces

## Directions

1. Cut the quinces into segments.
2. Remove the hard part of the core.
3. Leave the skin on and after weighing them, put them on the heat covered with water.
4. When well cooked, pass them and return them to the heat with the water in which they boiled and as much white powdered sugar as was their raw weight, waiting to pour it when they are boiling.
5. Stir often and when (pouring a few drops on a plate) you do not see it running too much, remove it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Conserva Soda di Cotogne (Firm Quince Preserve)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces, cleaned of skin and core, 800 grams
- Fine white sugar, 500 grams
- Water, half a glass and one glass

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar on the heat with half a glass of water.
2. Boil it a little and set it aside.
3. Cut the quinces into very thin slices.
4. Put them on the heat with one glass of water in a copper pan.
5. Keep them covered but stir often, trying to chop and crush them with a spoon.
6. When they become tender from cooking, add the prepared sugar syrup.
7. Stir often and let boil uncovered until the preserve is done, which you will know when it begins to fall in shreds taken up on a spoon.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Contorno di Filetti di Sogliole a un Fritto dello Stesso Pesce (Side Dish of Sole Fillets with a Fried Sole Preparation)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- sole fish, a pair of medium size or one large
- lemon juice
- milk
- flour
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Take a pair of medium sole or one large sole, remove the fillets after skinning them (there will be four), and cut them crosswise into fine strips like matches
2. If you cut them at an angle they will be somewhat longer and better
3. Put them in a bowl with the juice of one or more lemons as needed and let them marinate for two or three hours, which will make them firm, otherwise they would be limp
4. Shortly before serving, dry them with a cloth, immerse in milk, flour them, make sure they don't clump, and fry in oil
5. Salt them lightly
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Coratella d'Agnello alla Bolognese (Lamb Offal, Bolognese Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb liver
- lamb lungs (pasto)
- lard
- butter
- tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in water or broth
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into thin slices and the lungs into pieces and throw them as is into a pan with lard.
2. When the offal is almost cooked, drain it from all the fat and add a piece of butter; continue frying.
3. Shortly after, pour tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in water or broth into the pan.
4. Season with salt and pepper, serve it with this sauce, and be sure it will be praised.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cosciotto di Castrato Arrosto (Roasted Leg of Mutton)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a leg of mutton
- salt
- pepper
- degreased broth

## Directions

1. Let it age for several days before cooking, more or less depending on temperature.
2. Beat it well with a wooden mallet before threading onto the spit.
3. Skin it and remove the middle bone without damaging it too much.
4. Tie it so it stays compact and apply intense heat at first, then reduce heat halfway through cooking.
5. When it begins to release juices, collect them in the dripping pan and baste with the same juices and degreased broth, nothing else.
6. Salt when cooking is almost complete; ensure it is not overcooked nor bloody.
7. Serve at table with its juices in a sauceboat and wrap the end of the leg bone in fringed white paper for better presentation.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cosciotto o Spalla di Castrato in Cazzaruola I (Braised Mutton Leg or Shoulder I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg shoulder or leg of mutton, boned
- 40 grams butter
- Lardoon strips for larding
- Salt and pepper
- Strips of lardoon or prosciutto rind
- 1 bunch tied together of parsley, celery and carrot
- 1 onion of medium size
- Broken bones from the shoulder or leg
- Meat scraps, if available
- 1 glass of broth or half a glass
- 2 or 3 tablespoons of brandy
- Cold water to nearly cover the meat

## Directions

1. Bone the shoulder and lard it with lardoon strips
2. Season inside and out with salt and pepper
3. Roll it up and tie it to achieve a nice shape
4. Place it in a braising pan with 40 grams of butter to brown it
5. Add the lardoon or prosciutto rinds, the tied bunch of herbs, the whole onion, the broken bones, meat scraps if you have them, the glass of broth or half a glass, and 2 or 3 tablespoons of brandy
6. Add enough cold water so the liquid reaches just below the surface of the mutton
7. Cover the pan well and simmer slowly until the meat is cooked, which will take four or more hours if the animal is tough
8. Strain the sauce, degrease it and discard the excess
9. Serve the mutton, optionally garnished with carrots, turnips, or beans prepared according to the instructions given in the text
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cosciotto o Spalla di Castrato in Cazzaruola II (Braised Mutton Leg or Shoulder II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 mutton shoulder, boned
- Lardoon strips wrapped in salt and pepper
- Salt
- 40 grams butter
- 1 half onion studded with 1 clove
- 1 glass of water or better broth
- 1 tablespoon of brandy
- 1 aromatic bunch
- Tomatoes, if in season, broken into pieces

## Directions

1. Bone the shoulder and lard it with lardoon strips wrapped in salt and pepper
2. Salt it somewhat
3. Roll it up and tie it tightly
4. Place it on the fire with 40 grams of butter and the half onion studded with a clove to take on color
5. Remove the braising pan from the fire and pour in a glass of water or better broth, 1 tablespoon of brandy, an aromatic bunch, and if it is tomato season, some tomatoes broken up
6. Boil gently for about three hours with the pan covered with double sheets of paper, turning the meat frequently
7. When cooked, discard the onion, strain the sauce, degrease it and add it to the meat when serving
8. Do not cook it too much or it will not be able to be cut into slices
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Costolette d'Agnello Vestite (Dressed Lamb Chops)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine lamb chops
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Béchamel, thick
- Prosciutto
- Salted tongue
- Parmesan cheese
- Nutmeg
- Truffle or softened and chopped dried mushrooms
- Puff pastry
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Take fine lamb chops, denude the bone of the chop, flatten them, trim them, cook them naturally in butter sautéed, season them hot with salt and pepper and set them aside.
2. Make a thick béchamel and in it throw prosciutto and salted tongue into very small dice, a pinch of Parmesan, a pinch of nutmeg and a truffle sliced or softened and chopped dried mushrooms, and set this mixture aside so it cools well.
3. Make a puff pastry proportioned to the quantity of chops and with it wrap them one by one, leaving the bone of the chop exposed, but first spread them abundantly on one side and the other with the said mixture.
4. When you have closed them, gild them with egg yolk, place them upright around the edge of a baking dish, cook them in a country oven and serve them hot.
5. You can cut the puff pastry with a paper template, so the wrapping will be more precise; for more neatness and elegance, before sending them to the table, wrap the end of each chop with scalloped white paper.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Costolette di Daino alla Cacciatora (Venison Chops Hunter-Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- venison chops from the loin, sliced thin
- oil
- butter
- one clove of garlic, whole
- sage leaves
- salt
- pepper
- marsala wine

## Directions

1. Heat oil and butter in proportion to the quantity you have to cook, add one whole clove of garlic and several sage leaves.
2. When the garlic has browned, place the chops on top, season with salt and pepper and cook them quickly over high heat, basting them with marsala wine.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Costolette di Vitella di Latte alla Milanese (Milanese-Style Milk Veal Cutlets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Prosciutto, more fat than lean
- Parsley
- Grated Parmesan cheese
- Truffles (optional)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Lemon wedges

## Directions

1. After baring the bone of the cutlet and discarding the trimmings, flatten them with the blade of a large knife to widen and thin them.
2. Then make a mixture with prosciutto more fat than lean, a little parsley, grated Parmesan cheese, the aroma of truffles if you have them, and a little salt and pepper.
3. Spread this mixture on the cutlets on one side only, dip them in egg, then bread them and cook them sauté in butter, serving them with lemon wedges.
4. For five cutlets, if they are not very large, 50 grams of prosciutto and two heaping tablespoons of Parmesan will suffice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Costolette nella Carta (Chops in Paper)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk veal chops
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Prosciutto, fat and lean
- Parsley
- Breadcrumb marrow
- Raw truffle slices
- Thick white paper
- Oil

## Directions

1. Take milk veal chops, denude the bone of the chop by removing the meat, cook them in butter sautéed and season them with salt and pepper.
2. Make a proportioned mixture of fat and lean prosciutto and parsley, chop it fine, add butter and breadcrumb marrow to hold it together and with this spread the chops on both sides, then decorate them with slices of raw truffles.
3. Cut thick white paper to the pattern of as many chops as there are, oil it with butter or oil on both sides and with it wrap them tightly with the bone of the chop outside.
4. Now place them on a grill on light fire, being careful that the paper does not burn and send them to the table, for more neatness, with the end of the chop wrapped in scalloped white paper.
5. Lamb chops can also be used for this purpose if they are large.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Coteghino Fasciato (Wrapped Coteghino)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Coteghino weighing about 300 grams, skinned raw
- Lean veal or beef slice, 200 to 300 grams, wide and thin
- Butter
- Celery
- Carrot
- One quarter onion
- Dried mushroom pieces
- Water
- Flour
- Carrots for contorno

## Directions

1. Skin the coteghino raw and beat the veal or beef slice well
2. Wrap the coteghino with the meat slice, tie it all with string and put it on the fire in a pot with a piece of butter, celery, carrot and a quarter onion, all cut coarsely
3. When the piece has taken color on all sides, pour in enough water to cover it halfway and add some pieces of dried mushrooms, letting it boil slowly until completely cooked
4. Pass the gravy, unite the mushrooms to it and use this with cheese and butter to dress pasta, serving the wrapped coteghino, freed from the string, with some of its gravy around it as accompaniment
5. To thicken the gravy for the pasta, put a pinch of flour in a pot with a piece of butter and when it begins to take color pour the gravy into it and let it boil a little
6. The contorno of carrots works well with this dish, first boiled to two-thirds done, then finished in that gravy
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cotognata (Quince Paste)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces, 3 kilograms
- Fine white sugar, 2 kilograms

## Directions

1. Put the quinces on the fire covered with water and when they begin to crack, remove them, peel them and scrape them as best you can to remove all the pulp, then pass it through a sieve.
2. Put it back on the fire with the sugar and stir it constantly so it doesn't stick.
3. Seven or eight minutes of boiling will be enough; but then, if taken up with a spoon it begins to fall in shreds, remove it.
4. If you put it in vessels it can serve you as a preserve and made this way it will remain whiter than the one I will describe at No. 741, but with less fragrance, because part of the particular odor of this fruit is lost in the water.
5. To reduce it to quince paste, spread it on a board about the thickness of a coin and dry it in the sun covered with a veil because flies and wasps are very fond of it.
6. When it is dry on top, cut it in the form of chocolate tablets and by passing a knife under it to detach it from the board, turn it over on the opposite side.
7. If you would like to give it bizarre forms, procure some tin molds empty on both sides, fill them, smooth them and detaching the marmalade from the edges carefully, place it equally on the board and dry it in the same manner.
8. You can also coat it with sugar if you wish, and then put 100 grams of white sugar to melt with two spoonfuls of water and when it has boiled enough to form a thread (taking a drop between two fingers) spread each piece with a brush.
9. If the sugar hardens during the operation (which is best to do on a not humid day) put it back on the fire with another drop of water and boil it again.
10. When the sugar is dry on one side and on the edges, spread the opposite side.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cotolette col Prosciutto (Veal Cutlets with Prosciutto)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Prosciutto, a very thin slice fat and lean
- Egg, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Parmesan or gruyere cheese, sliced very thin
- Meat juice
- Lemon juice
- Tomato juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the cutlets as in the previous recipe and place them in the egg with a very thin slice of prosciutto fat and lean the size of the cutlet itself.
2. Coat them with the prosciutto stuck on top, salt lightly and brown them in butter on the side without the prosciutto.
3. Over the prosciutto, instead of truffles, spread very thin slices of parmesan or gruyere.
4. Finish cooking them with heat on top and serve with meat juice and lemon juice, or with tomato juice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cotolette di Arigusta (Lobster Cutlets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster (about 650 grams)
- béchamel sauce
- breadcrumbs
- egg (1)
- oil

## Directions

1. Boil the lobster as indicated in the previous recipe, then shell it to extract all the internal part and mince it coarsely with a crescent-shaped blade
2. Make a béchamel sauce in the proportions and manner of recipe no. 220
3. When removing it from the fire, add the lobster inside, salt it and after mixing well the mixture, pour it onto a plate and let it cool well for some hours
4. When ready to form the cutlets, divide the mixture into ten equal parts and touching them to breadcrumbs, shape them between the palms of your hands to the thickness slightly more than half a finger
5. Dip them in beaten egg, bread them again and fry them in oil
6. Make ten pieces from the long horns of the lobster that you will insert into the cutlets when sending them to table so that they attest to the noble material of which the cutlets are composed
7. Can be enough for five people and is a very delicate dish
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cotolette di Baccalà (Salt Cod Cutlets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt cod, boiled, 500 grams
- anchovies, 2
- parsley, a pinch
- pepper, a few pinches
- Parmesan cheese, grated, a handful
- bread crumb paste (made from bread marrow, water, and butter), 3-4 spoonfuls
- eggs, 2
- breadcrumbs
- lemon, cut into wedges or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the salt cod as in the preceding recipe.
2. Finely chop the anchovies, parsley, and salt cod together with a knife.
3. Add pepper, grated Parmesan, and the bread crumb paste made from bread marrow, water, and butter to make it more tender, along with two eggs.
4. Form the mixture into spoonfuls and dip each into breadcrumbs.
5. Flatten with your hands to give them the shape of cutlets.
6. Dip the cutlets into beaten egg and then coat again in breadcrumbs.
7. Fry in oil and serve with lemon wedges or tomato sauce.
8. Half this quantity will make 9 or 10 cutlets.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cotolette di Carciofi (Artichoke Cutlets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Two large artichokes
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour, 30 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 20 grams
- Milk, 2 deciliters
- Egg yolk
- Egg for coating
- Oil or lard for frying

## Directions

1. Take two large artichokes, clean them of hard leaves and scrape the stem, then boil them, but not too much, and while still boiling cut them lengthwise into five slices each, leaving some stem, and season with salt and pepper.
2. Make a béchamel sauce: melt the butter, add the flour, and gradually add the milk, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and mix in an egg yolk, the Parmesan, and a pinch of salt.
3. Taking them up one by one by the stem, dip the artichoke slices in the béchamel, lay them on a tray and cover them with the remaining béchamel using a spoon. After several hours, when they are completely cold, coat them with a beaten egg, bread them, and fry them in oil or lard.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cotolette di Vitella di Latte coi Tartufi alla Bolognese (Milk-Fed Veal Cutlets with Truffles Bolognese Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlet from the sotto-noce or lean parts of the thigh
- Lemon juice
- Pepper
- Salt
- Parmesan cheese, grated
- Egg, beaten
- Breadcrumbs, fine
- Butter
- Truffles, sliced thin
- Parmesan or gruyere cheese, sliced thin
- Broth or meat juice

## Directions

1. Cut the meat thin and the size of a palm of your hand. Beat them and give them a smooth and elegant shape, like a heart for example, wide at the top and narrowing at the bottom.
2. Prepare them on a plate with lemon juice, pepper, salt and very little grated parmesan.
3. Leave them in this infusion for an hour or two.
4. Pass them in beaten egg and keep them in it for the same amount of time.
5. Coat them with fine breadcrumbs and fry them with butter in a copper pan.
6. When they are just browned on one side, turn them and on the cooked side spread first slices of truffles and over these slices of parmesan or gruyere, cutting both as thin as possible.
7. Finish cooking them with heat under and over, adding broth or meat juice.
8. Remove them and arrange them on a platter with their juice around, squeezing the juice of a lemon over them, or half a lemon if there are few.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cotolette di Vitella di Latte in Salsa d'Uovo (Milk-Fed Veal Cutlets in Egg Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Butter
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Brown and cook the cutlets sauté style as in recipes 312 and 313.
2. Spread over them a sauce of egg yolks, butter and lemon juice.
3. Keep them a little longer on the fire and serve.
4. For seven or eight cutlets, three egg yolks, 30 grams of butter and half a lemon, beaten together in a small pan before pouring, will suffice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cotolette Imbottite (Stuffed Cutlets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 170 grams veal or chicken or turkey breast, trimmed of bone
- 70 grams flour
- 20 grams butter
- 2 decilitres milk
- salt
- 1 tablespoon parmesan cheese
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 egg, beaten
- breadcrumbs
- oil or lard for frying
- lemon wedges

## Directions

1. Lightly fry the thin cutlets in butter, salt them, and set aside.
2. Make a béchamel sauce with the flour, butter, and milk. Once removed from heat, salt it, add the parmesan cheese and egg yolk, mixing well.
3. Once the béchamel has cooled, spread it on both sides of the cutlets to the thickness of a coin, evening it out with a knife blade dipped in oil.
4. Dip the cutlets in beaten egg, coat with breadcrumbs, and fry them in oil or lard until golden.
5. Serve with lemon wedges.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crema (Custard)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 200 grams
- Egg yolks, 8
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. First work the egg yolks with the sugar and then pour in the milk little by little
2. To speed up the cooking you can put the mixture on high heat, but as soon as you see it smoke reduce the heat so it does not curdle
3. If this happens, pass the cream through a sieve
4. Cooking is complete when the cream sticks to the spoon, which should be moved continuously around
5. Add the flavoring just before removing it from the fire
6. This cream, without flour or starch, in the proportions given, is excellent for cream ice cream. It can also be used for a liquid English trifle by adding, when cooled, slices of sponge cake or ladyfingers lightly moistened with rosolio; but if you wish to make it even more pleasing, add very thinly cut pieces of candied fruit
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crema alla Francese (French Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, half a liter
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Eggs, one whole egg and 4 egg yolks
- Isinglass, 2 sheets
- Vanilla or lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar well together with the eggs, add the milk little by little and lastly the isinglass in its natural form.
2. Place the saucepan on the fire, stirring the spoon continuously always in one direction and when the cream begins to condense, sticking to the spoon, remove it.
3. Take a smooth mold with a hole in the middle, such that the quantity of cream can fill it, grease it with butter or with liqueur, so that a thin layer remains at the bottom and pour it into the same.
4. In summer place the mold in ice and in winter in fresh water and unmold it onto a folded towel on the platter.
5. If you do not trust the milk too much, give it a boil of at least a quarter of an hour before making the cream, or increase one sheet of isinglass.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crema Fritta I (Fried Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cornstarch, 100 grams
- sugar, 30 grams
- butter, 20 grams
- milk, 4 deciliters
- two whole eggs
- lemon zest
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Work the eggs with the sugar, then add the cornstarch reduced to powder, the grated lemon zest, the milk poured little by little and the butter.
2. Put the mixture on the fire stirring continuously as you would for a common cream and when it is condensed so that it no longer grows, add the pinch of salt and pour it onto a tray or board, spreading it to the thickness of a finger.
3. Cut it into almond shapes when it is well cold, coat it with egg and breadcrumbs, fry it in lard or oil and serve it hot as a side dish to other fried foods.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crema Fritta II (Fried Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour, 100 grams
- sugar, 20 grams
- two whole eggs
- milk, 5 deciliters
- vanilla or lemon zest

## Directions

1. Keep it on the fire until the flour has lost its raw taste.
2. For the rest, proceed as with the previous recipe number.
3. Half the amount, mixed with other fried foods, will be enough for four or five people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crema Montata (Whipped Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks, 6
- Powdered sugar, 70 grams
- Isinglass, 15 grams, equal to 6 or 7 sheets
- Water, three quarters of a tablespoon glass
- Flavoring, three whole cherry laurel leaves or other as you prefer

## Directions

1. Beat in a saucepan the egg yolks and the sugar, add the water and the said leaves and place it on the fire stirring the spoon, until it is cooked, which thing, as I have already told you, is known by its condensing and sticking to the spoon.
2. Then pour it into a basin and thus hot beat it firmly with a whisk until it has mounted; remove the leaves and continuing always to beat it, add, when it is mounted, the isinglass little by little.
3. Take a decorated mold, grease it with oil, surround it with ice and pour the whipped cream into it, in the middle of which, if you wish, you can put ladyfingers dipped in liqueur or smeared with fruit preserve.
4. Leave it in the ice for more than an hour, and if it does not want to unmold naturally, pass a cloth soaked in hot water around the mold.
5. The isinglass is prepared beforehand thus: it is first put in soak, then on the fire with two fingers of a glass of water, it is boiled until the water, evaporating in part, forms a somewhat dense liquid, which sticks between the fingers, and thus boiling is poured into the cream to which you can give the taste of alkermes, coffee or chocolate.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crescente (Bolognese Fried Flatbread)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- water
- salt
- lard

## Directions

1. Mix flour with cold water and salt, adding a little lard
2. Work and knead the dough
3. Heat lard in a pan until it boils and remove from fire
4. Drop the dough into the hot lard to fry
5. The flatbread will puff up better this way
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crescioni (Spinach Half-Moons)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- oil
- garlic
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- sapa (grape must reduction)
- dried grapes
- or sugar and raisins
- pasta matta dough (recipe N. 153)
- oil for frying

## Directions

1. Boil spinach without water according to common use
2. Squeeze it well and chop it roughly
3. Stew it with a soffritto of oil, garlic, parsley, salt and pepper
4. Season with a little sapa and dried grapes from which the seeds have been removed (or supply with sugar and raisins if sapa and dried grapes are lacking)
5. Wrap these seasoned spinach in pasta matta dough (recipe N. 153) mixed with a few drops of oil, rolled out thin
6. Cut with a disc about the size shown on the preceding page
7. Fold the discs in half to make a half-moon shape and pinch the fold tightly
8. Fry in oil
9. Serve as a course between meals
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cresentine (Garlic-Rubbed Toasted Bread)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread slices
- Garlic clove
- Salt
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Toast bread slices on both sides.
2. While hot, rub them with a garlic clove.
3. Season with salt, oil, vinegar, and sugar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Croccante (Almond Brittle)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet almonds, 120 grams
- powdered sugar, 100 grams
- butter or oil for the mold
- ash for the coals

## Directions

1. Shell the almonds and separate the lobes, which are the two parts naturally joined together
2. Cut each lobe into thin strips, either lengthwise or crosswise as you prefer
3. Heat the cut almonds over the fire and dry them until they take on a yellowish color, without roasting them
4. Meanwhile, place the sugar in a pot, preferably not tinned, and heat until it is perfectly liquefied
5. When the sugar is liquid, pour the well-heated almonds into it and stir
6. To prevent the brittle from becoming bitter from overcooking, throw a shovel of ash onto the coals
7. The exact point of doneness is known by the cinnamon color the brittle acquires
8. Then pour it gradually into a mold previously greased with butter or oil, pressing it with a lemon against the walls and spreading it as thin as you can
9. Unmold while cold; if this proves difficult, immerse the mold in boiling water
10. Alternatively, you may dry the almonds in the sun, grind them finely with a crescent-shaped tool, and add a piece of butter when they are in the sugar
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Croccante a Bagno-Maria (Almond Brittle Custard)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 150 grams
- Sweet almonds, 85 grams
- Egg yolks, 5
- Milk, 4 deciliters

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds and with a small curved knife reduce them to the size of grains of wheat approximately.
2. Put 110 grams of the said sugar on the fire and when it is completely melted, pour in the almonds and stir them continuously with a spoon until they have taken on a cinnamon color.
3. Throw them then into a pan greased with butter and, when they are cold, pound them in a mortar with the remaining 40 grams of sugar and reduce them very fine.
4. Add the egg yolks and then the milk, mix well and pour the mixture into a mold with a hole in the middle, which you will have first greased with butter.
5. Cook it in a water bath and after, if in summer, keep the mold in ice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crocchette di Animelle (Croquettes of Sweetbreads)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads, 150 grams
- onion and butter or gravy
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- béchamel, 2 tablespoons
- egg yolk, 1
- parmesan cheese, a good pinch
- breadcrumbs
- egg, 1 beaten
- optional: fat and lean ham, salted tongue, truffles

## Directions

1. Take 150 grams of sweetbreads and cook them in gravy or with a small mixture of onion and butter
2. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg
3. Cut them into small dice and mix with two tablespoons of rather firm béchamel, adding one egg yolk and a good pinch of parmesan
4. Take the mixture up with small spoons and pour it into breadcrumbs, shaping it into an oblong form like a spool
5. Dip them in beaten egg, then coat again in breadcrumbs and fry them
6. You can make them more tasty if you add fat and lean ham, salted tongue cut into small dice to the mixture, and if instead of nutmeg you flavor them with small pieces of truffles
7. With the stated quantity of sweetbreads you will get ten or twelve croquettes which you can add to other types of fried foods to make a mixed fry dish
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crocchette di Patate (Potato Croquettes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, 300 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 20 grams
- Egg yolks, 2
- Sugar, 1 teaspoon
- Nutmeg flavoring

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and if they are large cut them into four parts and put them to boil in salted water.
2. Pass them through a sieve while still hot and dry.
3. To the puree add the butter, melted if it is winter, and all the rest, mixing well.
4. Let the mixture cool well, divide it into ten or twelve parts and, over a light layer of flour, give them the shape of spools to obtain the croquettes.
5. Beat an egg and dip them one by one into it, coat them with breadcrumbs and fry them in oil or lard to serve as a side dish to fried meat or a roast.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crocchette di Patate (Potato Croquettes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes, 300 grams
- butter, 30 grams
- grated parmesan cheese, 20 grams
- egg yolks, 2
- sugar, one teaspoon
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and if they are large cut them into four pieces and put them to boil in salted water
2. Pass them through a sieve dry when they are cooked and still boiling
3. To the puree add the butter, melted in winter, and all the rest, mixing
4. Let the mixture cool well and divide it into ten or twelve parts
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crocchette di Riso Composte (Composed Rice Croquettes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- rice croquette mixture (recipe from preceding recipe)
- chicken giblets cooked in butter and gravy
- or onion mixture if gravy is lacking

## Directions

1. Use the recipe for simple rice croquettes and mix into the rice when cooked and measured the giblets of a chicken cooked down with butter and gravy (or supply with an onion mixture if the gravy is lacking)
2. Cut the giblets after cooking to the size of a chickpea
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crocchette di Riso Semplici (Simple Rice Croquettes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- milk, half a liter
- rice, 100 grams
- butter, 20 grams
- grated parmesan cheese, 20 grams
- eggs, 2

## Directions

1. Cook the rice very firm in milk, and at half cooking add the butter and salt it
2. Remove from the fire and pour in the parmesan
3. While still boiling, break in one egg stirring immediately to incorporate it
4. When well cooled, take it up in spoonfuls and roll it in breadcrumbs giving it a cylindrical form; with this amount you will get twelve croquettes
5. Beat the remaining egg, drop the croquettes into it one by one, roll them again in breadcrumbs and fry them
6. They can be served alone but better accompanied with other types of fried foods
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostate (Fruit Tarts)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- shortcrust pastry (full dose from recipe B No. 589, or half dose from recipe A)
- eggs, 1 whole egg and 1 yolk
- lemon zest or orange flower water for flavoring
- fruit preserves or cream
- butter for greasing the pan

## Directions

1. Take the full dose of the pastry recipe B No. 589, or half of recipe A
2. Beat one whole egg and one yolk together separately; reserve some egg for glazing the surface of the tart
3. Add flavoring to the shortcrust pastry, such as lemon zest or orange flower water; recipe C is best
4. Roll out half the pastry with a smooth rolling pin to form a round disk about the thickness of a shield and place it in a buttered pan
5. Spread the fruit preserves or cream on top, keeping them separate if using both
6. If the preserves are too firm, soften them over heat with a few spoonfuls of water
7. Cut the remaining pastry with a ridged rolling pin into thin strips about one finger-width wide
8. Arrange these strips in a grid pattern over the preserves
9. Cover the ends of the strips with a ring of pastry made from the remaining dough, moistened with water to attach it well
10. Brush the surface of the shortcrust pastry with the reserved egg
11. Bake the tart in an oven or outdoor oven
12. It improves after one or two days
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di acciughe (Anchovy Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Anchovies
- Butter

## Directions

1. Wash the anchovies and remove their spine and small bones
2. Mince them with a crescent-shaped chopper, add butter in proportion, and crush the mixture with the blade of a dinner knife to reduce it to a homogeneous paste
3. Spread on bread slices like an ointment
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di beccaccia (Woodcock Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcocks
- Woodcock innards, gizzards included but not emptied
- Parsley leaves
- Anchovy fillets, 2 per 3 woodcock innards
- Butter
- Pepper
- Meat juice
- Bread slices, elegant form, lightly toasted
- Woodcock for roasting
- Sage sprigs
- Thin slice of lard

## Directions

1. Clean the woodcocks and remove their innards, throwing away only the end of the intestine that borders the anus
2. Combine with these the gizzards without emptying them; some parsley leaves and the flesh of two anchovies for every three innards
3. No salt is needed
4. Mince everything very finely with a crescent-shaped chopper, then put it on the fire with a piece of butter and a pinch of pepper, moistening it with meat juice
5. Spread this mixture on elegant slices of bread, lightly toasted
6. Send these crostini to the table accompanied by the woodcocks that you will have cooked roasted with some small sage sprigs and wrapped with a thin slice of lard
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di capperi (Capers Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Capers in vinegar, 50 grams
- Powdered sugar, 50 grams
- Raisins, 30 grams
- Pine nuts, 20 grams
- Candied fruit, 20 grams
- Flour, 1 heaping teaspoon
- Sugar, 2 teaspoons
- Water, half a glass
- Vinegar, a small amount
- Bread slices, fried in good oil or lightly toasted

## Directions

1. Chop the capers coarsely
2. Clean the raisins of stems and wash them well
3. Cut the pine nuts into three parts lengthwise
4. Shape the prosciutto into very small dice and reduce the candied fruit into small pieces
5. Put a heaping teaspoon of flour and two teaspoons of sugar in a small saucepan over the fire; when this mixture has taken on a brown color, pour in half a glass of water mixed with a small amount of vinegar
6. When it has boiled until the lumps dissolve, throw all the ingredients into the saucepan at once and let them boil for ten minutes, tasting them meanwhile to feel if the sweet and strong flavor is right
7. When the mixture is still hot, spread it over thin slices of bread fried in good oil or simply lightly toasted
8. Serve these crostini cold, or in the middle of the meal to excite the appetite of your guests
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di caviale (Caviar Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Caviar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Mix equal amounts of caviar and butter together
2. If the caviar is hard, work them a little on the fire with a spoon at moderate heat
3. Spread on bread slices like an ointment
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di caviale con olio (Caviar Crostini with Oil)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Caviar
- Oil
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. If you wish to use oil instead of butter, add a few drops of lemon juice and mix all three ingredients well together
2. Spread on bread slices like an ointment
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di caviale, acciughe e burro (Caviar, Anchovy, and Butter Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 60 grams
- Caviar, 40 grams
- Anchovies, 20 grams

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of everything and work it to make it fine and smooth
2. Spread on bread slices like an ointment
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di fegatini di pollo (Chicken Liver Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken livers
- One shallot or one small white onion bulb
- A piece of prosciutto fat
- Several parsley leaves
- Celery
- Carrot
- Oil
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Dried mushrooms, 2-3 pieces, softened
- Broth
- Fine breadcrumbs
- Lemon juice
- Bread slices

## Directions

1. Remove the bile sac from the chicken livers carefully without breaking it, doing this best while working in a bowl of water
2. Place the livers on the fire together with a mixture (battutino) composed of one shallot or, if unavailable, one small white onion bulb, a piece of prosciutto fat, several parsley leaves, celery and carrot, a little oil and butter, salt and pepper; but everything in small quantity so as not to make the mixture piquant or nauseating
3. At half cooking, remove the livers and dry them; with two or three pieces of softened dried mushrooms, mince them finely with a crescent-shaped chopper
4. Return them to the fire in the sauce that remained from the half-cooking and with a little broth finish cooking them
5. Before serving, bind them with a pinch of fine breadcrumbs and add a bit of lemon juice
6. The crostini should be tender, so make the mixture somewhat liquid, or dip the bread slices briefly, barely, in the broth
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di fegatini di pollo con la salvia (Chicken Liver Crostini with Sage)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Very little onion
- Prosciutto, both fat and lean
- Butter
- Chicken livers, 3
- Sage leaves, 4-5 for 3 livers
- Salt and pepper
- Flour, 1 teaspoon
- Broth
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 3-4 teaspoons
- Bread, stale crumb, slices about 1 centimeter thick

## Directions

1. Make a mixture (battutino) with very little onion and both fat and lean prosciutto
2. Put it on the fire with a piece of butter and when it is well browned, throw in the finely minced chicken livers together with sage leaves (4 or 5 for 3 livers should be enough)
3. Season with salt and pepper and, once they have released their moisture, add another piece of butter and bind them with a teaspoon of flour
4. Wet them with broth to cook them
5. Before removing from the fire, pour in 3 or 4 teaspoons of grated Parmesan and taste to see if the seasoning is right
6. Form the crostini from the crumb of stale bread, just under 1 centimeter thick, and spread them generously on one side only with the mixture when it is no longer boiling
7. After several hours, when you are about to serve them, either alone or as a side to roasted meat, beat an egg mixed with a drop of water and, taking the crostini one by one, let them touch the flour from the side of the mixture only, then immerse them in the egg and throw them into the pan on the mixture side
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di fegatini e acciughe (Chicken Liver and Anchovy Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken livers, 2 pieces
- Anchovies, 1 piece
- Butter
- Broth
- Pepper
- Fresh marrow bread slices

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken livers in butter and when they have taken color, moisten them with broth; add a pinch of pepper, but no salt.
2. When cooked, mince them finely together with the washed and cleaned anchovy; then put the mixture back in the pan where the chicken livers were cooked, add a bit more butter, heat the mixture on the fire without letting it boil, and spread it on slices of fresh marrow bread.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di milza (Spleen Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton spleen, 120 grams
- Anchovies, 2 pieces
- Butter
- Meat juice or broth
- Small diced onion
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Bread crumbs, 1 teaspoon
- Bread slices
- Butter for brushing

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the spleen and cook it with butter and meat juice. In the absence of this, use a small mixture of a little onion, oil, butter, salt, pepper and spices for seasoning.
2. Then mince these ingredients together with the anchovies very finely, put them back on the fire in the sauce that remains, adding a teaspoon of bread crumbs to bind them together, and without letting them boil further, spread them on slices of bread that you have first dried by the fire without roasting and brushed with butter.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di tartufi (Truffle Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread sticks or bread slices
- Butter
- Truffles, prepared as described in recipe 269
- Sauce from truffle preparation

## Directions

1. Take bread sticks and cut them into diagonal slices, or prepare bread slices in an elegant form
2. Lightly toast them and brush them with butter while hot
3. Spread the truffles prepared according to recipe 269 over them
4. Drizzle with the sauce that remains
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini di Tre Colori (Three-Color Croutons)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- two chifels (elongated loaves)
- spinach, cooked with juice or butter and parmesan
- two hard-boiled eggs
- butter or oil

## Directions

1. Cut the two chifels into thick slices one centimeter wide and fry them in butter or oil.
2. Take spinach cooked with juice or butter and parmesan, finely chopped, and cover the slices of chifel with a layer of the same.
3. Take two hard-boiled eggs, shell them, cut them in half crosswise and set aside the yolks.
4. From the white cut many concentric circles and place them over the layer of spinach.
5. From the yolk make many small pieces or small dice and place them in the middle of the circles of white.
6. In this way you will form croutons that can surround a roast dish which, having as a base fried bread covered by the green of the spinach, with the white and the yellow-red of the eggs will appear in three colors.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Crostini fioriti (Flowered Crostini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine marrow bread, 1 centimeter thick
- Fresh butter
- Parsley leaves
- Anchovy fillets

## Directions

1. Cut slices of the finest marrow bread to the thickness of one centimeter, giving it the shape of almonds or small squares.
2. Spread them with fresh butter and lay two or three leaves of parsley on top, surrounding them with anchovy fillets in the form of little snakes.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Cuscussù (Couscous)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 750 grams veal breast
- 150 grams lean veal without bone
- 300 grams coarse semolina
- One chicken liver
- One hard-boiled egg
- One egg yolk
- Vegetables of various kinds such as onion, savoy cabbage, celery, carrot, spinach, beet or other
- Salt and pepper
- Water
- Oil
- Bread marrow
- Meat broth or tomato sauce or tomato conserve

## Directions

1. Put the semolina in a flat wide earthenware vessel or in a tinned copper pan, season it with a pinch of salt and a dash of pepper and, pouring over it drop by drop two fingers' worth (of a glass) scant of water, knead it with the palm of your hand to make it swell, become granular and loose.
2. When the water is finished, pour over it, little by little, a spoonful of oil and continue manipulating it in the same manner, lasting between the first and second operation more than half an hour.
3. Having conditioned the semolina in this way, put it in a soup bowl and cover it with a cloth, the excess of which, passing it underneath, you will tie tightly with twine.
4. Put the veal breast on the fire with three liters of water to make broth and after skimming the pot, cover its mouth with the bowl already prepared, so that the broth remains at some distance; but make sure that the mouths of the two vessels fit together and do not let smoke escape.
5. Leave the semolina thus for an hour and a quarter so it has time to cook by steam, open the wrapping halfway through cooking to mix it and then put it back as before.
6. Chop with a knife the 150 grams of lean meat, add to it a piece of bread marrow crumbled, season it with salt and pepper, make many small meatballs slightly larger than a hazelnut and fry them in oil.
7. Chop the vegetables somewhat and put first the onion to soften in oil and when it has taken color throw down the others, season them with salt and pepper, stir often and let them draw out the water they release.
8. Reduced almost to dryness, moisten them with meat juice, or with broth and tomato juice or conserve, to bring them to cooking together with the chicken liver cut into pieces and with the meatballs.
9. Remove the semolina from the wrapping, put it on the fire in a saucepan and without letting it boil dissolve into it the egg yolk, pour into it part of the said sauce, mix and pour it into a serving dish, but almost dry so it presents fullness, which you will decorate with the hard-boiled egg cut into small wedges.
10. Mix the rest of the sauce into the broth in the pot and send this broth to table divided into as many cups as there are diners, accompanied, of course, by the serving dish of semolina; thus each one draws down into his plate a portion of semolina and drinks after it the broth by spoonfuls.
11. The veal breast is served afterward as boiled meat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce alla Napoletana (Neapolitan Sweet)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchero, 120 grammi
- Farina d'Ungheria, 120 grammi
- Mandorle dolci, 100 grammi
- Uova, 4
- Rossi d'uovo, 2
- Latte, 3 decilitri
- Zucchero per la crema, 60 grammi
- Farina per la crema, 15 grammi
- Burro per la crema, 10 grammi
- Odore di vainiglia
- Zucchero per la glassa, 230 grammi
- Acqua, 1 decilitro
- Limone, 1/4

## Directions

1. Le mandorle sbucciatele, asciugatele al sole o al fuoco e, scegliendone un terzo delle più grosse, dividete queste in due parti nei due lobi naturali; le altre tagliatele in filetti sottili
2. Montate le uova e lo zucchero in una bacinella di rame o di ottone, sul fuoco, alla temperatura di 20 gradi, battendole con la frusta più di un quarto d'ora
3. Ritirato il composto dal fuoco uniteci la farina mescolando leggermente e versatelo in uno stampo liscio, tondo od ovale, che avrete prima imburrato e spolverizzato con un cucchiaino di zucchero a velo ed uno di farina uniti insieme
4. Cuocetelo al forno a moderato calore
5. Dopo corto e ben diaccio tagliatelo all'ingiro a fette sottili un centimetro
6. Fate una crema: mettete al fuoco il burro con la farina per cuocerla senza farle prender colore; poi, resa tiepida, vi aggiungerete i rossi, il latte e lo zucchero rimettendola al fuoco
7. Con questa crema a bollore spalmate da una sola parte le fette del dolce e ricomponetelo, cioè collocatele insieme una sopra l'altra
8. Mettete a bollire in una piccola cazzaruola grammi 230 di zucchero in un decilitro di acqua fino al punto che, preso il liquido fra le dita, appiccichi un poco, ma senza filo
9. Quando comincia a diacciare spremetegli un quarto di limone e lavoratelo molto col mestolo per ridurlo bianco come la neve
10. Se v'indurisse fra mano versateci un poco d'acqua per ridurlo scorrevole come una crema alquanto densa
11. Buttateci dentro le mandorle a filetti, mescolate e intonacate il dolce, e colle altre divise in due parti rifioritelo al disopra infilandole ritte
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce alle Chiare d'Uovo (Egg White Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg whites, 8 or 9
- Hungarian flour, 300 grams
- Powdered sugar, 150 grams
- Butter, 150 grams
- Sultana raisins, 100 grams
- Cream of tartar, 10 grams
- Baking soda, 5 grams
- Vanilla sugar flavoring

## Directions

1. Whip the egg whites and pour into them the flour and sugar
2. Mix and then add the melted butter
3. When the mixture is fully combined, add the powders and lastly the raisins
4. Pour the mixture into a baking pan greased with butter and dusted with powdered sugar and flour, where the cake reaches at least two fingers high
5. Bake it in the oven or in a country oven and serve it cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce di Chiare d'Uovo (Egg White Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg whites, 8 or 9
- Hungarian flour, 300 grams
- Powdered sugar, 150 grams
- Butter, 150 grams
- Sultana raisins, 100 grams
- Cream of tartar, 10 grams
- Baking soda, 5 grams
- Vanilla sugar flavoring

## Directions

1. Whip the egg whites and pour into them the flour and sugar; mix and then add the melted butter.
2. When the mixture is all combined add the powders and lastly the raisins.
3. Pour the mixture into a buttering pan buttered and sprinkled with powdered sugar and flour, where the cake results at least two fingers high, bake it in an oven or country oven and serve it cooled.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce di Ciliege (Cherry Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Black cherries, raw, whole and stemless, 200 grams
- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Rye breadcrumbs, 50 grams
- Sweet almonds, 40 grams
- Eggs, 4
- Rosolio, 2 spoonfuls
- Vanilla flavoring or lemon zest
- Butter, cold

## Directions

1. If you lack rye bread, use common bread.
2. Blanch the almonds, dry them and chop them finely to reduce them to about half the size of a grain of rice.
3. Work the egg yolks first with the sugar until they become foamy, add the breadcrumbs, the rosolio, the flavoring and continue to work the mixture a little more.
4. Add the well-beaten egg whites, mixing gently and pour it into a smooth mold that you have first well greased with cold butter and sprinkled throughout, and more at the bottom, with the said almonds.
5. Finally throw in the cherries, but to prevent them from sinking to the bottom due to their weight, mix among the mixture the remaining almonds.
6. Bake it in the oven or in a country oven and serve it hot or cold to four or five people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce di Cioccolata (Chocolate Sweet)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sponge cake, 100 grams
- Chocolate, 100 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Sugar, 30 grams
- Rosolio liqueur, as needed

## Directions

1. Cut the sponge cake into thin slices
2. Grate the chocolate
3. Melt the butter in a bain-marie and in it pour the sugar and chocolate, working the mixture with a ladle until you have reduced it very fine
4. With this and the sponge cake dipped in rosolio fill in layers a mold that you will have first moistened with the same rosolio to be able to better unmold the sweet
5. In summer keep the mold in ice so that the mixture sets
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce di Mandorle (Almond Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, 3 numbers
- Sugar, equal weight to the eggs
- Potato flour, 125 grams
- Butter, 125 grams
- Sweet almonds with three bitter ones, 125 grams
- Grated lemon zest for flavoring

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, dry them in the sun or by the fire and pound them very finely in a mortar with a third of the said sugar
2. Work with a wooden spoon the three egg yolks with the remaining sugar and the lemon zest until they become whitish
3. Add the potato flour, then the pounded almonds and the melted butter, working the mixture further
4. Lastly add the beaten egg whites and when everything is amalgamated together, bake it in the oven
5. Dust it with powdered sugar while still hot
6. Use a baking pan with a bottom diameter of approximately 22 centimeters for proper height
7. You can use the same butter to grease the pan, which, as you know, should be dusted with powdered sugar mixed with flour
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce di Marroni con Panna Montata (Sweet of Chestnuts with Whipped Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Healthy large chestnuts about thirty, 500 grams
- Powdered sugar, 130 grams
- Chocolate, 60 grams
- Citron rosolio, 3 tablespoons

## Directions

1. Cook the chestnuts in water as you would for roasted chestnuts, blanch them and pass them while hot
2. Reduce the chocolate to powder and then with all the ingredients form a paste
3. Take a large, round and presentable plate, place a small coffee cup inverted in the center and with a horsehair sieve on top pass all the mixture while rotating the plate so that it is distributed equally
4. Once the operation is complete, carefully remove and clean the coffee cup and the empty space left in the middle by filling it with 300 grams of whipped cream
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce Firenze (Florence Sweet)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 100 grams
- Fine bread, 60 grams
- Sultana raisins, 40 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Butter, as needed
- Milk, half a liter
- Lemon zest for flavoring

## Directions

1. Slice the bread into thin slices and toast them lightly
2. Butter the hot slices on both sides
3. Place the buttered bread slices in a concave and presentable dish that can be brought to the table
4. Scatter the raisins and grated lemon zest over the bread slices
5. Beat the eggs well in a pot with the sugar, then add the milk and pour this mixture into the dish over the ingredients without touching them
6. To cook it, place the dish over a low fire on the hearth, cover it with a countryside oven lid with fire on top, and serve it hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce Roma (Roman Sweet)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples of fine quality, not too ripe and medium-sized, 600 grams (5 or 6 apples)
- White alcoholic wine, 2 deciliters
- Sugar, 130 grams
- Milk, 4 deciliters
- Egg yolks, 3
- Sugar, 70 grams
- Flour, 20 grams
- Vanilla sugar flavoring
- Egg whites, 3
- Powdered sugar, 20 grams

## Directions

1. Choose fine apples that are not too ripe and of medium size, weighing about 600 grams.
2. Remove the cores with a tin tube and peel them.
3. Place them to cook with 2 deciliters of white alcoholic wine and 130 grams of sugar, taking care that they do not break while boiling, turning them, and that they do not overcook.
4. Remove them dried and place them with the hole vertical in a decent vessel that can be brought to the table and that withstands fire.
5. Prepare a cream with 4 deciliters of milk, 3 egg yolks, 70 grams of sugar, 20 grams of flour and vanilla sugar flavoring, then pour it over the apples.
6. Whip the three remaining egg whites, and when they are well stiff add 20 grams of powdered sugar and cover the cream with them.
7. Place the sweet in a country oven or only on the hearth with just its cover, with fire above and little below to brown the surface.
8. Before sending it to the table, brush it with the reduced syrup remaining from the cooking of the apples.
9. This will suffice for seven or eight people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce Tedesco (German Sweet)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Farina d'Ungheria, 250 grammi
- Burro, 100 grammi
- Zucchero in polvere, 100 grammi
- Uova, 4
- Latte, 4 cucchiaiate
- Odore di zucchero vanigliato
- Bicarbonato di soda, 5 grammi
- Cremor di tartaro, 5 grammi

## Directions

1. Lavorate dapprima ed insieme per mezz'ora il burro, lo zucchero e i rossi d'uovo
2. Aggiungete la farina e il latte e lavorate ancora bene il composto
3. Mescolate nel composto insieme con le chiare montate il bicarbonato di soda e il cremor di tartaro
4. Versate il dolce in uno stampo liscio, imburrato soltanto
5. Cuocete in forno
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Dolce Torino (Turin Sweet)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Savoiardi, 100 grams
- Chocolate, 100 grams
- Fresh butter, 100 grams
- Powdered sugar, 70 grams
- One egg yolk
