- Milk, 2 tablespoonfuls
- Vanilla sugar flavoring
- Rosolio or alkermes liqueur

## Directions

1. Cut the savoiardi in half lengthwise and soak them with rosolio, or better, half with rosolio and half with alkermes, so they can be alternated to make a better appearance.
2. First work the butter with the sugar and egg yolk.
3. Place the chocolate, grated or in pieces, on the fire with the milk, and when it is well dissolved pour it hot into the worked butter, add the flavoring and form thus a paste by mixing it well.
4. Arrange on the platter a first layer of the said savoiardi and spread them lightly with the said paste; then layer another layer of savoiardi, then a third layer still, always spreading them lightly.
5. Pour all the remaining paste over and at the sides, evening it out as well as you can.
6. The next day, before serving it, smooth it all over the surface with the blade of a knife heated by fire, and at the same time, if you like, decorate it with a sprinkling of pistachios or slightly toasted hazelnuts, both finely chopped.
7. 40 grams of hazelnuts weighed with shell or 15 grams of pistachios will suffice.
8. This is a portion for six or seven people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Donzelline (Little Maidens)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 100 grams
- Butter, one walnut-sized piece
- Milk, as needed
- Salt, a pinch
- Lard or oil for frying
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make a dough neither too stiff nor too soft, work it much by hand on a work surface and stretch it into a sheet the thickness of a coin. Cut it into small almond shapes, fry it in lard or oil, and you will see it puff, becoming tender and delicate to the taste.
2. Dust the donzelline with powdered sugar when they are no longer hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Donzelline (Little Maidens)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- water
- salt
- lard or oil
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make a dough that is neither too stiff nor too soft
2. Work it very well with your hands on the work surface
3. Draw out a sheet the thickness of a shield
4. Cut it into small almond shapes
5. Fry it in lard or oil, and you will see it puff up, becoming tender and delicate to the taste
6. Dust with powdered sugar when no longer hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Donzelline Aromatiche (Aromatic Fried Pastry Diamonds)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, approximately 180 grams
- Oil, two spoonfuls
- White wine or Marsala, two spoonfuls
- Sage, five or six leaves
- One egg
- Salt, as needed

## Directions

1. Chop the sage with a half-moon knife.
2. Mix the flour with all the ingredients, working it well and ensuring the dough remains rather soft.
3. Stretch it with a rolling pin to the thickness of a coin, dusting with flour if necessary, and cut into almond shapes.
4. Fry in oil or lard. This quantity is sufficient for four people.
5. Some people eat them together with figs and ham.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Donzelline Ripiene di Acciughe Salate (Fried Pastry Parcels Filled with Salted Anchovies)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 220 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Milk, as needed
- Salt, a pinch
- Salted anchovies, 4 pieces

## Directions

1. Mix flour with butter, milk, and salt to form a dough of proper consistency, working it well if you want the pasta to puff in the pan.
2. Let it rest briefly, then cut it in half and stretch both pieces open somewhat.
3. Clean the anchovies, divide them lengthwise in half, remove the bone, and cut them into small square pieces. Place these spread over one of the pasta pieces, cover with the other piece to seal them together.
4. Stretch the united pieces with a rolling pin to a thin sheet and cut into almond shapes.
5. Fry in oil. This amount is sufficient for six people and can serve as a first course at breakfast or as an accompaniment to a fish fry.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Elisir di china (Elixir of Quinine)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Bruised Peruvian quinine bark, 50 grams
- Dried rind of bitter orange bruised, 5 grams
- Alcohol, 700 grams
- Water, 700 grams
- White sugar, 700 grams

## Directions

1. First mix 250 grams of the said alcohol with 150 grams of the said water, and in this mixture place in infusion the quinine and orange rind, keeping it in a tepid place for about ten days, stirring the vessel at least once a day
2. Then strain it through cloth squeezing well so that all the substance comes out, and filter it through paper
3. After this, dissolve the sugar in the fire in the remaining 550 grams of water without letting it boil and pass it through a sieve, or better through cloth, to clean it of any impurity if there were any
4. Add the remaining 450 grams of alcohol, mix everything together and the elixir will be done
5. Before filtering it taste it and if it seems too strong to you add water
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fagiuoli a guisa d'uccellini (Beans Like Little Birds)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- shelled beans
- water
- oil
- several sage leaves
- salt
- pepper
- simple tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the beans first in water and drain them well
2. Put a pan on the heat with oil in proportion and several sage leaves
3. When the oil crackles strongly, throw in the beans and season with salt and pepper
4. Sauté them until they draw in the fat, shaking the pan from time to time to mix them
5. Pour a little simple tomato sauce over them
6. When the sauce has incorporated, remove from heat
7. These beans work very well as a side dish to boiled meat, if you don't want to eat them alone
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fagiuoli sgranati per contorno al lesso (Shelled Beans as a Side to Boiled Meat)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams shelled beans
- 30 grams dried meat
- 2 deciliters water
- 4 tablespoons oil
- a small bundle of 4 or 5 sage leaves
- salt and white pepper

## Directions

1. Put the beans on the heat with all the above ingredients
2. Let them boil slowly and shake them often
3. Remove the sage and dried meat
4. Serve
5. This side dish is enough for four people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fagiuolini colla balsamella (Green Beans with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- soda
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- béchamel sauce made with cream, butter and flour
- bread fried and cut into almond shapes

## Directions

1. Boil the green beans with soda so they remain a nice green color
2. Sauté them lightly in butter so they do not lose their color
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Pour a flowing béchamel sauce over them, not too abundant, made with cream, butter and flour
5. Serve with a border of fried bread cut into almond shapes
6. They can be served as an intermediate course in a meal
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fagiuolini con l'odore di vaniglia (Green Beans with Vanilla Scent)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- oil
- 1 shallot
- parsley
- carrot
- celery
- salt
- pepper
- onion (if substituting shallot)
- broth
- tomato sauce
- 2 teaspoons vanilla sugar
- nepitella (if substituting vanilla)

## Directions

1. Soak the green beans in fresh water
2. If they are tender, put them whole and raw into a stew without draining them too much
3. Make a soffritto with oil, shallot, parsley, carrot and celery, all finely chopped
4. Season with salt and pepper, and when it takes color, dilute with broth or pass it through a strainer, squeezing well
5. To this strained sauce, add tomato sauce
6. Throw in the green beans to cook them
7. Before removing them from heat, season with two teaspoons of vanilla sugar
8. If this flavor is not pleasing, substitute with nepitella
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fagiuolini dall'occhio in erba all'aretina (Fresh-Shelled Eye Beans Aretina Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh-shelled eye beans
- 2 cloves garlic
- raw tomato sauce
- cold water
- oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Trim both ends and cut into three parts
2. Put them in a pot with two whole cloves of garlic, raw tomato sauce, and enough cold water to cover
3. Season with oil, salt and pepper
4. Place over heat and boil slowly until completely cooked
5. Make sure some sauce remains concentrated to make them more pleasant
6. They can serve as an intermediate course or as a side dish to boiled meat
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fagiuolini e Zucchine alla Sautè (Green Beans and Zucchini Sautéed)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans or zucchini
- Butter
- Salt and pepper, in small quantity

## Directions

1. If it is green beans, boil them halfway; if zucchini leave them raw cut into wedges or small pieces.
2. Put them to brown in butter when this, boiling, will have taken a hazelnut color.
3. For seasoning put only salt and pepper in small quantity.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fagiuolini e Zucchine alla Sautè with Sauce (Green Beans and Zucchini Sautéed with Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans or zucchini
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Meat gravy or tomato sauce from recipe 125

## Directions

1. If it is green beans, boil them halfway; if zucchini leave them raw cut into wedges or small pieces.
2. Put them to brown in butter when this, boiling, will have taken a hazelnut color.
3. After cooked in this manner you may add a little meat gravy or a little tomato sauce from recipe 125, which will no longer follow the rules of foreign or modern cuisine; but you will feel, in my opinion, a better taste and the stomach will be more satisfied.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fagiuolini in salsa d'uovo (Green Beans in Egg Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams green beans
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 teaspoon flour
- juice of 1/4 lemon
- 1 ladle of degreased cold broth

## Directions

1. Remove both ends and the string from the green beans
2. Blanch them in salted water until half-cooked
3. Drain and cut into three pieces
4. Sauté with butter, salt and pepper to taste
5. Whisk together the egg yolk, flour, and lemon juice in a pot
6. Dilute the mixture with one ladle of degreased cold broth
7. Place over heat, stirring constantly with a spoon, until it becomes a flowing cream
8. Pour the sauce over the green beans
9. Keep them on the heat a little longer so the sauce incorporates well
10. Serve as a side dish to boiled meat
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Farinata Gialla di Magro (Yellow Cornmeal Polenta Lean Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Four deciliters of white beans
- Water in proportion
- Half a head of white or savoy cabbage, chopped
- Salt, pepper, and thyme leaves
- Oil in good measure
- Two cloves of garlic, whole and peeled
- Tomato juice or conserve dissolved in water
- Cornmeal flour

## Directions

1. Put the beans on the fire with proportioned water.
2. After they are cooked, pass them through a sieve and mix the paste in the broth of the same beans and in the same put to boil, for about two hours, the chopped cabbage which you will season with salt, pepper and thyme leaves.
3. Put a pan on the fire with oil in good measure and two whole peeled cloves of garlic; when these are well browned throw them away and add to the oil tomato juice or conserve dissolved in water and also here another little salt and pepper.
4. When it has boiled somewhat, pour also this seasoning into the pot where the broth and cabbage are.
5. Finally, when this is cooked, pour with one hand, little by little, the cornmeal flour; with the other hand mix well, so that lumps do not form, and when it has reached a certain consistency, that is somewhat liquid, let it boil a little more and serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fave alla romana o dei morti (Roman Beans or Beans of the Dead) - First Recipe
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 200 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Sweet almonds, 100 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Egg, 1
- Lemon peel flavoring, or cinnamon, or orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds and pound them with the sugar to the size of about half a grain of rice.
2. Mix them with the flour together with the other ingredients and form a somewhat soft dough with as much rosoli or brandy as needed.
3. Shape into small pastries, in the form of a large bean, which will yield about 60 or 70 for each recipe.
4. Arrange them on a copper baking sheet first greased with lard or butter and dusted with flour; brush with egg.
5. Bake in the oven or country oven, noting that, being small, they cook quickly.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fave alla romana o dei morti (Roman Beans or Beans of the Dead) - Second Recipe
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet almonds, 200 grams
- Flour, 100 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Egg, 1
- Lemon peel flavoring, or cinnamon, or orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds and pound them with the sugar to the size of about half a grain of rice.
2. Mix them with the flour together with the other ingredients and form a somewhat soft dough with as much rosoli or brandy as needed.
3. Shape into small pastries, in the form of a large bean, which will yield about 60 or 70 for each recipe.
4. Arrange them on a copper baking sheet first greased with lard or butter and dusted with flour; brush with egg.
5. Bake in the oven or country oven, noting that, being small, they cook quickly.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fave Fresche in Stufa (Fresh Fava Beans Braised)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pods of large fava beans
- Young onion
- Oil
- Fat and lean prosciutto
- Pepper
- Salt
- Lettuce
- Broth

## Directions

1. Take pods of large fava beans and shell and peel them.
2. Finely chop a young onion, put it on the fire with oil, and when it begins to brown, throw into the soffritto prosciutto cut into cubes.
3. After a short time add the fava beans, season with pepper and a little salt, and when they have taken on the seasoning, add one or two heads of lettuce cut into large pieces and finish cooking them with broth if needed.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fegatelli in Conserva (Pork Liver Pâtés in Preserves)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- pork liver pâtés
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- lard
- bay leaves or fennel seed (optional)

## Directions

1. Make pork liver pâtés seasoned with oil, pepper and salt, wrapped in netting and cooked on the grill, on a spit or in a pan.
2. After cooking, place them in a pot and fill it with rendered lard at boiling temperature.
3. Remove them as needed for use and reheat them.
4. Some use cooking the pâtés between two bay leaves, or as in Tuscany, add some fennel seed to the seasoning, but these are strong flavors that many stomachs cannot tolerate.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fegato alla Cacciatora (Hunter-Style Liver)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- liver, approximately 300 grams
- three large onions
- lard
- good red wine, less than half a glass
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Slice three large onions and soak them in fresh water for one or two hours.
2. Drain the water and put the onion in a pan to dry it; once dry, add the lard to fry it.
3. When it has taken on a brown color, add the liver cut into thin slices.
4. Let it soften somewhat, mixed with the onion; then pour less than half a glass of good red wine into the pan.
5. After five minutes, stirring constantly, serve it seasoned with salt and pepper.
6. Note that this is not a dish for delicate stomachs.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fegato col Vino Bianco (Liver with White Wine)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- oil
- butter
- flour
- one tablespoon
- excellent and dry white wine
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into thin slices and fry it in a pan with oil and butter as is.
2. Beat a tablespoon of flour with excellent dry white wine in a small pot to form a very liquid mixture.
3. When the liver is two-thirds cooked, pour this mixture over it.
4. Finish cooking and season with salt and pepper.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fegato d'Oca (Goose Liver)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked goose liver
- 20 grams butter
- 1 heaping teaspoon flour
- 1 ladle broth
- 3 tablespoons Marsala wine

## Directions

1. After cooking in the way indicated (in recipe 548), remove it dry and bind it with a mixture made of 20 grams of butter put on the fire with a heaping teaspoon of flour and, when this has taken a hazelnut color, dilute it with a ladle of broth and three tablespoons of Marsala wine, pour in the liver, let it boil again for a bit and serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fegato d'Oca (Goose Liver)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver with attached fat
- salt
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the fat into large pieces and place in a pot over heat
2. Add the heart cut into wedges
3. Add the liver in large slices last
4. Season with salt and pepper only
5. Serve at table, drained of excess fat, with lemon wedges
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fegato di Maiale Fritto (Fried Pork Liver)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork liver
- Pork pancreas (stomachino)
- Lard
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon

## Directions

1. To feel the true taste of pork liver you must fry it natural, in thin slices, in virgin lard and mixed with pancreas in small pieces
2. Remove it from the pan with a little of its fat, season with salt and pepper and while still boiling squeeze a lemon over it, whose acid serves to temper the greasiness
3. The thin slices of liver can also be floured before frying
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fegato di Vitella di Latte alla Militare (Milk-fed Calf Liver Military Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One shallot or young onion
- Oil and butter
- Salt and pepper
- Calf liver, thinly sliced
- Parsley, minced
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mince a shallot or young onion finely and brown it in oil and butter
2. When it has taken on a deep red color, add the liver cut into thin slices
3. At mid-cooking, season with salt, pepper and a pinch of minced parsley
4. Let it boil gently so it remains juicy and serve it with its juice, adding the juice of a lemon when you serve it
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Filetti di Sogliole col Vino (Sole Fillets with Wine)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- sole fish, at least 150 grams each
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- oil
- butter
- dry white wine
- parsley
- parmesan cheese
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take sole at least 150 grams each, remove the head and skin
2. With a sharp knife, separate the flesh from the bones to obtain four long fillets per sole, or eight if the sole are very large
3. Using the back of the knife, beat the fillets lightly and flatten them thin with the blade
4. Leave them in beaten egg seasoned with salt and pepper for several hours
5. Coat in breadcrumbs and fry in oil
6. Pour a little of the remaining oil from the pan and a piece of butter into a pan or dish where the fillets can lie flat
7. Arrange the fillets and season again lightly with salt and pepper
8. When they have browned a little, wet them with dry white wine, let boil for five minutes together with a little chopped parsley
9. Serve with the sauce, sprinkling with a pinch of parmesan
10. Serve with lemon wedges
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Filetto alla parigina (Fillet Parisian Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- round braciole from the best part of beef fillet, about half a finger thick
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- meat gravy or similar sauce
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Have the butcher cut for you round braciole from the best part of beef fillet, about half a finger thick.
2. Sauté them in butter after it has taken color over lively fire; salt and pepper for seasoning.
3. When they have formed a crust on all sides so that they remain juicy and slightly undercooked inside, sprinkle a pinch of chopped parsley over them and remove them immediately.
4. Before taking them to the table, cover them with meat gravy or similar sauce, or, which is simpler, into the juice remaining after cooking throw a pinch of flour and with broth make a mixture and use this instead of the sauce.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Filetto colla marsala (Fillet with Marsala Wine)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet of beef, approximately one kilogram
- onion of medium size
- slices of prosciutto
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- broth or water
- Marsala wine

## Directions

1. Tie the fillet and place it over the fire with a medium-sized onion sliced into thin strips, together with some slices of prosciutto and a piece of butter; season lightly with salt and pepper.
2. When it has been browned on all sides and the onion consumed, sprinkle a pinch of flour over it, let it take color and then moisten it with broth or water.
3. Cook slowly, then strain the sauce, degrease it and with this and three fingers (of a glass) of Marsala return it to the fire to boil again, but slowly; serve it with reduced sauce, but not too thick from too much flour.
4. You can also insert lard into the fillet and cook it with only butter and Marsala.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Finocchi colla balsamella (Fennel with Béchamel)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- finocchi polputi (fleshy fennel)
- burro (butter)
- latte (milk)
- sale (salt)
- parmigiano (Parmesan cheese)
- balsamella (béchamel sauce)

## Directions

1. Take fleshy fennel, clean them from hard leaves, cut them into small pieces, wash them and blanch them in salted water.
2. Put them to fry in butter and, when they have absorbed it, finish cooking them entirely with milk.
3. Taste them to see if they are well salted, then remove them dry and place them on a tray that can withstand fire.
4. Dust them with Parmesan and cover them with béchamel.
5. Brown them with fire from above and serve them with boiled meat or stew.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Finocchi per contorno (Fennel as a Side Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- finocchi (fennel)
- burro (butter)
- brodo (broth)
- farina (flour)
- parmigiano (Parmesan cheese)
- sale (salt)

## Directions

1. After cutting them into pieces and blanching them in salted water, fry them in butter, finish cooking them with broth, bind them with a pinch of flour and when you remove them, season them with a little Parmesan.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Focaccia alla Portoghese (Portuguese-Style Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet almonds, 150 grams
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Potato flour, 50 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Oranges, 1 and 1/2

## Directions

1. First work the egg yolks with the sugar, add the flour, then the almonds peeled and pounded fine with a spoonful of the said sugar.
2. Add the juice passed from the oranges and the superficial skin scraped from only one orange.
3. Last unite to the mixture the whites beaten until stiff, pour it into a paper box greased with butter, about the thickness of a finger and a half and cook it in the oven at very moderate heat.
4. After it is cooked, cover it with a white crust as at No. 789.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Focaccia alla Tedesca (German-style Flatbread)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar, 120 grams
- candied fruit in small pieces, 30 grams
- fine breadcrumbs, 120 grams
- sultana raisins, 30 grams
- eggs, 4
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Work first the egg yolks with the sugar until they have become almost white; add the breadcrumbs, then the candied fruit and raisins, and finally the well-beaten egg whites. Mix gently so as not to deflate them and when the mixture is all combined, pour it into a buttered and floured pan or dusted with breadcrumbs, where it rises about two fingers high and bake it in the oven; this cake will take on the appearance of sponge cake which you will dust, after baking, with powdered sugar.
2. If it is to serve ten or twelve people, double the amount.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Focaccia coi Siccioli (Flatbread with Cracklings)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour, 500 grams
- fine powdered sugar, 200 grams
- butter, 160 grams
- cracklings, 150 grams
- lard, 60 grams
- Marsala or white wine, 4 tablespoons
- eggs, 2 whole and 2 yolks
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Once you have formed the dough, working it little, add the cracklings chopped fine, grease a copper pan with lard and pour the dough in, pressing it with your knuckles so it comes out bumpy; but do not keep it higher than one finger.
2. Before passing it to the oven, make cuts if you want to serve it in pieces when cooked, square cuts with the point of a knife, repeating them halfway through cooking because they easily close, and when it is cooked dust it with powdered sugar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Folaghe in Umido (Braised Coots)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 folaghe (coots)
- 1 large onion
- sedano (celery)
- carota (carrot)
- prezzemolo (parsley)
- 80 grams butter
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- tomato sauce or tomato paste
- water
- 1.5 coot breast
- 40 grams butter
- Parmigiano cheese
- 3 eggs of pappardelle or 500 grams strips
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After plucking the coots and singing them by flame to remove the down, gut them and wash them well.
2. Pierce them lengthwise through the body with a hot spit, then cut them into four parts throwing away the head, feet and wing tips; then keep them soaking in vinegar for an hour and afterward wash them several times in fresh water.
3. Do the same with the gizzards after emptying, washing and cutting them into four pieces.
4. Make a finely chopped mixture with a large onion and all the herbs in proportion, that is celery, carrot and parsley, and put it on the fire with 80 grams of butter, and at the same time the coots and gizzards seasoning them with salt, pepper and spice flavor.
5. When they are dry bathe them with tomato sauce or paste dissolved in abundant water to cook them and so that much sauce remains.
6. Once cooked, strain the sauce and into it add a coot breast and a half finely chopped and another 40 grams of butter, to season with it and with Parmigiano cheese three eggs of pappardelle or 500 grams of strips which, for their particular taste, will be praised.
7. Serve the coots with some of their sauce afterward as a main dish that will not be despised.
8. This entire preparation should be enough for five or six persons.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fricandò (Braised Veal with Vegetables)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of milk-fed veal from the thigh, all in one piece
- Fat and lean prosciutto for larding
- Salt to taste
- One onion
- Two cloves
- One carrot, cut into strips
- Celery
- Parsley
- A piece of butter
- Beef broth

## Directions

1. Lard the veal with prosciutto.
2. Tie the piece and salt it sparingly or not at all because oversalting is the worst defect in food.
3. Stud an onion with two cloves and make a bouquet with carrot cut into strips, celery and parsley.
4. Put everything in a pot with a piece of butter, brown the meat and cook it through with broth.
5. When cooked, discard the onion and bouquet, strain the sauce, remove the fat and reduce it separately until it becomes a jelly that you will add to the fricandò when you serve it at table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fricassea (Veal Fricassee)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast of milk-fed veal, 500 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Flour, 5 grams or one scant tablespoon
- Hot water, not boiling, 2 deciliters
- Two egg yolks
- Half a lemon
- One aromatic bouquet
- Salt and white pepper to taste
- Fresh mushrooms, 100 to 150 grams (if in season), or a pinch of dried mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut the breast into pieces leaving all the bones.
2. Put a pot on the fire with half the butter and when it begins to melt, pour in the flour stirring until it takes on a hazel color.
3. Then begin to pour the water little by little and then the bouquet which you can compose of some strips of onion and carrot, threads of parsley, celery and basil, all tied together, excluding the leaves because these could fall apart and spoil the fricassee, one of whose merits is to have a beautiful straw color.
4. When the water boils throw in the meat and the rest of the butter and season with salt and white pepper, which is the finest pepper.
5. Cover the pot with a sheet of paper held firm by the lid and let it boil slowly.
6. At two-thirds cooking time remove the bouquet and, if it is mushroom season, you can make it more agreeable with 100 or 150 grams of these cut into thin slices; if not, a pinch of dried mushrooms.
7. When ready to serve, remove the pot from the fire and pour in little by little, stirring, the egg yolks whisked with lemon juice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittata a sgonfiotto ossia montata (Soufflé Omelette)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Egg yolks, 3
- Egg whites, 6
- Lemon zest for flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat first for several minutes the yolks with the sugar
2. Then whip the whites until very stiff and unite them with the yolks together, mixing gently
3. Grease a tray that can withstand the fire with cold butter, pour the mixture into it so that it fills it and place it immediately in a hot country oven
4. After five minutes in the oven make some cuts with a knife and dust it with powdered sugar, then finish cooking it, which will require ten or twelve minutes altogether
5. Be careful that it does not burn on the surface and send it immediately to the table
6. To make it puff better, some add a little lemon juice to the mixture
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittata colla Pietra di Vitella di Latte (Omelette with Veal Kidney)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal kidney
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- eggs
- parsley, chopped
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Take a veal kidney, open it lengthwise, and leave all its fat intact
2. Season it with oil, pepper, and salt; cook it on a grill and cut it into thin slices across the width
3. Beat eggs in proportion to the amount of kidney, season these also with salt and pepper, and mix in a pinch of chopped parsley and some Parmesan
4. Throw the kidney into the eggs, mix, and make an omelette cooked in butter, which when the bottom is set, fold in half so it remains tender
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittata di Cavolfiore (Cauliflower Omelette)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams of boiled cauliflower, without leaves and stem
- 60 grams of butter
- 2 heaping tablespoons of grated Parmesan
- 1 tablespoon of oil
- 6 eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the cauliflower finely and put it in a pan with butter and oil, seasoning with salt and pepper
2. Beat the eggs with Parmesan, salt them, and pour them over the cauliflower when it has drawn in the fat
3. Keep the omelette thin so you do not need to flip it; if the pan is small, make two instead
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittata di Cipolle (Onion Omelette)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- large white onions
- lard or oil
- eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice the onions into strips about half a finger wide
2. Place them in fresh water for at least an hour
3. Dry them well in a cloth before putting them in a pan with lard or oil
4. When they begin to take on color, salt them somewhat
5. Salt the eggs before pouring them over the onion, taking care the onion does not turn black from overcooking
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittata di Fagiolini in Erba (Green Bean Omelette)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- butter
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil the green beans in salted water and cut them into two or three parts
2. Sauté them in a pan with butter and oil, seasoning with salt and pepper
3. Beat the eggs with a pinch of Parmesan and a pinch of salt
4. When the green beans appear somewhat shriveled, pour the eggs over them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittata di Spinaci (Spinach Omelette)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams of raw spinach
- 40 grams of butter, divided
- 4 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- Parmesan cheese (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove spinach from water, boil it, and while still dripping, immediately after removing from heat place it in fresh water
2. Squeeze it well and chop it coarsely
3. Throw it in a pan with a piece of butter and season with salt and pepper
4. Stir frequently and when it has drawn out its fat, pour the beaten and lightly salted eggs over it
5. Once browned on one side, flip it with a plate and return it to the pan with another small piece of butter
6. If desired, add a pinch of Parmesan to the eggs
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittata in Riccioli per Contorno (Curled Omelette Strips as Garnish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a bunch of spinach
- 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- butter
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil a bunch of spinach and pass it through a sieve
2. Beat two eggs, season with salt and pepper, and mix in the spinach in such quantity as to make them only green
3. Put a pan on the fire with just a drop of oil to grease it, and when very hot pour in a portion of the egg mixture, turning the pan in every direction so the omelette comes out thin as paper
4. When well set and dry, remove it, flipping if necessary, and repeat or triple the operation with the remaining eggs
5. Roll these two or three omelettes together, cut them into fine strips like noodles, and lightly fry them in butter
6. Season with Parmesan and use these strips as a garnish for fricandeau or similar dishes
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittata in Zoccoli (Folded Egg Omelette with Prosciutto)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of prosciutto, both fatty and lean
- butter
- eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the prosciutto slices into pieces about the size of a 10 centesimo coin
2. Place the prosciutto pieces in a pan with butter
3. When they have browned slightly, pour in the eggs, salted very little
4. When the omelette has begun to set, fold it in half so it takes on the name of fish of eggs more properly
5. Add more butter to finish cooking it
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittelle di Polenta alla Lodigiana (Fried Polenta Cakes, Lodi Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, half a liter
- Yellow flour (cornmeal), 100 grams
- Gruyère cheese
- Eggs, 2
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard or oil for frying

## Directions

1. Make a polenta from the milk and cornmeal and before removing it from the fire, salt it
2. While boiling pour it onto a work surface and with a table knife dipped in water spread it to a thickness of slightly less than half a finger
3. When cold, using a stamp cutter of recipe N. 182 or similar, cut as many disks as will yield 30 or 32 if you add the scraps by kneading and flattening them with your hands
4. Pair these disks by putting a slice of Gruyère between them, obtaining thus 15 to 16 frittelle
5. Beat two eggs, which are necessary to be able to gild them with these and with breadcrumbs, and fry them in lard or oil
6. Serve them hot as a side dish to a roast
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittelle di riso I (Rice Fritters I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, half a liter
- Rice, 100 grams
- Flour, 100 grams
- Sultana raisins, 50 grams
- Pine nuts chopped to the size of rice grains, 15 grams
- Eggs, three yolks and one white
- Butter, the size of a walnut
- Sugar, two small spoonfuls
- Rum, one spoonful
- Lemon zest flavoring
- Beer yeast, 30 grams
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the beer yeast as for Krapfen No. 182, mixing it with 40 grams of the said flour.
2. Cook the rice in the milk so that it remains firm, but set aside some milk to add if needed; to prevent it from sticking, stir often and move the pot to the edge of the stove.
3. Once removed from the fire and lukewarm, pour in the already puffed yeast, the eggs, the remaining flour (the 60 grams left), the pine nuts, the rum, and a bit more milk if needed; after working it somewhat, add the raisins and place the pot near the fire so that the entire mixture rises at a very moderate heat.
4. When it has puffed up, drop it into the pan by spoonfuls to form fritters that will turn out large and light.
5. Dust them with powdered sugar when they have lost their first boil and serve them hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittelle di riso II (Rice Fritters II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, approximately half a liter
- Rice, 100 grams
- Butter, the size of a walnut
- Salt
- Sugar, one scant spoonful
- Lemon zest flavoring
- Rum, one spoonful
- Egg yolks, three
- Flour, 50 grams
- Egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the rice very thoroughly in the milk, giving it flavor and grace with butter, a little salt, a scant spoonful of sugar, and lemon zest flavoring.
2. Once cooled, add a spoonful of rum, three egg yolks, and 50 grams of flour.
3. Mix well and let the mixture rest for several hours.
4. When you are about to fry it, whip the whites as much as you can, add them while mixing slowly, and drop it into the pan by spoonfuls.
5. Dust them as usual with powdered sugar and serve them hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittelle di semolino (Semolina Fritters)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, half a liter
- Semolina, 130 grams
- Butter, the size of a walnut
- Rum, one spoonful
- Lemon zest flavoring
- Salt, as needed
- Eggs, 3

## Directions

1. Cook the semolina in the milk, salt it when it is cooked and, once cooled, add the eggs and rum.
2. Fry them in oil or lard and serve them at table dusted with powdered sugar.
3. This quantity should be enough for four or five people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frittelle di tondone (Tondone Fritters)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 250 grams
- Eggs, 6
- Water, 3 deciliters
- A pinch of salt
- Lemon zest flavoring
- Butter, oil, or lard
- Sugar, powdered

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with the said water poured a little at a time and salt it.
2. Drop this mixture into a pan to cook it in white with butter, oil, or lard and when it is set on one side, turn it over with a plate on the other side, and there is your tondone.
3. Now pound it in the mortar with the said flavoring and soften it with the eggs: two at once, the other four one at a time with whipped whites, working the mixture a lot.
4. Fry it by spoonfuls to obtain fritters that, puffing greatly, take on the appearance of bombs.
5. Dust them with powdered sugar.
6. If you like, you can add 100 grams of Málaga raisins to the mixture, but in that case first soak them for twenty-four hours in fresh water and then remove the seeds.
7. Should be enough for six people, or for four if you make half the recipe.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto alla Garisenda (Fried Sandwich, Garisenda Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread, not too spongy
- slices of prosciutto, fat and lean
- truffle slices
- Gruyère cheese slices
- cold milk
- 1 egg, beaten
- breadcrumbs
- lard or oil for frying

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from stale bread and cut it into almond shapes or squares of about four centimeters per side, all of equal size.
2. On each piece lay first a slice of prosciutto, then slices of truffle, and on top a slice of Gruyère cheese.
3. Cover the filling with matching pieces of bread, pressing them together to hold them in place. Cut all components thin so the fried pieces are not too large.
4. Lightly moisten with cold milk and once it is absorbed, dip each piece in beaten egg, then in breadcrumbs, repeating the egg and breadcrumb coating twice so the edges are also golden and sealed.
5. Fry in lard or oil and serve alone or mixed with other fried dishes.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto alla Romana I (Fried, Roman Style I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion
- Butter
- Lean veal of milk-fed calf
- Salt
- Pepper
- Marsala wine
- Broth
- Egg yolk
- Wafers (ostie)
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Put on the fire a small mixture of chopped onion and butter, and when it is well colored cook a piece of lean veal of milk-fed calf in it, seasoning with salt and pepper
2. When the meat is browned, wet it with Marsala wine to complete the cooking
3. Pound it in a mortar and to soften it somewhat use the cooking liquid left over, and if this is not enough add a drop of broth and lastly an egg yolk; but mind that the mixture must remain rather firm
4. Now take some wafers, that is, thin pastry sheets, and cut them into small squares similar to those used by pharmacists for small lint pads
5. Beat an egg and the remaining white; then take up a wafer with your fingers, dip it in the egg and place it over a layer of breadcrumbs; on the same place as much mixture as a small walnut, dip another wafer in the egg, make it touch the breadcrumbs on only one side, that which must remain on the outside, and with
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto alla romana II (Roman-style Fried Sweetbread)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked chicken breast, 50 grams
- Salted tongue, 40 grams
- Fatty and lean prosciutto, 30 grams
- Parmesan, one spoonful
- One small truffle or nutmeg flavoring
- Milk, 2 deciliters
- Butter, 30 grams
- Flour, 30 grams
- Wafers or host

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the chicken and cut it into very small dice, doing the same with the tongue and prosciutto; slice the truffle into thin strips.
2. Make a béchamel sauce with the milk, butter, and flour.
3. When the béchamel is cooked, pour in the aforementioned ingredients and let it cool well before using the wafers as in the preceding recipe.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto Composto alla Bolognese (Composed Fry, Bologna Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal of milk-fed calf, stewed
- Small brain, boiled or cooked in gravy
- Slice of fat and lean prosciutto
- Egg yolk or whole egg
- Béchamel sauce N. 137
- Grated Parmesan cheese
- Nutmeg flavoring
- Truffles, finely chopped
- Flour
- Egg
- Fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take a piece of lean veal of milk-fed calf that has been stewed, a small brain boiled or cooked in gravy, and a slice of fat and lean prosciutto
2. Mince everything with a crescent-shaped blade and then pound it fine in a mortar
3. After add an egg yolk or a whole egg, depending on the quantity, and a little béchamel sauce N. 137
4. Put the mixture on the fire and stirring always let the egg cook
5. Add last grated Parmesan, the flavor of nutmeg, very finely chopped truffles and pour it onto a plate
6. When it is completely cold make from it as many round balls the size of a small walnut and flour them
7. Then dip them in egg and after in finest breadcrumbs, repeating the operation twice and fry them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto d'Agnello alla Bolognese (Fried Lamb, Bolognese Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb loin or shoulder and neck
- lard
- butter
- tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in water or broth
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the lamb into pieces and fry it as the offal in the previous recipe number.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Cardoni (Fried Cardoons)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoons
- Butter
- Salt
- Flour
- Pastella (batter) N. 156

## Directions

1. After removing the strings from the cardoons, boil them in salted water
2. Cut them into pieces and sauté them in butter, salting them a little more
3. Flour them, place them in batter N. 156 and fry them
4. They can serve as a side dish with a meat fry or a stew
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Chifels (Fried Chifels)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Chifels, 2
- Milk, 2 deciliters
- Sugar, 20 grams

## Directions

1. Remove the points from the chifels and cut them into slices about one centimeter thick which you will place on a tray. Put the milk on the fire.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Chifels (Fried Chifels)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- chifels, 2
- milk, 2 deciliters
- sugar, 20 grams
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the tips from the chifels and cut them into thick slices one centimeter each, placing them on a tray
2. Put the milk on the fire with the sugar and when it boils pour it over them to soak them lightly
3. Once cooled, dip them in two beaten eggs, coat with breadcrumbs and fry them
4. For those easily pleased they can serve as a sweet dish if you give them vanilla flavoring, dusting them with powdered sugar after cooking
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Finocchi (Fried Fennel)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fennel
- Flour
- Pastella (batter) N. 156

## Directions

1. Cut fennel into wedges
2. Clean them of the tougher leaves and boil them in salted water
3. Before placing them in batter N. 156, dry them and flour them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Mele (Fried Apples)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large apples of good quality, not too ripe
- Pastella (batter) N. 156
- Powdered sugar
- Anise seeds (optional)

## Directions

1. Take large apples of good quality, not too ripe
2. Remove the core with a metal tube made for this purpose, which leaves a hole in the middle
3. Peel them and cut them into thick slices just under one centimeter
4. Place them in batter N. 156 when you are about to fry them; if you like the flavor of anise, which goes well here, add a pinch
5. Dust them with powdered sugar and serve hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Pasta Ripiena (Fried Stuffed Pastry)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pasta N. 212 or puff pastry N. 154
- Filling from the previous recipe (N. 161)

## Directions

1. Take the pasta and roll it out to the thickness of a shield
2. Cut it into scalloped discs approximately the size indicated in the illustration
3. Place the filling from recipe N. 161 on each disc
4. Cover with equal-sized discs of the same pastry, moistening the edges so they stick together
5. Fry them and serve hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Pesche (Fried Peaches)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches, burrone variety, not too ripe
- Batter N. 156
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the peaches into slices that are not too large
2. Like apples and fennel, wrap them in batter N. 156
3. Dust them with sugar after cooking
4. It is not necessary to peel them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Pesche (Fried Peaches)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pesche burrone (white peaches), not too ripe
- Pastella (batter) N. 156
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Take white peaches not too ripe and cut them into wedges not too large
2. Like the apples and fennel, wrap them in batter N. 156 and dust them with powdered sugar after cooking
3. It is not necessary to peel them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Ricotta (Fried Ricotta)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ricotta, 200 grams
- Flour, 40 grams
- Eggs, 2
- Sugar, 2 scant teaspoons
- Lemon zest flavoring
- Salt, a pinch
- Brandy, 2 tablespoonfuls

## Directions

1. Use any quality of ricotta provided it has not become strong; ricotta from Rome and Maremma are excellent
2. Let the mixture rest for several hours before frying
3. With the stated amounts the mixture will be somewhat firm, which is good so the fried pastry takes the shape of bombs approximately the size of a walnut
4. To give these bombs a round shape as possible, take up the mixture with a spoon moistened with the hot liquid from the pan, shaping it with the tip of a table knife, also moistened in the same hot liquid
5. Dust them with powdered sugar and serve hot as a side dish with a meat fry
6. Note: there is little sugar in the mixture because it burns and the fried pastry would not then take on a nice golden color
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto di Semolino (Fried Semolina)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Semolina of fine grain, 70 to 80 grams
- Milk, 3 deciliters
- Eggs, 1
- Sugar, three teaspoons
- Butter, the size of a walnut
- Salt, a pinch
- Lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. Put the milk on the fire with butter and sugar and when it begins to boil pour in the semolina little by little, stirring continuously with a spoon
2. Salt it and beat an egg into it; mix and when the egg has incorporated remove the semolina from the fire and spread it on a buttered tray or on a floured work surface to the height of a finger
3. Cut it into almond shapes and dip first in beaten egg then in fine breadcrumbs and fry it
4. Dust it with icing sugar if you desire it sweeter, and serve it alone or, better yet, as a side dish to a meat fry
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto negli Stecchini (Fried on Toothpicks)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large chicken livers, 2
- Salted tongue, 40 grams
- Gruyère cheese, 40 grams
- Béchamel sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken livers in butter and season with salt and pepper.
2. After cooked cut them into 12 pieces and do the same with the Gruyère and the tongue.
3. Take 12 toothpicks and thread the aforementioned 36 pieces on them; first the tongue, in the middle the Gruyère and at the top the chicken liver at a certain distance from each other.
4. Then, using béchamel sauce, coat with the same the three pieces so that they remain well covered; then pass them in beaten egg, coat with breadcrumbs and fry them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Fritto Ripieno di Mostarda (Fried Mustard Filling)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 220 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Salt, a pinch
- Milk, as much as needed to mix it
- Mostarda di Savignano or homemade mustard (recipe N. 788)

## Directions

1. Form a rather tender pasta with flour, butter, salt, and milk, working it well with your hands on a pastry board
2. Let it rest for half an hour
3. Roll it out with a rolling pin to the thickness of a thin shield
4. Cut many discs with the stamper from N. 162 (approximately 80 discs)
5. Place some mustard on 40 of them and cover with the other 40, first moistening their edges with a finger dipped in water to stick them together
6. Fry them and dust them with sugar before serving
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Frutta in Guazzo (Fruit Preserved in Syrup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries, currants, and raspberries, 50 to 100 grams each sort
- Sugar equal to half the weight of the fruit
- Brandy or cognac enough to submerge the fruit
- Cherries
- Plums
- Apricots
- Peaches
- Gooseberries
- Grapes
- Pears

## Directions

1. Begin with the first fruits that appear in spring: strawberries, currants, and raspberries, placing 50 to 100 grams of each sort in a vase
2. Cover them with sugar equal to half their weight and enough brandy or cognac to submerge them
3. Continue with cherries, plums, apricots, and peaches, all pitted and, except for the cherries, sliced into thin strips, always adding sugar and brandy in proportion
4. You may also add gooseberries, grapes, and some pears, but then taste the liquid and add sugar or brandy according to your taste
5. Once the vase is formed, leave it to rest for several months before serving
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Funghi Fritti (Fried Mushrooms)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized mushrooms
- flour
- oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Choose mushrooms of medium size which are also of proper ripeness
2. Scrape the stem, clean them from earth and wash them whole without soaking them, as they would lose their pleasant aroma in the water
3. Then cut them into rather thick slices and flour them before throwing them into the pan
4. Oil is the best fat for this frying, and the seasoning consists exclusively of salt and pepper which you scatter over them when they are still boiling
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Funghi in Gratella (Grilled Mushrooms)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- open ovolo mushrooms
- oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. The open ovoli are most suited to this preparation
2. After cleaning and washing them, dry them in the folds of a cloth and season with oil, salt and pepper
3. They serve very well as a side dish to steak or any roast
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Funghi in Gratella (Mushrooms on the Grill)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Open ovoli mushrooms
- oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. After cleaning and washing the mushrooms, dry them between the folds of a cloth.
2. Season them with oil, salt and pepper.
3. Serve very well as a side dish to steak or any roast.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Funghi in Umido (Mushrooms in Stew)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms smaller than medium size
- oil
- garlic cloves
- nepitella leaves
- salt
- pepper
- tomato juice

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mushrooms and cut them into thinner slices than the fried ones
2. Put a pan on the fire with oil, a few cloves of whole garlic, slightly crushed, and a good pinch of nepitella leaves
3. When the oil begins to crackle, throw down the mushrooms without flouring them, season with salt and pepper and, halfway through cooking, wet them with simple tomato juice; be however sparing with the seasonings because mushrooms do not absorb them well
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Funghi Sott'Olio (Mushrooms in Oil)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Porcini mushrooms (morecci), the smallest ones possible
- White vinegar
- Water, if needed to dilute strong vinegar
- Oil
- Optional aromatics: garlic cloves, cloves, or bay leaf

## Directions

1. Choose the smallest porcini mushrooms you can find; if there are large ones the size of walnuts, cut them in half
2. Clean them well from dirt and wash them
3. Boil them for twenty-five minutes in white vinegar; if the vinegar is too strong, correct it with a little water
4. Remove from heat and dry them well in a cloth
5. Leave them in the air until the next day
6. Place them in a glass jar or glazed earthenware vessel, cover with oil, and add whichever aroma you prefer: garlic cloves, cloves, or bay leaf (which can be boiled in the vinegar)
7. Serve with boiled meats
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Funghi Trippati (Mushrooms Prepared Like Tripe)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- ovolo mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parmesan cheese
- meat broth

## Directions

1. This preparation suits ovolo mushrooms well and they are called trippati perhaps because they are treated like tripe
2. After cleaning and washing the ovoli, cut them into thin slices
3. Cook them in butter and season with salt, pepper and grated parmesan cheese
4. If you add meat broth they will turn out even better
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gallina di Faraone (Guinea Fowl)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One guinea fowl
- A ball of butter mixed with salt
- Lard for larding
- Butter
- Salt
- Oil

## Directions

1. Place a ball of butter mixed with salt inside the guinea fowl.
2. Lard the breast with strips of lard.
3. Wrap it in a sheet smeared with cold butter dusted with salt.
4. Roast it on a spit.
5. Remove the wrapping at two-thirds of the cooking time to finish cooking it and color it over the fire, brushing it with oil and salting it again.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Galline di Faraone Arrosto allo Spiede (Guinea Fowl Roasted on a Spit)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Guinea fowl
- butter
- salt
- lard
- oil

## Directions

1. Place a ball of butter mixed with salt inside the guinea fowl
2. Lard the breast with strips of lard
3. Wrap in a sheet of cold butter spread and dusted with salt
4. Remove the sheet at two-thirds of cooking time to finish cooking and browning by the fire
5. Oil and salt again during the final cooking
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina (Aspic Jelly)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams boneless muscle meat
- One calf's foot or 150 grams of calf's foot
- Feet of two or three chickens
- Two chicken heads with their necks
- 70 grams lean veal meat
- One egg
- A finger's width (of glass) of water
- Two pinches of sugar
- A drop of water
- Salt to taste
- A small glass of Marsala (optional)

## Directions

1. Singe the chicken feet over fire and cut them into pieces
2. Put everything into a pot with two liters of cold water
3. Salt sufficiently and boil, skimming, slowly for seven or eight continuous hours so that the liquid reduces by half
4. Pour the broth into a basin and when it has set, remove the fat from the surface
5. If it does not congeal, return it to the fire to reduce further, or add two sheets of fish glue
6. To clarify and give it amber color, finely mince 70 grams of lean veal meat with a knife and then pound it in a mortar
7. Place it in a small pot with one egg and a finger's width of water, mix everything well and pour in the cold aspic jelly
8. Do not stop beating it with a whisk over the fire until it rises to a boil, then boil slowly for about twenty minutes, during which taste to check the salt and give it color
9. For the color, place two pinches of sugar and a drop of water in a non-tinned metal spoon, hold it over fire until the sugar becomes almost black, pouring it little by little into the boiling aspic jelly to achieve the correct color gradation
10. Take a towel, wet it in water, wring it well and pass the warm aspic jelly through it without squeezing, and pour it immediately into molds
11. In summer, if it does not set well, place the molds on ice
12. When you wish to unmold it, pass a cloth dipped in boiling water lightly around the molds
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina di Arancio in Gelo (Orange Gelatin in Ice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 150 grams
- Isinglass, 20 grams
- Water, 4 deciliters
- Alkermes, four tablespoons
- Rum, two tablespoons
- One large sweet orange
- One lemon

## Directions

1. Soak the isinglass and, changing the water once, leave it for one or two hours.
2. Boil the sugar in half of the aforementioned water for ten minutes and strain it through a cloth.
3. Squeeze the orange and lemon into this syrup, passing their juice through the same cloth.
4. Take the isinglass that has been soaked and bring it to a boil in the remaining two deciliters of water and pour this also into the said syrup.
5. Add to it the alkermes and rum, mix everything and when it begins to cool pour it into the mold held in ice in summer and in cold water in winter.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina di Cotogne (Quince Jelly)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces with yellow skin
- Water
- White fine sugar, equal weight to the water collected

## Directions

1. Take quinces with yellow skin, which are more mature than green ones.
2. Cut them into thick slices half a finger thick, excluding the core.
3. Put them on the heat covered with water.
4. Without ever touching them with a spoon, boil them covered until well cooked.
5. Pour them into a fine sieve over a bowl to collect all the water without squeezing.
6. Weigh the water and return it to the heat with an equal amount of fine white sugar.
7. Boil uncovered in a pan, skimming the foam, until condensed, which you will know by the small pearl that the sugar begins to form, or if a few drops poured on a plate do not run too much.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina di Fragole in Gelo (Strawberry Gelatin in Ice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Very red and well-ripened strawberries, 300 grams
- Sugar, 200 grams
- Isinglass, 20 grams
- Water, 3 deciliters
- Rum, three tablespoons
- The juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Squeeze the strawberries in a cloth to extract all their juice.
2. Boil the sugar for ten minutes in two deciliters of the said water and unite this syrup with the juice of the strawberries; add the lemon and strain everything again through a fine cloth.
3. To the isinglass, after having soaked it as in the previous recipe, bring it to a boil in the remaining deciliter of water and pour it hot into the aforesaid mixture; add rum last, mix and pour the mixture into the mold to set it in ice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina di Lampone in Gelo (Raspberry Gelatin Dessert)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish glue, 20 grams
- Water, 3 deciliters
- Raspberry syrup, 2 deciliters
- Marsala wine, 1 deciliter
- Rum, 1 tablespoon

## Directions

1. Dissolve 20 grams of fish glue in the fire in three deciliters of water
2. Mix in the raspberry syrup, Marsala wine, and rum
3. Sugar is not necessary or only very little, as the syrup is very sweet
4. Proceed as with the preceding gelatins
5. If making with fresh raspberries instead of syrup, use the doses from the strawberry gelatin recipe and follow the same procedure
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina di Lampone in Gelo (Raspberry Gelatin in Ice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberry syrup (recipe 723), 2 deciliters
- Isinglass, 20 grams
- Water, 3 deciliters
- Marsala, 1 deciliter
- Rum, one tablespoon

## Directions

1. Dissolve 20 grams of isinglass on the fire in three deciliters of water and mix into it the raspberry syrup, Marsala and rum.
2. Sugar is not necessary or, if at all, very little, as the syrup is very sweet.
3. Proceed as in the previous gelatins.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina di Marasche o di Visciole in Gelo (Gelatin of Morello or Sour Cherries in Ice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Morello cherries or sour cherries, 400 grams
- Sugar, 200 grams
- Isinglass, 20 grams
- Water, 3 deciliters
- Rum, three tablespoons
- A small piece of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries and break them apart with your hands, adding some crushed pits.
2. Leave them thus for a few hours and then strain their juice through a cloth, squeezing well; let it rest further and then strain it several more times, even through paper or cotton, so that it remains clear.
3. Boil the sugar for ten minutes in two deciliters of the said water with the cinnamon in it, strain this also through the cloth and mix it with the juice of the cherries.
4. Add the isinglass dissolved in the remaining deciliter of water and finally the rum, proceeding as for the previous gelatins.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina di Ribes (Currant Jelly)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- White fine sugar, 80 parts per 100 parts of juice

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from the currants through a cloth.
2. Put it on the heat without allowing it to ferment.
3. Add white fine sugar in the proportion of 80 parts sugar per 100 parts juice.
4. Boil, not too much, until it condenses into the form of a jelly.
5. Conserve in jars like preserves.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelatina di Ribes in Gelo (Currant Gelatin in Ice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants, 300 grams
- Sugar, 130 grams
- Isinglass, 20 grams
- Water, 2 deciliters
- Marsala, four tablespoons
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Proceed entirely as in the gelatin of morello cherries from the previous recipe.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Albicocche (Apricot Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flavorful and ripe apricots, weighed with the pit, 300 grams
- Fine white sugar, 200 grams
- Water, half a liter
- One garden lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in the water for 10 minutes
2. When cold, add the apricot pulp passed through a sieve and the lemon juice
3. Pass the mixture again before putting it in the sorbet maker
4. This is an abundant quantity for four people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Amaretti (Amaretti Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 200 grams
- Amaretti cookies, 100 grams
- Egg yolks, 6
- Vanilla sugar flavoring

## Directions

1. Pound the amaretti very finely in a mortar and then place them in a copper pot, adding the sugar, egg yolks, and flavoring
2. Mix and gradually pour in the milk
3. Put the copper pot on the fire to condense the mixture as you would for cream, then pour it into the sorbet maker to freeze it
4. You will have a delicious ice cream that can serve, with good measure, eight people. Half the quantity can serve four or even five people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Aranci (Orange Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large oranges, 4
- Garden lemons, 1
- Water, 6 deciliters
- Sugar, 300 grams

## Directions

1. Squeeze the oranges and lemon and strain their juice.
2. Boil the sugar in the water for 10 minutes, pour it into the juice, pass the mixture through a sieve one more time and put it in the sorbetierre.
3. Serve it in goblet glasses filled to the brim, or all in one piece.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Banane (Banana Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Bananas, 4, which when shelled amount to 240 grams
- White sugar, 200 grams
- One garden lemon
- Water, half a liter

## Directions

1. Pass the pulp of the bananas through a sieve, add to this the lemon juice, boil the sugar in the water for five minutes in an uncovered saucepan, mix everything together and pour the mixture into the sorbetierre, making no economy of ice and salt.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Ciliege Visciole (Sour Cherry Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cherries, 1 kilogram
- Sugar, 250 grams
- Water, 2 deciliters
- Cinnamon for flavoring

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from 150 grams of the said cherries without damaging them too much and place them on the fire with 50 grams of the said sugar and with a piece of whole cinnamon, which you will then discard.
2. When they are syruped, that is when they have lost their rawness and you see them shriveled, set them aside.
3. Crush with your hands the remaining 850 grams of cherries, pound a handful of their pits in a mortar and mix them back in.
4. Pass a few at a time through a cloth, squeezing hard, these crushed cherries, to extract their juice, and the scraps that remain put them on the fire to extract their juice with the aforementioned two deciliters of water, let them boil 4 or 5 minutes, then pass them through the same cloth and unite the extracted liquid with the previous one.
5. Put all this juice on the fire with two pinches of powdered cinnamon and when it is about to come to a boil pour in the remaining 200 grams of sugar, stir, let it boil for two minutes and pass it through a sieve.
6. Put the strained juice in the sorbetierre and when it is well frozen mix in the syruped cherries so that they are spread equally, serve the ice cream in small glasses and you will see that for its goodness it will be appreciated by all.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Cioccolata (Chocolate Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 1 liter
- Fine chocolate, 200 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate and place it on the fire with the sugar and four deciliters of the aforementioned milk in a saucepan where it will remain confined.
2. Let it boil for a few minutes, stirring it constantly so that it refines.
3. Remove it from the fire, add the rest of the milk and pour the mixture into the sorbetierre when it is cold.
4. If you want this ice cream more substantial, increase the sugar to 120 grams and add two egg yolks when you remove the chocolate from the fire and it is no longer boiling. Mix, and return it to the stove for a few minutes and then, as has been said, add the rest of the milk.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Crema (Cream Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 200 grams
- Egg yolks, 8
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Use the recipe for cream: make a cream with milk, sugar, egg yolks, and vanilla flavoring
2. You will hear it is a delicious ice cream, well churned and very firm, if you know how to work it properly
3. This quantity will serve ten people
4. Instead of vanilla flavoring you can give the cream the flavor of coriander, burnt coffee, or toasted almond. For coriander, see Portuguese-style Milk, N. 693; for coffee, boil several crushed coffee beans separately in the milk; for toasted almond, make a brittle like that of N. 617, somewhat more cooked, with 100 grams of almonds and 80 grams of sugar; pound it fine, boil it separately in a little milk, strain it and add it to the cream
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Fragole (Strawberry Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe strawberries, 300 grams
- Fine white sugar, 300 grams
- Water, half a liter
- One large garden lemon
- One orange

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in the water for 10 minutes in an uncovered copper pot
2. Pass the strawberries through a sieve along with the juice of the orange and lemon
3. Add the syrup after having strained it as well
4. Mix everything together and pour the mixture into the sorbet maker
5. This quantity will serve eight people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Latte di Mandorle (Almond Milk Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchero (sugar), 200 grams
- Mandorle dolci con 4 o 5 amare (sweet almonds with 4 or 5 bitter), 150 grams
- Acqua (water), 8 deciliters
- Panna (cream), 2 deciliters
- Odore di acqua di fior d'arancio o di coriandoli (flavoring of orange blossom water or coriander)

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in the water for ten minutes with coriander inside, as in the Portuguese Milk recipe, if you use it for the flavoring
2. Shell the almonds, pound them very fine in the mortar diluting them with a few spoonfuls of the syrup obtained and mix them to the same
3. Then pass them through a fine cloth squeezing well to extract from the almonds all the substance possible, repeating the mortar operation more times if needed
4. Unite the cream to the squeezed liquid, freeze it in the ice cream maker and when it is well firm serve it in small glasses
5. This dose should be enough for nine or ten persons
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Limone (Lemon Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine white sugar, 300 grams
- Water, half a liter
- Lemons, 3

## Directions

1. Preferably use garden lemons which have a more pleasant taste and greater fragrance than foreign ones, which often taste of reheating
2. Boil the sugar in the water with some pieces of lemon zest for 10 minutes in an uncovered copper pot
3. When this syrup is cold, squeeze the lemons into it one at a time, tasting the mixture to adjust the acidity
4. Strain it and pour it into the sorbet maker
5. This quantity will serve six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Marroni (Chestnut Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Marroni (chestnuts), 200 grams
- Zucchero (sugar), 150 grams
- Latte (milk), half liter
- Odore di vaniglia (vanilla flavoring)

## Directions

1. Put the chestnuts to boil in water as if making ballotte
2. When well cooked, clean them of both skins and pass the pulp through a strainer
3. Put this pulp on the fire with the milk and sugar and let it boil slowly in an uncovered pan for a quarter hour
4. Give the mixture the vanilla flavoring with the vanilla sugar and pour it into the ice cream maker
5. Serve it at table all in one piece; if it should serve nine or ten persons double the dose
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Pesche (Peach Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe white peaches, including the pit, 400 grams
- Sugar, 250 grams
- Water, half a liter
- One garden lemon
- Three kernels taken from the pits of the peaches

## Directions

1. Crush these kernels with the sugar and put them to boil in the water for 10 minutes
2. Pass the peach pulp, squeeze the lemon into it and, mixing everything, pass it all through a fine sieve
3. This will serve six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Pistacchi (Pistachio Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 8 deciliters
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Pistachios, 50 grams
- Egg yolks, 6

## Directions

1. Blanch the pistachios with hot water and pound them very fine with a spoonful of the said sugar, then put them in a saucepan with the egg yolks and the sugar, stirring everything well.
2. Add the milk and place the mixture on the fire stirring with a spoon, and when it is condensed like cream pour it cold into the sorbetierre. This dose will be enough for eight people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Ribes (Currant Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants, 500 grams
- Sugar, 300 grams
- Black cherries, 150 grams
- Water, half a liter
- One large garden lemon

## Directions

1. Crush the currants and cherries with your hands, add the lemon juice and pass everything through a sieve, pressing well.
2. Boil the sugar in the water for 10 minutes in an uncovered saucepan to obtain the syrup, and when it is cold mix it into the described mixture and pour it into the sorbetierre. It will be enough for seven or eight people, serving it in small glasses. The cherries, besides their special flavor, serve to give the ice cream a more beautiful color.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Torrone (Nougat Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 1 liter
- Sugar, 250 grams
- Candied red squash, 40 grams
- Candied citron, 30 grams
- Almonds, 30 grams
- Pistachios, 20 grams
- Egg yolks, 4
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Make a cream with the milk, the sugar and the egg yolks, giving it the flavor of vanilla, and pour it into the sorbetierre.
2. When it is frozen mix into it the aforementioned ingredients. Blanch the pistachios and almonds in hot water; divide those into three parts and mince these to the size of a vetch and toast them.
3. Cut the candied fruit into thin slices and the squash into small cubes, which being red will make a more beautiful appearance.
4. If the milk is good, by boiling it for half an hour with the sugar in it, you can do without the egg yolks, but the mixture will then be of less flavor.
5. The almonds in this and similar cases come out better toasted in the following manner. When peeled and minced, put them on the fire with a spoonful of the said sugar and a drop of water, stir them continuously and when they have taken color stop them with another drop of water; pour them then into a colander over the remaining sugar and use them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gelato di Tutti i Frutti (Mixed Fruit Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 200 grams
- Well-ripened apricots, weighed with the pit, 100 grams
- Raspberry, 100 grams
- Currants, 100 grams
- Candied citron, 20 grams
- Water, half a liter

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in the water for ten minutes, then add the pulp of the said fruits passed through a sieve, then the candied citron cut into small pieces.
2. Instead of apricots you can use burrone peaches, and strawberries can be substituted for currants.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Germano in Umido II (Braised Mallard Duck II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- mallard duck
- 30 grams butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 500 milliliters water
- salt
- pepper
- 1/4 orange peel in one piece
- 1 celery rib about a palm long
- 1 piece of carrot

## Directions

1. Put the mallard duck in a pot with 30 grams of butter and let it brown.
2. Remove it and throw a tablespoon of flour into the remaining fat, stirring with a spoon until it takes on a brown color.
3.
