Remove from heat and not at a boil anymore, pour half a liter of water over that mixture and put the duck back in; season with salt and pepper and let it boil covered until completely cooked with a quarter of an orange peel in one piece, a celery rib about a palm long and a piece of carrot, both cut roughly.
4. Finally strain the sauce, break apart the duck at its joints, put it back in its sauce squeezing the juice of the orange over it to boil it a few more minutes and serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Germano in Umido II (Braised Wild Duck II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild duck
- 30 grams of butter
- one spoonful of flour
- half a litre of water
- salt
- pepper
- one quarter of an orange peel, in one piece
- one celery rib, one palm long
- one piece of carrot, coarsely sliced

## Directions

1. Put the duck in the casserole with 30 grams of butter and let it take on color.
2. Remove it and throw into the remaining fat one spoonful of flour to have it take on, stirring with a wooden spoon, a brown color.
3. Removed from the fire and no longer boiling, pour onto that mixture half a litre of water and put the duck back in it; season it with salt and pepper and let it boil covered until completely cooked with one quarter of an orange peel in one piece, one celery rib one palm long and a piece of carrot, sliced coarsely.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Germano ossia Anatra Selvatica (Wild Duck)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild duck (Anas boscas), about one kilogram
- 30 grams of prosciutto, fat and lean together
- celery, parsley, carrot, one quarter of a large onion
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- water
- black cabbage or whole lentils for accompaniment

## Directions

1. When you buy a wild duck in the market, open its beak to observe the tongue.
2. If you find it very dried out, you can say that the animal has been dead for a long time, and then smell it to make sure it does not stink.
3. Empty the duck, keeping the liver, heart and onion; remove its head and the skin of the neck, after having opened it to extract the vertebrae, fold it back over the animal's breast.
4. For a duck that weighs about one kilogram, finely chop 30 grams of prosciutto, fat and lean together, with all the aromatics, that is celery, parsley, carrot and a quarter of a large onion.
5. Put everything together with oil in a casserole and on top of the mixture place the duck, seasoning it with salt and pepper.
6. Let it take on color from all sides and then add water to cook it through.
7. Cook the accompaniment of black cabbage or whole lentils in water and make it over in the aforementioned sauce: taste it to add, if necessary, a small piece of butter, which will make it more agreeable and flavorful, and combine it with the duck when you send it to the table.
8. Cut the cabbage coarsely and season it as well with salt and pepper.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gialletti I (Little Yellow Cakes I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cornmeal, 300 grams
- Wheat flour, 100 grams
- Raisins, 100 grams
- Sugar, 50 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Lard, 30 grams
- Beer yeast, 20 grams
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. With half the wheat flour and the beer yeast, make a dough moistened with tepid water and set it to rise
2. Meanwhile, knead with hot water the two flours mixed together with all the above ingredients, except the raisins
3. Add the risen dough to the mixture when it is ready, work it somewhat, and finally add the raisins
4. Divide it into fifteen or sixteen parts forming as many loaves in the shape of a spool, and with the back of a knife incise on the surface of each an almond-shaped lattice
5. Set them to rise in a warm place, then bake them in the oven or a country oven at moderate heat so they remain tender
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gialletti II (Little Yellow Cakes II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cornmeal, 300 grams
- Wheat flour, 150 grams
- Powdered sugar, 200 grams
- Butter, 150 grams
- Lard, 70 grams
- Raisins, 100 grams
- Eggs, 2
- Lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. Holding them to the thickness of half a finger, you will make about twenty of these; but you can also give them whatever shape you prefer and instead of 20, keeping them small, make 40
2. Bake them as the previous ones and to knead them follow the directions as if making short crust pastry
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Girello alla Brace (Garetto - Braised Beef Shank)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 700 grams beef girello (rump) without bone
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Place the meat in a pot with only salt and pepper seasoning, no water or other ingredients
2. Close the lid of the pot with several layers of paper held firm by the lid
3. Let it cook very slowly
4. The meat will release a copious amount of sauce which it will gradually reabsorb
5. When it has reabsorbed all the sauce, remove it from heat and serve
6. It is almost better served cold than hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gnocchi (Gnocchi)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, large and floury, 200 grams
- Breast of chicken, boiled and finely minced, 40 grams
- Parmigiano cheese, grated, 40 grams
- Egg yolks, 2
- Salt, to taste
- Nutmeg, to taste
- Flour, 30-40 grams

## Directions

1. Cook 200 grams of large floury potatoes in water, or better by steam, and pass them through a sieve.
2. To these add the boiled chicken breast minced very finely with a crescent cutter, 40 grams of grated parmigiano, two egg yolks, salt to taste and a hint of nutmeg.
3. Mix and pour the mixture onto a work surface over 30 or 40 grams of flour to bind it and be able to pull it into sticks as thick as the little finger.
4. Cut these into small pieces and throw them into boiling broth where a cooking of five or six minutes will be sufficient.
5. This dose may be enough for seven or eight people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gnocchi alla Romana (Roman-Style Gnocchi)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 150 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Gruyère cheese, 40 grams
- Parmesan, 20 grams
- Milk, half a liter
- Eggs, 2 pieces

## Directions

1. Mix flour with eggs and milk poured little by little into a saucepan.
2. Add Gruyère cheese in small pieces and place the mixture on the fire, stirring continuously.
3. When it is solidified by the cooking of the flour, salt it and add half the butter.
4. Let the mixture cool, then in the same manner as cornmeal gnocchi, cut it into small pieces in a tray that can withstand fire.
5. Season as you go with the remaining butter in small pieces and the grated Parmesan; but not on the surface, because Parmesan becomes bitter under heat.
6. Brown them under an iron lid or in a country oven and serve them hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gnocchi di Farina Gialla (Polenta Gnocchi)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cornmeal or semolina
- salt
- water
- cheese
- butter
- tomato sauce or tomato preserve

## Directions

1. Salt the water and, when it boils, pour the flour with your left hand little by little and with a ladle in your right hand, stir continuously.
2. It is necessary that this flour boils a lot, and when it is reduced in such a way as to hold well on the ladle, throw it with a table knife in pieces into a tray and season each layer with cheese, butter, tomato sauce or preserve dissolved in water.
3. Fill the tray and send it hot to the table.
4. If you prefer them more seasoned, you can treat them like polenta with sausages or like bolognese macaroni.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gnocchi di latte (Milk Gnocchi)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one litre
- Sugar, 240 grams
- Starch reduced to powder, 120 grams
- Egg yolks, 8
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix everything in the same way you would for a cream and place it on the fire in a saucepan, constantly stirring with a spoon
2. When the mixture has become firm, keep it a few more minutes on the fire and then pour it into a baking dish or plate at a height of one and a half fingers and cut it into almond shapes when cooled
3. Arrange these pieces one on top of the other with symmetry on a copper or porcelain tray that can withstand the fire, interspersing them with some pieces of butter, and brown them somewhat in a country oven to serve them hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gnocchi di Patate (Potato Gnocchi)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- flour
- cheese
- butter
- tomato sauce or milk

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes in water or, better, steamed and, while hot, peel them and pass them through a sieve.
2. Mix them with the flour and work the dough somewhat with your hands, stretching it into a thin cylinder so that it can be cut into pieces about three centimeters long.
3. Dust them lightly with flour and, taking them one at a time, hollow them out with your thumb on the reverse side of a grater.
4. Cook them in salted water for ten minutes, remove them dry and season them with cheese, butter and tomato sauce, if you like.
5. If you want them more delicate, cook them in milk and serve them without draining; if the milk is of good quality, apart from salt, no seasoning is necessary or at most a pinch of parmesan.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gnocchi di Patate (Potato Gnocchi)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large yellow potatoes, 400 grams
- Wheat flour, 150 grams
- Salt
- Parmesan cheese
- Butter
- Tomato sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes in water or, better, steamed, and while hot and boiling, peel them and pass them through a sieve
2. Then knead them with the said flour and work the dough somewhat with your hands, stretching it into a thin cylinder so you can cut it into pieces about three centimeters long
3. Lightly dust them with flour and, taking them one at a time, hollow them with your thumb on the back of a grater
4. Cook them in salted water for ten minutes, remove them dry and season them with cheese, butter and tomato sauce, if you like
5. If you want them more delicate, cook them in milk and serve them without draining; if the milk is of good quality, apart from salt, it is sufficient
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gnocchi di Semolino (Semolina Gnocchi)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 4 deciliters
- Semolina, 120 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Grated Parmesan, 40 grams
- Eggs, 2 pieces
- Salt, as needed

## Directions

1. Cook the semolina in milk.
2. When you are about to remove it from the fire, salt it and add half the butter and half the Parmesan.
3. When it is still very hot, add the eggs and mix.
4. Pour it onto a work surface or a tray, spread it to the thickness of a finger and a half, and let it cool to cut it into almond shapes.
5. Arrange the gnocchi one on top of the other in a nice display in a proportional tray, interspersing them with pieces of the remaining butter, and dusting them layer by layer, but not on the surface, with the remaining Parmesan.
6. Finally, brown them in a country oven and serve them hot, either alone or as an accompaniment to a plate of stewed meat or prepared in another manner.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Granelli Fritti (Fried Testicles)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton testicles
- Salt
- Flour
- Egg

## Directions

1. Boil them in salted water, then make a superficial incision lengthwise to remove the outer membrane composed of the tunic and epididymis
2. Cut them into thin fillets, salt them a little more, flour them well, pass them through beaten egg and fry them
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Granelli Lessati Filettati (Boiled and Sliced Grain)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Grain (from recipe N. 174)
- Egg yolk, 1
- Flour, 1/2 teaspoon
- Lemon, 1/2
- Broth, as needed

## Directions

1. Boil the grain and cut it into fillets.
2. Cook it with the egg yolk, flour and lemon in the manner described for the main preparation.
3. Add more broth if needed to make it more fluid.
4. Serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Gâteau à la Noisette (Hazelnut Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice flour, 125 grams
- Sugar, 170 grams
- Butter, 100 grams
- Sweet almonds, 50 grams
- Shelled hazelnuts, 50 grams
- Eggs, 4
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Peel the hazelnuts with hot water as you would almonds, and dry both the hazelnuts and almonds well in the sun or by the fire. Then, after pounding them very finely, mix them with the rice flour using two tablespoons of the sugar.
2. Work the eggs well with the remaining sugar, then pour the almond and hazelnut mixture into it and stir very much with a whisk. Finally add the melted butter and work the mixture again.
3. Pour it into a smooth, rounded mold that is somewhat narrow so that it comes to a height of four or five fingers, and bake it in an oven at moderate heat. Serve it cold.
4. This amount will serve six or seven people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Insalata di Patate (Potato Salad)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, 500 grams
- Capers in vinegar, 30 grams
- Pickled peppers, 2
- Pickled gherkins, 5
- Pickled small onions, 4
- Salted anchovies, cleaned, 4
- Celery rib about one palm long
- A pinch of basil
- Hard-boiled eggs, 2
- Oil, good measure
- Vinegar, small amount
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oregano, optional

## Directions

1. Boil or steam 500 grams of potatoes
2. Peel while hot and slice thinly into a salad bowl
3. Finely chop capers, pickled peppers, gherkins, pickled onions, cleaned anchovies, celery rib and basil together and place in a bowl
4. Chop hard-boiled eggs finely, then crush with a knife blade and add to the chopped mixture
5. Season the mixture with good oil, little vinegar, salt and pepper
6. Mix well until it becomes almost liquid
7. Use this mixture to dress the potatoes
8. Add oregano if desired
9. This portion serves six or seven people and can be kept for several days
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Insalata Maionese (Mayonnaise Salad)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- romaine lettuce or lettuce
- beets
- potatoes, boiled
- anchovies, washed and cleaned of bones
- boiled fish, cut into pieces
- capers (optional)
- pitted olives (optional)
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- eggs
- mustard
- lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Take romaine lettuce or lettuce, cut it into strips one finger wide, mix with beets and boiled potatoes cut into thin slices, some washed anchovies, cleaned of the bone and cut into four or five parts, and finally boiled fish in small pieces.
2. You can add at most some capers and the flesh of two or three sweetened olives.
3. Season everything together with salt, oil and not too much vinegar, turn it over so it takes up the seasoning well and heap it all together so that it makes a mound.
4. Make a mayonnaise sauce as in recipe N. 126 which, in the dose indicated there, can be enough for seven or eight people; but instead of pepper give it the piquancy with a teaspoon of mustard and to the lemon add a drop of vinegar in which you can dissolve the mustard.
5. With this sauce spread all the salad on the surface and then decorate it with other slices of beets and potatoes alternated so that they make a fine show; if you have a suitable mold, place on top of the salad, for beauty, not to eat it, a flower made with butter.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Insalata Russa (Russian Salad)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lettuce, 120 grams
- Beets, 100 grams
- Green beans, 70 grams
- Potatoes, 50 grams
- Carrots, 20 grams
- Capers in vinegar, 20 grams
- Gherkins in vinegar, 20 grams
- Salted anchovies, 3
- Hard-boiled eggs, 2
- Egg yolks, 2 raw
- Oil, 2 deciliters
- Lemon juice
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fish glue, 3 leaves
- Water

## Directions

1. The lettuce, which can be of two or three varieties, such as romaine lettuce (lattugoni), radicchio, or lettuce, cut it into thin strips.
2. The beets, green beans, potatoes and carrots weigh them after boiling and cut them into small dice smaller than a chickpea and likewise the whites and one yolk of the two hard-boiled eggs.
3. Leave the capers whole and cut the gherkins to the same size as the capers.
4. The anchovies, cleaned and with the spine removed, cut them into small pieces, and having done all this mix everything together.
5. Now prepare a mayonnaise (see N. 126) with two raw yolks and the remaining hard-boiled yolk and 2 deciliters of finest oil.
6. When it is whipped, add the juice of a lemon, season it with salt and pepper and pour it into the said mixture stirring well so that it covers everything.
7. Dissolve on the fire three leaves of fish glue in two fingers of water height in a glass after having kept it soaked for a few hours and, once dissolved, pour an amount the size of a coin on the bottom of a smooth mold and mix the rest into the mixture which you will then pour into the said mold to put it on ice.
8. To unmold it easily, wet the mold with hot water and if you wish to give it a more beautiful and elegant appearance, when you have poured the thin layer of fish glue in the mold, before adding the mixture you can make an ornament of various colors on it with the vegetables, the whites and yolk of the hard-boiled eggs mentioned above.
9. This amount will serve eight or ten people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Intonaco di Cioccolata (Chocolate Coating)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 30 grams
- Chocolate, 100 grams, in small pieces
- Powdered sugar, 30 grams

## Directions

1. Put the butter and chopped chocolate on the fire and when well melted add the powdered sugar and spread the mixture on the cake when it is no longer boiling
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Krapfen (Filled Pastries)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Eggs
- Butter, melted
- Sugar
- Salt
- Egg yolk, minced filling with gravy and béchamel
- Chicken livers
- Sweetbreads
- Prosciutto
- Salted tongue
- Truffles or mushrooms
- Water
- Lard or oil for frying

## Directions

1. Mix the remaining ingredients with eggs, melted butter, sugar, and salt
2. If the dough is too soft, add enough flour so it can be rolled out to the thickness of half a finger
3. Cut discs using a tin circle of the designated size
4. Make 24 discs total
5. Using an egg or similar tool, press the point in the middle of each disc to make an indentation
6. On 12 of the discs, place a teaspoon of the filling made from minced liver, sweetbreads, prosciutto, salted tongue, flavored with truffles or mushrooms, all cut into small dice, combined with gravy and béchamel
7. Wet the edges of the discs with a finger dipped in water
8. Cover each filled disc with one of the remaining 12 empty discs
9. Press a tin circle of equal size on top to form an indentation all around
10. Let them rise with gentle heat, by the fire or in a stove
11. When well puffed, fry them in lard or oil until covered by the fat
12. Serve hot as a fried dish or intermediate course
13. For a sweet version, fill with firm cream or fruit preserve and dust with powdered sugar after cooking
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Krapfen I (German Yeast Pastries I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Hungarian flour, 150 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- Beer yeast, the size of a large walnut
- Eggs, one whole and one yolk
- Sugar, one spoonful
- Salt, a good pinch
- Milk

## Directions

1. Take a handful of the said flour, place it on a work surface and, making a hole in the middle, mix in the beer yeast with lukewarm milk and form a loaf of proper firmness, on which you will make a cross-shaped cut to better know if it has risen.
2. Place this loaf in a small pan or pot in whose bottom there is a very thin layer of milk, cover it and leave it near the fire so that the loaf rises at a very moderate heat: you will see that twenty minutes will be enough.
3. Once risen, put it in the middle of the remaining flour and knead it with the eggs, the melted butter, the sugar, and the salt.
4. If this dough turns out too soft, add enough flour to reduce it so that it can be stretched with a rolling pin to the thickness of half a finger.
5. Thus you will have a sheet from which, with a tin circle, you will cut out many discs the size indicated.
6. Assuming you make 24, take an egg or another tool of similar shape and with the point of it press in the middle of each of the discs to imprint it with a hole.
7. In 12 of said discs place a spoonful of a fine mixture made with juice and béchamel sauce, composed of chicken livers, sweetbreads, prosciutto, salted tongue, truffle flavor or
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Krapfen II (Fried Pastry Balls)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams Hungarian flour
- 50 grams butter
- 20 grams beer yeast
- less than one deciliter milk or cream, so that the mixture is rather firm
- 3 egg yolks
- one teaspoon sugar
- one good pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Put the yeast in a cup with a spoonful of the flour and, dissolving it with a little of the said warm milk, set it to rise near the fire.
2. Then in a bowl pour the butter, melted in winter in a water bath, and work it with a spoon, throwing in the egg yolks one at a time, then pour the rest of the flour, the yeast when it has doubled in size, the milk little by little, the salt and the sugar, working the mixture with a hand until it reaches the desired consistency.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Kugelhupf (Hungarian Yeast Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams Hungarian or very fine flour
- 100 grams butter
- 50 grams sugar
- 50 grams sultana raisins
- 30 grams beer yeast
- one whole egg and two egg yolks
- a pinch of salt
- lemon zest
- milk as needed

## Directions

1. Mix the yeast with warm milk and a handful of the said flour to form a rather firm small loaf; make a cross cut in it and place it in a small pan with a veil of milk underneath, covered and near the fire, making sure the fire does not heat it too much.
2. In winter, melt the butter in a water bath then work it somewhat with the whole egg, then pour in the sugar and then the flour, the egg yolks, the salt and the flavoring, mixing well.
3. Now add the yeast which in the meantime will have already swollen and with spoonfuls of warm milk, pour one at a time, work the mixture with a spoon in a bowl for more than half an hour reducing it to a somewhat liquid consistency, but not too much.
4. Finally pour in the raisins and put it in a smooth buttered mold dusted with powdered sugar mixed with flour, where the mixture does not reach half the pan which you will place well covered in a warm place or in a place of warm temperature to rise, which will take two or three hours.
5. When it is well risen to reach the mouth of the pan, put it in the oven at moderate heat, turn it out while warm, dust it with powdered sugar or if you like (this is optional) sprinkle it with rum.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Latte alla Portoghese (Portuguese Milk Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Egg yolks, 8 and two whites
- Vanilla flavoring, or coriander seeds, or coffee flavoring, which are those that suit best

## Directions

1. If you prefer the last one, grind several grains of roasted coffee; if you like the flavor of coriander seeds, which is as pleasant as that of vanilla, pound a pinch of it and, both the one and the other, put them to boil in the milk which you will then strain.
2. If the milk is not of much substance, let it boil also for an hour and a quarter.
3. Never forget the veil of melted sugar at the bottom of the mold. Follow the same procedure as the previous recipe, omitting only the burnt sugar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Latte Brûlé (Crème Brûlée)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 180 grams
- Egg yolks, 8 and two whites

## Directions

1. Put the milk on the fire with 100 grams of the said sugar and let it boil for a full hour, then remove it from the fire so it becomes cold.
2. Dissolve in a pan apart the 80 grams of sugar that remains and when it is well melted, pour into a smooth mold enough to cover the bottom like a veil; what remains in the pan continue to keep on the fire until it becomes black.
3. Then stop it with a ladle of water and you will hear it crackle as it hardens; but continue to keep it on the fire turning the spoon to obtain a thick and dark liquid.
4. Set it aside and beat in a pot the said eggs, then mix everything together, that is: the milk, the eggs and the burnt sugar.
5. Taste it to see if it is sweet enough, pass it through a not too fine tin strainer and pour it into the already prepared mold.
6. Cook it in a water bath with fire above and when the surface begins to color, place under the lid a sheet greased with butter.
7. To ascertain the cooking, immerse a small stick and if this comes out clean and dry it will be a sign that it should be removed from the fire.
8. Let it cool well and before pouring it into the dish, with or without a napkin, detach it all around with a thin knife. In summer, before turning it out, you can freeze it with ice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Latteruolo (Delicate Milk Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Egg yolks, 8
- Egg whites, 2
- Vanilla or coriander seed flavoring

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with sugar for an hour and also an hour and a quarter if you are not quite sure of its quality.
2. If for flavoring you use coriander seeds, use them as indicated in the previous recipe.
3. In the milk, break the surface skin with a spoon from time to time, pass it through a strainer for extra precaution, and when it is cold, mix it well with the beaten eggs.
4. Prepare a pan lined with pasta matta No. 153, arrange it as in the polenta of Romagna No. 702, pour the mixture into it, cook it with moderate heat below and above and so that it does not brown on top, cover it with paper greased with butter.
5. Wait until it is well cold before cutting it into almond shapes with the dough underneath like the said polenta.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Latteruolo (Milk Custard Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one litre
- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Eggs, 6
- Lemon zest for flavoring

## Directions

1. Remove a thin strip of zest from a lemon using a knife and place it in the milk, then bring the milk to a boil with the sugar inside for half an hour
2. When cooled, remove the lemon zest and mix in the beaten eggs
3. Cook it as the previous recipe, or without the pasta underneath; in that case, grease the baking dish abundantly with cold butter so it does not stick to the bottom
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Latteruolo Semplice (Simple Milk Pudding)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Eggs, 6
- Lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. The fragrance of citrus fruits, being the product of a volatile oil enclosed in superficial cells, it will suffice to cut with a penknife a very thin ribbon of their rind, at least a palm long, and let it boil in the liquid that you wish to aromatize.
2. From the surface of a lemon lift with a penknife a strip of rind of a certain length and put it in the milk, which you will boil for half an hour with the sugar inside.
3. When it is cold, remove the lemon strip.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Le Tazzine (Almond Cream Cups)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 300 grams
- Sweet almonds, 60 grams
- Egg yolks, 10
- Water, 1 deciliter
- Orange blossom water flavoring
- Cinnamon powder, as needed

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, toast them to a hazelnut color, pound them very finely and set aside.
2. Boil the sugar with the water for one or two minutes, ensuring it does not brown, and after, when it is lukewarm, begin adding the egg yolks, one or two at a time and, over very moderate heat, work it continuously stirring the spoon always in one direction.
3. When the mixture has condensed somewhat so there is no longer danger it will curdle, you can then, in order for it to puff up better, beat it with a whisk from bottom to top until the yolks have not lost their bright color and taken on the appearance of a dense cream.
4. Then give it the orange blossom water flavoring and add the almonds, mixing well.
5. Pour it into the small cups and on the surface of each one, in the middle, place a pinch of the said cinnamon, which, when mixed into the sweet by whoever must eat it, will acquire more fragrance.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lenticchie Intere per Contorno (Whole Lentils as a Side Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- aromatic herb bouquet
- prosciutto, fat and lean, chopped
- butter
- onion
- defatted broth from cotechino or zampone (or regular broth if the cotechino is not very fresh)
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils in water with an aromatic herb bouquet.
2. Once cooked and well drained from water, prepare a small mixture of fat and lean prosciutto, a piece of butter, and a little onion.
3. Brown the onion well, then add one or two ladles of defatted broth from the cotechino or zampone.
4. Let it boil a little, strain it, and use this sauce to prepare the lentils.
5. Add another piece of butter, salt, and pepper.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lenticchie Passate per Contorno (Lentil Purée as a Side Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- butter
- onion
- parsley
- celery
- carrot
- broth (defatted broth from cotechino can be used)
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils in water with a piece of butter until cooked but not mushy.
2. Pass them through a sieve.
3. Make a small mixture of onion (a little, as it should not be noticeable), parsley, celery, and carrot.
4. Place it on the fire with enough butter and brown it well.
5. Stop the cooking with a ladle of broth (which can also be the defatted broth from the cotechino).
6. Strain it and use that sauce to flavor the purée.
7. Do not forget salt and pepper, and note that it should remain as firm as possible.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lesso Rifatto (Reheated Boiled Meat)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef, a piece of short and thick meat weighing not less than half a kilogram
- Cured meat
- Onion
- Celery
- Carrot
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Tomato juice or conserve
- Broth
- Dried mushrooms

## Directions

1. Sometimes to eat the boiled meat more willingly, one uses to reheat it in a stew; but then wait to have a short and thick piece of meat, weighing not less than half a kilogram. Remove it from the broth before it is cooked all the way and put it in a pot on top of a finely chopped mixture of cured meat, onion, celery, carrot and a piece of butter, seasoning it with salt, pepper and spices. When the mixture is rendered, finish cooking the meat with tomato juice or conserve dissolved in broth. Strain the sauce, degrease it and put it back on the fire with the piece of meat and with a handful of dried mushrooms softened.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lesso Rifatto all'Inglese (Reheated Boiled Meat English Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef for boiling, half a kilogram
- Egg
- Flour, 20 grams
- Milk, two deciliters
- Butter, 50 grams
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Take half a kilogram of beef for boiling, which can suffice for three people, and blend in a pot one egg with 20 grams of flour and two deciliters of milk. Slice the boiled meat thinly and, taking a tray that can withstand fire, melt 50 grams of butter inside and spread it over this, then season it with salt, pepper and spices. When it has browned on one side and the other sprinkle on top a heaping spoonful of Parmesan and then pour the contents of the pot over it. Let the liquid set and send it to table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lesso Rifatto all'Italiana (Reheated Boiled Meat Italian Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef for boiling
- Small onions, 150 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Garlic
- Salt
- Pepper
- Broth
- Parsley
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. If onion does not bother you, this turns out better than the previous one. For the same quantity of boiled meat mince 150 grams of small onions, put them in a pan with 50 grams of butter and when they begin to brown throw in the boiled meat cut into thin slices, one clove of garlic whole, dressed and lightly crushed, which you will then remove, and season it with salt and pepper. As it begins to dry out wet it with broth and after seven or eight minutes add a pinch of chopped parsley and the juice of half a lemon, and serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lingua di Bue al Sugo di Carne (Beef Tongue in Meat Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef tongue weighing over 1 kilogram without the pappagorgia
- 60 grams of lard cut into thin lardons, seasoned with salt and pepper
- 30 grams of butter
- salt
- pepper
- meat sauce (sugo di carne) or broth
- a small piece of butter
- less than half a tablespoon of flour
- boiled vegetables

## Directions

1. Skin the tongue as indicated in recipe N. 289 and lard it with 60 grams of lard cut into thin strips seasoned with salt and pepper.
2. Tie it so it remains extended and place it on the heat with 30 grams of butter, season with salt and pepper and brown it somewhat, then finish cooking it with meat sauce or broth poured in gradually.
3. When cooked, strain the remaining sauce and reduce it on the heat with a small piece of butter and less than half a tablespoon of flour to bind it to the tongue.
4. Serve sliced and surrounded by boiled vegetables finished with butter and the sauce.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lingua di Vitella (Cured Veal Tongue)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal tongue from a calf or beef
- 20-30 grams saltpeter
- salt
- cold water
- 1 bouquet garni
- 1/2 onion studded with 2 cloves
- gelatin (recipe No. 3)

## Directions

1. Take a veal tongue and rub it thoroughly with 20-30 grams of saltpeter depending on size until it has drawn in the salt.
2. After twenty-four hours, wash it several times with cold water.
3. While still damp, rub it generously with salt and leave it for eight days, turning it each morning in the brine produced by the salt dissolving in water.
4. To cook, place it in a pot with cold water, its natural brine, a bouquet garni, and half an onion studded with two cloves.
5. Boil for three or four hours.
6. Peel it while still boiling, let it cool, and serve it.
7. It will be an excellent and refined cold dish if you surround it with gelatin (recipe No. 3).
8. It can also be served hot, alone or accompanied by potatoes or spinach.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lingua di Vitella di Latte in Salsa Piccante (Milk-fed Veal Tongue in Spicy Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole milk-fed veal tongue
- salt for cooking water
- celery
- carrot
- oil
- 2 salted anchovies
- 50 grams capers
- parsley
- 1 piece of bread crumb about the size of an egg
- vinegar
- onion about the size of a hazelnut
- less than half a clove of garlic
- lemon juice from half a lemon
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the whole veal tongue in salted water, which will take about two hours.
2. Make a fine battutino (minced mixture) of celery and carrot, bring it to boil with a good measure of oil for five minutes and set aside.
3. Make another battuto with two salted anchovies that have been washed and cleaned of their bones, 50 grams of capers squeezed of vinegar, a good pinch of parsley, a piece of bread crumb about the size of an egg barely dampened in vinegar, onion about the size of a hazelnut, less than half a clove of garlic.
4. When everything is well chopped, work it with a knife blade and a drop of oil to make it united and pasty, then mix it with the previous celery and carrot battuto.
5. To make it liquid, add more oil and the juice of half a lemon, season with pepper and salt if needed. This is the sauce.
6. While the tongue is still hot, peel it, discard the papilla with its small bones, and cut the rest of the tongue into thin slices.
7. Cover the slices with the described sauce and serve it cold.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lingua Dolce-Forte (Veal Tongue in Sweet-Strong Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole veal tongue with its pappagorgia (soft palate), from milk-fed calf
- water for boiling
- as for wild boar sweet-strong sauce: raisins, chocolate, pine nuts, candied fruit, sugar, vinegar

## Directions

1. Skin the tongue and partially boil it. For the rest, proceed as for the wild boar (recipe N. 285), using the water in which it boiled to finish cooking it.
2. To skin the tongue, heat a pallet and place it on top, repeating the operation several times if necessary.
3. Prepare the sweet-strong sauce as indicated in recipe N. 285 and incorporate it into the cooking liquid.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lingua in Umido (Braised Beef Tongue)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One beef tongue, without the papilla, weighing approximately one kilogram
- Ham, fatty and lean, 50 grams
- Half of a medium-sized onion
- Celery
- Carrot
- Parsley
- Butter, 50 grams
- Salt and pepper
- Broth
- Tomato juice or conserve
- Butter, 20 grams
- Flour, one level spoonful
- Celery or other greens (for garnish)

## Directions

1. Take a beef tongue and boil it just enough to be able to peel it
2. Make a generous soffritto with 50 grams of ham (fatty and lean), half a medium onion, celery, carrot and parsley, and place on heat with 50 grams of butter together with the tongue seasoned with salt and pepper
3. Once browned, finish cooking it with broth poured little by little and tomato juice or conserve; then strain the sauce
4. Separately make a beurre manié with 20 grams of butter and one level spoonful of flour
5. When it has taken on a hazelnut color, pour the sauce into it and return the tongue to it for further cooking over low heat
6. Serve it cut into thick slices about one centimeter with a garnish of celery or other vegetables finished in the same sauce
7. A dish that will serve seven or eight people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lingue di Gatto (Cat Tongues)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 100 grams
- White powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Hungarian flour, 100 grams
- One egg white

## Directions

1. Place the butter in a bowl just as it is and begin to stir it with a spoon
2. Then pour in the sugar, then the flour and lastly the egg white, always working the mixture to reduce it to a homogeneous paste
3. Place it in a syringe with a disc with a round or square hole about one centimeter in size, and push it into a pan lightly greased with butter in the form of pieces one finger long, keeping them spaced out because, as they melt, they spread
4. Bake them in a country oven at moderate heat. With this dose you will obtain about fifty
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lombata di Castrato Ripiena (Stuffed Mutton Loin)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece of mutton loin with its attached membrane, weighing 1 kilogram
- 150 grams lean veal from milk-fed calf
- 50 grams fat and lean prosciutto
- 40 grams grated Parmesan
- 1 egg
- Salt and pepper
- 50 grams butter
- A finger (measure) of Marsala
- 1 small onion, cut in two pieces
- 2 or 3 pieces of celery
- 2 or 3 pieces of carrot
- Parsley stems
- Water or broth

## Directions

1. Take a piece of mutton loin with its attached membrane, weighing 1 kilogram, degrease it somewhat but not completely, bone it and season with salt and pepper
2. Prepare the filling by mixing together 150 grams lean veal from milk-fed calf, 50 grams fat and lean prosciutto, 40 grams grated Parmesan, 1 egg, salt and pepper
3. Mince the filling very fine
4. Spread this filling all over the inside of the loin
5. Roll it up, pulling the membrane over it, and sew it so the filling does not burst out
6. Place it on the fire with 50 grams of butter and when browned, moisten it with a finger measure of Marsala
7. Add to the pan to braise slowly with it: half a fairly small onion cut into two pieces, 2 or 3 pieces of celery, the same amount of carrot, and parsley stems
8. Moisten with water or broth to complete the cooking
9. Finally strain the sauce and the rest, degrease it and serve it
10. This is a dish that can serve eight people and deserves to be recommended
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Lombo di Maiale Ripieno (Stuffed Pork Loin)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork loin, 1 kilogram
- Pork caul, 100 grams
- Lean milk-fed veal, 100 grams
- Fat and lean prosciutto, 50 grams
- Mortadella, 50 grams
- Bone marrow, 30 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 30 grams
- One egg yolk
- Nutmeg flavor to taste

## Directions

1. Brown the milk-fed veal in butter and mince both this and the prosciutto and mortadella with a knife and then pound them in a mortar to reduce them to a fine paste. Pour this paste onto the cutting board, add to it the marrow, the Parmesan and the egg yolk, season sparingly with salt, pepper and nutmeg, and with the blade of a knife reduce it to a uniform paste. Now remove the superficial fat from the loin, debone it and then cut it into seven or eight scaloppine, but in such a way that they all remain joined at the base so that they can be opened like the pages of a book and spread a spoonful of the said paste over each one; then join them together to form a roll that you will sprinkle with salt and pepper and tie tightly with string.
2. Cover it with the pork caul, binding it with thread so that it adheres well, and cook it at low heat in a pot with nothing else. Three hours of cooking should be sufficient and it will serve eight people. It is a good dish, both hot and cold, and not heavy; but if served hot you can send it to the table accompanied by vegetables reheated in its fat. To cut it into slices one must carve it not in the direction of the divisions, but the opposite, which will make it look fine.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Luccio in Umido (Pike in Braise)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike weighing approximately 600-700 grams
- Lard strips seasoned with salt and pepper
- Onion about the size of a large walnut
- Small garlic clove
- Celery rib
- Small piece of carrot
- Pinch of parsley
- Oil
- Tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in water
- Salt and pepper
- Butter
- Flour
- Marsala or wine

## Directions

1. Scale the pike, clean it, remove the head and tail, and divide it into four or five pieces
2. Stud each piece lengthwise with two lard strips seasoned with salt and pepper
3. Make a proportionate battute with onion the size of a large walnut, a small garlic clove, a celery rib, a small piece of carrot, and a pinch of parsley, all finely chopped
4. Put the battute in a pan with oil and when it takes color, stop it with tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in water, salt and pepper to season
5. Thicken this sauce somewhat with a small piece of butter mixed with flour, stir well and place the fish in it, letting it boil slowly and turning it
6. Finally add a spoonful of Marsala or, if lacking, a splash of wine, and let it boil a little more before serving in the middle of its sauce
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Maccheroni alla Bolognese (Bolognese Macaroni)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal meat (preferably from the fillet), 150 grams
- Dried cured meat (carnesecca), 50 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- One quarter of a common onion
- Half a carrot
- Two stalks of white celery about one palm in length, or the aroma of green celery
- A pinch of flour, but very scarce
- A small pot of broth
- Very little salt or none, due to the cured meat and broth which are flavorful
- Pepper and, if desired, the aroma of nutmeg
- Parmesan cheese
- Macaroni pasta, 500 grams or more

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small dice, finely mince with a half-moon knife the cured meat, onion and aromatics, then put everything together over heat, including the butter
2. When the meat has taken on color, add the pinch of flour, moistening with broth until fully cooked
3. Drain the macaroni well from water and season it with parmesan and this sauce
4. The sauce can be made more agreeable with pieces of dried mushrooms or some slices of truffles, or with a chicken liver cooked among the meat and cut into pieces
5. Finally, when the sauce is done, if you wish to make them even more delicate, add half a glass of cream
6. Serve the macaroni not dry but swimming in a little sauce
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Maccheroni alla francese (Macaroni French Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams long Neapolitan macaroni
- 70 grams butter
- 70 grams Gruyère cheese
- 40 grams parmesan cheese
- one small pot of broth

## Directions

1. Give the macaroni two-thirds cooking in not too salted water
2. Place the broth on the fire and when it boils throw in the grated Gruyère and butter to dissolve them well with a spoon
3. Once obtained, pour it immediately over the macaroni already drained of water, and I say immediately, because otherwise the Gruyère sinks to the bottom and becomes pasty
4. Keep the macaroni on the fire until completely cooked making sure some sauce remains
5. When you remove it, season it with the aforesaid parmesan and serve it with more parmesan on the side, for those who not having delicate taste love spiciness
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Maccheroni alla napoletana I (Neapolitan Macaroni I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of meat from the loin
- slices of fatty and lean prosciutto
- raisins
- pine nuts
- lard
- garlic
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- three or four pieces of peeled tomato
- tomato sauce
- water
- tomato paste (if fresh tomatoes unavailable)

## Directions

1. Take a piece of meat from the loin and lard it with slices of fatty and lean prosciutto, raisins, pine nuts and with a small mixture of lard, garlic, parsley, salt and pepper
2. Once the meat is prepared in this manner and tied with string to keep it more united, place it on the fire with a soffritto of finely chopped lard and onion
3. Turn it often and pierce it from time to time with a larding needle
4. Once the meat is browned and the soffritto consumed, add three or four pieces of peeled tomato and when these are destroyed, gradually add tomato sauce
5. Wait for this to be somewhat reduced, then pour in enough water to cover the piece, season with salt and pepper and let boil on low heat
6. In the absence of fresh tomatoes use paste
7. Season the macaroni with the sauce and spicy cheese, as the Neapolitans do, and the meat serves as a side dish
8. Boil the macaroni in a wide vessel with plenty of water and do not cook them too much
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Maccheroni alla napoletana II (Neapolitan Macaroni II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams long macaroni
- two large slices of onion
- 30 grams butter
- two tablespoons oil
- 500 grams tomatoes
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- 50 grams raw butter
- parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. In a pan or pot, brown two large slices of onion with 30 grams of butter and two tablespoons of oil
2. When the onion, which naturally breaks apart while boiling, is well browned, squeeze it with a spoon and throw it away
3. In that fat at boiling point pour 500 grams of tomatoes and a good pinch of coarsely chopped basil; season with salt and pepper
4. Prepare the tomatoes beforehand because they must be peeled, cut into pieces and cleaned of seeds as much as possible
5. Season the macaroni with the condensed sauce, with 50 grams of raw butter and parmesan and send them to the table
6. These will be especially appreciated by those who would swim in tomato sauce
7. Instead of long macaroni, penne can be used, indeed these will take the sauce better
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Maccheroni col Pangrattato (Macaroni with Breadcrumbs)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Long macaroni that hold up well to cooking, 300 grams
- Flour, 15 grams
- Butter, 60 grams
- Gruyère cheese, 60 grams
- Parmesan cheese, 40 grams
- Milk, 6 deciliters
- Breadcrumbs, as needed

## Directions

1. Give the macaroni half cooking time, salt them and pour them onto a strainer to drain.
2. Put half the butter and the flour in a pot on the fire, stirring continuously; when the flour begins to take on color, pour the milk a little at a time and let it boil for about ten minutes.
3. Then throw the macaroni and grated or diced Gruyère into this béchamel and move the pot to the edge of the stove so that, simmering gently, they absorb the milk.
4. Then add the rest of the butter and grated Parmesan; pour them into a fireproof dish on which they make a heap and cover them all with breadcrumbs.
5. Prepared in this manner, put them in a country oven or under an iron lid with fire on top and when they are browned, serve them hot as a course or, better, accompanied by a plate of meat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Maccheroni colla Balsamella (Macaroni with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Long Neapolitan-style macaroni
- Butter
- Salted water
- Milk
- Béchamel sauce
- Egg yolk
- Grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Take long Neapolitan-style macaroni and cook them two-thirds through in salted water.
2. Remove them dry and return them to the fire with a piece of butter and when they have absorbed it, add enough milk to finish cooking them over moderate heat.
3. Meanwhile prepare béchamel sauce as indicated, and when it is no longer boiling bind it with an egg yolk and then pour it over the macaroni together with grated Parmesan in proportion.
4. Macaroni prepared this way are very suitable as an accompaniment to a piece of stewed beef or a piece of milk veal in fricandeau.
5. In this case you can take a tray that can withstand fire, place a metal form in the middle, and the macaroni around it.
6. Place the tray in a country oven or under an iron lid.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Maccheroni con le Sarde alla Siciliana (Sicilian Macaroni with Sardines)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Long macaroni Neapolitan style, 500 grams
- Fresh sardines, 500 grams
- Salted anchovies, 6 pieces
- Wild fennel (called young fennel), 300 grams
- Oil, as needed

## Directions

1. Remove the head, tail and spine from the sardines, dividing them in two parts, flour them, fry them, salt them somewhat and set them aside
2. Boil the fennel, squeeze out the water, mince them finely and set them aside
3. After cooking the macaroni whole in salted water, drain them well and set these aside as well
4. Put abundant oil in a pan over heat and in it dissolve the six anchovies, after having cleaned them and removed the spine
5. Pour into this sauce the fennel, season with little salt and pepper and let them boil for ten minutes with tomato juice or conserve dissolved in water
6. Now that you have everything ready, take a plate that can withstand heat or a baking dish and season the macaroni layer by layer with the sardines and with the anchovy-fennel sauce so that they form a mound
7. Brown them between two fires and serve them hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Macedonia (Mixed Fruit Ice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ribes (currants)
- Fragole (strawberries)
- Lampone (raspberries)
- Ciliege (cherries)
- Susine (plums)
- Albicocche (apricots)
- Una pesca (one peach)
- Una pera (one pear)
- Zucchero (sugar), 100 grams for 500 grams of fruit
- Sugo di un limone di giardino (juice of one garden lemon)

## Directions

1. Take many varieties of fruit of the season, ripe and of good quality
2. Beginning with the cherries, peel all the said fruits and cut them into thin slices like pumpkin seeds approximately, throwing away the cores and pits
3. Use very little currant because it has seeds that are too large and hard; instead it would be good to add some fragrant melon
4. Having prepared the fruit in this manner, weigh it and, assuming it is in total 500 grams, scatter over it 100 grams of powdered sugar and the juice of one garden lemon
5. Mix them and let them rest for half an hour
6. Place a sheet of paper at the bottom of the said vessel, fill it by spreading the fruit pressed somewhat, close it and place it in a bucket surrounded by ice and salt, which should remain completely covered for several hours
7. If it does not unmold naturally, wet it with hot water and serve it as you will see it makes a fine appearance as a hard frozen and marbled piece
8. This is a dose for four or five persons
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Maiale Arrostito nel Latte (Pork Roasted in Milk)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of pork loin weighing approximately 500 grams
- 2.5 deciliters of milk
- salt
- fresh milk

## Directions

1. Take a piece of pork loin weighing approximately 500 grams, salt it and place it in a pot with 2.5 deciliters of milk.
2. Cover it and let it boil slowly until the milk is consumed.
3. Then increase the heat to brown it, and once this is done, drain away the fat and remove the piece of meat.
4. Add a little fresh milk to the remaining coagulated milk residue.
5. Mix, bring to a boil and use it to spread on slices of lightly toasted bread to serve as an accompaniment to the pork when serving hot at table.
6. A total of three deciliters of milk will suffice. Cooked this way the pork will have a delicate flavor and will not be cloying.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Malfattini (Simple Pasta Bits)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Eggs
- Parmesan cheese (optional)
- Spices (optional)
- Breadcrumbs (optional alternative)

## Directions

1. Mix flour with eggs and work with hands on a work surface to form a firm dough
2. Cut into thick slices about half a finger thick and let them dry in the air
3. Chop finely with a half-moon chopper until reduced to tiny pieces about half the size of a grain of rice
4. Pass through a sieve to obtain uniform pieces, or grate from whole bread
5. Keep pieces very small for easier digestion
6. Can alternatively be made with breadcrumbs, optionally enriched with a pinch of Parmesan cheese and a hint of spices
7. Can be combined with peas, chopped beets, or both
8. Add to boiling broth to serve
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Mandorle Tostate (Toasted Almonds)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet almonds, 200 grams
- Sugar, 200 grams
- Water

## Directions

1. Rub the almonds with a cloth
2. Put the sugar in a non-tinned pan on the fire with two fingers' width of water and when it is dissolved pour in the almonds, stirring constantly
3. When you feel them crackling remove the pan to the edge of the stove and you will see the sugar thicken and become grainy
4. Lift it out and separate the almonds from the sugar
5. Put half of this sugar back on the fire with another two fingers' width of water and when it smells of caramel pour in the said almonds, stir and when they have taken on the sugar remove them
6. Put the other half of the remaining sugar on the fire with another two fingers' width of water and repeat for the third time, which will be the last
7. Pour the almonds onto a plate and separate those that have stuck together
8. They are very good without any flavoring, but if you like you can give them the fragrance of vanilla with vanillin sugar, or the taste of chocolate with 30 grams of this grated
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Manicaretto di piccioni (Pigeon Stew)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Ham
- Butter
- Herb bouquet
- Salt and pepper
- Broth
- Squab giblets
- Sweetbreads
- Fresh mushrooms or dried mushrooms
- Truffles
- White wine
- Flour
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Cut them into quarters or large pieces at the joints and put them on the fire with a slice of ham, a small piece of butter, and a garnished herb bouquet, seasoning them with salt and pepper.
2. When they begin to dry, wet them with broth and, at half cooking, add their giblets, sweetbreads in pieces, and fresh mushrooms cut into slices, or also dried ones but first softened in hot water, or truffles; these however should be put in near the end of cooking.
3. After wetting them with broth, pour in, if you have two squabs, half a glass of white wine that you will have first reduced by half on the fire in a separate vessel.
4. Continue to simmer gently, then add another small piece of butter mixed in flour, or flour alone, to thicken the sauce, and lastly, before sending them to the table, remove the ham and bouquet, and squeeze a lemon over the squabs.
5. Blanch the sweetbreads first and skin them if they are from a large animal.
6. In this same way young chickens can be cooked, garnishing them with giblets instead of sweetbreads.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Marena (Black Cherry Syrup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- True marasca cherries, very sour even when mature
- Cinnamon stick, one large piece
- Depurated juice, 6 kilograms
- Whitest powdered sugar, 8 kilograms
- Citric acid, 50 grams
- Cherry pits, a handful reserved, 30 grams of kernels extracted

## Directions

1. Take the marasca cherries and remove the stems and crush them like grapes when pressed to make wine.
2. Set aside a handful of pits for later use and place the cherries with a large piece of whole cinnamon in a cool place, inside an earthen vessel to await fermentation, which must last at least forty-eight hours.
3. From the moment the cherries have begun to rise, press them down and mix them from time to time.
4. Extract the juice by squeezing the cherries a few at a time through a thin cloth.
5. When the juice has rested, decant the clear part, and pass the remaining part several times through a wool filter.
6. Once the liquid is purified, put it on the fire in the following proportions with the remembered piece of cinnamon.
7. For pouring the sugar and citric acid wait until the liquid is very hot and then stir often so that the sugar does not settle at the bottom and does not scorch.
8. The boiling must be brief; four or five minutes are sufficient to incorporate the sugar into the liquid.
9. Do not boil for too long as prolonged boiling would cause the fruit to lose its aroma, while insufficient boiling would produce sugar settling over time.
10. When you remove the marena from the fire, pour it into an earthen vessel and bottle it while cold.
11. Cork the bottles with cork without tar and keep them in the cellar where both the marena and the syrups will remain unchanged for several years.
12. For the kernels recalled: dry them in the sun, then crush them and extract 30 grams of almonds, which you will pound very finely in a mortar and mix with the cherries before fermentation; with their pleasant bitter taste these almonds serve to give more elegance to the syrup.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Marena da Trastullarsi (Preserved Black Cherries to Eat)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Marasca cherries, 1,500 kilograms
- Finest powdered sugar, 2 kilograms
- Cinnamon stick, one small piece

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries and keep them in the sun for five or six hours.
2. Then put them on the fire with a small piece of cinnamon and when they have released a certain amount of moisture, pour the sugar into them, being careful to stir slowly to keep the cherries whole.
3. When they have become wrinkled and have taken on a brown color remove them and use them for the intended purpose.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Mele all'Inglese (English-style Apples)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rose apples or other firm variety
- Water, sufficient to cook them
- Cinnamon, a small piece
- Sugar, as needed
- Candied fruit, chopped

## Directions

1. Take firm apples, remove the core with a tin corer, peel them and cut them into thin round slices
2. Put them on the fire with enough water to cook them and a small piece of cinnamon
3. When half cooked, add enough sugar to make them sweet and some chopped candied fruit
4. Take a copper plate or porcelain serving dish that can withstand heat, pour them into it, cover with puff pastry, put them in the oven or in a country oven, and serve them hot as a sweet course
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Mele in gelatina (Apples in Jelly)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Reinette apples or other fine quality, not too ripe, 650-700 grams
- Sugar, 120 grams
- Water, half a litre
- Kirsch, one spoonful
- Lemon, half
- Currant jelly (recipe 739)

## Directions

1. Remove the cores from the apples using a tin tube, peel them and immediately place them in fresh water where half a lemon has been squeezed
2. Dissolve the sugar in half a litre of water on the fire, united with a spoonful of kirsch, and pour it over the apples that you will have placed all together in a saucepan
3. Cook them so that they remain whole, then remove them dry and place them in a fruit dish
4. When cooled, fill and top the holes left by the cores with Currant Jelly (recipe 739), which being red stands out against the white of the apples and makes them beautiful
5. Reduce the remaining liquid in the saucepan over the fire to make a syrup, then pass it through a damp cloth, add another spoonful of kirsch and pour it around the prepared apples, which you will serve cooled
6. If you do not have kirsch, use rosolio and if you lack currant jelly, substitute with preserves
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Migliaccio di Farina Dolce (Chestnut Flour Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnut flour, 500 grams
- salt
- water, 8 decilitres
- pine nuts, one handful
- walnuts
- raisins
- rosemary leaves
- oil

## Directions

1. Pass the chestnut flour through a sieve to make it soft since it clumps easily
2. Put it in a container and season with a scant pinch of salt
3. Mix it with 8 decilitres of cold water poured gradually to make a liquid batter
4. Add a handful of whole pine nuts
5. Some add walnut pieces and raisins with a few rosemary leaves on top
6. Take a baking pan where the migliaccio will be about one and a half fingers thick
7. Cover the bottom with a light layer of oil
8. Spread two spoonfuls of oil over the batter when it is in the pan
9. Bake in an oven or between two fires
10. Turn it out hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Migliaccio di Farina Gialla I (Polenta Cake I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cornmeal flour
- salt
- boiling water
- dried grapes or raisins
- lard
- rosemary sprigs

## Directions

1. Put whatever amount of cornmeal flour you wish to use in a container
2. Salt it well and mix it firmly with boiling water
3. When mixed so no dry flour remains at the bottom of the vessel, add dried grapes or raisins in proper amount
4. Take a copper pan and place it on the fire with abundant virgin lard
5. When the lard begins to crackle, pour in the mixture, which because it is thick must be spread and leveled with a spoon
6. Spread the surface with a little more lard and sprinkle with sprigs of fresh rosemary
7. Bake in an oven or between two fires until it browns somewhat
8. Turn it out
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Migliaccio di Farina Gialla II (Polenta Cake II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cornmeal flour, 300 grams
- raisins or dried grapes, 100 grams
- lard, 40 grams
- pine nuts, 30 grams
- sugar, three teaspoons

## Directions

1. Remove seeds from the raisins
2. Cut the pine nuts in half lengthwise
3. Grease the pan with lard and dust it with flour
4. Proceed as for the previous recipe
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Migliaccio di Romagna (Pork Blood Cake from Romagna)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 7 decilitres
- Pork blood, melted, 330 grams
- Sapa or fine honey, 200 grams
- Sweet blanched almonds, 100 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Very fine breadcrumbs, 80 grams
- Candied fruit, 50 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Fine spices, two teaspoons
- Chocolate, 100 grams
- Nutmeg, one teaspoon
- One strip of lemon zest

## Directions

1. Pound the almonds in a mortar together with the candied fruit, which you will have first cut into small pieces, moisten them from time to time with a few spoonfuls of milk and pass them through a sieve.
2. Place the milk on the fire with the lemon zest, which is then to be removed, and let it boil for ten minutes; add the grated chocolate to it, and when this is melted, remove it from the fire and let it cool a little.
3. Then pour the blood, already passed through a sieve, into the same pot along with all the other ingredients, reserving the breadcrumbs for last, of which, if there is too much, you can leave some behind.
4. Put the mixture to cook in a bain-marie and stir it often with a spoon so it does not stick to the pot. The cooking and the degree of density required are known from the spoon which, left in the middle of the mixture, must remain upright. If this does not happen, add the rest of the breadcrumbs, supposing you have not poured it all in.
5. Pour it into a pan lined with Pasta matta and, when it is completely cooled, cut it into almond shapes. Cook the pasta matta briefly so you can cut it easily and do not let the migliaccio dry out over the fire, but remove it when a pomegranate twig immersed in it comes out clean.
6. If you use honey instead of sapa, taste before adding the sugar so it does not turn out too sweet, and note that one of the merits of this dish is that it be well blended, that is, of fine composition.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra del Paradiso (Paradise Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons fine breadcrumbs from hard bread
- 4 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese
- nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Whip the egg whites until stiff, then incorporate the yolks
2. Add to this mixture four tablespoons not quite heaping of fine breadcrumbs from hard bread, the same amount of grated Parmesan cheese, and the aroma of nutmeg
3. Mix gently so that the mixture remains light and drop it into boiling broth by teaspoons
4. Boil for seven or eight minutes and serve
5. This amount is sufficient for six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra del Paradiso (Soup of Paradise)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg whites, 4
- Egg yolks, 4
- Fine breadcrumbs of hard bread, 4 heaping spoonfuls
- Parmigiano cheese, grated, 4 heaping spoonfuls
- Nutmeg, to taste

## Directions

1. Beat four egg whites until stiff, incorporate the yolks into them, then pour four heaping spoonfuls of fine breadcrumbs of hard bread into it, as much grated parmigiano and the hint of nutmeg.
2. Mix gently so the mixture remains soft and throw it into boiling broth by spoonfuls.
3. Make it boil for seven or eight minutes and serve it.
4. This dose may be enough for six people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di bomboline di patate (Potato Puffs Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, 500 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 40 grams
- Egg yolks, 3
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes in water or, better, steamed, peel them, pass them hot through a sieve and salt them.
2. Add the aforementioned ingredients and work them somewhat.
3. Spread a veil of flour on the work surface and pour the mixture onto it so you can draw it into sticks without the flour penetrating inside, and with these form balls about the size of hazelnuts.
4. Fry them in oil or lard where they can swim around and place them in a tureen pouring boiling broth over them.
5. This dose will serve eight or ten persons.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di bomboline di riso (Rice Puffs Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 100 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 20 grams
- One egg yolk
- Nutmeg
- Salt, to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the rice very well and firm in milk (half a liter will do); before removing it from the fire add the butter and salt and when it is no longer boiling add the rest.
2. For the rest proceed as with the previous recipe.
3. These puffs turn out tastier than those made of potatoes.
4. This dose will serve six persons.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di Carne Passata (Strained Meat Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 grams lean veal
- 25 grams fatty prosciutto
- 25 grams grated Parmesan cheese
- 2 tablespoons pap made with bread marrow, water and a small piece of butter
- 1 egg
- nutmeg to taste
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. First mince the meat and prosciutto with a knife by chopping, then with a crescent-shaped blade, then pound them in a mortar and pass through a sieve
2. Make a paste with the egg and the other ingredients
3. When the broth boils, drop it in by teaspoons or pass it through a syringe to give it a graceful shape, and after sufficient boiling to cook it, serve the soup
4. This quantity is enough for four or five people, but you can also make it serve twelve by mixing it in a soup. Take the finest bread from the day before, cut it into small dice and quickly brown it in a pan with plenty of fat. When you are about to serve, place the said bread in the tureen and pour the above-described strained meat soup over it
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di due colori (Two-Color Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 180 grams
- Butter, 60 grams
- Parmesan, 40 grams
- Milk, 4 decilitres
- Eggs, two whole and two yolks
- Salt, to taste
- Nutmeg
- A handful of spinach

## Directions

1. Boil the spinach, squeeze it well of water and pass it through a sieve.
2. Put the butter on the fire and when dissolved throw in the flour mixing well; then pour in the hot milk little by little, salt it and while it cooks work it with a spoon to make a homogeneous paste.
3. Remove it and when it is lukewarm thin it with the eggs adding the Parmesan and nutmeg.
4. Then divide this mixture into two equal parts, in one of which you will mix the said spinach in sufficient quantity to make it take on a green color and no more.
5. Place the mixture in a syringe with the round-hole stamp and push it into boiling broth like the passatelli of No. 48; but this operation must be done in two times, first with the yellow mixture and then with the green.
6. This dose will be sufficient for eight or ten persons.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di Erbe Passate (Strained Herb Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bunch beet greens
- 1 bunch spinach
- 1 head of lettuce
- 1 wedge of cabbage
- 1/4 onion
- parsley
- celery
- carrot
- basil or dill
- butter
- tomatoes
- 1 potato
- salt
- pepper
- water
- rice or bread for soup
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Remove the coarse ribs from the beet greens, cut all the greens roughly and soak them in fresh water for several hours.
2. Make a mixture with a quarter onion and all the herbs (parsley, celery, carrot and some basil or dill leaves); put it in a pan with a piece of butter and when well colored, add the herbs (drained somewhat), together with some tomatoes cut into pieces and a potato sliced.
3. Season with salt and pepper and let boil, stirring often.
4. When the greens are wilted, add hot water and cook until completely disintegrated.
5. Pass through a strainer, keeping the strained liquid for cooking rice or for wetting soup.
6. Before serving, taste it and add seasoning, especially butter, which will almost always be necessary.
7. Serve with Parmesan cheese on the side, but avoid making it too thick so it does not resemble plaster.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di Krapfen (Krapfen Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Hungarian flour, 100 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Beer yeast, the size of a walnut
- Eggs, 1
- Salt, a pinch

## Directions

1. Roll out the dough to a sheet slightly less than half a finger thick, cut it with a tin tube of the marked diameter to make like so many pastilles which you will set to rise.
2. You will see them grow in the form of little balls and then fry them in oil, if you have excellent oil, otherwise in lard or butter.
3. When you are about to send to table place them in the soup tureen and pour boiling broth over them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di latte composta (Composed Milk Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 60 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- Parmesan, 30 grams
- Milk, 4 decilitres
- Eggs, 4
- Salt, to taste
- Nutmeg, if desired

## Directions

1. Put the butter on the fire and as soon as it is melted pour in the flour.
2. Stir, and when it begins to take color pour in the milk little by little.
3. Let it boil for a while, then remove the mixture from the fire and season it by adding the eggs last when it is cooled.
4. Cook it in a bain-marie as with semolino soup No. 15 and proceed as with the same.
5. This dose will serve eight or ten persons.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di Mattoncini di Ricotta (Ricotta Cubes Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams ricotta
- 30 grams grated Parmesan cheese
- 2 eggs
- salt to taste
- lemon zest and nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Break up the ricotta by passing it through a sieve, add the rest and the eggs one at a time
2. Mix well and pour the mixture into a smooth mold to cook it in a bain-marie
3. Unmold it when cool, remove the paper with which you will have covered the bottom of the mold and cut it into cubes of approximately one centimeter in size
4. Place them then in the tureen, pour boiling broth over them and send them to the table
5. This amount will be enough for five or six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di Mille Fanti (Thousand Soldiers Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- half an egg per person
- flour equal in tablespoons to the number of eggs
- grated Parmesan cheese
- nutmeg to taste
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Take a pot and at the bottom of it place as many heaping tablespoons of flour as there are eggs; add grated Parmesan, nutmeg aroma, a pinch of salt and finally the eggs
2. Beat everything together very well and pour the mixture into the broth when it boils, passing it through a tin strainer with large holes, stirring the broth at the same time
3. Let it boil somewhat and serve
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di mille fanti (Thousand Soldiers Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Half an egg per person
- Flour (as many teaspoons full as there are eggs)
- Grated Parmesan cheese
- Nutmeg
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place as many teaspoons full of flour as there are eggs in the bottom of a pot.
2. Add grated Parmesan, nutmeg, a pinch of salt, and lastly the eggs.
3. Beat everything together very well and pour the mixture into boiling broth, passing it through a colander with large holes while stirring the broth at the same time.
4. Let it boil for a while and serve.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di Nocciuole di Semolino (Semolina Hazelnut Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 deciliters milk
- 100 grams semolina
- 20 grams grated Parmesan cheese
- 1 whole egg and 1 yolk
- butter the size of a walnut
- salt to taste
- flour to taste
- nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Put the milk on the fire with butter and when it boils pour in the semolina little by little
2. Salt it; when it is cooked and still hot, but not boiling, beat the eggs into it, add the Parmesan and the aroma and stir
3. Let it cool well and then pour it onto a board over a layer of flour
4. Roll it lightly on the same flour pulling out a small stick which you will cut into equal pieces to make as many balls the size of a hazelnut
5. Drop them into the broth when it boils and shortly after, pour them into the tureen and send them to the table
6. This amount can be sufficient for five or six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Minestra di pane angelico (Angelic Bread Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Crumb of fine bread, 150 grams
- Fat and lean ham, 50 grams
- Beef marrow, 40 grams
- Parmesan, 40 grams
- Flour, as needed
- Eggs, 2 minus one white
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Soak the bread crumb with boiling broth just enough to soak it well and squeeze it hard in a cloth.
2. Mince the ham very finely; flatten the beef marrow with the flat blade of a knife, and stir it with the blade until it becomes like a salve.
3. Mix these three things together with the Parmesan and add the eggs.
