2. Mince with a crescent knife the 120 grams of lean meat together with the 20 grams of ham, then pound them in the mortar adding the Parmesan, butter, egg, little salt and little pepper to make from these ingredients the compound to keep together the filling you will form as follows.
3. Cut into strips more than one centimeter thick the chicken breast and the two slices of mortadella and ham.
4. Spread the compound on one part of the braciole and on top of it place one third of the fillets, intercalating them, then spread more on top and thus for two more times.
5. Roll up the braciole with the filling inside and tie it like a salami to put it on the fire with 30 grams of butter, salt and pepper in scant measure.
6. When it has taken color, drain off the fat, which can be used for some other dish, and continue cooking for about three hours with broth added little by little.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Ripieno di Carne per Pasticcini di Pasta Sfoglia (Meat Filling for Puff Pastry Pastries)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Animelle (calf sweetbreads) or milk veal or chicken livers
- Butter, a small piece
- Salt and pepper
- Sauce N. 4 (as needed for cooking)
- Béchamel sauce N. 137, 1 or 2 spoonfuls
- Salted tongue or prosciutto (fat and lean), finely diced
- Parmigiano cheese, a pinch
- Nutmeg, a pinch
- Truffles (in season, for flavoring)

## Directions

1. If using animelle, place them in a pan with a small piece of butter, season with salt and pepper, and when they take on color, finish cooking them with sauce N. 4
2. Cut the cooked animelle to the size of a chickpea or smaller
3. To the animelle add 1 or 2 spoonfuls of béchamel sauce N. 137, some salted tongue or finely diced prosciutto (fat and lean), a pinch of parmigiano, and a pinch of nutmeg
4. Ensure the ingredients are in proportions that render the mixture of pleasant and delicate flavor
5. Allow the mixture to cool thoroughly so it becomes firm and is easier to work with
6. To enclose it in puff pastry N. 154, you may cook the pastry with the filling in the middle (placing the filling in the center of a disc, moistening the edges with a wet finger, covering with another similar disc and cooking), or fill it after cooking
7. For the second method, unite two discs without filling; in the upper disc, before placing it, cut a small circle the size of a 10-centesimi coin using a metal cutter
8. The pastry will naturally puff during cooking and leave a hollow inside; lift the incised circle with the tip of a knife (it will be shaped like a small lid), optionally enlarge the hollow, fill it, and replace the lid
9. Before cooking, always brush the puff pastry surface with egg yolk
10. If filling a vol-au-vent, prepare a sauce instead with chicken giblets and sweetbreads, cut into large pieces
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Ripieno pei Polli (Filling for Chickens)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean milk-fed veal, approximately 100 grams, a small piece of veal breast and the giblets of the same chicken
- Small mixture of shallot or onion, parsley, celery, carrot and butter
- Salt, pepper and spices
- Broth
- Dried mushroom pieces, softened
- A slice of fatty and lean prosciutto
- Bread marrow for one spoonful of thick paste
- Good pinch of Parmesan cheese
- Two eggs

## Directions

1. Cook this meat together with a small mixture of shallot or onion, parsley, celery, carrot and butter. Season with salt, pepper and spices, moistening it with broth. Remove it dry from the heat, remove the tender parts from the gizzard, add several pieces of softened dried mushrooms, a slice of fatty and lean prosciutto and chop everything very finely with a chopper. Into the liquid remaining from cooking the meat, throw a piece of bread marrow to make one spoonful of thick paste. Mix it into the mixture, add a good pinch of Parmesan and two eggs, and fill the chicken with all of this and sew it shut. You can cook it boiled or braised. If you cook it boiled you will have excellent broth, but be careful to carve it to extract the filling all in one piece which you will then slice. Another filling for chickens is that of roasted chicken no. 539.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risi e Luganighe (Rice and Sausage)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Sausages (luganighe)
- Broth
- Turnip or cabbage
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Do not wash the rice, only clean it and rub with a cloth to remove dust
2. Cut the turnip into cubes or the cabbage into thin strips
3. Blanch the turnip or cabbage until half-cooked
4. Sauté the blanched vegetables in butter
5. Crumble the sausages and add to the broth as it cooks with the rice
6. Shortly before the rice is done, add a generous pinch of Parmesan cheese to bind it better and give it a more pleasant flavor
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Riso alla Cacciatora (Hunter's Rice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Lard
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Water
- Rice
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into pieces, excluding the head and feet
2. In a pan, make a soffritto with lard, garlic, and parsley until colored
3. Add a piece of butter and season with salt and pepper
4. Brown the chicken in this mixture
5. Pour into boiling water in a pot
6. Add the rice and cook
7. Before removing from heat, flavor with a good handful of Parmesan cheese
8. The rice should not be too watery nor too dry
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Riso con Zucchini (Rice with Zucchini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini, small ones of the same weight as the rice to be cooked
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Broth or water
- Tomato sauce (recipe N. 125), optional
- Parmesan

## Directions

1. Take small zucchini of the weight of the rice you will cook and cut them into chunks the size of hazelnuts
2. Put them to brown in butter, season with salt and pepper, and once just browned, throw them still firm into the rice when it will have reached half cooking, so they finish cooking together
3. The rice should remain somewhat brothy and the zucchini should not fall apart
4. Instead of broth you can use water and make it dry: but then give it flavor with tomato sauce N. 125, pour it also into the rice at half cooking, and with Parmesan
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Riso in Composta (Rice in Compote)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 7 deciliters
- Rice, 100 grams
- Sugar, 50 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Salt, a pinch
- Lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in six deciliters of milk and at half cooking time add the aforementioned ingredients into it
2. Stir it frequently with a spoon because it sticks easily and when it has reduced, remove it from the fire and add the deciliter of milk set aside
3. Take a smooth mold with a hole in the middle, into which the mixture comes at least two fingers high, and pour into the bottom and around 50 grams of sugar dissolved on the fire, where you will let it take on a light brown color
4. Pour the rice into this mold and return it to the fire in a water bath so it sets further and the sugar at the bottom dissolves
5. To unmold it, wait until it is cold
6. Now you must fill the hollow in the middle of the rice with a compote that can be made of any fruit; suppose we use apples or dried prunes
7. If apples, prefer rose apples which are hard and fragrant; 200 grams will suffice
8. Peel them, cut them into small wedges, removing the part of the core, and drop them as you peel them into fresh water in which lemon has been squeezed, to keep them white
9. Put these apples in a saucepan with just enough water to cover them and when they have begun to boil, pour in the sugar
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Riso per contorno (Rice as a Side Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- rice, 200 grams
- broth from boiled chicken or capon
- butter
- Parmesan cheese, small quantity
- egg or two egg yolks
- spinach, two or three spoonfuls, if making green rice variation

## Directions

1. Blanch the rice in water and finish cooking it with the broth from the boiled chicken or capon
2. Cook it firm and when almost done, flavor it with butter and Parmesan in small quantity
3. When you remove it from the fire, bind it with an egg or better with two yolks
4. If the rice is to serve as a side to braised veal or bracioline, add to the above ingredients two or three spoonfuls of cooked spinach passed through a strainer to make green rice
5. These side dishes can be given a better appearance by reducing the rice in a bain-marie within a mold, but be careful it does not become too hard, as that would be a serious defect
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto alla milanese I (Milanese Risotto I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 500 grams
- Butter, 80 grams
- Saffron, as much as needed to make it well yellow
- Half an onion of medium size
- Broth

## Directions

1. For cooking follow the method of No. 75
2. To make this risotto more substantial and more pleasing to the taste, broth is needed
3. If you have a bronze mortar at home, buy the saffron in its natural form, grind it fine and dissolve it in a few drops of hot broth before throwing it into the rice
4. Serve with Parmesan cheese
5. Saffron has an exciting action, stimulates the appetite and promotes digestion
6. This quantity can be enough for five people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto alla milanese II (Milanese Risotto II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 500 grams
- Butter, 80 grams
- Beef marrow, 40 grams
- Half an onion
- Good white wine, two-thirds of a glass
- Saffron, as needed
- Parmesan cheese, as needed
- Broth

## Directions

1. Chop the onion and put it on the fire with the marrow and half the butter
2. When well browned, pour in the rice and after a few minutes add the wine and cook it with broth
3. Before removing from the fire, season it with the other half of the butter and with Parmesan cheese and send it to the table with more Parmesan on the side
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto alla milanese III (Milanese Risotto III)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 300 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- One quarter of a medium-sized onion
- Marsala wine, two fingers of a common glass
- Saffron, as needed
- Parmesan cheese
- Broth

## Directions

1. Brown the finely chopped onion with half the butter
2. Pour in the rice and after a few minutes the Marsala
3. Cook it with broth and when cooked add the remaining butter and the saffron dissolved in a little broth
4. Finally add a handful of Parmesan cheese
5. Serves three people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto coi funghi (Risotto with Mushrooms)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Porcini mushrooms in nature, half the weight of the rice (to be cleaned and trimmed)
- A little onion, parsley, celery, carrot for a battuto
- Three tablespoons of oil, if the rice is 300 grams (to serve three people)
- Tomato juice and water
- Salt and pepper
- One clove of garlic, whole
- Butter, a piece
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Make a battuto with a little onion, parsley, celery, carrot and put it on the fire with three tablespoons of oil
2. When the battuto has taken color, stop it with tomato juice and water, season with salt and pepper, and boil a whole clove of garlic in it, which you will then discard before passing the soffritto
3. Put the soffritto back on the fire to cook the mushrooms, first chopped to a size slightly smaller than corn kernels
4. Once cooked, set them aside
5. Brown the raw rice with a piece of butter, then cook it with hot water poured one ladle at a time
6. At half cooking, mix the mushrooms into it and before serving give it flavor with Parmesan cheese
7. It will be willingly eaten also made with a handful of dried mushrooms instead of fresh ones
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto coi gamberi (Risotto with Shrimp)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams shrimp
- 700 grams rice
- half an onion
- three cloves garlic
- carrot
- celery
- parsley
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- tomato sauce or paste
- hot water
- butter
- parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Make an abundant soffritto with half an onion, three cloves of garlic, carrot, celery and parsley and place it on the fire with oil in proportion
2. When the soffritto has taken color, throw in the shrimp and season with salt and pepper
3. Turn them often and when all have become red, moisten them with tomato sauce or paste and shortly after pour in enough hot water to suffice for the rice
4. Let boil not too long, since shrimp cooks quickly, then remove them dried and set aside a quarter of the largest ones after shelling them
5. Pound the remaining shrimp in a mortar, pass them through a sieve and mix the strained pulp into the broth where they were cooked
6. Place a piece of butter on the fire in a pot and pour in the cleaned rice without washing it; stir continuously and when the rice has taken the sheen of the butter, pour in the broth hot little by little
7. At more than half cooking add the whole shelled shrimp and before serving give it grace with a handful of parmesan cheese
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto coi piselli (Risotto with Peas)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 500 grams
- Butter, 100 grams
- Parmesan cheese, as needed
- One onion of medium size
- Peas from recipe No. 427, in proper proportion

## Directions

1. Clean and rub the rice in a cloth without washing it
2. Finely chop the onion with a crescent knife and put it on the fire with half the butter
3. When it has taken on a red color, pour in the rice and stir constantly with a wooden spoon until it has absorbed all the soffritto
4. Begin pouring hot water one ladle at a time, being careful that if it boils too tightly it will become hard in the center and break apart on the surface
5. Salt it and cook until dry, adding the remaining butter
6. Before removing from the fire, add the peas in proper proportion and give it flavor with a good handful of Parmesan cheese
7. This portion will be enough for five people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto coi pomodori (Risotto with Tomatoes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 500 grams
- Butter, 100 grams
- Parmesan cheese, as needed
- Tomato sauce from recipe No. 125
- Hot water

## Directions

1. Pour the rice onto the melted butter and when it has absorbed it begin to add hot water, little by little
2. When it reaches half cooking, give it flavor with the tomato sauce from No. 125
3. Before removing from the fire add a good handful of grated Parmesan cheese
4. In the said sauce, to season the risotto, you can substitute cured meat for the oil if you wish, or use the tomato juice described in No. 6
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto coi ranocchi (Risotto with Frogs)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Frogs, twelve large ones for 300 grams of rice
- Rice, 300 grams
- One quarter of a large onion
- One clove of garlic
- Carrot, celery, parsley, basil
- Oil, pepper, salt
- Tomatoes, in pieces
- Hot water
- Butter, a piece
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. To make the frog meat tender, throw them in hot water just after skinning and then pass them in fresh water, but be careful—only half a minute, or you will cook them
2. Leave behind the thighs; do not use the eggs in this case
3. Make a battuto with one quarter of a large onion, one clove of garlic, carrot, celery, parsley, basil and put it on the fire with oil, pepper and salt
4. When it has taken color throw in the frogs, stir from time to time and once browned throw in pieces of tomatoes, which you will let fall apart
5. Then pour in as much hot water as may be needed
6. Boil slowly until the frogs are well cooked and then pass everything through a strainer, squeezing well
7. In a little of this broth cook the thighs set aside, bone them and unite them with the rest
8. Put the rice on the fire with a piece of butter, stir, and when the butter has been completely absorbed pour the hot frog broth one ladle at a time until completely cooked
9. Before removing the rice throw into it a handful of Parmesan cheese and serve it
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto col brodo di pesce (Risotto with Fish Broth)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- fish broth
- quarter of an onion
- one or two cloves garlic
- parsley
- carrot
- celery
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- tomato sauce or paste
- rice
- butter
- parmesan cheese
- dried mushrooms (optional for soup)
- ladle

## Directions

1. Make a soffritto with a quarter of an onion, one or two cloves of garlic, parsley, carrot and celery and place it on the fire with oil, salt and pepper
2. When it has taken color, stop it with tomato sauce or paste dissolved in a ladle of the said broth
3. Let boil a little and then pour in the rice which you will cook to completion with the same boiling broth, poured little by little
4. At half cooking add a piece of butter, and when the rice is cooked, a small handful of parmesan cheese
5. For soup you can add a pinch of dried mushrooms and serve the parmesan separately
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto colle telline (Risotto with Clams)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Clams in shell, 1.350 kilograms
- Rice, 500 grams
- Oil
- Garlic
- Onion
- Parsley
- Carrot
- Celery
- Dried mushrooms
- Pepper
- Salt
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese
- Tomato juice or tomato preserve

## Directions

1. To remove sand from the clams, wash them first, then place them in fresh salted water or sea water in a bowl with an inverted plate underneath, and after at least two hours, remove them dry and put them on the fire with water in proportion to the rice to be cooked.
2. When they have opened, remove the shells and keep the water, but be careful that at the bottom of the same a deposit of sand will have formed which must be thrown away.
3. Make a soffritto with oil, garlic, little onion, parsley, carrot and celery, all finely chopped with a crescent knife, and when it has browned well, throw in the clams removed from the shell, some pieces of soaked dried mushrooms, a pinch of pepper and some of that reserved water.
4. After a few minutes throw the rice into this sauce and cook it firm with the rest of the aforementioned water.
5. Taste it to see if it is well-flavored with only the natural salt of the clams and the seasonings given to it; if not, add salt with tomato juice or preserve, and also with a piece of butter and a pinch of parmesan.
6. The clams can be substituted with arselle or mussels as in Venice, in whose trattorias if rice with mussels (specialty of the country) were cooked in this manner, it would be much more pleasing.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto colle tinche (Risotto with Tench)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 500 grams
- Tench, approximately 400 grams
- Garlic, two cloves
- Parsley, a pinch
- Basil leaves, if you like its smell
- Carrot, one large
- White celery, two pieces one palm long
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Tomato juice or tomato preserve
- Water
- Dried mushrooms, optional
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese
- Peas, 200 grams, optional
- Spring onion, one whole, optional

## Directions

1. Make a battuto with two cloves of garlic, a pinch of parsley, some basil leaves if you like its smell, one large carrot and two pieces of white celery one palm long.
2. Put it on the fire in a pot with oil, salt and pepper, adding at the same time the tench already gutted and cut into pieces, including the heads.
3. Turn them often so they do not stick to the bottom, and when they are well browned begin to moisten them first with tomato juice or preserve, then with water poured little by little at first and finally, in quantity such as to cook the rice, but keeping it rather scarce than abundant.
4. Boil until the tench are completely falling apart, then pass everything through a strainer, so that nothing remains but the bones and small bones.
5. This is the sauce that will serve to cook the rice, pulling it dry and of proper cooking.
6. To grace it you can add some pieces of dried mushrooms and a piece of butter and then serve it at table with grated parmesan for those who want it.
7. If using peas instead of mushrooms: cook 200 grams of shelled peas separately with a little oil, a little butter and one whole spring onion. Add the peas when the onion begins to brown, let them fry a little, season with salt and pepper and cook them with little water. Discard the onion and mix the peas with the rice when it is almost cooked.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto nero colle seppie alla fiorentina (Black Risotto with Cuttlefish, Florentine Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cuttlefish
- Beet greens, approximately 600 grams
- Rice, 600 grams
- Cuttlefish ink
- Onion, two small or one medium with two garlic cloves
- Oil, fine and abundant
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water
- Parmesan cheese
- Butter
- Peas, optional (as mentioned in the risotto with tench)
- Tomato juice or tomato preserve, alternative version
- Rice, 600 grams

## Directions

1. Peel and clean the cuttlefish to remove useless parts which are the bone, the mouth apparatus, the eyes and the digestive tube; set aside the ink sac, and after washing them well, cut them into small squares and the tails into pieces.
2. Finely chop two small onions, or better one onion and two cloves of garlic, and put them on the fire in a pot with fine oil and in abundance.
3. When the soffritto has taken on a reddish color, throw in the cuttlefish and wait until these, boiling, begin to turn yellow, then throw in approximately 600 grams of beet greens, cleaned of the thickest ribs and chopped somewhat.
4. Mix and let boil for about half an hour; then pour in 600 grams of rice (which will be the weight of the cuttlefish in nature) and their ink and, when the rice has been well impregnated with that sauce, cook it with hot water.
5. The rice, by general rule, should be barely cooked, and when it is said 'dry' it should form a mound on the plate in which you serve it.
6. Always accompany it with grated parmesan; but if you have a delicate stomach abstain from its use when cooked with these and similar ingredients of difficult digestion.
7. Alternative method: No beet greens, no ink, and when the cuttlefish, as stated, begin to take on yellow, pour the rice and cook it with hot water and tomato juice or preserve, giving it more grace and flavor with a piece of butter; when almost cooked add parmesan.
8. For an even better result, add peas at two-thirds of cooking time, as mentioned in the risotto with tench.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Risotto nero colle seppie alla fiorentina - Variante (Black Risotto with Cuttlefish, Florentine Style - Variant)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Cuttlefish
- Rice, 600 grams
- Onion, two small or one medium with two garlic cloves
- Oil, fine and abundant
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water
- Tomato juice or tomato preserve
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese
- Peas, optional

## Directions

1. Peel and clean the cuttlefish to remove useless parts which are the bone, the mouth apparatus, the eyes and the digestive tube.
2. Finely chop two small onions, or better one onion and two cloves of garlic, and put them on the fire in a pot with fine oil and in abundance.
3. When the soffritto has taken on a reddish color, throw in the cuttlefish and wait until these, boiling, begin to turn yellow.
4. Pour in 600 grams of rice and cook it with hot water and tomato juice or preserve, giving it more grace and flavor with a piece of butter.
5. When almost cooked, unite parmesan cheese.
6. If desired, add peas at two-thirds of cooking time as mentioned in the risotto with tench.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Roast-Beef I (Roast Beef)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin of beef weighing more than one kilogram, from a young animal
- Oil
- Broth
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Put it on the fire half an hour before sending the soup course to the table
2. Cook it over an ardent and well-lit fire from the beginning so that it is seized quickly at the surface
3. Anoint it with oil, which you will then drain from the pan
4. Finally pass a ladle of broth over it, which, united with the fat fallen from the roast beef, will serve as sauce for the piece when you send it to the table
5. Salt it at half cooking, using somewhat sparingly as this quality of meat is flavorful in itself
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Roast-beef I (Roasted Beef)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of beef suitable for roasting
- oil
- salt
- broth
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook it over a brisk and well-lit fire from the beginning so the surface is seared immediately.
2. Oil it, then strain the oil from the dripping pan, and finally pass a ladle of broth over it, which, united with the fat that drips from the roast-beef, will serve as sauce for the piece when you send it to the table.
3. Salt it at half cooking, being somewhat sparing because this quality of meat is flavorful by itself.
4. Place it over the fire half an hour before sending the soup course to the table, which is sufficient if the piece is not very large.
5. To check if it is cooked, prick it in the thickest part with a thin larding needle, but do not prick it frequently so it does not dry out. The juice that comes out should be neither the color of blood nor dark.
6. Cook potatoes separately as a side dish, browning them in oil from raw and peeled, whole if small and quartered if large.
7. The roast-beef can also be sent to the oven, but it does not turn out as well as on a spit. In this case season it with salt, oil and a piece of butter, surround it with raw peeled potatoes, and pour a glass of water into the pan.
8. If leftover roast-beef is not pleasing cold, slice it, prepare it again with butter and meat or tomato juice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Roast-beef II (Roasted Beef)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of beef
- butter, softened
- salt
- charcoal fire

## Directions

1. Thread the beef onto a spit.
2. Wrap it in a sheet of white paper that is not too thin and well buttered with cold butter.
3. Tie it at both ends so it remains well sealed and place it over a very hot charcoal fire.
4. Turn it, and when it is almost cooked, tear away the paper.
5. Salt it and let it take on color.
6. Remove from the fire, close it between two plates and after ten minutes serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Roschetti (Almond Cookies)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 200 grams
- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Sweet almonds, 100 grams
- Butter, 80 grams
- Lard, 30 grams
- One whole egg and one egg yolk

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, dry them well in the sun or by the fire, toast them to a hazelnut color and chop them to the thickness of a grain of rice
2. Mix these with the sugar into the flour
3. Make a well in the mass to place the remaining ingredients and knead it working as little as possible
4. Let it rest for a few hours in the form of a round loaf
5. Lightly flour the work surface and roll out the loaf first with a smooth rolling pin then with a ridged one, to a thickness slightly less than one centimeter
6. Cut with a disc or similar tool to obtain about 50 small pastries
7. Bake in a countryside oven after placing them on a baking pan greased lightly with cold butter
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Roschetti (Little Roses)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 200 grams
- Powdered sugar, 100 grams
- Sweet almonds, 100 grams
- Butter, 80 grams
- Rendered fat (strutto), 30 grams
- One whole egg and one yolk

## Directions

1. Shell the almonds, dry them well in the sun or by fire, toast them to a hazelnut color, and chop them to the thickness of half a grain of rice
2. Mix both these and the sugar among the flour
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Rosolio d'anaci (Anise Liqueur)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 grams of Romagna anise
- Alcohol (as prepared in previous recipe)
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Make it in the same manner as the previous recipe
2. Make the infusion with 50 grams of Romagna anise instead of lemon zest, as these anise from Romagna, for their pleasant flavor and strong fragrance, are without exaggeration the best in the world
3. Before using them, throw the anise into water to clean them from the earth that they probably contain, as it is deliberately mixed in to adulterate the merchandise
4. Proceed as with the previous recipe
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Rosolio d'Anaci (Anise Liqueur)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Anise from Romagna, 50 grams
- White fine powdered sugar, 800 grams
- Rainwater or spring water, 1 liter
- Strong spirit, 8 deciliters

## Directions

1. Make it in the same manner as the preceding recipe.
2. Make the infusion with 50 grams of anise from Romagna instead of lemon peel, and I specify from Romagna.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Rosolio di Cedro (Citron Liqueur)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- White fine powdered sugar, 800 grams
- Rainwater or spring water, 1 liter
- Strong spirit, 8 deciliters
- Somewhat greenish garden lemons, 3

## Directions

1. Pour the sugar into water and stir it every day until it is dissolved.
2. At the same time grate the peel of the lemons and keep it infused in two deciliters of the said spirit for eight days; for three or four days stir it often, and in winter keep it in a place protected from the cold.
3. After eight days pass the infusion of lemons through a damp cloth, squeeze it well and mix the extract with the remaining six deciliters of spirit and let it rest for twenty-four hours.
4. The next day mix everything together, pour the liquid into a flask, which you will shake from time to time, and after fifteen days pass it through paper or several times through cotton.
5. This is to be placed at the bottom of the funnel and in the middle of it make a broom stick with several branches in the upper part pass through it in order to give the liquid the means to pass.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Rosolio di Portogallo (Portuguese Liqueur)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine white sugar, 650 grams
- Water, 360 grams
- Wine spirit at 36 degrees, 250 grams
- Saffron, a pinch
- Orange, 1

## Directions

1. Remove with a knife the superficial peel of the orange and place it in the spirit with the saffron, in a vessel covered with perforated paper, leaving it there for three days.
2. Pour the sugar into water in another vessel, stirring it from time to time so it dissolves well, and on the fourth day mix the two liquids together and leave them at rest for another eight days.
3. At the end of these, pass the liqueur through cloth, filter it through paper or cotton and bottle it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Rosolio tedesco (German Liqueur)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine alcohol, 500 grams
- White powdered sugar, 500 grams
- Milk, half a liter
- One garden lemon
- Half a vanilla pod

## Directions

1. Mince the whole lemon, removing the seeds and including the zest that you have previously grated
2. Divide the vanilla into small pieces
3. Mix everything else together in a glass vessel and you will see that the milk immediately curdles
4. Shake the vessel once a day and after eight days strain it through cloth and filter it through paper
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Rossi d'Uovo al Canapè (Egg Yolks on Canapé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks, 5
- Bread crumbs (midolla di pane), for 5 slices about a finger thick and as wide as a palm
- Butter
- Milk, 3 scant deciliters
- Flour, 40 grams
- Parmesan cheese, 3 heaping tablespoons
- Cinnamon or nutmeg
- Salt

## Directions

1. Form five square slices of bread crumbs, about a finger thick and roughly the width of a palm, and make a cavity in the center of each, but not all the way through.
2. Fry them in butter and place them on a platter that can be put in the fire.
3. Place one raw, whole egg yolk in the cavity of each.
4. Make a béchamel sauce with 3 scant deciliters of milk, 40 grams of flour, and 40 grams of butter.
5. Remove from the fire and add three heaping tablespoons of Parmesan, the flavor of cinnamon or nutmeg, and salt.
6. Let it cool, then pour it over the platter to cover the egg yolks and the bread pieces.
7. Brown it somewhat under the cover of a country oven so the eggs do not harden too much.
8. Serve it hot.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Rotelle di Palombo in Salsa (Smooth-hound Slices in Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- thick slices of smooth-hound (palombo) a finger's thickness
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- oil
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- tomato juice or tomato paste
- water
- parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Wash the smooth-hound slices and dry them in a cloth, then skin them with a sharp knife
2. Season with salt and pepper and soak in beaten egg for several hours
3. Coat with breadcrumbs, dipping twice in egg, then fry in oil
4. In a wide pan or dish, heat oil in proportion, add a piece of butter mixed well with flour (to bind the sauce), a pinch of chopped parsley, tomato juice or paste diluted with water, and a pinch of salt and pepper
5. Let the sauce fry a little on the fire
6. Add the fried smooth-hound slices to the sauce, turn them on both sides and add water so the sauce becomes liquid
7. Remove from heat, sprinkle with grated parmesan and serve
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salame Inglese (Striped Sponge Cake Filled with Jam)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- powdered sugar, 200 grams
- very fine flour, 170 grams
- eggs, 6 whole
- white liqueur for coloring strips
- alkermes liqueur for coloring strips
- white liqueur infused with chocolate for coloring strips
- fruit preserves, liquid
- candied fruit slices
- powdered sugar for frosting
- egg white for frosting
- chocolate powder, 30 grams (for chocolate frosting)

## Directions

1. Make a sponge cake with the given amounts of sugar, flour, and eggs
2. To bake it in the oven, spread it to a depth of about half a finger in a rectangular pan, greased with butter and dusted with flour
3. Beat the egg yolks with sugar for about half an hour
4. Add the well-beaten egg whites and after mixing them gently, let the flour fall from a fine sifter
5. When removed from the oven, cut the hot sponge cake into strips about 2 centimeters wide and as long as the piece of sponge cake
6. To make these strips effective, they must be different colors; spray some with white liqueur and they will remain yellow; spray others with alkermes and they will appear red; make the last ones black with white liqueur in which chocolate has been infused
7. Arrange these colored fillets one on top of the other, alternating them, in the middle of the remaining whole piece of sponge cake, whose surface you have previously spread with liquid fruit preserves, also spreading the fillets so they stick together
8. Pull the edges of the sponge cake over them and form a tightly rolled log which, when sliced, will present a multicolored checkerboard filling
9. For a simpler family version, half the amount suffices even for a large pan
10. Spread the sponge cake with liqueur and fruit preserves, whether quince, apricot, or peach, spread thin slices of candied fruit on top and roll the entire piece like a sheet upon itself
11. To give it a nicer appearance, decorate the surface either with a pattern of sugar or with a chocolate crust as pastry chefs do
12. For a simpler method: mix powdered sugar with egg white, making it very stiff, and spread it uniformly over the dessert, or place it in a cone-shaped pastry bag and squeeze it to form a design
13. For a dark crust, take 60 grams of powdered sugar and 30 grams of chocolate powder, mix, combine with egg white, and spread the mixture on the dessert
14. If it does not dry naturally, place it under moderate heat
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salami dal Sugo di Ferrara (Ferrara Salami with Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ferrara salami
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the salami several times with tepid water to clean them of the fatty patina that covers them
2. Place them in a pot with abundant cold water
3. Boil them slowly for one and a half hours only, tied tightly in a cloth to prevent the skin from splitting
4. Serve them hot with side dishes like coteghini
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa alla Maître d'Hôtel (Maître d'Hôtel Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley, a pinch
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mince a pinch of parsley and to remove its acidity (as some suggest) place it within the point of a napkin and squeeze it lightly in fresh water
2. Form a paste with butter, salt, pepper and lemon juice
3. Place it over the fire in a pan or plate and, without letting it boil, dip the steak when you remove it from the grill, or fried cutlets
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Bianca (White Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 100 grams
- Flour, one spoonful
- Vinegar, one spoonful
- One egg yolk
- Salt and pepper
- Broth or water, as needed

## Directions

1. First place on the fire the flour with half the butter and when it has taken on a hazelnut color pour the broth or water little by little stirring with the spoon and, without letting it boil too much, add the rest of the butter and the vinegar
2. Remove from the fire, dissolve the egg yolk in it and serve it
3. Its consistency should be equal to that of cream made without flour
4. For a common bunch of asparagus, 70 grams of butter with the flour and vinegar in proportion may be sufficient
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa con Capperi per Pesce Lesso (Sauce with Capers for Boiled Fish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 50 grams
- Capers pressed from vinegar, 50 grams
- Flour, 1 heaping teaspoon
- Salt, pepper and vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the fish and while you keep it warm in its broth, prepare the sauce.
2. Place the flour with half the butter on the fire, stir, and when it begins to take color add the remaining butter.
3. Let it boil a little then pour in a ladle of fish broth.
4. Season abundantly with salt and pepper and remove the pan from the fire.
5. Add the capers half whole and half chopped, plus a drop of vinegar; but taste to adjust the sauce so that it has good flavor and the density of a liquid cream.
6. Place the dried and hot fish in a serving dish, pour the sauce over it, also hot, surround it with whole parsley and serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa del Papa (Pope's Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Capers
- Pitted sweet olives, equal quantity to the capers
- Finely chopped onion
- Butter
- Water
- Flour
- 1 anchovy
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a handful of capers and press them from vinegar; take as many sweetened olives as, after removing the pit, equal the quantity of capers and chop both finely with a crescent-shaped knife.
2. Put on the fire a fine mixture of chopped onion with a small piece of butter and when it has taken color, wet it little by little with water so that it breaks down.
3. Pour into it the mixture of capers and olives and let it boil for a while.
4. Finally add a drop of vinegar, a pinch of flour and another small piece of butter.
5. Last add a chopped anchovy, and without letting the sauce boil anymore, serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa di capperi e acciughe (Caper and Anchovy Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Capers, sweetened
- Anchovy, 1 piece
- Oil
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take a pinch of sweetened capers, squeeze them of vinegar and mince them with a crescent-shaped knife together with an anchovy that you have first cleaned of its scales and spine.
2. Put this mixture to heat on the fire with oil, and pour it on the steak that, just removed from the grill, you will have seasoned with salt and pepper and brushed with butter; in this case, however, brush it lightly, because otherwise the butter would fight with the vinegar of the capers in the stomach.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa di Magro per Paste Asciutte (Lean Sauce for Dried Pasta)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Spaghetti, 500 grams
- Fresh mushrooms, 100 grams
- Butter, 70 grams
- Pine nuts, 60 grams
- Salted anchovies, 6
- Tomatoes, 7 or 8
- One quarter of a large onion
- Flour, one teaspoon

## Directions

1. Place half the butter in a pot and with it brown the pine nuts
2. Remove the pine nuts dried and pound them in a mortar with the indicated flour
3. Mince the onion very finely, place it in the remaining sauce and when it has taken on much color, add the tomatoes in pieces
4. Season with pepper and little salt, and when they are cooked, pass them through a sieve
5. Return the sauce to the fire with the mushrooms cut into thin slices no larger than a pumpkin seed, the pine nut paste which you can first dissolve with a little water, and the rest of the butter
6. Bring to a boil for half an hour, adding water to make the sauce more liquid, and lastly dissolve the anchovies on the fire with a little of this sauce, without letting them boil, and combine them with it
7. Remove the spaghetti dried, season it with this sauce and if you want it better add parmesan cheese
8. Serves five people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa di Peperoni (Pepper Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large green peppers
- Oil
- Butter
- Garlic, 1 clove
- Salt
- Tomato juice

## Directions

1. Take large green peppers, open them, clean them of seeds and cut them lengthwise into four or five strips.
2. Give them a small scald in a pan with a little oil so you can peel them.
3. After peeling, put on the fire a finely chopped clove of garlic with oil and butter and when it is browned throw in the peppers, salt them, let them take on flavor a little and add tomato juice.
4. Do not cook them too much because they would lose their spiciness, which is what gives them charm, and serve them with boiled meat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa di Pomodoro (Tomato Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter of an onion
- One clove of garlic
- One piece of celery as long as a finger
- Some basil leaves and parsley as needed
- Oil
- Salt and pepper
- Seven or eight tomatoes

## Directions

1. Make a battuto with a quarter of onion, one clove of garlic, a piece of celery as long as a finger, some basil leaves and parsley as needed
2. Season it with a little oil, salt and pepper
3. Break seven or eight tomatoes and put everything together on the fire
4. Stir from time to time and when you see the juice condensed like a liquid cream, pass it through a sieve and serve it
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Genovese per Pesce Lesso (Genoese Sauce for Boiled Fish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pine nuts, 40 grams
- Capers pressed from vinegar, 15 grams
- 1 salted anchovy
- 1 hardboiled egg yolk
- The pulp of three olives in brine
- Half a clove of garlic
- A good pinch of parsley, including the stems
- A crumb of bread as large as an egg, soaked in vinegar
- A pinch of salt and a pinch of pepper
- Oil, 60 grams
- A drop of vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop very finely with a crescent-shaped knife the parsley and garlic and then put these and everything else in a mortar and after having reduced the mixture very finely pass it through a sieve and dilute it with 60 grams of oil and a drop of vinegar; but taste it first to adjust it properly.
2. It is an excellent sauce and sufficient for 600 grams of fish.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Gialla per Pesce Lesso (Yellow Sauce for Boiled Fish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 20 grams
- Flour, one heaping teaspoon
- Fish broth, two ladles
- Oil, two spoonfuls
- One egg yolk
- Lemon juice, half of one
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Place on the fire in a small pot 20 grams of butter with one heaping teaspoon of flour and after it has taken on a hazelnut color, pour over it little by little two ladles of broth from the fish itself
2. When you see that the flour, while boiling, no longer rises, remove the sauce from the fire and pour into it two spoonfuls of oil and one egg yolk, stirring well
3. Finally add the juice of half a lemon, salt and pepper in good measure
4. Let it cool and then pour it over the fish which you will send to the table surrounded by fresh parsley
5. This sauce should have the appearance of a cream not too liquid to remain attached to the fish
6. You will find it is good and delicate
7. For those who do not like cold fish serve it hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Maionese (Mayonnaise Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Two raw and fresh egg yolks
- Olive oil, six or seven spoonfuls or more if absorbed
- Lemon juice
- Salt and white pepper

## Directions

1. Place two raw and fresh egg yolks in a bowl and after whisking them somewhat, let fall on them little by little and almost drop by drop, especially at first, six or seven spoonfuls or even more if they absorb it, of olive oil
2. Then have them absorb the juice of one lemon
3. If the sauce turns out well it should have the appearance of a dense cream; but it is necessary to work it for more than 20 minutes
4. Finally season it with salt and white pepper in good measure
5. To be more certain of the result, to the two raw egg yolks it is customary to add another hard-boiled one
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Olandese (Dutch Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 70 grams
- Egg yolks, 2
- Lemon juice, 1 tablespoon
- Water, half an eggshell
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Melt the butter separately without heating it too much.
2. Put the egg yolks with the water in a small basin, and over a light fire or on the edge of the stove begin to beat them with a whisk and gradually pour in the butter little by little.
3. When the mixture has thickened, add the lemon juice and finally the salt and pepper.
4. Prepare it at the moment of serving; it is a delicate sauce for boiled fish or similar things, and will be sufficient for approximately 500 grams of fish.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Olandese (Hollandaise Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 70 grams
- Egg yolks, 2
- Lemon juice, one spoonful
- Water, half an eggshell
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Dissolve the butter separately without heating it too much
2. Place the yolks with the water in a basin, and over a light fire or on the edge of the stove begin to beat them with the whisk and pour in the butter little by little
3. When the mixture has condensed add the lemon and finally the salt and pepper
4. Prepare it at the moment of serving; it is a delicate sauce for boiled fish or for something similar, and will be sufficient for a quantity of approximately 500 grams
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa per Pesce in Gratella (Sauce for Grilled Fish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks
- Salted anchovies
- Fine oil
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs in their shells for 10 minutes and for each hardboiled egg yolk take one large anchovy or two small ones.
2. Remove the backbone and pass them through a sieve together with the yolks.
3. Dilute the mixture with oil and lemon juice to reduce it to the consistency of a cream.
4. Cover the fish already cooked on the grill with this sauce before sending it to table, or serve it separately in a sauce boat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Piccante I (Spicy Sauce I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Two spoonfuls of capers in vinegar
- Two anchovies
- A pinch of parsley
- An abundant amount of pepper
- Oil
- Vinegar or lemon juice, if needed

## Directions

1. Take two spoonfuls of capers in vinegar, two anchovies and a pinch of parsley
2. Mince everything together very finely and place this battuto in a sauce boat with an abundant pinch of pepper and much oil
3. If it is not sufficiently acidic, add vinegar or lemon juice, and serve it with boiled fish
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Piccante II (Spicy Sauce II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- A little onion
- Parsley
- Some basil leaves
- Lean prosciutto
- Capers squeezed from vinegar
- Good oil
- Broth
- One or two anchovies
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make a finely minced battutino with little onion, parsley, some basil leaves, all lean prosciutto and capers squeezed from vinegar
2. Place it on the fire with good oil, let it boil slowly and when the onion is browned stop it with a little broth
3. Let it boil a few more times, then remove it adding one or two minced anchovies and lemon juice
4. This sauce can serve for poached eggs, for steaks, which in this case do not need salt, and also for cutlets
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Tartufata (Truffle Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion, size of a walnut
- Half a clove of garlic
- A little parsley
- Butter, 20 grams
- Marsala or white wine, two fingers worth
- Flour, 1 heaping teaspoon
- Salt, pepper and spices
- Broth
- Truffles, sliced very thin

## Directions

1. Make a very fine mixture with a piece of onion the size of a walnut, half a clove of garlic and a little parsley.
2. Put it on the fire with 20 grams of butter and when it has taken color pour in two fingers of marsala or white wine in which you have first dissolved a heaping teaspoon of flour.
3. Season the sauce with a pinch of salt, a pinch of pepper and a pinch of spices and stir it always with a spoon.
4. When the flour has thickened, add a little broth and then throw very thin slices of truffles into this sauce.
5. Let it stay another moment on the fire and use it to garnish fried veal cutlets, steaks or other roasted meat.
6. I warn you that wine, as a condiment, does not suit all stomachs.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa Tonnata (Tuna Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Tuna in oil, 50 grams
- Capers pressed from vinegar, 50 grams
- Anchovies, 2
- Hardboiled egg yolks, 2
- A good pinch of parsley
- Juice of half a lemon
- A pinch of pepper
- Oil, as needed

## Directions

1. Clean the anchovies and then chop them with a crescent-shaped knife together with the tuna, capers and parsley.
2. Pound them afterward in a mortar with the egg yolks and a little oil to soften the mixture and be able to pass it better through a sieve.
3. Then dilute it with plenty of oil and lemon juice, to reduce it to the consistency of a liquid cream.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa verde (Green Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Capers, squeezed of vinegar
- Anchovy, 1 piece
- Small amount of onion
- Very little garlic
- Parsley, generous amount
- Basil leaves
- Fine oil
- Lemon juice
- or bell peppers as substitute for capers

## Directions

1. Mince everything together with a crescent-shaped knife: capers squeezed of vinegar, an anchovy, a little onion and very little garlic.
2. Crush the mixture with the blade of a knife to make it fine and place it in a sauce boat.
3. Add a generous amount of parsley, minced with some basil leaves, and dissolve everything with fine oil and lemon juice.
4. Lacking capers, bell peppers can serve as a substitute.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsa verde, Sauce Ravigote (Green Sauce, French-style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley
- Basil
- Chervil
- Pimpinella (salvestrella)
- Celery leaves
- Shallots, 2 or 3 or a small onion if shallots are lacking
- Anchovy or 2 if small
- Capers, sweetened
- Raw egg yolk, 1
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Mince everything finely, or pound it and pass it through a sieve, then put it in a sauce boat with a raw egg yolk, season it with oil, vinegar, salt and pepper; mix it well and serve it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsiccia coll'uva (Sausage with Grapes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- sausages
- lard or butter
- grapes

## Directions

1. Prick the sausages with the tip of a fork and place them whole in a pan with a little lard or butter
2. When they are cooked, add the grapes, not in large quantity, as whole berries and let it boil until they are half dissolved
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Salsiccia colle uova (Sausage with Eggs)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- sausage
- eggs
- butter or lard, if the sausage is too lean

## Directions

1. If the sausage is fresh, split it lengthwise into two parts and cook it in a pan without any fat or seasoning, as it contains its own
2. If it is aged, cut it into slices and remove the skin
3. As soon as the sausage is cooked, break the eggs into the pan and serve when they are set
4. For each common link of sausage, one egg is sufficient or at most two
5. If the sausages are too lean, it is good to cook them with a little butter or lard
6. If instead of sausage it is cured meat, add a small piece of butter and pour the eggs in after beating them separately
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Saltimbocca alla Romana (Roman Saltimbocca)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams lean veal breast, sliced thin
- salt
- pepper
- sage leaves
- thin slices of prosciutto, fat and lean
- toothpicks
- butter

## Directions

1. Pound the veal scallops and dampen them to flatten.
2. Season each scallop lightly with salt and pepper.
3. Place half a sage leaf on each scallop and on top of the sage place a thin slice of prosciutto.
4. Secure the three components together with a toothpick.
5. Cook in butter in a sauté pan, keeping the prosciutto side on the heat only briefly so the prosciutto does not harden.
6. Serve with any side dish desired.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sandwichs
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Very fine bread from one day old, or rye bread
- Fresh butter
- Cooked prosciutto, both fat and lean, or salted tongue

## Directions

1. Take very fine bread from one day old or rye bread, remove the crust and cut it into slices half a centimeter thick and approximately 6 centimeters long and 4 centimeters wide
2. Spread them with fresh butter on one side only and stick them together, putting between them a thin slice of either cooked prosciutto (both fat and lean) or salted tongue
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sandwichs (Sandwiches)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine bread from the previous day, or rye bread
- Fresh butter
- Sliced ham, fatty and lean
- or salted tongue

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from the fine bread of the previous day or rye bread and cut it into slices about half a centimeter thick and approximately 6 centimeters long and 4 centimeters wide.
2. Spread them with fresh butter on only one side and stick them together, placing between them a thin slice of either fatty and lean cooked ham or salted tongue.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sarde Ripiene (Stuffed Sardines)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sardines, 20 to 24 large ones
- Bread crumb, 30 grams
- Salted anchovies, 3 pieces
- One egg yolk
- Half a clove of garlic
- A pinch of oregano
- Milk
- Egg white
- Breadcrumbs
- Oil
- Salt
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Wash the sardines, remove the head, and open them from the belly using your fingers to extract the spine.
2. Soak the bread crumb in milk and then squeeze it out.
3. Clean the anchovies of their scales and spine, then chop them.
4. Mix together the bread crumb, anchovies, egg yolk, half a clove of garlic, and a pinch of oregano, using the blade of a knife to make the mixture fine.
5. Spread this mixture onto the sardines and close them.
6. Dip them one by one into the remaining beaten egg white, coat them with breadcrumbs, and fry them in oil.
7. Salt them lightly and serve with lemon wedges.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sauer-kraut I
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Head of white cabbage
- Salt
- Strong vinegar
- Water
- Slice of fat prosciutto or cured meat
- Butter
- Broth from sausages or boiled meat

## Directions

1. Take a head of white cabbage, clean it of green leaves and large ribs and cut it into four wedges starting from the stem.
2. Wash them well in fresh water and then with a long sharp knife cut them crosswise very thin as you would for noodles.
3. Once the cabbage is reduced this way, place it in an earthenware vessel with a pinch of salt and pour boiling water over it until covered.
4. When it is cold, remove it squeezing it well, then put it back in the dry vessel with a finger of strong vinegar mixed in a glass of fresh water. If the head of cabbage is very large, double the dose.
5. Let it infuse for several hours, squeeze it well again and put it to cook in the following manner.
6. Finely chop a proportionate slice of fat prosciutto or cured meat and put it with a piece of butter in a pot; when they have softened a little, throw in the cabbage and cook it with broth from sausages or boiled meat if these are freshly made and not too spicy, otherwise use regular broth.
7. Before serving taste it to see if it has the right amount of vinegar, which should be slightly perceptible, and salt.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sauer-kraut II
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Head of cabbage or savoy cabbage
- Onion
- Cured meat
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Broth
- Vinegar
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Take a head of cabbage or savoy cabbage, cut it into strips about one centimeter wide and keep it soaked in fresh water.
2. Remove it from the water without squeezing it and press it in a pot over the fire to release its water, which you will then drain by squeezing it with a spoon.
3. Make a mixture with a quarter of a large onion, some finely chopped cured meat, and a piece of butter; when it takes color, add the said cabbage with a piece of whole cured meat in the middle, which you will remove later, and season with salt and pepper.
4. Let it boil slowly, moistening it with broth to cook it through and finally add a little vinegar and a teaspoon of sugar, but in such a way that the vinegar is barely noticeable.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Savarin (French Ring Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Hungarian flour or finest flour, 180 grams
- Butter, 60 grams
- Sugar, 40 grams
- Sweet almonds, 40 grams
- Milk, 2 deciliters
- Eggs, 2 yolks and 1 white
- A pinch of salt
- Beer yeast, about the size of a small egg

## Directions

1. Grease the mold with cold butter, dust it with common flour mixed with powdered sugar, and scatter in the bottom the said almonds, peeled and cut into short strips. If you toast them, you will do better.
2. Mix and knead the yeast with a drop of the said lukewarm milk and a good pinch of Hungarian flour to make a small dough, which you will allow to rise like the Babà in recipe N. 565.
3. Put the remaining flour and the other ingredients, except for the milk which is to be added little by little, in a bowl, and begin to work them with a spoon; then add the yeast, and when the mixture is worked so that it detaches well from the bowl, pour it into the mold over the almonds.
4. Now allow it to rise in a barely warm place, well protected from air, noting that for this second rising you will need four or five hours. Bake it in a common oven or a country oven.
5. In the meantime prepare the following mixture: boil 30 grams of sugar in two fingers' depth of water in a glass, and when you have reduced it to dense syrup, remove it from the fire and, once cooled, add a teaspoon of vanilla sugar and two tablespoons of rum or kirsch.
6. Then turn out the savarin and, while still hot, brush it all over with this syrup using a brush until you have used it all. Serve the cake hot or cold as you prefer.
7. This amount, though small, can serve five or six people. If you see that the dough becomes too liquid with the entire amount of milk, leave some of it aside. It can also be made without the almonds.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppe di Lingua Farsite in Bella Vista (Stuffed Tongue Cutlets in Fine Presentation)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 slices of cured tongue from the thickest part, totaling about 130 grams
- 100 grams of cooked ham, fat and lean
- 70 grams of butter
- 20 grams of white fragrant truffle
- gelatin (recipe No. 3)

## Directions

1. Have your charcutier cut ten thin slices of cured tongue from the thickest part, totaling about 130 grams in weight.
2. Also have cut into thin slices 100 grams of cooked ham, both fat and lean portions.
3. Trim the edges of the tongue slices all around to give them an elegant shape and set the trimmings aside.
4. From the ham, cut out ten slices the same size as those of tongue, and put the trimmings of both the ham and tongue into the mortar with 70 grams of butter and 20 grams of a white fragrant truffle.
5. Pound these things together to reduce them fine as an ointment, which you will use to spread on one side of the tongue slices, and stick the ham slices on top.
6. Now that you have composed these ten pieces, you can take as long as you need to coat them with gelatin (recipe No. 3).
7. There are two ways to decorate these pieces with the gelatin. The first consists in taking a wide plate or pan, pouring a light layer of melted gelatin, and when it begins to set, placing the pieces on it and covering them with another layer of melted gelatin, then removing them one by one after it has set.
8. The second way would be to place the pieces upright in a mold at some distance from each other after pouring a light layer of melted gelatin on the bottom, cover them all with the same gelatin, then unmold and send them to the table all in one piece, which will make a finer appearance.
9. For a meal of several courses this dose could serve ten people, but to be safe it is better not to serve it to more than eight.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppine (Thin Breaded Veal Cutlets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lean meat from beef
- salt
- pepper
- grated Parmesan cheese
- spices
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- tomato juice or tomato preserve
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Take lean beef meat, clean it of tendons and skin, and mince it very finely with a knife and then with a crescent-shaped tool if you do not have a meat grinder.
2. Season the meat with salt, pepper, and grated Parmesan cheese; add the aroma of spices if you like, but be careful it does not taste like a meatball.
3. Mix well and shape the meat into a ball; then with breadcrumbs underneath and on top so it does not stick, roll it out with a rolling pin on a work surface, moving it often to make it a thin flattened shape slightly thicker than a coin.
4. Cut it into square pieces as wide as the palm of a hand and cook them in a pan with butter.
5. When the scaloppine have taken on color, water them with tomato juice or preserve diluted in broth or water and serve them.
6. You can also, without using a rolling pin, flatten them with your hands and give them, for more elegance, the shape of a heart.
7. If you have leftover overcooked meat, it is convenient to make them with this and with raw meat mixed together.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppine alla bolognese (Bolognese-Style Veal Cutlets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean milk veal without bone, 300 grams
- Potatoes, 300 grams
- Fat and lean prosciutto cut fine, 80 grams
- Butter, 70 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 30 grams
- Nutmeg flavoring

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes not too much, or cook them in steam, which would be better, and afterwards cut them into very thin slices.
2. Cut the prosciutto crosswise into strips about one finger wide.
3. Mince the meat very finely with a knife and season it with salt, pepper and a little nutmeg, as these and spices in general are appropriate for windy foods.
4. Divide this meat into twelve parts to form as many scaloppine, flatten them with the blade of the knife, then cook them in white, that is without browning, with half the said butter.
5. Take a metal plate or platter, pour the fat that may remain from cooking and place four scaloppine on it, covering them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppine alla Genovese (Genoese-Style Thin Breaded Veal Cutlets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lean veal meat, 500 grams without bone
- one quarter of a medium-sized onion
- oil
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- parsley
- garlic
- good white wine or Marsala
- hot water
- tomato juice or tomato preserve
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Form small braised rolls with lean veal meat, and having 500 grams without bone, mince one quarter of a medium-sized onion and place it at the bottom of a saucepan with oil and a small piece of butter.
2. Lay the braised rolls on the base, one layer on top of the other, season them with salt and pepper and place them on the fire without touching them so that by sticking together they do not shrivel.
3. When the underside has taken on color, sprinkle a teaspoon of flour and after a little while a pinch of chopped parsley and half a clove of chopped garlic and two sparse fingers of a glass of good white wine, or if lacking this, Marsala.
4. Then separate the braised rolls from one another, mix, let them draw moisture, then pour hot water and a little tomato juice or preserve.
5. Let them boil slowly and not for very long to finish cooking them and serve them with abundant sauce, and with slices of toasted bread underneath, or if you prefer, with a side of rice cooked in water, drawn dry and lightly seasoned with butter, Parmesan and the same sauce. Indeed, the rice works very well with this and thus it pleases everyone.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppine alla Livornese (Livornese-Style Thin Meat Slices)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- thick meat slices (bracioline)
- butter
- broth
- salt and pepper
- flour
- marsala wine
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take thick meat slices and beat them well to tenderize them
2. Throw them in a pan with a piece of butter
3. When they have absorbed the butter, wet them with a few spoonfuls of broth to complete cooking
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Bind them with a pinch of flour
6. Give them the flavor of marsala
7. Before removing from heat, make them more agreeable with a pinch of chopped parsley
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppine con la Panna Acida (Thin Veal Cutlets with Sour Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lean veal or milk-fed veal meat
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- sour cream (cream that has turned)
- water or broth
- lemon wedges

## Directions

1. Take lean veal or milk-fed veal meat, cut it into thin braised rolls, beat them, flour them and place them on the fire with a proportionate piece of butter.
2. Season them with salt and pepper and let them boil slowly until they have taken on color from both sides.
3. Then bathe them with the said sour cream and lastly with a little water or broth if it is milk-fed veal, so that the sauce does not become too thick and they can cook better.
4. Serve them with lemon wedges on the side.
5. For four people: 500 grams of lean meat without bone, 70 grams of butter and two deciliters of cream.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppine con Prosciutto e Patate (Veal Scallops with Ham and Potatoes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Ham, cut into strips
- Veal meat, finely minced
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes not too much, or steam them, which would be better, and after slice them as thinly as possible.
2. Cut the ham crosswise into strips about one finger's width.
3. Mince the meat very finely with a knife by striking, and season it with salt, pepper and a little nutmeg, because this and herbs in general, as you already know, are suitable in windy foods. Divide this meat into twelve parts to form as many scallops, flattening them with the blade of the knife, then cook them in white, that is without browning them, with half of the said butter.
4. Take a metal plate or tray, pour into it any fat that may have remained from cooking and four scallops, covering them with a third of the ham and on top of this place a third of the potatoes which you will season with Parmesan and with pieces of remaining butter. Repeat the same operation three times, and lastly place the plate to simmer between two fires and serve it. This is a quantity that can suffice for four or five people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppine di Carne Battuta (Thin Slices of Ground Meat)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lean meat from large beef
- salt and pepper
- grated parmesan
- spices as desired
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- tomato sauce or tomato paste diluted in broth

## Directions

1. Clean the meat of sinews and skin, and mince it finely with a knife and then with a crescent cutter
2. Season with salt, pepper, and grated parmesan; add spices if desired
3. Mix well and form the meat into a ball
4. With breadcrumbs under and on top so it doesn't stick, roll it out with a rolling pin on a work surface, moving it often to make a thin patty slightly larger than a scudo coin
5. Cut it into square pieces the width of a hand's palm
6. Cook them in a baking pan with butter
7. When the scaloppine have taken color, sprinkle them with tomato sauce or paste diluted in broth
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scaloppine di Vitella di Latte in Tortino (Milk-Fed Veal Scaloppine in a Casserole)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- milk-fed lean veal without bone, cut into very thin scaloppine, 300 grams net
- lardoon cut into thin slices, 70 grams
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- spice aroma
- grated Parmesan cheese
- chopped parsley
- spinach
- butter for the spinach

## Directions

1. Melt a little butter on the fire in a proportionate saucepan and on the bottom and around it lay out the lardoon, on top of which you will place a first layer of scaloppine seasoning them with salt, pepper, the aroma of spices, grated Parmesan and chopped parsley.
2. Then another layer of scaloppine seasoned in the same manner, and so on until you have used up everything.
3. On the last layer of scaloppine scatter various small pieces of butter and cook them between two fires, more from below than above, until they remain almost dry and the lardoon is browned.
4. Turn the casserole over a layer of spinach cooked with butter and serve it to four people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Scannello Annegato (Drowned Beef Shank)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 800 grams lean beef or veal without bone from the shank
- 80 grams ham fat
- 1 large carrot or 2 medium ones
- 3 or 4 celery ribs about a palm's length
- half a glass of dry white wine, or if lacking, two fingers of marsala
- salt and pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Lard the meat with the ham fat cut into strips rolled in salt and pepper
2. Salt and tie the meat so it stays together
3. Cut the carrot and celery into small pieces and place them on the bottom of a rather small pot
4. Place the meat on top and cover with water
5. Let it boil slowly with the pot covered
6. When it has reabsorbed the water, strain the sauce and vegetables through a sieve
7. Return the sauce and vegetables to the fire together with the meat and wine
8. When cooked, serve it sliced with its sauce on top
9. This dish can serve six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sedani per Contorno (Celery as a Side Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Celery with full ribs
- Butter
- Meat stock
- Parmesan cheese
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Prosciutto, 30 grams
- Half a medium onion
- Flour
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard or oil for frying

## Directions

1. Select celery with full ribs and use only the white ribs and tender stems
2. Cut into 10 centimeter pieces (or 5 centimeters for the fried version)
3. After peeling the stem, cut it crosswise and keep it attached to the rib
4. Boil in salted water for no more than five minutes and remove, draining well
5. For the first preparation: sauté in butter, finish cooking with meat stock, and add Parmesan cheese when serving
6. For the second preparation: place 30 grams butter and a battuto made with 30 grams of prosciutto (fat and lean mixed together) and a quarter of a medium onion in a saucepan with two cloves; bring to a boil; when the onion colors, add broth and finish cooking the soffritto; pass everything through a sieve and place the sauce in a pan with the celery pieces; season with pepper (salt is not needed) and serve with the sauce
7. For the third preparation: flour the celery, dip in batter (recipe N. 156) and fry in lard or oil; or better yet, after flouring, dip in egg, bread it, and fry; this last preparation works better as a side dish to braised meat dishes, which you can use to moisten it
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sedani per Contorno al Lesso (Celery as a Side Dish for Boiled Meat)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- white celery ribs
- butter
- béchamel sauce (recipe 137)
- parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Use white celery ribs and cut them into pieces about two centimeters long.
2. Boil them for five minutes in salted water and then sauté them in butter.
3. Bind them with the béchamel sauce (recipe 137), kept rather firm, and flavor with parmesan cheese.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Semolino di Magro (Semolina Lean Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Semolina
- Water
- A piece of butter proportioned to the quantity of semolina
- Salt
- Tomato juice or conserve
- Two or three eggs mixed with grated Parmesan cheese
- For one person: one egg yolk with two tablespoons of Parmesan

## Directions

1. Cook the semolina in water and before removing it from the fire, salt it, dissolve in it a piece of butter proportioned to the quantity of semolina and flavor it with a little tomato juice or conserve.
2. Beat in the tureen two or three eggs mixed with grated Parmesan and pour the semolina into it.
3. If it is a soup for only one person, one egg yolk with two tablespoons of Parmesan may be enough.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Seppie coi Piselli (Cuttlefish with Peas)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- cuttlefish
- onion
- garlic
- parsley
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- water
- peas

## Directions

1. Make a fairly generous mixture with onion, one clove of garlic and parsley. Put it on the fire with oil, salt and pepper, and when it has taken color pass it through a colander, squeezing well. In this soffritto add the cuttlefish cut into fillets, but first clean them as indicated elsewhere, moisten them with water if needed, and when they are almost cooked add the peas drained from the fresh water in which you had soaked them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sfilettato tartufato (Truffle-Studded Beef Loin)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- a large piece of beef loin (sfilettato)
- truffles, white preferred, cut into points about three centimeters long
- butter
- oil
- salt

## Directions

1. Lard the entire piece of beef with pieces of truffle, adding to each piece a small piece of butter to fill the hole you have made with the knife tip to insert them.
2. Make incisions across the skin so it does not shrink.
3. Tie it and thread it onto the spit for cooking.
4. When two-thirds cooked, brush it lightly with oil and salt it sparingly, as these meats from large animals are quite flavorful and do not need much seasoning.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sfogliata di Marzapane (Marzipan Puff Pastry Tart)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry dough (quantity and proportions as in recipe N. 154)
- Marzipan mixture (as in recipe N. 579)
- Egg yolk for gilding
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make a puff pastry dough in the quantity and proportion of N. 154.
2. When spread out, cut two rounds the size of a common plate, with large scalloped edges.
3. Over one of them, leaving a bit of margin, spread the marzipan mixture of N. 579, which should be about one centimeter in height.
4. Lay the other round of puff pastry over it, attaching them together at the edges with a finger dipped in water.
5. Gild the surface of the tart with egg yolk.
6. Bake it in the oven or in a country oven.
7. After baking, dust it with powdered sugar.
8. This quantity will serve seven or eight people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene
