title: Sformato cogli Amaretti Coperto di Zabaione (Molded Almond Macaroon Dessert Covered with Zabaglione)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Amaretti biscuits, 100 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Potato starch, 80 grams
- Milk, half a liter
- Eggs, 3
- Cold butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Place the sugar and potato starch in a saucepan and gradually pour in the cold milk while stirring
2. Pound the amaretti in a mortar to reduce them to powder; if this does not happen due to their quality, moisten them with a few drops of milk, pass them through a sieve and then add them to the mixture
3. Put the mixture on the fire to firm it up
4. When removed from the fire and lukewarm, pour in the eggs, first the yolks, then the beaten whites
5. Grease a ring mold with cold butter and pour in the mixture to cook in a countryside oven
6. Once cooked, fill and cover it with the zabaglione from recipe no. 684 and send it to the table
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato della Signora Adele (Lady Adele's Mold)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, 100 grams
- Flour, 80 grams
- Gruyère cheese, 70 grams
- Milk, half a liter
- Eggs, 4
- Bread crumbs
- Chicken offal and sweetbreads for filling

## Directions

1. Make a béchamel with the flour, milk, and butter. Before removing from heat, add the grated or diced Gruyère and salt.
2. Remove from boiling heat and add the eggs, first the yolks one at a time, then the stiffly beaten whites.
3. Pour into a smooth mold with a hole in the center after buttering it and dusting with bread crumbs.
4. Bake in a country oven and serve filled with a ragout of chicken offal and sweetbreads.
5. This serves six people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Carciofi (Artichoke Molded Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salted water
- eggs
- béchamel sauce
- butter
- parmesan cheese
- salt
- nutmeg
- meat broth or stracotto broth

## Directions

1. Remove the hardest leaves from the artichokes, trim the tips and peel the stems, keeping them all, even if they are long
2. Cut them into four wedges and boil them in salted water for only five minutes
3. Remove them dry, pound them in a mortar and pass them through a sieve
4. Measure the pulp thus obtained with all those usual ingredients in other vegetable molded dishes, that is: eggs, not being stingy of one more to make it set, two or three spoonfuls of béchamel where butter does not lack, parmesan cheese, salt and the aroma of nutmeg, but taste the mixture several times to bring it to the right flavor
5. If you have meat broth or stracotto broth, it is not bad to add a little of this too, and if the artichokes are tender, instead of passing them you can leave them in small wedges
6. Cook it in a water bath in a perforated mold if you have a meat sauce to fill it with; if not, put it in a smooth mold and serve it as a course between other dishes
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di cavolfiore (Cauliflower Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of cauliflower (approximately 350 grams net)
- 3 deciliters milk
- 3 eggs
- 60 grams butter
- 30 grams grated Parmesan cheese
- 1 tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Boil the head of cauliflower halfway in water and then cut it into small pieces
2. Sauté them with half of the butter, salting them, and when they have absorbed the fat, finish cooking them with a little of the milk; you can then leave them as is or pass them through a sieve
3. With the remaining butter and milk and one tablespoon of flour make a béchamel and add it to the mixture together with the eggs, first whisked, and the Parmesan
4. Cook it in a smooth mold like the green bean soufflé and serve it hot
5. This quantity is enough for six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di fagiuolini (Green Bean Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams tender green beans
- salt
- meat sauce or oil, shallot, parsley, celery
- pepper
- 30 grams butter
- 1 scant tablespoon flour
- 2 deciliters milk
- 1 handful grated Parmesan cheese
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the ends and string from the green beans
2. Throw them into boiling water with a pinch of salt and as soon as they return to a boil, drain them and throw them into fresh water
3. If you have meat sauce, season them with this and butter; if not, make a soffritto with a quarter shallot, some parsley leaves, a piece of celery and oil, and when the shallot takes color, throw in the green beans, seasoning with salt and pepper and cooking them with a little water if necessary
4. Prepare a béchamel with 30 grams of butter, one scant tablespoon of flour and two deciliters of milk
5. With this, with a handful of grated Parmesan and four whisked eggs, bind the green beans, already cooled
6. Mix and pour the mixture into a smooth mold, first buttered and whose bottom is covered with a sheet
7. Cook it over heat or in a bain-marie and serve it hot
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Farina Dolce (Molded Chestnut Flour Dessert)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet flour, that is chestnut flour, 200 grams
- Chocolate, 50 grams
- Sugar, 30 grams
- Butter, 25 grams
- Candied citron, 20 grams
- Sweet almonds, 12 and some pistachios
- Milk, half a liter
- Eggs, 3
- Whipped cream scented with vanilla, 150 grams

## Directions

1. First blanch the almonds and pistachios; cut the pistachios in half, the almonds into thin strips or small pieces and toast them
2. Shape the candied citron into small pieces
3. Melt the chocolate over the fire in a deciliter of the said milk, then add the sugar and butter and set it aside
4. Place the flour in a pan and gradually pour in the rest of the milk while stirring well so no lumps form; then add it to the chocolate and put the mixture on the fire to cook it
5. Once cooked, let it cool to add the eggs, first the yolks, then the beaten whites, and lastly the almonds, pistachios and candied citron
6. Now take a ring mold, grease it with cold butter and pour in the mixture to firm it in a water bath
7. Before unmolding, surround it completely with chopped ice mixed with salt to freeze it, and send it to the table with the whipped cream filling mentioned above
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Finocchi (Fennel Molded Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- fennel
- salted water
- butter
- salt
- milk
- béchamel sauce
- parmesan cheese
- eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the hardest leaves from the fennel, cut them into small wedges and cook them two-thirds of the way in salted water, then drain them well and put them to brown with a piece of butter
2. Season with salt and when they have absorbed the butter, wet them with a little milk; when they have absorbed this too, add some béchamel
3. Remove from the fire and leave them as they are, or pass them through a sieve; when they are cold mix in grated parmesan cheese and, depending on the quantity, three or four beaten eggs
4. Pour the mixture into a smooth mold, or with a hole in the middle, proceeding as for the other molded dishes; cook it in a water bath and serve it hot as a course between other dishes or with a boiled capon
5. You can also garnish it with a delicate preparation of giblets and sweetbreads
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Funghi (Mushroom Mold)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms, 600 grams
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Meat stock
- Béchamel
- Eggs, 5
- Parmigiano

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms well from soil and wash them, then chop them finely to the size of a chickpea or less.
2. Put them on the fire with butter, salt and pepper and when they have softened somewhat, cook them through with meat stock.
3. Remove from fire, bind them with béchamel, eggs and parmigiano and set the mixture in a bain-marie.
4. 600 grams of mushrooms in nature with five eggs will make a mold sufficient for ten people.
5. Serve it hot and as an intermediate course.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di pasta lievita (Molded Leavened Pastry)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Hungarian flour, 300 grams
- butter, 70 grams
- butter, 30 grams
- brewer's yeast, 30 grams
- egg yolks, 3
- cream or very good milk, 2 deciliters
- salt, to taste

## Directions

1. Note that the cream may be too much if needed.
2. With a quarter of the said flour, the brewer's yeast and a little of the said lukewarm cream form a small loaf like that of Krapfen and let it rise.
3. Mix the rest of the flour with the 70 grams of butter dissolved in winter, the egg yolks, the salt, the small loaf when it has doubled in size and enough lukewarm cream to obtain a dough of proper consistency that can be worked with a wooden spoon within a basin.
4. When with the working it shows signs of detaching from the walls of the vessel, let it rise in a warm place and, once achieved, turn it onto the work surface over a dusting of flour and with floured hands flatten it to the thickness of half a centimeter.
5. Take a smooth mold with a hole in the middle with a capacity of about a liter and a half of water, because with the dough it must only be filled halfway, grease it and flour it and, the dough cut into strips, arrange them in this manner.
6. At each layer of strips, as long as you have them, grease them with the 30 grams of butter mentioned above using a brush.
7. Cover the mold and having let the mixture rise again, when it reaches the opening, bake it in the oven or in a country oven.
8. Fill it after having turned it out and serve it at the table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Piselli Freschi (Fresh Pea Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Shelled peas, 600 grams
- Fat and lean prosciutto, 50 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Flour, 20 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Parmesan, one spoonful

## Directions

1. Make a battutino with the prosciutto, a small young onion, and a pinch of parsley. Put it on the fire with oil and when it takes color, pour in the peas, seasoning them with salt and pepper.
2. When cooked, pass a quarter of them through a sieve and unite the puree to a roux made with the indicated butter and flour and diluted on the fire with meat stock or broth.
3. Then mix everything together, including the Parmesan, and cook the mixture in a bain-marie in a smooth mold with buttered paper underneath.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Riso col Sugo Guarnito di Rigaglie (Rice Mold with Meat Sauce and Offal Garnish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 300 grams
- Butter
- Water
- Meat broth
- Offal (liver, heart, etc.)
- Prosciutto slices (optional)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mushrooms or truffles (optional)
- Parmesan cheese
- Eggs, 2

## Directions

1. Make a good meat broth and use it both for the rice and the offal.
2. Sauté the offal in butter with optional prosciutto slices, season with salt and pepper, and cook through with the broth. The aroma of mushrooms or truffles enhances it.
3. Sauté the rice in butter until dry, then cook it with boiling water and finish with the broth and parmesan cheese. When the rice has cooled slightly, add the two beaten eggs.
4. Butter a smooth, round or oval mold, line the bottom with buttered paper, and pour in the rice to set it in a country oven.
5. Once unmolded, pour the offal sauce (condensed slightly with a pinch of flour) over the top and serve the offal around it, allowing it to be immersed in the sauce.
6. This serves five or six people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di riso colle rigaglie (Rice Mold with Giblets)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 150 grams
- Parmesan cheese, 30 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Milk, about 7 deciliters
- Eggs, 3
- Salt, to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in milk, adding butter, salt it and lastly, when it is cold, add the eggs.
2. Pour it into a smooth mold with a hole in the middle with buttered paper underneath, place it briefly in a water bath so it does not harden, unmold it hot and garnish the center with giblets.
3. This amount will serve five persons.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Savoiardi con lo Zabaione (Ladyfinger Mold with Zabaglione)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ladyfingers, 100 grams
- Malaga raisins, 70 grams
- Sultana raisins, 50 grams
- Candied citron, 30 grams
- Marsala wine, as needed
- Milk, 2 deciliters
- Whole eggs, 2
- Sugar, 50 grams
- Vanilla flavoring
- Whole eggs, 2
- Marsala wine, 1-1/2 deciliters
- Sugar, 50 grams

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds from the malaga raisins
2. Cut the candied citron into small pieces
3. Take a ring mold and butter it with cold butter, then lightly dip only the surface of the ladyfingers in marsala and fill the mold with them, interspersing layers with the raisins and candied fruit
4. Make a cream with the milk, 2 whole eggs, 50 grams sugar, and vanilla flavor
5. Pour this raw cream into the mold over the ladyfingers
6. Cook it in a water bath, unmold it while hot, and before bringing it to the table, fill the hole overflowing with a zabaglione made from 2 whole eggs, 1-1/2 deciliters marsala, and 50 grams sugar
7. Whip the zabaglione mixture with a whisk in a basin over the fire
8. You can substitute the raisins with candied fruits, or a mixture of raisins and candied citron and orange
9. This will serve six people. It is a dessert that pleases
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Spinaci (Spinach Molded Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon powder
- béchamel sauce (number 137)
- butter
- eggs
- parmesan cheese
- raisins or currants with seeds removed, or dried raisins

## Directions

1. Boil the spinach in very little water or only with the water that drips from it when you lift it from the fresh water where it was soaking
2. Pass it through a sieve and, gauging by the quantity, season it with salt, pepper, cinnamon powder, a few spoonfuls of béchamel, butter, eggs, parmesan cheese, a handful of raisins or currants from which the seeds have been removed, or dried raisins
3. Mix well and pour the mixture into a smooth mold with a hole in the middle, cooking it in a water bath
4. Unmold it while still hot and serve it filled with a delicate sauce of chicken giblets or of sweetbreads, or of milk-fed veal, or of all these things together, mixed with pieces of dried mushrooms
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sformato di Zucchini Passati (Zucchini Mold)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini, 600 grams
- Parmigiano, 40 grams
- Eggs, 4
- One quarter onion
- Celery
- Carrot
- Parsley
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter, 60 grams
- Flour, 2 tablespoons
- Milk, 4 deciliters

## Directions

1. Make a battuto with a quarter onion, celery, carrot and parsley.
2. Put it on the fire with oil and when it takes color pour in the zucchini cut into small pieces seasoned with salt and pepper.
3. When they are browned, cook them through with water, pass them dry through the sieve and add the parmigiano and eggs.
4. Make a béchamel with 60 grams of butter, two tablespoons of flour and 4 deciliters of milk.
5. Mix everything together and, using a smooth perforated mold, cook it in a bain-marie.
6. Unmold it hot, fill the center with a delicate sauce and serve it.
7. This amount will serve eight or ten people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sgonfiotto di Farina Gialla (Cornmeal Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, half a liter
- Cornmeal, 170 grams
- Sugar, 30 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Egg whites, 6
- Egg yolks, 3
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Make a farinata: that is, pour the cornmeal into boiling milk, or better, if you wish to preserve it from lumps, first dilute the meal with a little cold milk and pour it into the boiling milk mixing well
2. Let it boil briefly, and when you remove it from the fire, add the butter, sugar and salt
3. When it has cooled, break the egg yolks into it and lastly pour in the well-beaten egg whites; mix gently and pour the mixture into a smooth mold or pan that you have buttered and dusted with wheat flour
4. Cook it in an oven with heat below and above and when it has risen, serve it immediately so that, if possible, it remains well fluffy and does not fall
5. It is better, in my opinion, to make this dish in a heat-resistant pan and bring it to the table without moving it
6. This dose will serve six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Siroppo di Cedro (Lemon Syrup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Garden lemons, 3 number
- Fine white sugar, 600 grams
- Water, one table glass corresponding to about 3 decilitres

## Directions

1. Remove from the lemons, without squeezing it, the internal pulp cleaning it well from the membranes and seeds.
2. Put the water on the fire with the peel of one of the said lemons cut into strips and thinly with a knife and when it begins to boil pour in the sugar.
3. Wait for it to bubble a few times, then remove the peel and pour in the pulp of the lemons.
4. Boil until the syrup has reduced and cooked to the point, which is known by the pearl that forms boiling and by the color of white wine that it acquires.
5. Preserve it in a glass vessel if possible, to take it by the spoonful and dissolve it in fresh water: thus obtaining an excellent and refreshing drink which I am amazed is lacking among the drinks of cafés, in various provinces of Italy.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Siroppo di Cedro (Lemon Syrup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Garden lemons, 3
- Fine white sugar, 600 grams
- Water, 1 tablespoon glass (about 3 deciliters)

## Directions

1. Remove the internal pulp from the lemons without squeezing it, cleaning it well of peel and seeds
2. Put the water on the fire with the peel of one of the said lemons cut into thin ribbons with a knife, and when it begins to boil pour in the sugar
3. Wait for it to boil a few times, then remove the peel and pour in the lemon pulp
4. Let it boil until the syrup has reduced and cooked to the right point, which is known by the pearl it makes when boiling and by the white wine color it acquires
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Siroppo di Lampone (Raspberry Syrup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries
- Sugar
- Citric acid

## Directions

1. After mashing the fruit well with your hands, proceed the same way as the currant syrup recipe with the same proportions of sugar and citric acid
2. Since this fruit contains less gluten than currants, the fermentation period will be shorter
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Siroppo di Ribes (Currant Syrup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants, 3 kilograms (liquid after fermentation)
- Powdered white sugar, 4 kilograms
- Citric acid, 30 grams

## Directions

1. Remove the currants from their bunches as you would do when making grape must and leave them in a cool place in an earthenware or wooden vessel
2. When it has begun to ferment (which can happen even after three or four days), push down the cap and stir it with a ladle twice a day, continuing this operation until it stops rising
3. Pass it through cloth a little at a time, squeezing it well with your hands if you do not have a press, and pass the pressed juice through a filter two or three times and more if necessary to obtain a very clear liquid
4. Put it on the fire and when it begins to boil, pour in the sugar and citric acid in the following proportions: 3 kilograms of liquid, 4 kilograms of finest powdered white sugar, and 30 grams of citric acid
5. Stir continuously with a ladle so the sugar does not stick, let it boil hard for two or three minutes, taste it to add more citric acid if necessary, and when cool bottle it and store it in the cellar
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sogliole in Gratella (Sole on the Grill)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- large sole fish
- lard, virgin and cold
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the guts, scrape off the scales, wash and dry the sole well
2. Lightly coat with cold virgin lard that does not smell rancid
3. Season with salt and pepper and roll in breadcrumbs
4. Melt a little more lard in a small pan and brush the sole with a feather as you grill it and when you turn it over on the grill
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Soufflet di Cioccolata (Chocolate Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 120 grams
- Potato starch, 80 grams
- Chocolate, 80 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Milk, 4 decilitres
- Eggs, 3
- Rum, one spoonful

## Directions

1. Put the butter on the fire and when it is melted pour in the grated chocolate; once this is also liquefied, pour in the potato starch and then the hot milk little by little, and, stirring always with force, add the sugar.
2. Once the mixture is well combined and the starch is cooked, let it cool. Last, add the rum and the eggs, first the yolks, then the beaten whites; and if there were more than three, the dessert would turn out even better.
3. Butter a baking pan that can withstand heat, pour the mixture into it, place it in a country-style oven or simply over a burner between two fires, and when it has puffed up serve it hot.
4. This is a sufficient portion for six people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Soufflet di Farina di Patate (Potato Starch Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 100 grams
- Potato starch, 80 grams
- Milk, half a litre
- Eggs, 3 and two or three whites
- Flavour of vanilla or lemon zest

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and flour in a saucepan and pour the cold milk into it little by little, stirring. Put the mixture on the fire.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Soufflet di Luisetta (Luisetta's Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, half a litre
- Sugar, 80 grams
- Flour, 70 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Sweet almonds, 30 grams
- Eggs, 3
- Flavour of vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, dry them and pound them very fine with a spoonful of the said sugar.
2. Make a béchamel with the butter, flour and milk poured hot. Before removing it from the fire add the almonds, the sugar and the flavour. Once it is cooled, combine with the eggs, first the yolks and then the beaten whites.
3. Butter a baking pan that can withstand heat, pour the mixture into it and finish cooking it in a country-style oven.
4. This will be sufficient for five or six people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Soufflet di Pollo (Chicken Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken meat without skin, 80 grams
- Flour, 50 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Grated Parmesan, 20 grams
- Milk, 2.5 deciliters
- Eggs, 4
- Salt, a pinch

## Directions

1. Make a béchamel sauce with the butter, flour and milk.
2. When cooked and no longer boiling, add the Parmesan, salt, egg yolks and the chicken finely minced with a half-moon cutter.
3. Then whip the egg whites until stiff.
4. Gently add these to the mixture.
5. Pour it into a pan that can withstand fire.
6. Brown it lightly in a country oven and serve it hot, though it is also good cold.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Soufflé di Castagne (Chestnut Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large chestnuts, 150 grams
- Sugar, 90 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- Eggs, 5
- Milk, 2 deciliters
- Maraschino, 2 tablespoons
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Boil the chestnuts in water for only five minutes, which is enough to shell them while hot and remove their inner skin
2. Then cook them in the said milk and pass them through a sieve, then blend them with the sugar, melted butter, maraschino, and vanilla
3. Finally add the eggs, first the yolks, then the well-whipped whites
4. Grease a metal baking dish with butter, pour the mixture into it, cook it in a country oven and before sending it to the table dust it with powdered sugar
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Soufflé di Farina di Patate (Potato Starch Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 100 grams
- Potato starch flour, 80 grams
- Milk, half a liter
- Eggs, 3 whole eggs plus 2 or 3 extra whites
- Vanilla flavoring or lemon zest

## Directions

1. Place the sugar and flour in a saucepan and pour in the cold milk little by little, stirring
2. Set the mixture on the fire so it thickens, stirring constantly, without worrying about making it boil
3. Add the vanilla or lemon zest, and when it becomes lukewarm, mix in the 3 egg yolks, then whip the whites and fold them in gently
4. Pour it into a metal baking dish and place it on the stovetop, cover it with the lid of a country oven between two fires and wait for it to puff up and brown lightly
5. Then dust it with powdered sugar and send it immediately to the table, where it will be praised for its delicacy; if any remains, you will find it is also good cold
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Soufflé di Riso (Rice Soufflé)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice, 100 grams
- Sugar, 80 grams
- Milk, 6 deciliters
- Eggs, 3
- A small piece of butter
- Rum, one tablespoon
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Boil the rice in the milk, but if you do not cook it very much you will make nothing good
2. At half cooking time add the butter and sugar, including the vanillaed sugar for the flavoring, and after it is cooked and cooled, mix in the yolks, the rum, and the whipped whites
3. For the rest, follow the same procedure as for the potato starch soufflé
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spaghetti alla Rustica (Rustic Spaghetti)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cloves garlic
- parsley
- basil (if desired)
- oil
- 6 or 7 tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- spaghetti or vermicelli
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Make a small mixture with two cloves of garlic and a good pinch of parsley and the aroma of basil if desired; put it in a pan with a good amount of oil.
2. As soon as the garlic begins to color, add six or seven tomatoes cut into pieces, seasoning them with salt and pepper.
3. When well cooked, pass the sauce through a strainer.
4. Dress the spaghetti or vermicelli (cooked briefly in plenty of salted water) with the sauce and grated Parmesan cheese.
5. Send immediately to table so they remain succulent.
6. Tagliatelle can also be seasoned this way.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spaghetti coi Naselli (Spaghetti with Hake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Spaghetti, 500 grams
- Hake (cod), 300 grams
- Butter, 60 grams
- Oil, 4 spoonfuls
- Marsala, 4 spoonfuls
- Scent of nutmeg
- One onion of medium size
- Salt and pepper
- Tomato juice or conserve dissolved in water
- Parmesan

## Directions

1. Chop a medium-sized onion and squeeze it between your hands to remove its bitterness
2. Put it on the fire with the oil and when it begins to brown, throw in the hake cut into pieces and season them with salt and pepper
3. Once browned, pour in tomato juice or conserve dissolved in water to cook them, and then pass them through a wire sieve, wetting them with a little hot water if needed, to extract all the pulp
4. Put the passed mixture back on the fire with the butter, Marsala, nutmeg and when it has come to a boil, if the sauce does not need to be reduced to the right consistency, dress the spaghetti cooked in salted water with this sauce and Parmesan
5. This is a portion for five people and is a pasta course that will please because it is not a jumble as the description might suggest
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spaghetti coi Piselli (Spaghetti with Peas)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams spaghetti
- 500 grams shelled peas
- 70 grams cured meat
- 1 small onion
- 1 clove fresh garlic
- celery ribs
- parsley
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- dill stems (if available)
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Make a mixture with the cured meat, a small onion, fresh garlic and some celery ribs and parsley.
2. Put it in a pan with oil and when it begins to take color, add the peas together with some chopped dill stems if you have them.
3. Season with salt and pepper and cook.
4. Chop the spaghetti by hand to make them short, less than half a finger long, cook them in salted water, drain well, mix them with the peas and serve with Parmesan cheese on the side.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spaghetti col Sugo di Seppie (Spaghetti with Cuttlefish Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 medium cuttlefish (650-700 grams total)
- 100 grams crumb of bread
- parsley
- 1 clove garlic
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- 1 onion of medium size
- tomato juice or conserve in large quantity
- water
- 500 grams spaghetti
- 30 grams Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the cuttlefish by removing the bone, mouth apparatus, eyes, digestive tube, and ink sac.
2. Make a mixture with 100 grams bread crumb, a good pinch of parsley and one clove of garlic, add the tentacles (two per cuttlefish) finely chopped, season with oil, salt and pepper, and use this to fill the cuttlefish sac, sewing the mouth closed.
3. Chop one onion of medium size, squeeze it to remove bitterness and put it in a pan with oil over heat; when it takes color, add the cuttlefish and season with salt and pepper.
4. Wait until they color, then cook them slowly with much tomato juice or conserve, adding water gradually.
5. Boil for three hours, ensuring enough sauce remains to season the pasta.
6. Season 500 grams of spaghetti with the sauce and grated Parmesan cheese.
7. Serve the cuttlefish afterwards as a fish course in sauce.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spaghetti colle Acciughe (Spaghetti with Anchovies)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium spaghetti, 350 grams
- Five anchovies
- Good oil in abundance
- A pinch of pepper
- 50 grams of butter
- Tomato juice or conserve

## Directions

1. Wash the anchovies and clean them well of spines and bones, chop them somewhat with the crescent blade and put them on the fire with good oil in abundance and a pinch of pepper
2. Do not let them boil, but when they begin to heat, add 50 grams of butter, a little tomato juice or conserve and lift them from the fire
3. Dress the spaghetti cooked in lightly salted water with this sauce, making sure they remain firm
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spaghetti con il Sugo di Midolla (Spaghetti with Bone Marrow Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumbs soaked in broth
- butter
- Marsala wine
- nutmeg
- spaghetti
- salted water
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Pass bread crumbs through a wire sieve, moistening them with a little hot water if needed to extract all the pulp.
2. Return the strained mixture to the heat with butter, Marsala, nutmeg and when it reaches a boil, if the sauce does not need to be reduced to the right consistency, season the spaghetti (cooked in salted water) with this sauce and Parmesan cheese.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spaghetti con la Balsamella (Spaghetti with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams spaghetti
- 3 deciliters very good milk
- 20 grams butter
- 5 grams flour
- Parmesan cheese
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the spaghetti dry from the water and season on a platter with Parmesan cheese and butter in the usual way.
2. Pour over it a béchamel sauce made from 3 deciliters of very good milk, 20 grams of butter and 5 grams of flour (corresponding to half a tablespoon).
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spaghetti da Quaresima (Lenten Spaghetti)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams shelled walnuts
- 60 grams breadcrumbs
- 30 grams powdered white sugar
- 1 heaping teaspoon fine spices
- 400 grams spaghetti
- oil
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pound the walnuts together with breadcrumbs, add powdered sugar and the aroma of spices.
2. Remove the spaghetti dry from the water and season first with oil and pepper, then with this paste in good measure.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spalla d'Agnello all'Ungherese (Lamb Shoulder Hungarian Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- two new onions or three or four small white onions
- a small piece of butter
- salt
- pepper
- another small piece of butter
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Cut the shoulder into thin pieces about three fingers square.
2. Slice two new onions or three or four small white onions; put them to soften with a small piece of butter, and when they have taken on a deep red color, throw in the lamb and season with salt and pepper.
3. Wait for the meat to begin to color and add another small piece of butter mixed with flour; stir and let it take on a nice color, then finish cooking it with broth poured in little by little.
4. Do not serve it dry, but with a certain quantity of its own sauce.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sparnocchie (Scampi)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- scampi
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- flour
- oil

## Directions

1. It is best to roast them on a grill without any seasoning, then shell them and season with oil, pepper, salt and lemon juice.
2. Small scampi can also be dusted with flour and fried as they are, or in the manner indicated for mantis shrimp.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spezie Fini (Fine Spice Blend)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Nutmeg, 2 whole
- Ceylon cinnamon (also called queen cinnamon), 50 grams
- Clove pepper, 30 grams
- Cloves, 20 grams
- Sweet almonds, 20 grams

## Directions

1. Pound everything together in a bronze mortar
2. Pass the spices through a fine silk sieve
3. Conserve them in a glass jar with a ground glass stopper, or in a small bottle with a cork stopper, and they will keep for years with the same fragrance as the first day
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spezie Fini (Fine Spice Mixture)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Nutmegs, 2 whole
- Ceylon cinnamon, 50 grams
- Clove pepper, 30 grams
- Cloves, 20 grams
- Sweet almonds, 20 grams

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients together
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spinaci di Magro all'Uso di Romagna (Lean Spinach Romagna Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Oil
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sugar
- Raisins

## Directions

1. Boil spinach with only the water that drains from soaking
2. Squeeze well
3. Stew with a soffritto of oil, garlic and parsley, salt and pepper
4. Leave whole
5. Season with a pinch of sugar and some raisins from which the stems have been removed
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spinaci per Contorno (Spinach as a Side Dish)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach, boiled and chopped
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Boil spinach and chop finely with a crescent blade
2. Prepare in any of these ways:
3. Method 1: Cook with only butter, salt and pepper, adding a little meat sauce if available, or a few tablespoons of broth or cream
4. Method 2: Cook with a very small soffritto of finely chopped onion drawn with butter
5. Method 3: Cook with only butter, salt and pepper as the first, adding a pinch of Parmesan
6. Method 4: Cook with butter, the merest drop of oil and tomato sauce or tomato paste
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Spumone di The (Tea Mousse Ice Cream)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Panna montata (whipped cream), as that which dairymen prepare, 250 grams
- Acqua (water), 200 grams
- Zucchero (sugar), 100 grams
- The (tea), the finest quality, 15 grams
- Rossi d'uovo (egg yolks), 3 number
- Colla di pesce (fish gelatin), 3 leaves

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the tea and keep it infused, in a state almost of ebullition, for 40 minutes
2. Then strain it through a fine cloth, squeezing hard to extract all the flavor, and you will see it appear black as coffee
3. With this liquid, with the egg yolks and with the sugar make a cream as in recipe 753, and regulating yourself in the same manner, add the gelatin, then unite the cream to the whipped cream mixing slowly and, having poured the mixture into an ice cream mold, place it among ice and salt as with biscuit
4. It should be enough for eight persons
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stiacciata alla Livornese (Livornese Flatbread)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve eggs
- Finest flour, 1.8 kilograms
- Sugar, 600 grams
- Superfine oil, 200 grams
- Butter, 70 grams
- Baker's yeast, 30 grams
- Anise, 20 grams
- Vin Santo, 1.5 deciliters
- Marsala, 0.5 deciliter
- Orange blossom water, 1 deciliter

## Directions

1. Mix the two kinds of wine and infuse the anise in a little of this liquid after washing them well
2. Late in the evening perform the first operation: dissolve the baker's yeast in half a glass of lukewarm water, add enough flour to form a dough of proper consistency, place it on top of the flour mound inside a bowl, cover it with a layer of the same flour and keep the bowl protected from air and in the kitchen
3. In the morning when the dough is well risen, place it on the work surface, flatten it and remix it with one egg, one tablespoon of oil, one of sugar, one of wine and enough flour to form a larger dough again, mixing everything well without overworking it, and replace it on the flour and cover it as before
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stiacciata coi Siccioli (Flatbread with Cracklings)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread yeast, 650 grams
- Powdered sugar, 200 grams
- Cracklings, 100 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- Lard, 40 grams
- Five eggs
- Scent of orange or lemon zest

## Directions

1. Prepare it the evening before; first work the yeast on the work surface without the seasonings, then in a bowl for more than half an hour by hand, adding the ingredients and eggs gradually
2. Cover well and place in a warm location so it rises during the night
3. The following morning remix it and pour it into a buttered and floured copper baking pan where it should be no more than two fingers thick
4. Send it to the warm chamber for the second rising and pass it to the oven
5. You can also complete the entire operation at home and cook it in a countryside oven, but be warned this is a somewhat difficult dough to succeed with, especially if the season is very cold
6. It is better to make it when the weather is mild, but do not be discouraged at the first attempt
7. If in the morning it has risen little or not at all, add baker's yeast in a quantity slightly more than a walnut, letting it first rise separately with a pinch of flour and lukewarm water
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stiacciata Unta (Oiled Flatbread)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread dough, 700 grams
- Lard, 120 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Cracklings, 60 grams
- Four egg yolks
- A pinch of salt
- Scent of orange or lemon zest

## Directions

1. Work it moderately so it does not lose its strength
2. Made in the evening and left in a warm place it rises by itself; made in the morning it will need three hours in an earthen chamber
3. If you want it without cracklings add two more egg yolks and another 30 grams of lard
4. Half this amount is enough for five or six people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stiacciate alla Livornese (Livornese Flatbread)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- brewer's yeast
- water
- flour
- egg
- oil
- sugar
- wine
- butter
- orange flower water
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Dissolve the brewer's yeast with half a glass of lukewarm water, adding enough flour to form a dough of proper consistency, which you will place on the mound of flour within a bowl, covering it with a layer of the same flour. Keep the bowl protected from air and in the kitchen, or in the warmest place in your house if you have one.
2. In the morning, when the said dough has risen well, place it on the work surface, flatten it and remix it with one egg, one tablespoon of oil, one of sugar, one of wine and enough flour to form once again a larger dough, mixing everything well without working it too much. Replace it on the flour and cover it as before.
3. After six or seven hours, which will be needed for the dough to rise again, add three eggs, three tablespoons of oil, three of sugar, three of wine, and enough flour to form the usual dough and let it rise again, proceeding always in the same manner. To know the point of fermentation, calculate that the dough must increase about three times in volume.
4. Add five eggs, five tablespoons of sugar, five of oil, five of wine and the necessary flour.
5. Add the three remaining eggs and everything else, melting the butter over heat, and mix very well to make the dough homogeneous. If the dough becomes somewhat soft, which is unlikely, add more flour to bring it to the proper consistency.
6. Divide it into three or four parts forming balls and place each one in a baking pan on a sheet of paper that extends beyond the rim, greased with butter, where it lies flat; and since as the dose of ingredients increases, fermentation is slower, the last time, if you wish to hasten it, place the flatbreads to rise in a warm place and when they are well puffed and trembling, brush them with a brush first dipped in orange flower water, then in egg yolk. Bake them in the oven at a very moderate temperature, noting that this last part is the most important and difficult because, being large in volume, there is the chance that strong heat reaches the surface immediately, and the interior remains soft.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stoccafisso in Umido (Stockfish in Stew)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Stockfish, soaked, 500 grams (300 grams backbone, 200 grams belly)
- oil, in abundance
- garlic, 1 large clove or 2 small cloves
- parsley, a good pinch
- salt and pepper
- tomato sauce or fresh tomatoes without skin and seeds, 6-7 spoonfuls
- water, hot
- potato, cut into small pieces

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and all spines from the stockfish.
2. Cut the backbone part into thin slices and the belly into squares two fingers wide.
3. Make a soffritto with abundant oil, garlic, and parsley.
4. When colored, add the stockfish and season with salt and pepper.
5. Stir to allow it to absorb flavor, then add tomato sauce or fresh tomatoes without skin and seeds.
6. Boil gently for at least three hours, adding hot water little by little.
7. After two hours of boiling, add a potato cut into small pieces.
8. This quantity is sufficient for three or four people.
9. This is an appetizing dish, but not for weak stomachs.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Storione in Fricandò (Sturgeon Braised)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of sturgeon weighing at least 500 grams
- Lardons of cured pork fat, seasoned with salt and pepper
- Oil
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Broth
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Take a large piece of sturgeon weighing at least 500 grams, skin it and lard it with lardons of cured pork fat seasoned beforehand with salt and pepper.
2. Tie it crosswise, dust it with flour, place it on the fire with oil and butter and season it again with salt and pepper.
3. When it is browned on all sides, baste it with broth to finish cooking.
4. Before serving, squeeze lemon juice over it and serve it with its own sauce.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Storni in istufato (Stewed Starlings)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Six starlings
- One quarter of a large onion
- Lard fat, 30 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Three or four strips of fatty and lean prosciutto
- Juniper berries, 2
- Sage leaves
- Salt and pepper
- Dry white wine or marsala and water

## Directions

1. Make a fine battuto (chopped mixture) with a quarter of a large onion and 30 grams of lard fat.
2. Heat this in a pot with 20 grams of butter, three or four strips of prosciutto, and 2 juniper berries.
3. Place the starlings on top without gutting them, garnish with sage leaves, and season with salt and pepper.
4. Turn them often until they absorb the flavors and the onion is well colored.
5. Moisten with a little dry white wine, then add enough wine (or a mixture of water and marsala) so that the total liquid added is three deciliters, or if lacking white wine, use two deciliters water and one of marsala.
6. Cover the pot with a sheet of paper folded four times, held down by a heavy lid, and simmer gently until completely cooked.
7. Serve the birds with their sauce.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stracotto alla Bizzarra (Bizarrely Braised Veal)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal without bone, 700 to 800 grams
- Lard, 100 grams
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Two sage leaves
- A bunch of rosemary
- Half a garlic clove
- Water
- One teaspoon of flour
- A piece of butter
- Beef broth, one ladle
- Marsala wine, one finger measure of a glass

## Directions

1. Lard the veal with pieces of lard the thickness of a finger, which you will have first seasoned with salt and pepper, and season the meat the same way.
2. Tie it so it stays compact and place it in a pot half covered with water with two sage leaves, a bunch of rosemary and half a garlic clove; if the meat is very tender use less water.
3. When, in boiling, it has become dry, let it take on color with a teaspoon of flour.
4. Add a piece of butter, then wet it with a ladle of broth and a finger measure of a glass of marsala.
5. Strain the sauce without squeezing it and pour it over the piece of meat when you serve it at table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stracotto alla Fiorentina (Florentine Braised Veal)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal with bone or joint, 500 grams
- Cured meat (carnesecca), 50 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- One quarter of a large onion
- One small carrot
- Two pieces of celery
- Salt and pepper to taste
- A pinch of flour
- Tomato juice or tomato paste
- Water
- Dried mushrooms, a few pieces (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop the onion, carrot, and celery coarsely and cut the cured meat into small dice.
2. Put everything together in a pot and season with salt and pepper.
3. Turn the meat frequently and when it is browned, sprinkle a pinch of flour over it.
4. Pour tomato juice or paste over it and cook it through with water added little by little.
5. The flour serves to bind the sauce and give it color, but be careful that it does not burn or it will give an unpleasant taste and a nearly black color which the sauce should not have.
6. Strain the sauce, and if desired, add aroma with a few pieces of dried mushrooms, first softened in hot water and boiled a little in the sauce.
7. Cook the macaroni in salted water and drain well, then keep it briefly in the sauce near the fire and season with butter and a modest amount of parmesan cheese, as this can be added at table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stracotto di Vitella (Braised Veal)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- lean veal meat, including bone or joint, 500 grams
- cured meat, 50 grams
- butter, 30 grams
- one quarter of a large onion
- one small carrot
- two pieces of celery
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the onion, carrot and celery into large pieces and the cured meat into small dice.
2. Put everything together on the fire and season with salt and pepper.
3. Turn (further instructions are cut off at the end of the page)
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Strichetti alla Bolognese (Bolognese Pasta Knots)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Eggs, two
- Grated Parmesan, 40 grams, fine
- Nutmeg flavor
- Broth

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with two eggs, 40 grams of finely grated Parmesan and the flavor of nutmeg
2. Pull out a sheet not too thin and cut it with the fluted wheel into so many strips wide a finger and a half
3. Then, with the same wheel, cut these strips diagonally and at the same distance of a finger and a half to make so many pieces in the shape of an almond
4. Take them one at a time and squeeze with your fingers the four points, two above and two below to form as it were two little rings attached together
5. Cook them in broth with little cooking
6. The dose of two eggs will be sufficient for five people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Strudel (Apple Strudel)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams reinette apples or tender apples of good quality
- 250 grams flour
- 100 grams butter
- 85 grams currants
- 85 grams powdered sugar
- zest of one lemon
- two or three pinches of ground cinnamon
- hot milk
- one egg
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with hot milk, a nut-sized piece of butter, one egg and a pinch of salt to make a rather firm dough and let it rest a little before using it.
2. Roll out with this dough a thin sheet like that of taglierini and, leaving the edges uncovered, spread the apples on top, which you will have first peeled, cleaned of cores and cut into thin slices.
3. On the layer of apples scatter the currants, the lemon zest, the cinnamon, the sugar and finally the 100 grams of melted butter, leaving a little aside for later use.
4. Once done, wrap the sheet over itself to form a filled roll and fit it into a buttered copper pan, accommodating the rounded shape as necessary; with the remaining butter grease the entire outer part of the pastry and send it to the oven.
5. Note that currants, or sultana raisins, are different from raisins. The latter are small and black; the former are twice as large, light chestnut in color and also without seeds. Scrape the lemon with a piece of glass.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stufatino di Muscolo (Braised Veal Muscle)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams of veal muscle or milk veal
- Oil
- Two cloves garlic, unpeeled but bruised
- Salt
- Pepper
- Half a spoonful of flour
- Tomato sauce or tomato paste
- Butter
- Water or broth
- Bread for toasting
- Fresh mushrooms or potatoes (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the muscle into small pieces
2. Put oil on the fire with two cloves of garlic slightly bruised; let it soften and then throw in the meat, seasoning with salt and pepper
3. Once browned, sprinkle half a spoonful of flour over it, add tomato sauce or paste and a piece of butter; then water or broth, little by little, and cook to completion, but arrange it so that there remains some sauce
4. Arrange slices of toasted bread on a platter, pour the stufatino over them and serve
5. You can also serve it without bread and add fresh mushrooms cut into slices, or potatoes when the meat is almost cooked
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stufatino di Petto di Vitella di Latte coi Finocchi (Braised Milk Veal Breast with Fennel)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk veal breast with bones
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Celery
- Carrot
- Cured meat
- Oil
- Pepper
- Salt
- Flour
- Tomato sauce or tomato paste
- Broth or water
- Butter
- Fennel

## Directions

1. Chop the milk veal breast leaving its bones
2. Make a battuto with garlic, parsley, celery, carrot and a proportional slice of cured meat; add oil, pepper, salt and put it on the fire together with the meat
3. Turn it often, and when it is somewhat browned, sprinkle a pinch of flour over it, a little tomato sauce or paste and cook it to completion with broth or water
4. Finally add a piece of butter and fennel cut into large pieces already reduced to half-doneness in water and sautéed in butter
5. Keep the pot always covered
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Stufato di lepre (Braised Hare)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a hare, cut into pieces
- One medium onion
- Garlic, 2 cloves
- Celery, one palm-length piece
- Rosemary leaves
- Butter, a small piece
- Oil, 2 tablespoons
- Prosciutto, four or five finger-width strips
- Salt, pepper, and spices
- White wine or marsala, half a glass
- Fresh or dried mushrooms, a handful
- Broth
- Tomato juice or tomato paste
- Butter for finishing

## Directions

1. Chop the hare into pieces.
2. Make a fine battuto with one medium onion, two garlic cloves, one palm-length piece of celery, and various rosemary leaves.
3. Heat a small piece of butter with two tablespoons of oil and four or five finger-width strips of prosciutto in a pot.
4. After five minutes of cooking, add the hare and season with salt, pepper, and spices.
5. Brown the meat, then moisten with half a glass of white wine or marsala.
6. Add a handful of fresh mushrooms, or dried mushrooms that have been softened.
7. Finish cooking with broth and tomato juice or paste.
8. Before serving, taste and add more butter if needed.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sugo di carne (Meat Gravy)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin slices of lard or cured meat (the latter is preferable)
- One large onion
- One carrot
- One celery rib
- Butter
- Lean beef meat in pieces or thin slices
- Salt
- Two cloves
- Small ladle of cold water (repeated three times)
- 1.5 liters hot water or broth made from spongy bones (for approximately 500 grams of meat)
- Chicken necks and heads (optional)

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of a pot with thin slices of lard or cured meat
2. Above these, slice a large onion, a carrot, and a celery rib
3. Add here and there small pieces of butter and above these ingredients spread lean beef meat in pieces or thin slices
4. Season with only salt and two cloves
5. Place the pot over fire without ever touching it
6. When you smell the odor of burnt onion, turn the meat, and when you see it well browned, indeed almost black, pour cold water from a small ladle
7. Repeat the operation of pouring water three times as it evaporates
8. Finally, if the quantity of meat is about 500 grams, pour into the pot 1.5 liters of hot water or preferably a broth made from spongy bones
9. Let it boil gently for five or six consecutive hours to reduce the gravy and extract all the substance from the meat
10. Pass it through a strainer and when its fat has congealed, forming a large layer on top, remove it all to make the gravy less heavy on the stomach
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Sugo di carne alla spagnuola (Spanish-Style Meat Gravy)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One kilogram of lean beef meat, including bone or joint
- 400 grams of beef cut into thin slices
- 1.5 liters of water
- Thin slices of lard and prosciutto
- Small pieces of butter
- One onion
- Salt
- One clove or nine to ten crushed peppercorns
- One teaspoon sugar
- One carrot cut into slices
- A bouquet garni composed of parsley, celery and other aromatic herbs

## Directions

1. From one kilogram of lean beef meat (including bone), take 400 grams cut into thin slices
2. With the rest, make broth as usual with 1.5 liters of water
3. Cover the bottom of a pot with slices of lard and prosciutto and some small pieces of butter
4. Slice an onion and place the thin slices of beef arranged on top
5. When the meat has taken on color over high heat from below, wet it with a ladle of the said broth, then turn it so it also colors on the other side, and after pour another ladle of broth
6. Then season with salt, one clove or nine to ten crushed peppercorns, and one teaspoon of sugar
7. Pour now all the rest of the broth, add a carrot cut into slices and a decorated bouquet that can be composed of parsley, celery and some other aromatic herb
8. Boil gently for about two hours, then remove the thin slices of meat, strain the gravy and remove the fat
9. With this you can wet soup and use it to flavor vegetables, or, thickening it with a mixture of potato flour and butter, dress dry soups
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tagliatelle all'Uso di Romagna (Tagliatelle in the Style of Romagna)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs and flour for pasta dough
- Ingredients for spaghetti alla rustica sauce
- Butter

## Directions

1. Make the sheet and cut it as tagliatelle
2. Cook them briefly, drain them well from the water and put them in a saucepan over the fire for a moment to absorb the seasoning
3. Season with spaghetti alla rustica sauce plus a piece of butter proportionate to the quantity of pasta
4. Mix gently and serve
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tagliatelle col Prosciutto (Ribbon Pasta with Prosciutto)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs and flour for pasta dough, no water
- One thick slice of prosciutto, both fat and lean
- Celery
- Carrot
- Butter
- Tomato juice or tomato paste
- Broth or water (if using tomato paste)
- Parmesan cheese
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cut a thick slice of prosciutto into small dice
2. Finely chop celery and carrot in quantities such that both together make approximately the volume of the prosciutto
3. Put these three ingredients on the fire together with a piece of butter proportionate to the amount of pasta
4. When the mixture has taken on color, add tomato juice or tomato paste, but with paste you need a ladle of broth or water
5. Make and roll out the pasta dough into a sheet somewhat thicker than thin noodles and cut into wider strips
6. Cook the tagliatelle briefly and salt them very little because of the prosciutto; remove them dry and season them with the sauce and parmesan cheese
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tagliatelle Verdi (Green Ribbon Pasta)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach, cooked and drained, a handful
- Eggs, 2
- Flour

## Directions

1. Cook spinach, squeeze it well and chop it with a curved knife
2. With 2 eggs and a handful of this chopped spinach, mix flour on a work surface to obtain a very firm pasta dough which you will work a lot by hand
3. With a rolling pin, roll it out to a thin sheet and when it begins to stick because of the herb which produces stickiness, sprinkle it lightly with flour
4. Wrap the sheet in a cloth and when it is dry cut it somewhat wider than thin noodles for broth, noting that the beauty of such pasta is its length which indicates the skill of who made it
5. As soon as the water comes to a boil remove the pasta dry and season it as with spaghetti alla rustica, or as with macaroni or tagliatelle, or simply with cheese and butter
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Taglierini di Semolino (Semolina Noodles)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine semolina
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Fine semolina is required and it needs to be soaked with eggs several hours before pulling out the sheet.
2. If when you are about to pull it out it turns out too soft, add a few pinches of dry semolina to reduce the dough to the necessary hardness so it does not stick to the rolling pin.
3. No salt or other ingredients are needed.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tartufi alla Bolognese (Bolognese-Style Truffles)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Black or white truffles
- Parmesan cheese
- Salt
- Pepper
- Best quality oil
- Lemon
- Butter (optional, in small amount)

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the truffles with a soft brush dipped in fresh water
2. Slice them very thin
3. Alternate the truffle slices with equally thin slices of Parmesan cheese
4. Arrange in layers on a tinned copper platter, starting with the truffles
5. Season with salt, pepper, and plenty of best quality oil
6. Bring just to a boil, then squeeze lemon juice over them and remove from heat immediately
7. Optionally add small pieces of butter, but use sparingly to avoid making them too heavy
8. Alternatively, serve raw by slicing very thin and seasoning with salt, pepper, and lemon juice
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Telline o arselle (Clams or Arkshells)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Clams or arkshells
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Serve them without sauce, pouring them over bread slices.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Telline o arselle in salsa d'uovo (Clams or Arkshells in Egg Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Clams or arkshells
- garlic
- oil
- parsley
- pepper
- egg yolks
- lemon juice
- flour
- broth
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Put the clams or arkshells on the fire with a soffritto of garlic, oil, parsley, and a pinch of pepper.
2. Shake them and keep the vessel covered so they do not dry out.
3. Remove them when they have opened.
4. For the sauce, use one or more egg yolks depending on the quantity, lemon juice, one teaspoon of flour, broth, and some of the juice that came out from the clams.
5. Cook it like a cream and pour it over the clams when you serve them at table.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Telline o Arselle in Salsa d'Uovo (Clams or Mussels in Egg Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- clams or mussels
- garlic
- oil
- parsley
- pepper
- egg yolks
- lemon juice
- flour
- broth
- juice from the clams

## Directions

1. Rinse the mussels well in fresh water. Place them in a pot with a soffritto of garlic, oil, parsley and a pinch of pepper, cover and shake the pot so they do not dry out. Remove them when they open and prepare the following sauce: one or more egg yolks depending on quantity, lemon juice, a teaspoon of flour, broth and some of the juice that came from the clams. Cook it like a cream and pour it over the clams when serving. Alternatively, serve without sauce poured over slices of bread dried by the fire.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Testicciuola d'Agnello (Braised Lamb's Head)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb's head
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Oil
- Broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Tomato sauce or tomato paste

## Directions

1. Cut the head lengthwise into two natural pieces
2. Make a soffritto with garlic, parsley and oil
3. When it has taken color, stop it with a ladle of broth
4. Add the head and season with salt and pepper
5. At half-way through cooking, add a piece of butter and a little tomato sauce or paste
6. Finish cooking with more broth if needed
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Timballo di piccioni (Pigeon Timbale)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Onion
- Celery
- Carrot
- Butter
- One or two squabs
- Squab giblets
- Chicken giblets
- Salt and pepper
- Broth
- Macaroni
- Béchamel sauce
- Parmesan cheese
- Ham, fatty and lean
- Nutmeg
- Truffles or dried mushrooms
- Pie dough

## Directions

1. Make a battuto with ham, onion, celery, and carrot, add a small piece of butter and put it on the fire with one or two squabs, depending on the number of people to be served.
2. Add to them their giblets along with chicken giblets if you have them.
3. Season with salt and pepper and, when the squabs are browned, wet them with broth to finish cooking, but make sure some sauce remains.
4. Strain this sauce and throw in the macaroni that you will have already cooked, but not completely, in salted water, and keep them near the fire, stirring from time to time.
5. Make a little béchamel, then break the squabs at their joints, excluding the neck, head, feet, and backbone bones if you don't like to debone them completely, which would be better.
6. Cut the giblets into rather large pieces and remove the tender core from the onions.
7. When the macaroni have absorbed the sauce, season them with Parmesan, small pieces of butter, dice or better slices of fatty and lean ham, nutmeg, slices of truffles or, lacking these, a handful of dried mushrooms softened.
8. Finally add the béchamel and mix.
9. Take a pot of proportional size, grease it entirely with cold butter, and line it with pie dough.
10. Pour in the mixture, cover it with the same dough, and cook it in the oven; turn it out hot and serve immediately.
11. With 300 grams of macaroni and two squabs you will make a timbale for ten or twelve people if they are not big eaters. If you wish, you can also give it the form of a pastry like that in No. 349.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tinche alla Sautè (Tench Sautéed)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- large tench fish
- flour
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- lemon
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Take large tench, cut off their fins, head and tail; open them along the back, remove the backbone and small bones and divide them in two parts lengthwise. Coat them in flour, then dip them in beaten egg that you have first seasoned with salt and pepper; coat them in breadcrumbs, repeating this last operation twice. Cook them in a sauté with butter and serve at table with lemon wedges and a side of fried mushrooms in their season. To remove or diminish the muddy smell of marsh fish, throw them in boiling water, keeping them there a few minutes until the skin begins to crack, and refresh them in cold water before cooking them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tinche in Zimino (Tench in Tomato Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Tench, whole
- onion, garlic, parsley, celery, carrot (for soffritto)
- oil
- salt and pepper
- tomato sauce or tomato preserve dissolved in water
- beet greens or spinach, for garnish
- peas (optional)

## Directions

1. Make a soffritto with onion, garlic, parsley, celery, and carrot.
2. Heat it in oil and when it takes color, add the tench heads cut into small pieces.
3. Season with salt and pepper and cook well, moistening with tomato sauce or preserve dissolved in water.
4. Pass the sauce through a sieve and set aside.
5. Clean the tench, cut off the fins and tail, and leave whole.
6. Place them in a pan with oil when it begins to sizzle.
7. Season with salt and pepper and cook them with the sauce added little by little.
8. You can serve them as they are, which is excellent.
9. To give the zimino its true character, serve with a side of greens (beet greens or spinach) that have been boiled and flavored in the cooking liquid.
10. Peas also go well with this dish.
11. Salt cod can be prepared in zimino the same way.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tonno Fresco coi Piselli (Fresh Tuna with Peas)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh tuna
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Tomato juice or tomato paste
- Water
- Peas

## Directions

1. Cut the tuna into thick slices about half a finger thick.
2. Put it on heat over an abundant soffritto of garlic, parsley and oil.
3. When the garlic begins to take color, season with salt and pepper.
4. Turn the slices on both sides and halfway through cooking, add tomato juice or tomato paste dissolved in water.
5. When cooked, remove the tuna and cook the peas in its sauce.
6. Place the tuna back on top of the peas to reheat it and serve with this side dish.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tonno in Gratella (Tuna Grilled)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Tuna slices
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Lemon wedges

## Directions

1. Cut the tuna into slices like the previous recipe, preferably using the belly part.
2. Season with oil, salt and pepper.
3. Coat in breadcrumbs.
4. Cook on the grill.
5. Serve with lemon wedges.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tonno Sott'olio in Salsa alla Bolognese (Canned Tuna in Bolognese Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Canned tuna in oil (150 grams)
- Boiling water
- Small onion (half)
- Garlic (one quarter clove)
- White celery stalks (two, one palm long)
- Carrot
- Parsley (abundant handful)
- Oil (three tablespoons)
- Butter (15 grams, plus 15 grams more)
- Water (two fingers of glass)
- Lemon (half)

## Directions

1. Put the canned tuna in boiling water and let it boil gently for half an hour, changing the water every ten minutes, for a total of three changes.
2. Meanwhile, make a finely chopped mixture with half a small onion, a quarter clove of garlic, two white celery stalks each one palm long, a good piece of carrot and an abundant handful of parsley.
3. Put it on heat with three tablespoons of oil and 15 grams of butter.
4. When it has taken color, stop it with two fingers of glass of water and let it boil briefly.
5. Once the tuna is cool, cut it into the thinnest slices you can.
6. In a pan, spread it in layers, alternating with the sauce and 15 grams of butter scattered in pieces.
7. Bring to a boil over heat to liquify the butter.
8. Squeeze half a lemon over it and serve hot.
9. This will be enough for four people as a first course at a meatless lunch or as an intermediate course at a family dinner.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tordi Disossati in Gelatina (Boned Thrushes in Aspic)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal, 100 grams
- Salted tongue, 40 grams
- Fat and lean prosciutto, 30 grams
- Black truffles, about 30 grams
- Egg yolk, 1
- Marsala, a small amount
- Aspic broth (Recipe No. 3)
- Cheesecloth for wrapping

## Directions

1. Set aside half of the tongue and one third of the prosciutto (more fat than lean), and mince and pound the veal together with the remaining tongue and prosciutto in a mortar with the truffle skin, softening the mixture with a drop of marsala.
2. Pass the mixture through a sieve and add one egg yolk.
3. Bone the thrushes as you would for stuffed chicken (Recipe No. 258), leaving the neck and head attached.
4. Fill them with the described mixture, to which you have already mixed the truffle, tongue, and prosciutto that were set aside, all cut into small cubes.
5. Sew them closed so that the thread can be removed when cooked.
6. Wrap each thrush in a piece of cheesecloth and boil for one hour in aspic broth (Recipe No. 3).
7. Serve as a cold dish on the same aspic, and if you form six molds shaped like nests with the aspic, it will appear that the thrush is sitting on top to brood.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tordi finti (Mock Thrushes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean veal without bone for six thrushes, 300 grams
- Juniper berries, 6
- Chicken livers, 3
- Salted anchovies, 3
- Oil, 3 tablespoons
- Lard, as needed
- Sage leaves

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into six thin slices and flatten them, shaping them nicely and setting aside the trimmings.
2. Mince together the trimmings, chicken livers, a piece of lard, the juniper berries, cleaned anchovies, and a sage leaf very finely, seasoning with a little salt and pepper to form the filling.
3. Roll the meat slices around this filling, then wrap each with a thin slice of lard, placing half a sage leaf between the lard and the meat, and tie in a cross pattern. About 60 grams of lard in total should suffice.
4. Heat three tablespoons of oil in a sauté pan or open pot over high heat, place the rolled meat inside and lightly season with salt and pepper.
5. When browned on all sides, drain off the fat, leaving the browned bits on the bottom of the pan, and finish cooking with broth added little by little so they remain almost dry at the end.
6. Serve untied over six slices of lightly toasted bread moistened with the concentrated pan sauce.
7. They are also good served cold.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta alla Marengo (Marengo Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Short crust pastry, half the dose
- Milk, 4 deciliters
- Sugar, 60 grams
- Flour, 30 grams
- Three egg yolks
- Vanilla for flavoring
- 100 grams of sponge cake
- Cedar rosoli liqueur
- Egg white
- Powdered sugar, 130 grams

## Directions

1. Make a short crust pastry.
2. Make a cream with milk, sugar, flour, three egg yolks, and vanilla.
3. Take 100 grams of sponge cake and cut it into slices half a centimeter thick.
4. Use a medium-sized copper pan, butter it and cover the bottom with a sheet of the said pastry; then superimpose on this all around an edge of the same pastry one finger wide and two high, and to attach it well, wet the edge with a wet finger.
5. After making this frame for the pan, cover the bottom pastry with half of the sponge cake slices lightly dipped in cedar rosoli.
6. Over these spread the cream and cover this with the remaining sponge cake slices equally sprinkled with rosoli.
7. Now beat with a whisk two of the three remaining egg whites from the cream and when they are very firm add to them little by little 130 grams of powdered sugar and mix slowly to thus have the meringue with which you will cover the surface of the sweet, leaving uncovered the edge of the short pastry to gild it with egg yolk.
8. Bake it in the oven or country oven and when the meringue has hardened cover it with a sheet so it does not take color.
9. Turn the cake out cold and dust it lightly with powdered sugar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta coi Pinoli (Cake with Pine Nuts)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, half a liter
- Medium-grain semolina, 100 grams
- Sugar, 65 grams
- Pine nuts, 50 grams
- Butter, 10 grams
- Two eggs
- Salt, a pinch
- Vanilla for flavoring
- Flour, 200 grams
- Butter, 100 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- One egg
- White wine or Marsala

## Directions

1. The amount of semolina is not entirely strict, but make sure it comes out rather firm.
2. Chop the pine nuts with a pastry cutter to the thickness of half a grain of rice.
3. When the semolina is cooked in the milk add everything else and last the eggs mixing them quickly.
4. Make a short crust pastry with flour, butter, sugar, and one egg. If this is not enough to mix the flour, use a drop of white wine or Marsala.
5. Take a pan in which the sweet does not come higher than two fingers, butter it and cover the bottom with a thin sheet of the said pastry; pour the mixture into it and make the top with the same pastry.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta di Mandorle e Cioccolata (Almond and Chocolate Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Almonds, 150 grams
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Chocolate, 100 grams
- Potato flour, 60 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Milk, 3 deciliters
- Eggs, 4
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, dry them well in the sun or by fire and pound them very finely in a mortar together with one third of the sugar.
2. Make a mixture on the fire with the butter, potato flour and milk poured in little by little.
3. When it reaches the right consistency, add the grated chocolate and the remaining sugar, and after they are well dissolved, add the pounded almonds, stirring continuously.
4. When the mixture is well combined, give it flavor with vanilla sugar and let it cool to add the beaten eggs.
5. Make a paste with 100 grams of flour following recipe N. 153 and with it, following the method as in the Ricotta Cake N. 639, pour it into a baking pan to a thickness of over a finger to cook in a country oven.
6. Cut into almond-shaped pieces when well cooled.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta di Noci (Walnut Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Shelled walnuts, 140 grams
- Powdered sugar, 140 grams
- Chocolate in powder or grated, 140 grams
- Candied citron, 20 grams
- Eggs, 4
- Vanilla sugar flavoring

## Directions

1. Pound the walnuts finely in a mortar together with the sugar, then pour them into a bowl to add the chocolate, the vanilla flavoring, the eggs, placing first the yolks and then the whipped egg whites, and lastly the candied fruit chopped very finely
2. Take a pan where the cake reaches no more than two fingers high, grease it with butter and sprinkle it with breadcrumbs to bake it in the oven or in a country oven at moderate heat
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta di Pane Bruno alla Tedesca (German-Style Dark Bread Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet almonds, 125 grams
- Sugar, 125 grams
- Cognac, 4 spoonfuls
- Grated rye bread crust, 3 heaping spoonfuls
- Eggs, 5
- Butter
- Powdered sugar and flour for dusting

## Directions

1. First work the sugar with two of the whole eggs, then add the blanched and finely pounded almonds with one spoonful of the said sugar; work the mixture again, then add the breadcrumbs and three yolks, lastly the cognac
2. Whip the three remaining egg whites and fold them in
3. Prepare a proportioned baking pan, butter it and dust it with powdered sugar and flour
4. Bake it in the oven or in a country oven
5. Cover it with a tender crust like that of recipe 645, or with a chocolate coating made as follows: Put 30 grams of butter and 100 grams of chopped chocolate on the fire and when well melted add 30 grams of powdered sugar and spread the mixture on the cake when it is no longer boiling
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta di Patate (Potato Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large floury potatoes, 700 grams
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Sweet almonds with three bitter ones, 70 grams
- Eggs, 5
- Butter, 30 grams
- A pinch of salt
- Lemon zest for flavoring

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes (better if steamed), peel them and pass them through a sieve while still hot
2. Peel and pound the almonds very finely together with the sugar
3. Pour the almonds into the potatoes with the other ingredients, working everything together with a spoon for a full hour, adding the eggs one at a time and the melted butter
4. Pour the mixture into a baking pan greased with lard or butter and sprinkled with breadcrumbs
5. Bake it in the oven and serve it cooled
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene
