title: Torta di Ricotta (Ricotta Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ricotta, 500 grams
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Sweet almonds, 150 grams
- Bitter almonds, 4 or 5
- Whole eggs, 4; egg yolks, 4
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Prepare it as the said Budino N. 663; but the almonds, after being pounded with one egg white, it is good to pass them through a sieve
2. Grease a pan abundantly with lard and line it with a sheet of pastry
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta di Riso (Rice Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Rice, 200 grams
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Sweet almonds with 4 bitter ones, 100 grams
- Candied citron, 30 grams
- Whole eggs, 3
- Egg yolks, 5
- Lemon peel flavoring
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Shell the almonds and pound them in the mortar with two tablespoons of the said sugar
2. Cut the candied fruit into very small dice
3. Cook the rice very firm in the milk, pour in the condiment after, and when it is cold, the eggs
4. Put the mixture in a pan greased with butter and sprinkled with breadcrumbs, set it firm in the oven or between two fires, the next day cut the cake into almonds and only when you serve it at table dust it with powdered sugar
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta di Semolino (Semolina Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 1 liter
- Fine semolina, 130 grams
- Sugar, 130 grams
- Sweet almonds with three bitter almonds, 100 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Eggs, 4
- Zest of 1 lemon
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds in hot water and pound them very finely in a mortar with all the sugar, which you will add one spoonful at a time
2. Cook the semolina in the milk and before removing it from the fire add the butter and almonds, which, being mixed with the sugar, dissolve easily
3. Salt it and wait until it is lukewarm to add the eggs beaten separately
4. Pour the mixture into a buttered baking pan, sprinkled with breadcrumbs and of such size that the cake turns out one and a half fingers high or at most two
5. Bake it in the oven or in a country oven, unmold it when cold and serve it whole or cut into almond-shaped pieces
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta di Zucca Gialla (Yellow Squash Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Squash, 1 kilogram
- Sweet almonds, 100 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Butter, 30 grams
- Breadcrumbs, 30 grams
- Milk, half a liter
- Eggs, 3
- A pinch of salt
- Cinnamon powder for flavoring

## Directions

1. Peel the squash and clean it of surface fibers, then grate it over a cloth
2. Gather the four corners of the cloth and squeeze it to remove most of the moisture it contains; the kilogram will reduce to about 300 grams
3. Boil it in the milk until cooked, which can take from 25 to 40 minutes depending on the quality of the squash
4. Meanwhile, pound the peeled almonds together with the sugar in a mortar, reducing them to a fine powder
5. When the squash is cooked, add all the ingredients except the eggs, which you will add when the mixture is cool
6. For the rest, proceed as for the Torta di Ricotta of the previous recipe
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta Frangipane (Frangipane Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potato flour, 120 grams
- Powdered sugar, 120 grams
- Butter, 80 grams
- Four eggs
- Cream of tartar, 5 grams
- Baking soda, 3 grams
- Grated lemon zest for flavoring

## Directions

1. First work the egg yolks with the sugar, then add the potato flour and continue stirring with a spoon; then pour in the melted butter and finally the beaten egg whites and the powders.
2. Use a small pan so it remains two fingers high; butter it and dust it with flour mixed with sugar.
3. You can bake it at home in the country oven.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta Mantovana (Mantuan Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 170 grams
- Sugar, 170 grams
- Butter, 150 grams
- Sweet almonds and pine nuts, 50 grams
- Whole eggs, 1
- Egg yolks, 4
- Lemon zest flavoring

## Directions

1. First work very well with a wooden spoon, inside a bowl, the eggs with the sugar; then pour into it the flour a little at a time, working it still, and finally the butter liquefied in a bain-marie
2. Put the mixture in a buttered copper baking pan dusted with powdered sugar and flour or breadcrumbs and decorate the top with the almonds and pine nuts
3. Cut the pine nuts in two lengthwise and the almonds, after blanching them with hot water and splitting them lengthwise, cut them crosswise, making four or five small pieces from each half
4. Be careful that this cake does not come out thicker than one and a half fingers or two at most so that it has a chance to dry well in the oven, which should be kept at moderate heat
5. Dust it with powdered sugar and serve it cold, which will be much appreciated
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta Milanese (Milanese Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean boiled or roasted meat of beef or veal, cleaned of sinews or tenderness, 200 grams
- Chocolate, 100 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Pine nuts, 50 grams
- Sultana raisins, 50 grams
- Candied citron cut into small pieces, 25 grams
- Wheat flour, 170 grams
- Cornflour, 80 grams
- Powdered sugar, 80 grams
- Butter, 70 grams
- Virgin lard, 25 grams
- One egg
- White wine or marsala, as much as needed to blend it

## Directions

1. Chop the meat very finely with a mincing knife
2. Toast the pine nuts
3. Soak the raisins in marsala and dry them before use
4. Brown the meat in the butter, stirring continuously so it does not stick, and when it has taken on a somewhat reddish color, remove it from the fire to let it cool
5. Dissolve the chocolate, grated or in small pieces, over the fire in three spoonfuls of water, and when dissolved add the sugar and then pour it into the meat, adding the pine nuts, raisins and candied citron and mixing everything together
6. Now prepare a shortcrust pastry to enclose the cake
7. Take a proportionate baking pan where the mixture is no more than a finger high, grease it with butter or lard, and with one sheet below and another on top, the latter rolled out with a fluted roller, seal it between
8. Brush the surface with egg yolk, bake it in the oven or in a country oven and serve it cooled
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta Ricciolina I (Curly Cake I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet almonds with some bitter ones, blanched, 120 grams
- Powdered sugar, 170 grams
- Candied fruit, 70 grams
- Butter, 60 grams
- Lemon zest
- Two eggs
- Flour

## Directions

1. Mix two eggs with flour and make thin noodles equal to the finest ones you would cook for broth soup.
2. On one corner of the work surface make a mound with the almonds, sugar, candied fruit cut into pieces, and scraped lemon zest, and with a pastry cutter and rolling pin flatten and chop this pile of ingredients until it becomes as fine as grains of wheat.
3. Take a copper pan and without oiling it, begin to spread a layer of noodles in the middle, if it is large, and season them with the ingredients described above, spread another layer of noodles and season them again, repeating the operation until the ingredients are finished, making sure the cake comes out round and at least two fingers thick.
4. When it is thus prepared, pour the liquefied butter over it using a brush to oil it well on the surface and so the butter penetrates equally in all parts.
5. Bake it in the oven or in the country oven; in fact, to save coal, the lid of this alone may suffice.
6. Dust it abundantly with powdered sugar when it is hot and serve it cold.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta Ricciolina II (Curly Cake II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 170 grams
- Sugar, 70 grams
- Butter, 60 grams
- Lard, 25 grams
- One egg
- Sweet almonds with three bitter ones, blanched, 120 grams
- Sugar, 100 grams
- Butter, 15 grams
- Candied orange, 15 grams
- One egg yolk
- Water
- Very thin noodles
- Butter, 20 grams liquefied
- Candied citron, 10 grams
- Vanilla-powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make a short crust pastry with flour, sugar, butter, lard, and one egg.
2. Spread one part to the thickness of a coin on the bottom of a copper pan 20 or 21 centimeters in diameter (first buttered) and over it pour a marzipan made with the following proportions: blanched sweet almonds with three bitter ones, sugar, butter, candied orange, one egg yolk.
3. Pound the almonds with sugar in the mortar, add the orange in small pieces, and with the butter, egg yolk and a spoonful of water make everything into a paste.
4. With the rest of the short crust pastry form a circle and with a wet finger attach it all around the edges of the pan.
5. Spread the marzipan evenly and cover it with a layer half a finger high of very thin noodles because these should be like a flowering, not the base of the sweet, and oil them with 20 grams of liquefied butter using a brush.
6. Bake the cake in the oven at moderate heat and after scatter over it 10 grams of candied citron in small pieces.
7. Dust it with vanilla-powdered sugar and serve it one or two days after cooking, because time softens it and makes it more delicate.
8. For the noodles make enough for one egg, but a little more than half will suffice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta Svizzera (Swiss Tart)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Farina, 300 grammi
- Burro, 100 grammi
- Sale, quanto basta
- Odore di scorza di limone
- Latte, quanto basta per intriderla
- Mele reinettes o altre di qualità tenera, 500 grammi
- Zucchero, 100 grammi
- Cannella in polvere, due prese
- Burro liquefatto, 20 grammi

## Directions

1. Fate una pasta di giusta consistenza con farina, burro, sale, odore di scorza di limone e latte quanto basta per intriderla, e lasciatela per un poco in riposo
2. Prendete una teglia di mezzana grandezza, ungetela col burro e copritene il fondo colla detta pasta tirata alla grossezza di due monete da 5 lire
3. Col resto della pasta formate un orlo all'ingiro
4. Collocate dentro grammi 500 di mele reinettes o altre di qualità tenera, sbucciate e tagliate a tocchetti grossi quanto le noci
5. Sopra le medesime spargete grammi 100 di zucchero mescolato a due prese di cannella in polvere e grammi 20 di burro liquefatto
6. Mandatela in forno e servitela calda o diaccia a sette od otto persone
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Torta Tedesca (German Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 250 grams
- Flour, 125 grams
- Sweet almonds, 125 grams
- Butter, 100 grams
- Cream of tartar, 15 grams
- Baking soda, 5 grams
- Egg yolks, 8
- Egg whites, 5
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, dry them well in the sun or by the fire and pound them very finely in a mortar with one of the said egg whites
2. Work the butter alone with a spoon, softening it a little in winter in a bain-marie, add the yolks one by one, then the sugar and work these things together for at least half an hour
3. Add the almonds to the mixture and stir again, then the four whipped egg whites and the flour let fall from a small sieve, mixing slowly
4. Lastly pour the powders that serve to make the cake softer and lighter and bake it in the oven in a pan, not too full, buttered with cold butter and dusted with powdered sugar and flour
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortelli (Cheese-Filled Pasta)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ricotta or raviggiuolo, or both together, 200 grams
- Parmesan, 40 grams
- Whole eggs, 1 and one yolk
- Nutmeg and spice scent
- A pinch of salt
- Some chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Enclose them in a pasta sheet made like that of cappelletti and cut with a round disc somewhat larger.
2. You can leave them with the first fold as a half moon, but the form of cappelletti is to be preferred.
3. Cook them in sufficiently salted water, remove them dry and season with cheese and butter.
4. With this amount you will obtain 24 or 25 and they can suffice, being large, for three people.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortelli di Ceci (Chickpea Fritters)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried chickpeas, 300 grams
- Dried chestnuts, 7 or 8
- Sodium carbonate, 3 grams
- Salt, a pinch
- Sapa, necessary amount to soften the mixture
- Mostarda di Savignano, half a small jar
- Candied fruit, 40 grams in small pieces
- Sugar, a little if the sapa has not sweetened enough
- Ground cinnamon, two teaspoons
- Flour, 270 grams
- Butter, 20 grams
- Sugar, 15 grams
- Eggs, 1
- White wine or marsala, about 3 spoonfuls
- Salt, a pinch

## Directions

1. Soak the chickpeas overnight in fresh water.
2. In the morning unite with them 7 or 8 dried chestnuts and put them on the fire with equally fresh water in an earthenware pot with 3 grams of sodium carbonate tied in a small cloth (the people call this the secret and it serves to facilitate the cooking of chickpeas).
3. When they are cooked, remove them dry and pass them through a sieve while hot and boiling, together with the chestnuts; and if, despite the secret, they had remained hard due to the quality of the water, pound them in the mortar.
4. When you have passed them, season and flavor them with a pinch of salt, with sapa in the quantity necessary to make the mixture somewhat soft, half a small jar of mostarda di Savignano, 40 grams of candied fruit in small pieces, a little sugar if the sapa has not sweetened them enough, and two teaspoons of ground cinnamon.
5. For the dough: work together flour, butter, sugar, 1 egg, white wine or marsala, and a pinch of salt.
6. Draw out a sheet of dough about the thickness of half a coin and cut it with the scalloped round stamp.
7. Make the filling abundant in the discs and you will have, joining the edges, the tortelli in the form of a quarter moon.
8. Fry them in lard or oil and when they are no longer bubbling, dust them with powdered sugar.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortellini all'italiana (Italian Tortellini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork loin chops, approximately 300 grams
- One lamb brain or half of a larger animal brain
- Beef marrow, 50 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 50 grams
- 3 egg yolks and, if needed, add one egg white
- Nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Bone and trim the fat from the pork chops, then cook them in a saucepan with butter, salt and a pinch of pepper. In the absence of pork, you may use lean turkey breast in the proportion of 200 grams, cooked in the same manner.
2. Pound or mince very finely the meat with a lunette; then add to it the boiled and skinned brain, the raw marrow and all the other ingredients, mixing them well together.
3. The tortellini are then enclosed in a sheet of pasta as cappelletti are, and folded in the same way, except that these are made much smaller.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortellini all'italiana (Italian-Style Tortellini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork chops from the loin, about 300 grams
- One lamb brain or half of a larger animal's brain
- Beef marrow, 50 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 50 grams
- Three egg yolks and, if needed, add one white
- Nutmeg flavor

## Directions

1. Debone and degrease the pork chops, then cook them in a small pot with butter, salt and a pinch of pepper.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortellini alla bolognese (Bolognese Tortellini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fatty and lean prosciutto, 30 grams
- Bologna mortadella, 20 grams
- Beef marrow, 60 grams
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 60 grams
- 1 egg
- Nutmeg to taste
- Salt and pepper, none

## Directions

1. Mince finely with a lunette the prosciutto and mortadella, mince equally the marrow without breaking it over heat, add it to the other ingredients and bind everything together with the egg, mixing well.
2. Enclose them in an egg pasta sheet as the others, cutting it with the small stamp of N. 8.
3. They keep well for days and even for a few weeks and if you wish them to retain a beautiful yellow color, place them, as soon as they are made, to dry in a warm place.
4. With this amount you will make just under 300, and you will need a pasta sheet made with three eggs.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortellini di carne di piccione (Pigeon Meat Tortellini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One young pigeon, plucked, weighing approximately half a kilogram
- Grated Parmesan cheese, 80 grams
- Fatty and lean prosciutto, 70 grams
- Nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Empty the pigeon of its organs, as the liver and gizzard are not used in this case, and boil it.
2. To boil it, throw it into boiling water and salt it; half an hour of boiling is sufficient, as it should be undercooked.
3. Remove it from the heat, bone it, then mince very finely both this meat and the prosciutto first with a knife then with a lunette, and finally, adding the Parmesan and nutmeg, work the mixture with the blade of the knife to make it all homogeneous.
4. Use the stamp N. 8 to close them, and with three eggs of pasta you will obtain approximately 260.
5. You may serve them in broth, as a soup course, or dry dressed with cheese and butter, or better with sauce and giblets.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortini di Ricotta (Ricotta Croquettes)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- ricotta, 200 grams
- grated parmesan cheese, 50 grams
- flour, 30 grams
- eggs, 2
- chopped parsley, a pinch
- spices, to taste
- salt, as needed
- butter
- tomato sauce or conserve dissolved in water

## Directions

1. Form a mixture with the aforementioned ingredients, pour it onto a work surface over a light layer of flour and make it take up enough flour, with floured hands, to form twelve soft croquettes which you will flatten somewhat.
2. Put a sautè or a pan on the fire with a piece of butter to brown them, and when they have taken color on both sides wet them with tomato juice or conserve dissolved in water.
3. They can serve as a course and can be brought to the table accompanied by a steak or a piece of hot roast beef.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortino di Patate I (Potato Tart I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, large and floury, 500 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Good milk or cream, half a glass
- Grated Parmesan cheese, two tablespoons
- Eggs, 2
- Salt, to taste
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare as in the mashed potatoes recipe (N. 443)
2. Let cool
3. Add Parmesan and eggs
4. Butter a copper pastry pan or proportionate baking dish
5. Dust with fine breadcrumbs
6. Pour in the mixture after mixing well
7. Shape into a flat cake about one to one and a half fingers high
8. Place under a camp oven to brown
9. Serve hot from the bottom or top side, whichever is more attractive
10. Alternatively, make small tarts or use molds for elegant shapes
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortino di Patate II (Potato Tart II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, 500 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Flour, 30 grams
- Eggs, 2
- Parmesan cheese, two tablespoons
- Milk, as needed
- Salt, to taste

## Directions

1. Make a béchamel sauce with the flour, half the butter and necessary milk
2. Pour the already cooked and passed potatoes into it
3. Work over the fire, adding the remaining butter, salt and enough additional milk to make a not too soft paste
4. Let cool
5. Add the remaining ingredients
6. Brown the mixture as in the previous recipe
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortino di Petonciani (Eggplant Cake)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven or eight eggplants
- salt
- flour
- oil for frying
- grated Parmesan cheese
- tomato sauce (N. 125)
- one egg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Peel seven or eight eggplants, cut them into round slices and salt them so they release their water.
2. After a few hours, flour them and fry them in oil.
3. Take a pan that can withstand fire and, layer by layer, season them with grated Parmesan and tomato sauce N. 125, arranging them so they form a nice mound.
4. Beat an egg with a pinch of salt, a spoonful of said sauce, a teaspoon of Parmesan, two of breadcrumbs, and cover the surface with this mixture.
5. Place the pan under the lid of the country oven, with fire on top, and when the egg has set, serve the cake to the table.
6. It can be served alone, as an intermediate course or accompanied by a meat dish.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortino di Pomodori (Tomato Tart)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes, cut into pieces
- garlic
- parsley
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- parmesan cheese
- croutons cut into almond shapes
- butter or lard
- nepitella or oregano (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil the cut tomatoes in a soffritto of garlic, parsley and oil; season with salt and pepper.
2. When they are cooked so that their juice is condensed, pass it and return it to the fire with eggs in proportion, beaten beforehand.
3. Add a pinch of parmesan, mix and when the eggs are set, pour them into a serving dish and surround with croutons cut into almond shapes and fried in butter or lard.
4. A few leaves of nepitella or a pinch of oregano, after passing the juice, gives the tart a pleasant aroma.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortino di pomodori (Tomato Tart)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes, cut into pieces
- garlic
- parsley
- oil
- salt and pepper
- eggs, in proportion
- Parmesan cheese, a pinch

## Directions

1. Boil the tomatoes cut into pieces in a soffritto of garlic, parsley and oil with salt and pepper for seasoning
2. When they are cooked so that their juice has condensed, pass it and return it to the fire with eggs in proportion, beaten beforehand
3. Add a pinch of Parmesan, mix and when the eggs are set, pour them out
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Tortino di Zucchini (Zucchini Tart)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini, cut into chunks
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parmesan cheese
- Nutmeg
- Béchamel sauce, fairly thick

## Directions

1. Cut zucchini into chunks slightly larger than hazelnuts
2. Brown them in butter and season with salt and pepper
3. Pour them into a fireproof dish
4. Sprinkle lightly with Parmesan mixed with a pinch of nutmeg
5. Cover with fairly thick béchamel sauce
6. Brown the surface with a camp oven cover
7. Serve as an intermediate course or alongside boiled meat or a meat stew
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Totani in Gratella (Squid on the Grill)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Squid
- Tentacles of squid
- Parsley
- Garlic
- Breadcrumbs
- Oil
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut off the tentacles from the squid, leaving the sack and head intact.
2. Chop the tentacles finely with a knife together with parsley and very little garlic.
3. Mix this mixture with plenty of breadcrumbs, season with oil, pepper and salt.
4. Use this mixture to fill the sack of the squid.
5. Close the opening of the sack by piercing it with a small stick, which you will remove later.
6. Season with oil, pepper and salt and cook on the grill as described.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Triglie alla Livornese (Red Mullet Livorno Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Red mullet
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Celery
- Oil
- Tomatoes
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Make a finely chopped mixture with garlic, parsley and a piece of celery.
2. Put it in a pot with a good measure of oil over heat.
3. When the garlic has taken color, add tomatoes in pieces and season with salt and pepper.
4. Let the tomatoes cook well, stir frequently and pass the sauce through a sieve.
5. Place the mullet in this sauce and cook them.
6. If they are small, they do not need to be turned over.
7. If the pot they have boiled in is not presentable, remove them one by one carefully not to break them and place them on a serving dish.
8. Just before removing from heat, lightly sprinkle with chopped parsley.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Triglie alla Viareggina (Red Mullet Viareggio Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Red mullet (about half kilogram)
- Garlic (two cloves)
- Parsley
- Oil
- Tomato juice
- Red wine
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Make a finely chopped mixture with two cloves of garlic and a good pinch of parsley.
2. Put it in a pot or pan with a good measure of oil over heat, large enough for the mullet to lay flat.
3. When the soffritto is browned, stop it with plain tomato juice.
4. Let it boil briefly, then place the mullet in it, turning them one by one in the sauce.
5. Cover and let them boil slowly.
6. When they have absorbed a good part of the liquid, add a finger of glass of red wine diluted with two fingers of water.
7. Let them boil a little longer and serve.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Triglie col Prosciutto (Red Mullet with Prosciutto)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullet of medium size
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- lemon juice
- thin slices of fat and lean prosciutto
- sage leaves
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. After cleaning and washing the mullet, dry them well with a cloth
2. Place them in a serving bowl and season with salt, pepper, oil and lemon juice
3. Leave them thus for several hours
4. When ready to cook, cut thin slices of fat and lean prosciutto as wide as the mullet and in quantity equal to the number of mullet
5. Spread some whole sage leaves at the bottom of a metal serving dish or pan
6. Coat the mullet well in breadcrumbs and arrange them as follows: place them upright close together and insert the prosciutto slices between one and the other, sprinkling more sage leaves on top
7. Finally pour the remaining seasoning over them and cook them between two fires
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Triglie di Scoglio in Gratella (Rock Red Mullet Grilled)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Rock red mullet (500-600 grams)
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Parsley
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Season the fish with oil, salt and pepper.
2. Cook over high heat.
3. When removing from heat, coat while hot with a mixture of butter, chopped parsley and lemon juice prepared beforehand.
4. Serve immediately.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Triglie in Gratella alla Marinara (Red Mullet Grilled Sailor Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Red mullet
- Garlic
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil
- Rosemary leaves
- Breadcrumbs
- Lemon wedges

## Directions

1. Remove the intestines from the mullet using a knife tip at the gills, wash and dry them.
2. Place a small piece of garlic where the intestine was.
3. Season with salt, pepper, oil, and rosemary leaves and let sit.
4. When ready to cook, coat in breadcrumbs and oil with the seasoning while on the fire.
5. Alternatively, after cleaning, washing and drying, season with just salt and pepper and cook over high heat.
6. Place on a serving dish and season only then with oil, more salt and pepper.
7. Serve with lemon wedges.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Trippa alla Corsa (Corsican Tripe)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw tripe, 700 grams
- Beef foot without bone, 100 grams
- Butter, 80 grams
- Lard, 70 grams
- Half of one large onion
- Two small cloves of garlic
- Nutmeg and spice flavoring
- Meat broth, as needed
- One handful of parmesan cheese
- Toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Wash the tripe well and cut into strips no wider than half a finger, and cut the foot the same way
2. Mince the onion finely and place on heat with butter
3. When it begins to take color, add the finely chopped lard with the crescent-shaped chopping tool together with the garlic
4. When this soffritto has taken on a hazelnut color, add the tripe and foot, seasoning with salt, pepper and the indicated aromatics in small measure
5. Let it boil until dry, then moisten with broth and finish cooking it over gentle heat to make it tender, which will take 7 to 8 hours total; if the broth runs out, help yourself with more broth
6. When ready to serve, enhance the flavor with parmesan and pour it over slices of toasted bread that should be swimming in the broth
7. Sufficient for five people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Trippa col Sugo (Tripe with Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Tripe, boiled
- Butter
- Meat broth or tomato sauce
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Tripe, however cooked and seasoned, is always an ordinary dish. Whether boiled already in some cities or boiled at home, preferring the thick cordoned type, once boiled, cut it into strips half a finger wide and dry it between the folds of a kitchen cloth. Then put it in a pot to sauté in butter and when it has drawn the butter, add meat broth or, not having this, tomato sauce; season it with salt and pepper, cook it as much as you can and when you are about to remove it, throw in additional ingredients.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Trippa Legata colle Uova (Tripe Bound with Eggs)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Tripe, boiled and cut as in the previous recipe
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Eggs, beaten
- Lemon juice
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil and cut the tripe as in the previous recipe
2. Place on heat in a soffritto of garlic, parsley and butter
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. When cooked, bind with beaten eggs, lemon juice and parmesan cheese
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Uccelli in salmì (Birds in Salmis)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Small birds or thrushes, left whole if small, or cut in four if large
- Salt and oil for initial roasting
- Butter
- Prosciutto
- Meat broth or marsala
- Shallot, 1
- Juniper berries, 1 or 2 (if thrushes) or bay leaf (if other birds)
- Salt and pepper
- Bread, sliced and toasted

## Directions

1. Roast the birds on a spit over a fire, seasoned with salt and oil, until not quite fully cooked.
2. If using small birds or thrushes, leave them whole; if large, cut into four parts.
3. Remove all heads and pound them in a mortar together with either a small roasted bird or scraps from larger birds.
4. In a small pot, heat a battuto made of butter, a few pieces of prosciutto, meat sauce or broth, and marsala in approximately the same amount as the broth.
5. Add a sliced shallot and either one or two juniper berries (if using thrushes) or a bay leaf (if using other birds).
6. Season with salt and pepper, bring to a boil, and let simmer for half an hour.
7. Pass the sauce through a sieve and add the roasted birds to it.
8. Simmer until completely cooked and serve with slices of toasted bread underneath.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Umido di rigaglie di pollo col sedano (Chicken Giblets Stew with Celery)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken giblets: livers, crests, unborn eggs, bean-like organs and gizzards
- Celery
- Prosciutto, fat and lean
- Onion, small amount
- Butter
- Potato flour, a pinch
- Salt, pepper and spice flavoring
- Broth
- Tomato juice or tomato conserve, small amount

## Directions

1. Give the celery, cut into pieces about half a finger long, one third of its cooking time in salted water.
2. Make a small battue of fat and lean prosciutto and a little onion, place it on the fire with butter and when well browned, add first the gizzards cut into three pieces, then a pinch of potato flour, then the livers in two pieces and all the rest.
3. Season with salt, pepper and spice flavoring and when it has absorbed the flavors, moisten with broth and a little tomato juice or conserve.
4. Fry the celery separately in butter and when cooked add the giblets to it, let it boil a little longer, add more broth if needed and serve.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Umido incassato (Encased Stew)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour, 150 grams
- Butter, 70 grams
- Parmesan cheese, 30 grams
- Milk, 6 deciliters
- Eggs, 3
- Salt, to taste
- Spinach, one bunch

## Directions

1. Boil the spinach, squeeze it well and pass it through a sieve.
2. Make a béchamel sauce with the flour, butter, Parmesan and milk.
3. Break the eggs when you remove the béchamel from the fire, and color half of the béchamel green with the spinach.
4. Grease a ring-shaped copper mold with a hole in the middle and fluted all around with cold butter; fill it first with the green béchamel, then with the yellow béchamel and let it set in a water bath.
5. Unmold it while hot and fill the center with a well-made sauce of chicken giblets and sweetbreads, or braised milk veal rolls flavored with mushrooms or truffles.
6. Cook the dish with butter and meat sauce or in another manner, ensuring it turns out delicate, and this dish will make a fine showing and be praised.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Un Uovo per un Bambino (An Egg for a Child)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh egg, 1
- Powdered sugar, 2-3 teaspoons
- Bread slices

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolk well in a cup or bowl shape with 2-3 teaspoons of powdered sugar
2. Whip the egg white until stiff and fold it in gently so it does not deflate
3. Place the cup in front of the child with slices of bread to dip, with which he will make yellow whiskers
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Uova Affogate (Poached Eggs)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cheese
- Butter

## Directions

1. Break the eggs when the water boils and let them fall from a low height.
2. When the white is well set and the yolk is no longer trembling, remove them with a slotted spoon.
3. Season them with salt, pepper, cheese and butter.
4. They may be served with tomato sauce, green sauce (recipe N. 119), sauce N. 127, or a specially made sauce prepared by dissolving an anchovy in hot butter and adding capers squeezed of vinegar and chopped somewhat.
5. They can also be served on a layer about a finger-thick of potato purée, or over spinach sautéed in butter.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Uova di Neve (Floating Islands)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one liter
- Sugar, 150 grams
- Eggs, 6
- Powdered sugar, as needed
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Put the milk on the fire in a wide pan and while it heats, beat with a whisk the egg whites that you will have already separated from the yolks; but throw into them a pinch of salt if it is true that it serves to strengthen the consistency.
2. When the whites are well beaten, take the perforated shaker where powdered sugar is ordinarily kept and, continuing always to beat them, throw in enough to make them somewhat sweet. Twenty or thirty grams of powdered sugar may suffice, but it is better to taste them.
3. This done, take up the beaten whites with a tablespoon, and giving them the approximate shape of an egg, throw them into the milk when it boils.
4. Turn these so-called eggs to cook them on all sides and when you see them set, take them with a slotted spoon and put them to drain in a colander.
5. Strain that milk and, when it is cold, make with the same, with the yolks and with sugar a cream like that of No. 685, giving it the flavor of vanilla.
6. Arrange on a platter the floating islands in fine display, one on top of the other, pour the cream over them and serve them cold.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Uova Ripiene I (Stuffed Eggs I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, hard-boiled
- Anchovies
- Parsley
- Onion
- Butter
- Mayonnaise sauce

## Directions

1. After hard-boiling the eggs, cut them in half lengthwise and extract the yolks.
2. Take one anchovy for every two eggs, wash them, remove the bone, and chop them finely with a little parsley and very little onion.
3. Mix in the yolks and enough butter so that you can knead everything together with a knife blade.
4. Fill the cavities left by the yolks with this mixture and arrange the stuffed egg halves neatly on a platter.
5. Cover them with mayonnaise sauce (recipe N. 126).
6. They can also be eaten simply seasoned with salt, pepper, oil and vinegar, and they are quite palatable; it also seems that the stomach is not offended by them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Uova Ripiene II (Stuffed Eggs II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, 6
- Butter, 30 grams
- Bread crumbs (midolla di pane), 20 grams
- Parmesan cheese, 2 heaping tablespoons
- Dried mushrooms, a pinch
- Parsley, several leaves
- Salt, to taste
- Milk
- Potatoes, 350 grams raw (or spinach, peas, or other vegetables)

## Directions

1. Hard-boil the eggs, cut them lengthwise, and set the yolks aside.
2. Soak the bread crumbs well in milk and squeeze them out.
3. Soften the mushrooms in tepid water.
4. Pound everything very finely to fill and even mound up the cavities of the whites.
5. Arrange these 12 filled egg halves on a platter, convex side down, on a layer of potatoes passed through a ricer (recipe N. 443), using 350 grams raw weight.
6. Instead of potatoes, you can place them on a layer of spinach, peas, or other vegetables.
7. Before serving, warm them with heat from a country oven cover.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Uova Stracciate (Scrambled Eggs)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, 4
- Butter, 40 grams
- Cream, 1 deciliter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread, toasted slices
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Put the butter on the fire and when it begins to fry, pour in the beaten eggs.
2. Season with salt and pepper and, constantly stirring with a spoon, add the cream little by little.
3. Once the mixture is set, cover three slices of toasted bread, nearly a finger thick and without crust, which you will have previously arranged on a platter and buttered while hot.
4. Dust them with grated Parmesan cheese and serve.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Vitella di Latte in Guazzetto (Milk Veal Stew)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk veal from the loin or rump, 500 grams without bone
- Cured meat, 30 grams, sliced very thin
- Butter, 30 grams
- Lemon, less than half, sliced thin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Warm milk, 1 glass

## Directions

1. Beat the veal, tie it so it stays gathered and place it in a pot as follows
2. Cover the bottom of the pot with very thin slices of cured meat and butter, and on top of this layer place less than half a lemon cut into four thin slices from which you will remove the peel and seeds
3. On top of these things place the veal to brown it well on all sides; but be careful that it does not take on any burnt color because of the little liquid found there
4. Having done this, drain the excess fat, season with salt and pepper and shortly after moisten it with a glass of warm milk, which you will have first boiled a little separately, but do not be alarmed if it curdles, as is likely
5. Cover the pot with double paper and, on low heat, cook the piece of meat to completion; when you are about to serve it, pass the gravy
6. This amount should suffice for four persons
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Vitello Tonnato (Veal with Tuna Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram of milk-fed veal from the thigh or rump, all in one piece and boneless
- 2 anchovies
- 1/4 onion studded with 2 cloves
- 1 bay leaf
- celery
- carrot
- parsley
- salt for cooking water
- 100 grams of canned tuna in oil
- 2 anchovies
- fine oil
- lemon juice
- capers

## Directions

1. Take 1 kilogram of milk-fed veal from the thigh or rump, all in one piece and boneless, remove the thin skin and fat, then lard it with two anchovies.
2. Wash the anchovies, split them in two, remove the spine, and cut them crosswise making eight pieces total.
3. Tie the meat not too tightly and put it to boil for an hour and a half in enough water to cover it, in which you have put a quarter of an onion studded with two cloves, a bay leaf, celery, carrot, and parsley.
4. Salt the water generously and wait for it to boil before putting in the meat.
5. When cooked, untie it, dry it, and when cold cut it into thin slices and keep it soaking for a day or two in a narrow vessel in the following sauce in sufficient quantity to cover it.
6. Pound 100 grams of canned tuna in oil and two anchovies, break them down well with a knife blade or better pass them through a sieve, adding fine oil in abundance little by little and the juice of a lemon or even more so that the sauce comes out liquid.
7. Finally mix in a handful of capers squeezed of vinegar.
8. Serve the veal with tuna sauce with its sauce and with lemon wedges.
9. Strain the broth and use it for a risotto.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zabaione (Zabaglione)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks, 3
- Powdered sugar, 30 grams
- Cyprus wine, Marsala, or Madeira wine, 1.5 deciliters or about 9 tablespoons

## Directions

1. Work first with a ladle the egg yolks with the sugar until they have become almost white
2. Add the liquid and mix
3. Place it over a hot fire, beating it continuously and being careful not to let it boil as it would curdle
4. Remove it as soon as it begins to rise
5. Double this dose will be sufficient for eight people
6. If you want it more spirited add a tablespoon of rum; also a teaspoon of powdered cinnamon is not bad
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zampa Burrata (Buttered Beef Foot)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef foot
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Meat broth, a little
- Parmesan cheese
- Tomato juice or tomato conserve (if meat broth is unavailable)

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of beef foot and boil it
2. Bone it, cut into small pieces and place on heat with butter, salt and pepper, a little meat broth and parmesan when serving
3. If meat broth is lacking, tomato juice or tomato conserve can adequately substitute
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zorama (Chocolate and Cream Marbled Ice)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Colla di pesce (fish gelatin), 3 leaves
- Zucchero (sugar), 100 grams
- Cioccolata in polvere (chocolate powder), 80 grams
- Rossi d'uovo (egg yolks), 3 number
- Latte (milk), 3 deciliters
- Chiare d'uovo (egg whites), 3 number
- Panna montata (whipped cream), as that which dairymen prepare, 150 grams
- Acqua (water), small amount
- Rosolio (rosoli liqueur)

## Directions

1. First soak in fresh water three leaves of fish gelatin and meanwhile prepare a cream with sugar, chocolate powder, egg yolks, and milk
2. When cooled unite to it the three beaten egg whites and after 150 grams of whipped cream, as that which dairymen prepare, mixing in such a way that the white of this appears scattered here and there
3. Then dissolve on the fire in a small amount of water the said three leaves of fish gelatin and this liquid thus hot scatter it over the mixture mixing
4. Then pour it into the ice cream mold or another vessel moistened with rosoli and closed hermetically, keeping it for three or four hours surrounded and covered with much ice mixed with salt
5. It should be enough for eight persons
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zucchini col Regamo (Zucchini with Oregano)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Long zucchini, a good quantity as they reduce much
- Oil, a good measure
- Salt and pepper, to taste
- Oregano, a good pinch

## Directions

1. Cut the zucchini into round slices the thickness of a coin.
2. Put a sauté pan or copper pan with oil on the heat in good measure and when it begins to boil throw in the zucchini as they are natural and, on high heat, stir them often.
3. Season them halfway through cooking with salt and pepper and when they begin to brown sprinkle a good pinch of oregano over them and remove them immediately with a slotted spoon.
4. They can be served alone or as a side dish and will be pleasing.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zucchini Fritti I (Fried Zucchini I)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini of elongated form, finger-sized or larger
- Flour
- Salt
- Oil, lard, or other fat for frying

## Directions

1. Take zucchini of elongated form the size of a finger and larger; wash them and cut them into strips about one centimeter wide or less, remove some of the pulp and salt them not too much. After one or two hours from this preparation drain them of the liquid they have released and, without drying them at all, throw them into flour and from there into a small sieve, shaking them well of excess flour; immediately after throw them into a pan where the fat, oil or lard, is found in abundance and boiling. At first do not touch them so as not to break them and only when they have solidified move them with a slotted spoon and remove them when they begin to take on color.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zucchini Fritti II (Fried Zucchini II)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Large, thick zucchini
- Salt
- Flour

## Directions

1. Take large and thick zucchini that cannot be grasped with one hand. Peel them to make the fried dish more beautiful, open them in two parts lengthwise and remove the pulp from the part that shows the seeds. Then cut them into long and thin strips, a finger's width wide and place them with salt to make water, leaving them there for several hours. When you are about to fry them take them up with both hands and squeeze them hard to press out the water that they still contain, then throw them into flour loosening them with your fingers, then into the small sieve and throw them immediately in a pan with plenty of fat.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zucchini Ripieni (Stuffed Zucchini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini
- Lean veal, cut into pieces
- Onion, parsley, celery, carrot, finely chopped
- Salt pork, finely cut
- Oil
- Salt and pepper, to taste
- Water
- Egg
- Grated Parmesan cheese
- Bread crumb, boiled in broth or milk
- Spices
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the zucchini whole and hollow them out using a tin tube passed from bottom to top; if the hollow does not seem large enough, widen it with a thin knife.
2. For the filling, take lean veal, cut it into pieces and put it on the heat in a pan with a finely chopped mixture of onion, parsley, celery, carrot, a little salt pork cut into small pieces, a little oil, salt and pepper.
3. Turn it frequently with a spoon and when the meat has drawn in all moisture and taken color, pour in a ladleful of water; after it has drawn in this, pour in another and after a little another still, to finish cooking it, noting that some sauce remains.
4. Pass this sauce and set it aside.
5. Finely mince the dry meat with a crescent cutter, and with an egg, a little grated Parmesan cheese, a bread crumb boiled in broth or milk and the aroma of spices, make all into a mixture and use it for filling.
6. With the zucchini thus prepared, put them to brown in butter which you will first brown to a hazelnut color and lastly cook them through with the sauce already set aside.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zucchini Ripieni di Magro (Lean Stuffed Zucchini)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini
- Canned tuna in oil, finely minced
- Eggs
- Parmesan cheese, a pinch
- Zucchini marrow removed during hollowing
- Spices
- Pepper
- Butter
- Tomato sauce from recipe 125

## Directions

1. Prepare them like the previous recipe and fill them with a mixture made with canned tuna in oil finely minced with a crescent cutter and moistened with eggs, a pinch of Parmesan and a little of that marrow removed from the zucchini, the aroma of spices, a pinch of pepper and no salt.
2. Put them to cook in butter when this has taken a hazelnut color and grace them with the tomato sauce from recipe 125.
3. If you make them with care they will turn out so good you will not believe it.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa (Soup with Brain and Chicken Livers)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain, or lamb brain, or similar animal brain, 120 grams
- Chicken livers, 3
- Eggs, 3
- A pinch of parsley and a pinch of basil
- Juice of one quarter lemon
- Butter
- Meat broth
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Blanch the brain so you can skin it, and together with the chicken livers, sauté them in butter and cook them with meat broth; season with salt and pepper.
2. Put the eggs in a pot, add the chopped parsley and basil, lemon juice, a little salt and pepper and beat them; then with the cold broth, which should serve to dampen the soup, dilute the mixture little by little.
3. Add the brain and chicken livers cut into small pieces, and let it condense over gentle heat, stirring continuously with a spoon, but without letting it boil.
4. Once condensed, pour it into the soup tureen over bread, which you will have already cut into cubes and sautéed in butter or oil, but first sprinkle a handful of grated Parmesan cheese over the bread itself.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa alla Certosina (Carthusian Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams of small fish of various species
- One quarter of an onion
- Parsley
- Celery
- Oil
- Water
- Tomato juice or conserve
- Salt
- Pepper
- Two eggs
- Three tablespoons Parmesan cheese
- Bread for toasting or frying
- Butter, oil, or lard for frying

## Directions

1. Make a battuto with a quarter of an onion, parsley, and celery.
2. Put it on the fire with oil.
3. When it has colored, add the fish, moistening it when dry with water, tomato juice, or conserve.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Let it cook well, then add the necessary water for the soup: about one liter or slightly more in total can suffice.
6. Pass everything through a sieve or strainer, pressing well, and return it to the fire to bring it to a boil.
7. In the soup tureen, beat together two eggs with three tablespoons of Parmesan cheese beforehand.
8. Pour the broth slowly and gradually into the tureen with the egg mixture.
9. Before sending the soup to the table, add the bread, which, in small dice, can be simply toasted or fried in butter, oil, or lard as you prefer.
10. The eggs and Parmesan can also be beaten separately and poured into the pot when the broth is boiling, stirring strongly, if you do not mind seeing them set in shreds.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa alla Spagnuola (Spanish Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- A breast of young hen or capon
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Broth
- One marrow from bread, large as a fist

## Directions

1. Take a breast of young hen or capon, cut it into pieces and put it to cook in butter over a slow fire; season it with salt and pepper
2. If the butter is not enough, wet it with broth
3. Remove the breast and in the remaining sauce throw a marrow from bread, large as a fist, and with broth make a somewhat thick mush
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa alla Spagnuola (Spanish Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken breast (petto di pollastra o di cappone)
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Broth
- Bread crumb (midolla di pane)
- Egg yolks
- Nutmeg
- Flour
- Bread cut into cubes
- Lard or butter for frying

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken breast into small pieces and cook slowly in butter, seasoning with salt and pepper
2. If needed, moisten with broth
3. Remove the cooked chicken and keep aside
4. In the remaining sauce, add a bread crumb about the size of a fist and make a thick paste with broth
5. Place this paste and the cooked chicken in a mortar
6. Add two egg yolks and a pinch of nutmeg, pound everything finely
7. Leave the mixture in a cool place to set
8. When ready to use (can be the next day), dust a work surface with flour
9. Form the mixture into a stick about the thickness of a finger or less
10. Cut with a floured knife into equal pieces
11. Round them into small balls the size of a hazelnut or smaller, using flour-dusted hands
12. Drop into boiling broth and cook for five or six minutes
13. Serve in a bowl lined with bread cubes fried in butter or virgin lard
14. Serves ten to twelve people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa alla Stefani (Stefani's Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal brain, or lamb brain, or other similar animal brain, 120 grams
- Chicken livers, 3
- Eggs, 3
- A pinch of parsley and one of basil
- The juice of a quarter of a lemon
- Butter
- Meat sauce
- Salt and pepper
- Broth, cold

## Directions

1. Blanch the brain to be able to skin it and, both this and the livers, brown them in butter and cook them with meat sauce; salt and pepper for seasoning
2. Place the eggs in a pot, add to it the chopped parsley and basil, the lemon juice, a little salt and pepper and beat them; then with the cold broth, which must serve to soak the soup, dilute the mixture little by little
3. Pour into it last the brain and the
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa col Brodo di Muggine (Soup with Mullet Broth)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- One mullet (or baldigara), weighing at least one kilogram
- Onion
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Carrot
- Celery
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Tomato juice
- Water
- Dried mushrooms
- Bread for toasting
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Scrape the scales from the mullet, clean it, and boil it with water in proportion.
2. Make a generous battuto with onion, garlic, parsley, carrot, and celery.
3. Put this mixture on the fire with oil, salt, and pepper.
4. When it has taken color, stop it with tomato juice and let it boil with the fish broth.
5. Strain this broth and with a little of the same broth cook a small quantity of celery, carrot, and dried mushrooms, all cut into small pieces, which serve to give flavor.
6. Toast the bread for the soup and cut it into dice.
7. Put the bread in the soup tureen and pour the hot broth over it along with the said flavoring vegetables.
8. Serve at the table with Parmesan cheese on the side.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa con le Cipolle alla Francese (French Onion Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- White bread, 250 grams
- Grated Gruyère, 80 grams
- Butter, 50 grams
- Grated Parmesan, 40 grams
- Beaten eggs, 3
- Large white onions, 2
- Broth or milk, about 1.5 liters

## Directions

1. Cut the onions into very thin slices and put them on the fire with the butter; when they begin to take color, cook them thoroughly with broth, or with milk if you make it with that, so you can strain them well through a sieve
2. Mix the strained liquid into the remaining liquid to soak the soup
3. Toast the bread cut into slices or into cubes, and place it in layers in the soup tureen, season it with the eggs, the Gruyère and the Parmesan as you go
4. Finally pour the hot broth or milk and send it to the table
5. If you make it with milk it will be good to salt the eggs abundantly
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Acetosa (Sorrel Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams of sorrel
- One head of lettuce
- Salt
- 30 grams of butter
- Two egg yolks
- Broth
- Bread cut into cubes and fried

## Directions

1. After soaking these vegetables, drain them well, cut them into strips and put them on the fire
2. When they are cooked, season them with a pinch of salt and 30 grams of butter
3. Put in the soup tureen two egg yolks with a little lukewarm broth, add the said vegetables and then, little by little while stirring, add all the necessary boiling broth for the soup
4. Throw in the bread cut into cubes and fried, and serve at table with Parmesan on the side
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Fagiuoli (Bean Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Beans
- One quarter onion
- One garlic clove
- A pinch of parsley
- A nice piece of white celery
- Oil
- Pepper
- Bean broth
- Tomato juice or paste
- Bread, sliced and toasted
- Cavolo nero (optional)

## Directions

1. Choose beans with fine skin or pass them; those with eyes have less of this defect than others.
2. Make a soffritto in this proportion: take a quarter onion, one garlic clove, a pinch of parsley and a nice piece of white celery. Chop these aromatics very finely with a crescent knife and put them on the fire with a good measure of oil; be generous with pepper.
3. When the soffritto has taken color, add two ladle-fuls of the bean broth, add a little tomato juice or paste, let them come to a boil and pour it into the pot of beans.
4. For those who like a little greens in the soup, you can add cavolo nero, first boiled and then cooked a bit in the liquid of the aforesaid soffritto.
5. Now all that remains is to dampen the bread, already prepared beforehand with thick slices toasted and then cut into cubes.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Gamberi col Sugo di Carne (Shrimp Soup with Meat Sauce)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp, 150 grams (for four people)
- Broth, two ladles
- Bone marrow, 30 grams, browned in butter
- Meat sauce (recipe N. 4) or meat for making it, 30 centesimi worth
- Bread, simply roasted or in cubes, fried in lard or oil
- Grated Parmesan

## Directions

1. Wash the shrimp and put them on the fire with two ladles of broth; once cooked, remove them dry and in the remaining liquid dissolve 30 grams of bone marrow browned in butter, to moisten them when you pass them through the sieve, after having pounded them in the mortar
2. Once all the pulp has been extracted, unite it to meat sauce like that of recipe N. 4 and if you do not have it in the kitchen you can make it with only 30 centesimi worth of meat suitable for that use
3. Mix now this mixture with the rest of the broth to moisten the soup, which can be simply roasted bread, or in cubes, fried in lard or oil
4. Serve it with grated Parmesan
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Limone (Lemon Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, 135 grams
- Egg yolks, 2
- Egg whites, 5
- Juice of one large lemon
- Water, half a glass
- Flour, one scant tablespoon
- Savoiardi biscuits or sponge cake

## Directions

1. Dilute the flour with water, whisking it well, pour it into a pan and add the rest.
2. Stir everything together and place the mixture on the fire, stirring continuously with the spoon as you do for cream.
3. When it is thickened, pass it through a sieve if necessary, then pour part of it into a dish, spread savoiardi or sponge cake over it, and cover it with the rest of the mixture.
4. Serve it cold.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di ovoli (Caesar's Mushroom Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Caesar's mushrooms (ovoli), 600 grams before cleaning (about 500 grams after)
- Lard, 50 grams
- Parsley, a pinch
- Butter, 50 grams
- Oil, three spoonfuls
- Salt, to taste
- One whole egg and one yolk
- Grated Parmesan cheese, a handful
- Toasted bread dice

## Directions

1. Clean and peel the mushrooms, then wash them whole and cut them into small, thin slices or pieces.
2. Make a soffritto (sauté mixture) with 50 grams of lard and a pinch of parsley and put it on the fire with 50 grams of butter and three spoonfuls of oil.
3. When it has softened, pour in the mushrooms and salt them somewhat to give them partial cooking, then pour them into the broth with all the soffritto to let them boil another ten minutes.
4. Before removing from heat, break a whole egg and one yolk in the tureen with a handful of grated Parmesan and pour the soup over it little by little stirring, then add dice of toasted bread; but note that the soup should remain very brothy.
5. This dose will serve six or seven persons.
6. If you make half the amount, only the whole egg will suffice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Pane d'Uovo (Egg Bread Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, 3
- Flour, 30 grams
- Butter, about the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Work the three egg yolks with the flour and butter
2. Add the three egg whites beaten until stiff
3. Cook the mixture in the oven or camp oven in a smooth mold with an oiled paper on the bottom
4. When the bread is cooked and cooled, cut it into cubes or small almond shapes
5. Pour hot broth over it
6. Serve with grated Parmesan cheese on the side
7. Serves six or seven people
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Purè di Piselli di Magro (Pea Puree Soup, Lean)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh shelled peas, 400 grams
- Fatty and lean prosciutto, 40 grams
- Butter, 40 grams
- One young onion no larger than an egg
- One small carrot
- A pinch of parsley, celery and some basil leaves
- Bread fried in butter
- Salt and pepper
- Water

## Directions

1. Finely chop the prosciutto with a knife and make a battuto with this and the other ingredients.
2. Put it on the fire with butter, a little salt and a pinch of pepper.
3. When it has browned, pour the water you judge sufficient to make the soup and when it has come to a boil throw in the peas to cook them together with two slices of bread fried in butter.
4. Then pass everything through a sieve.
5. Obtained in this way a puree for six people, dampen with the same the bread that you will have already prepared as in the fat purees.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Purè di Piselli Secchi (Soup of Dried Pea Purée)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried peas, 500 milliliters
- Water, 2 liters (plus water from cooking the peas)
- Half an onion
- One carrot
- Two pieces of celery, one finger in length
- Dill stems (if available)
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Tomato juice
- Bread, cut into small cubes
- Butter for frying the bread

## Directions

1. Put the peas on the fire in 2 liters of water
2. Make a soffritto with half an onion, one carrot, two pieces of celery and some dill stems if you have them; chop everything and put it on the fire with a piece of butter and let it take on color
3. Pour the half-cooked peas and drain them from the water, season with salt and pepper and let them absorb all the soffritto, then add tomato juice and the water from the peas to finish cooking them
4. Pass everything through a strainer and if the purée is too thick, add hot water; taste it and add another small piece of butter if needed
5. Cut the bread into small cubes and fry it in butter
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Purè di Piselli, di Grasso (Pea Puree Soup with Fat)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 grams of shelled peas
- Broth
- A bunch of parsley, celery, carrot, and some basil leaves
- Two slices of bread fried in butter
- Meat gravy, if available
- Fine, day-old bread for the soup, cut into cubes and fried in butter

## Directions

1. Cook the peas in broth with a bunch composed of parsley, celery, carrot, and some basil leaves, which you will then discard
2. When the peas are cooked, throw in two slices of bread fried in butter to soak them up
3. Pass everything through a sieve
4. Dilute this mixture with the necessary broth, add a little meat gravy if you have it
5. Serve the soup with fine, day-old bread cut into cubes and fried in butter
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Ranocchi (Frog Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Two dozen frogs, large ones
- Two cloves garlic
- Parsley
- Carrot
- Celery
- Basil, if desired
- Onion, if garlic is disliked
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil
- Tomatoes or tomato conserve
- Water
- Dried mushrooms
- Bread for toasting

## Directions

1. Remove the thighs from the frogs and set them aside.
2. Make a generous battuto (finely chopped mixture) with two cloves of garlic, parsley, carrot, celery, and basil if desired; or use onion if you dislike garlic.
3. Put this mixture on the fire with salt, pepper, and a good measure of oil.
4. When the garlic begins to take color, add the frogs.
5. Stir them from time to time so they do not stick.
6. When they have released a good portion of their liquid, add pieces of tomatoes or, if lacking, diluted tomato conserve with water.
7. Continue to boil, and finally add the necessary water to make soup, keeping everything on the fire until the frogs are cooked and falling apart.
8. Pass everything through a sieve, pressing well so that only the small bones remain.
9. Put the thighs set aside earlier to boil in a little of this strained broth.
10. Debone them when cooked and mix them into the soup along with pieces of dried mushrooms that have been softened.
11. Toast bread into slices, then cut into rather large dice.
12. Serve the soup with the toasted bread dice.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Visciole (Sour Cherry Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cherries, sufficient quantity
- Very little water
- Piece of cinnamon
- Sugar, as needed
- Thin slices of fine toasted bread, or sponge cake or savoiardi biscuits
- Rosolio liqueur

## Directions

1. Remove the pit from the quantity of sour cherries that you think sufficient and put them on the fire with very little water and a piece of cinnamon that you will then throw away.
2. When they begin to boil, add sugar as needed, stir gently so as not to damage them, and when they begin to form a syrup taste them to see if they have enough sugar and remove them from the fire when you see them shriveled and they have lost their rawness.
3. After you have lightly dipped the slices of bread or the savoiardi in rosolio, place them layer by layer together with the cherries in a dish or on a tray so that they form a dome.
4. You can also give this soup a more regular form in a smooth mold and keep it on ice before unmolding it, since in cherry season one begins to appreciate cooling foods.
5. One third of sugar by the weight of the cherries is sufficient.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di Zucca Gialla (Yellow Squash Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellow squash, peeled and cut into thin slices, one kilogramm
- Two ladles of broth
- 60 grams of butter
- Two level tablespoons of flour
- Fried bread cubes
- Grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Put the squash to cook with two ladles of broth, then pass it through a sieve
2. Make a roux over the fire with 60 grams of butter and two level tablespoons of flour, and when it takes on a golden color, stop the cooking with broth
3. Add the strained squash and the rest of the broth sufficient for six people
4. Pour it boiling over fried bread cubes and serve the soup at table with grated Parmesan cheese on the side
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa di zucca gialla (Yellow Squash Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellow squash, peeled and cut into thin slices, 1 kilogram
- Broth, two ladles
- Butter, 60 grams
- Oil, two level spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Put the squash to cook with two ladles of broth and then pass it through a sieve.
2. Make a mixture on the fire with 60 grams of butter and two level spoonfuls of oil.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa Inglese (English Trifle)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, 5 decilitres
- Sugar, 85 grams
- Flour or, better, powdered starch, 40 grams
- Egg yolks, 4
- Vanilla flavoring
- Butter, cold
- Fruit preserve (apricots, peaches, or quinces)
- Savoiardi biscuits, 120-130 grams
- White rosolio liqueur
- Alkermes liqueur

## Directions

1. Work the sugar with the egg yolks first, add the flour and lastly the milk a little at a time.
2. Place over high heat, stirring the spoon continuously; when you see it smoking, cover the coals with a shovelful of ash or move the pan to the corner of the stove if you do not want lumps to form.
3. Once it has thickened, continue to keep it on the fire for eight or ten minutes, then let it cool.
4. Take a fluted mold, grease it well with cold butter and begin to fill it as follows: if you have a good fruit preserve, such as apricots, peaches, or quinces, place this first at the bottom of the mold, then a layer of cream and one of savoiardi dipped in white rosolio.
5. If the mold has, for example, eighteen grooves, dip nine savoiardi in alkermes and nine in white rosolio and fill the grooves with them, alternating them.
6. Pour more cream and lay other savoiardi dipped in rosolio on top and repeat the operation until the mold is full.
7. Be careful not to soak the savoiardi too much in the rosolio or they will give it back; if the liqueur is too sweet, correct it with rum or cognac.
8. If the fruit preserve has hardened over time, soften it on the fire with a few spoonfuls of water, but pour it cold into the mold.
9. Let it sit in ice for at least three hours, then unmold it by immersing the mold in hot water for a moment so the butter melts.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa Regina (Queen's Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- White meat of roasted chicken, cleaned of skin and tendons
- Five or six shelled sweet almonds
- One marrow from bread soaked in broth or milk
- Broth
- Bread cut into cubes
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese
- Optional: some cooked egg yolks softened in broth

## Directions

1. Finely chop the white meat of roasted chicken with a crescent knife, then pound it in a mortar with five or six shelled sweet almonds and with a marrow of bread soaked in broth or milk, in proportion of one fifth or one sixth of the quantity of meat
2. When the mixture is well pounded, pass it through a sieve, put it in the soup tureen and dissolve it with a ladle of hot broth
3. Cut the bread into cubes, fry it in butter and also throw this into the soup tureen
4. After, pour in the boiling broth, stir and send the soup to table with Parmesan on the side
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa ripiena (Filled Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Half breast of capon or large chicken
- A slice of fat and lean ham
- A small piece of marrow
- Grated Parmesan cheese
- Nutmeg
- One egg
- Stale bread, a small loaf
- Virgin lard or oil or butter for frying

## Directions

1. Mince together the half breast of capon or chicken, the slice of ham, and the piece of marrow.
2. Season it with grated Parmesan, give it the scent of nutmeg and bind it with an egg. Salt is not necessary as there is ham.
3. Take a small loaf of stale bread, slice it round to the thickness of half a finger, remove the crust from the slices and on half of them spread the aforesaid mixture.
4. On each of these spread slices, lay a slice without mixture and press them together so they stick.
5. Then cut these paired slices into small dice and fry them in virgin lard or oil or butter, according to the taste of the region or your own.
6. When it is time to serve the soup at table, place the fried dice in the tureen and pour boiling broth over them.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa Santé (Health Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Various vegetables, for example carrots, sorrel, celery, and white cabbage
- Salt
- Pepper
- A piece of butter
- Broth
- Fine, day-old bread (at least one day old), cut into small cubes
- Butter or virgin oil or lard for frying the bread

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage like taglierini and make it release water over the fire, squeezing it well
2. Cut the carrots and celery into strips about three centimeters long, and together with the cabbage and sorrel cleaned of stems, put them on the fire with a little salt, a pinch of pepper, and a piece of butter
3. When the vegetables have drawn out the fat, finish cooking them with broth
4. Meanwhile prepare the bread, which should be of fine quality and day-old at least; cut it into small cubes and fry it in butter or also in virgin oil or lard; but so that it absorbs little fat, keep the latter abundant and throw in the bread when it is boiling well, or roast it only as thick slices and cut it into cubes afterward
5. Put the bread in the soup tureen, pour over it the boiling broth together with the vegetables, and send the soup immediately to table
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa sul Sugo di Carne (Soup with Meat Gravy)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- About 500 grams of beef suitable for making gravy
- Some chicken necks
- Kitchen scraps if available
- Meat gravy
- Vegetables in good measure: celery, carrot, cabbage, sorrel, zucchini, peas, etc., according to the season
- One potato
- Butter
- Bread slices half a finger thick
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare a good meat gravy (see N. 5)
2. Cut the potato and zucchini into chunks, all the others into strips
3. Boil all the vegetables and then sauté them in butter, wetting them with the said gravy
4. Keep the bread slices half a finger thick, toast them and cut them into cubes
5. Take a pan or, better, a similar vessel, decent enough because it must be brought to table, and in this prepare the soup in the following manner: a layer of bread, one of vegetables and over it a sprinkling of Parmesan, and so on
6. Finally pour the gravy over it and, without touching it, cover it with a plate and a towel and keep it for half an hour in warmth near the fire before serving it
7. Note that this soup should remain almost dry, so it is well to hold back a little gravy to add when you send it to table, in case it turns out too dry
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa Tartara (Tartar Soup)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Ricotta, 200 grams
- Milk
- Powdered sugar, 30 grams
- Ground cinnamon, two pinches
- Savoiardi biscuits
- Rosolio or alkermes liqueur
- Fruit preserve (apricots or peaches), not too liquid
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take 200 grams of ricotta, soften it somewhat with milk and season it with 30 grams of powdered sugar and two pinches of ground cinnamon, mixing well.
2. Take a worked mold and wet the internal walls with rosolio or grease them with butter.
3. Dip savoiardi in rosolio or alkermes and, starting with these or with a not-too-liquid fruit preserve, cover the bottom of the mold.
4. Fill it, alternating in layers, with the ricotta, the savoiardi, and the preserve, which can be apricots or peaches.
5. Unmold it after a few hours and, if you have arranged it carefully, in addition to the taste the eyes of the diners will also be satisfied.
6. The ricotta can be softened with citron rosolio instead of milk, and then cinnamon is not needed.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene

title: Zuppa Toscana di Magro alla Contadina (Tuscan Lean Soup Peasant Style)
author: Pellegrino Artusi
body:
## Ingredients

- Stale brown bread of soft dough, 400 grams
- White beans, 300 grams
- Oil, 150 grams
- Water, two liters
- Cabbage or savoy cabbage, half a head of medium size
- Black cabbage, the same amount in volume or more
- A bunch of beet greens and a little thyme
- One potato
- Some strips of dried pork or prosciutto rind

## Directions

1. Put the beans on the fire with the water mentioned, adding the rind.
2. While they boil, make a mixture with a quarter of a large onion and two cloves of garlic, two pieces of celery a palm long and a good pinch of parsley.
3. Chop it finely, put it on the fire with the oil mentioned above and when it has taken color, pour into it the vegetables cut roughly, first the cabbages, then the beet greens and the potato cut into pieces.
4. Season with salt and pepper and then add tomato juice or conserve, and if during boiling they become somewhat dry, moisten them with the bean broth.
5. When the beans are cooked, throw a quarter of them, left whole, among the vegetables, adding the rinds; pass the others through a sieve and dissolve them in the broth, pouring also this into the vessel where the vegetables are.
6. Mix, let boil a little more and pour everything into the tureen where you have already placed the bread cut into thin slices and cover it to serve after about twenty minutes.
7. This quantity may be enough for six people; it is good hot and better cold.
source_title: La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene
